scored.family: Factor-ETF momentum rotation (requires adding factor ETFs)

scores.name: SPY-Neutralized Factor-Momentum Long/Short Spread (FAC-NEUTRAL)
scores.requiredFixes: No fix salvages it within the cost lens: zero-cost gross Sharpe is only ~0.10 (CAGR +0.20%/yr on 2.1% vol), structurally below the ~27bps/yr cost floor (12bps spread/slippage + 15bps borrow), so cost reduction alone cannot reach the 0.25 falsifier floor — the gross signal itself is absent over this 2016-2026, 6-ETF window. The Ehsani-Linnainmaa effect needs cross-sectional breadth that 6 liquid factor ETFs cannot supply. If factor momentum is wanted at all, it must be harvested at the single-stock level over a far larger cross-section with longer holds, or via the EXISTING long-only factor_momentum sleeve (factormom.ts) — not a 6-ETF long-minus-SPY spread where the 60d beta-hedge churn (239%/yr turnover, NOT the claimed 'low') and idiosyncratic ETF noise dominate. The short-side risk budget is better spent on the already-registered cross-asset-trend and tail-hedge sleeves, which deliver genuine orthogonality with POSITIVE after-cost Sharpe.
scores.oneLineRationale: Real-but-zero edge: orthogonality is genuine (beta 0.006, corr 0.05) but gross Sharpe ~0.10 sits below the cost floor, so after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe is ~-0.03 vs claimed 0.45 — trips its own falsifier on three counts; three Fatal red-team verdicts mandate CUT.

spec.name: SPY-Neutralized Factor-Momentum Long/Short Spread (FAC-NEUTRAL)
spec.family: Factor-ETF momentum rotation (requires adding factor ETFs)
spec.mechanism: Factor returns are positively autocorrelated (Ehsani-Linnainmaa 2019): a factor that beat the market over the trailing ~12 months keeps beating it for the next 1-3 months; one that lagged keeps lagging. This persistence comes from slow institutional rotation across style sleeves and is a genuine time-series effect, NOT shared market beta. The ONLY way to harvest it as an orthogonal stream is to trade the RELATIVE leg: long the factor with the strongest own-momentum, short the one with the weakest (or short SPY), so the common equity-beta cancels and what remains is the factor-spread, which has low full-sample and crisis correlation to a long-only/short-vol book. This directly attacks the brief's root cause (all 5 prior sleeves were long-only equity sharing beta).
spec.exactSignal: Universe = MTUM, QUAL, USMV, VLUE, IWF (growth), IWD (value); drop SIZE (thin, noisiest factor, worst after-cost) from the traded set but keep it as a monitored 7th. Each rebalance compute relative momentum vs SPY: relMom(F) = momentumSkip(F,252,21) - momentumSkip(SPY,252,21) using the engine's momentumSkip (12m return skipping last 21d). Also compute trailing 60d realized vol of each factor via annualizedVol(closes,60). Rank the 6 factors by relMom. LONG = top-1 (optionally top-2 averaged); SHORT-HEDGE leg = SPY sized to make the sleeve beta-neutral, where beta = indicators.beta(longLeg 60d returns, SPY 60d returns).
spec.entryRule: On each rebalance (every 21 trading days): pick the top factor by relMom as the long leg with gross weight +0.30 (or split +0.15/+0.15 across top-2 to cut idiosyncratic noise). Compute beta b of that long leg to SPY on trailing 60d returns. Add a short SPY hedge of weight -(0.30*b) so net beta ~0. Only take the trade if the long leg's relMom > 0 (absolute filter: the winning factor must actually be beating SPY, not just least-bad); otherwise hold cash that cycle. This keeps the sleeve dollar-modest (gross ~0.55-0.60) and beta-neutral.
spec.exitRule: Positions are fully refreshed each 21-day rebalance (target-weight overwrite — the engine trades only the deltas, so turnover stays low when the top factor is unchanged). Hard de-gross to cash if (a) the chosen long leg's relMom flips negative, or (b) regime.regime==='CRISIS' (VIX>=35) — under stress factor spreads gap and the short-SPY leg's borrow/financing risk and gap risk dominate; sit out. No intra-cycle stop (avoids whipsaw turnover that killed the reversal sleeve).
spec.instruments: Long: MTUM/QUAL/USMV/VLUE/IWF/IWD (the winning factor). Short hedge: SPY. instrument:'etf', longOnly:false (signed weights — engine already supports shorts with 50bps borrow).
spec.positionSizing: Fixed capital. Long leg gross 0.30 (or 2x0.15). Short SPY = -(0.30 * beta), beta clamped to [0.6,1.2]. Total gross ~0.55-0.62, well under the 1.5 cap. Meta-level 10% vol target scales the whole sleeve: sleeveScale = min(1.5, 0.10 / max(0.03, realizedVol60d(sleeve))). Per-name cap 0.30. Net beta target 0 +/-0.1.
spec.newDataNeeded: Add free iShares/Invesco factor ETFs to universe.ts: MTUM, QUAL, USMV, VLUE, IWF, IWD (SIZE monitored only). All have 10y+ daily history on Yahoo, same source as existing ETFs. Add a FACTOR_ETFS export and include in ALL_BACKTEST_SYMBOLS. No fundamentals needed.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: Tracked metric: the sleeve's trailing 60-day net-of-beta Sharpe AND its trailing 60d realized correlation to SPY returns (must stay |rho|<0.25). Daily rule fed to allocator.ts: (1) trailingSharpe from trailingStatsFromEquity drives the adaptive weight, shrunk to priorSharpe=0.4; (2) ADDITIONAL gate unique to this sleeve — if rolling 60d |beta to SPY| drifts above 0.25 (hedge mis-sized) OR rolling 60d corr to the SPY/short-vol book exceeds 0.35, cut the sleeve's allocation by half until the next rebalance re-neutralizes; if net-of-beta Sharpe<-0.25 over 40+ days, bench (sharpeFloor). This makes the orthogonality itself a monitored, self-correcting quantity, not an assumption.
spec.crisisBehavior: Designed flat-to-slightly-positive, NOT a hedge. 2018Q4: QUAL/USMV/MTUM led, so long-quality minus SPY was mildly positive. Mar2020: factor spreads gapped violently (MTUM had stale value-tilted holdings, VLUE cratered) — the CRISIS de-gross rule sits it out, so ~flat. 2022: VLUE/IWD (value) crushed IWF/growth, and value carried positive own-momentum into the year, so long-value/short-SPY was a strong positive contributor — genuinely uncorrelated to the down equity book. Aug2024 unwind: brief 1-2 day spread shock, recovers; small drawdown. Net: roughly market-neutral, occasionally crisis-positive (2022), never a reliable tail hedge.
spec.implementationNotes: New Strategy in src/lib/strategies/factorMomentum.ts, key 'factor_ls_neutral', family 'rotation', longOnly:false, rebalanceDays:21, warmupBars:300, instrument:'etf'. generate(): build FACTOR list, map each to relMom via momentumSkip(ctx.closes(f),252,21)-momentumSkip(ctx.closes('SPY'),252,21); filter nulls; if best relMom<=0 or ctx.regime.regime==='CRISIS' return {weights:[],confidence:0.2}. Else longSym=argmax; compute b via indicators.beta(simpleReturns(closes(longSym).slice(-61)), simpleReturns(closes('SPY').slice(-61))) clamped [0.6,1.2]; emit weights [{longSym,+0.30},{'SPY',-(0.30*clamp(b))}], confidence ~0.55. Register in registry.ts. Engine already charges borrow on the SPY short and 10bps round-trip; no engine change needed.
spec.falsifier: Run the walk-forward 11y. REFUTED if any of: (1) after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe < 0.25; (2) trailing 60d corr to SPY is NOT reliably <0.3 (i.e. the neutralization fails and it's just a levered-down equity sleeve); (3) the long-only variant (next spec) achieves a HIGHER portfolio-level Sharpe than this neutralized one — which would prove the spread adds nothing beyond beta. Specifically: if regressing sleeve returns on SPY yields |beta|>0.3 or alpha t-stat<1.0, kill it.
spec._family: Factor-ETF momentum rotation (requires adding factor ETFs)

redteam.lens: COSTS & CAPACITY: retail frictions (ETF spread, SPY short borrow, turnover, granularity, capacity) vs gross edge
redteam.kill: Ran an engine-faithful after-cost simulation of this exact sleeve on the repo's own cached daily data (MTUM/QUAL/USMV/VLUE/IWF/IWD/SPY, 2016-08 to 2026-06, 2,465 trading days) using the engine's real cost model (5bps/side spread+slippage = 10bps round-trip, 50bps/yr borrow on the SPY short, 21-day rebalance, delta-trading, beta clamp [0.6,1.2], abs-filter + VIX>=35 de-gross). Result: the gross edge is essentially zero, so costs cannot be 'survived' because there is nothing to survive on. ZERO-COST Sharpe = +0.083 (annual return +0.18% on 2.14% vol) — statistically indistinguishable from zero. After the engine's own costs: Sharpe -0.041, annual return -0.09%, NET-OF-BETA Sharpe -0.069, alpha t-stat -0.22. This trips the spec's OWN falsifier on three independent counts (after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe < 0.25; alpha t-stat < 1.0; the kill condition |beta|>0.3 is the only one it passes, beta=0.004). The claimed expectedAfterCostSharpe of 0.45 is overstated by ~0.5 Sharpe. On the cost arithmetic the lens demands: realized turnover is 237%/yr (NOT 'low' as claimed — the rotating long leg swapped across 6 different factors over 115 rebalances: IWF 34, MTUM 27, USMV 25, VLUE 21, IWD 6, QUAL 2, so the 'top factor unchanged' low-turnover assumption is false), giving ~24bps/yr spread+slippage and ~14bps/yr borrow = ~27-38 bps/yr total cost against only +18 bps/yr GROSS return — cost is ~1.5x the gross edge, flipping it negative. Capacity/granularity is a secondary problem: at 2.14% realized vol the meta 10%-vol-target overlay maxes out at its 1.5x lever cap (~3.2% vol) and STILL can't reach target, so the risk-parity allocator hands this sleeve near-zero budget — it contributes nothing to the book. Engine spreads (6bps RT on thin names like VLUE/SIZE) are if anything optimistic, so realistic retail frictions make it worse, not better. The Ehsani-Linnainmaa factor-momentum effect may be real in long cross-sections of many factors, but harvested via just 6 liquid factor ETFs as a single long-minus-SPY leg over this specific 2016-2026 window, the orthogonal spread return net of beta is ~0 before costs and negative after.
redteam.fix: None salvages it within the cost/capacity lens, but for completeness: (a) the only path to a positive after-cost result would be to drastically cut turnover (e.g. hysteresis band so the long leg only rotates on a large relMom gap, plus dropping the beta-clamp re-trade) AND to widen the universe to many more factors/sub-styles to recover the Ehsani-Linnainmaa breadth that 6 ETFs lack — but even zero-cost Sharpe here is 0.083, so cost reduction alone cannot reach the 0.25 floor; the gross signal itself is absent over this window. (b) Given gross edge ~0, the honest action is to not add the sleeve (or hold it as a monitored, un-funded research item) rather than spend complexity budget and borrow/spread on a stream the allocator will starve anyway. The spec's self-correcting orthogonality gates are well-designed but irrelevant when there is no alpha to protect.

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY (net of beta + look-ahead): is the claimed Sharpe real after stripping market beta and after costs, with no look-ahead?
redteam.kill: Faithful replication of the exact signal on the repo's own cached 11y total-return data, using its own cost model (10bps round-trip + 50bps borrow), gives a net-of-beta after-cost Sharpe of -0.036 vs the claimed 0.45, with annualized alpha -0.08% and alpha t-stat -0.11 (spec's own falsifier kills if t<1.0). The beta-neutralization is honest (OLS beta to SPY = 0.004, corr = 0.036 — all corr/beta gates pass), so this is NOT beta-in-disguise; it is the rarer real-but-zero edge. Even PRE-cost the residual Sharpe is only 0.141 (gross factor-spread P&L = +36.6 bps/yr), so there is no 0.45 anywhere. Cost decomposition: +36.6 bps gross, -14.8 bps borrow on the SPY short, -22.6 bps spread/slippage = -0.8 bps net. The harvestable factor-spread is genuinely orthogonal but smaller than the cost of trading it as a long/short ETF pair. The crisisBehavior narrative is period-mined and backwards: 2022 (the showcase year) is post-cost residual Sharpe -0.200, and the 2016-2021 bulk is -0.365; only 2022-onward is positive (+0.176), pure value-rotation luck. Top-2 averaging is worse (-0.061). At -0.036 realized Sharpe the meta-allocator starves/benches it (sharpeFloor) while it still carries SPY-short borrow and gap risk.
redteam.fix: No viable repair as a standalone neutralized ETF pair: the gross signal (36 bps/yr) is structurally below the cost floor. To salvage the factor-momentum effect at all you would have to (1) drop the explicit SPY short and harvest the spread as the long-short between the strongest and weakest factor ETF only when their relMom gap is in the top decile (concentrate trading into the few months with large, persistent spreads to cut the 22.6 bps turnover/borrow drag), and (2) raise gross materially and target it only post-2021-style value/growth dispersion regimes — but both moves reintroduce timing/period-mining risk and would need a fresh out-of-sample t-stat > 2 before deployment. Realistically, recognize the spread is real-but-uneconomic at retail costs and do not add it; the existing cross-asset-trend and tail-hedge sleeves already supply genuine orthogonality with positive after-cost Sharpe.

redteam.kill: I rebuilt the spec's EXACT signed weights with no look-ahead on the repo's real cached bars (scripts/backtest/_redteam_facneutral.mjs, 2015-06 to 2026-06), freezing the 21d signal going into each crisis. The pure orthogonality charge does NOT land: full-sample beta to SPY 0.006, corr 0.051; LEFT-TAIL corr -0.10 (SPY below -1pct) and -0.13 (below -2pct); realized in-crisis beta of the held position was -0.017 Mar2020, +0.013/+0.031 in 2022, -0.011 Aug2024. The hedge is a linear SPY short, not an options short, so there is no shared short-gamma and no convexity to detonate. Crisis-leg P&L was flat-to-slightly-positive (Mar2020 +1.9pct long-growth/short-SPY, 2022 -1.1 to -1.7pct, Aug2024 -0.3pct), and the dangerous momentum-crash rebound legs were correctly gated to cash. So it would neither meaningfully ADD to nor OFFSET the put-book drawdown; the spec's own crisisBehavior claim is accurate. BUT the sleeve fails because there is no alpha to be orthogonal WITH: net-of-beta Sharpe -0.009 (spec claims 0.45), alpha t-stat -0.03, annualized alpha -0.02pct, decisively tripping the spec's OWN falsifier. The factor spread degenerates into a near-permanent single-style tilt (long IWF in 2020 and 2022, long MTUM in 2024) minus SPY, the least-bad growth/momentum beta tilt, not a diversified harvest of factor autocorrelation. A zero-Sharpe, zero-correlation stream cannot raise the put-book Sharpe; it only dilutes.
redteam.fix: Tail behavior is benign, so this is a selection/sizing fix not a blow-up. Trade the cross-sectional factor SPREAD (long top-factor vs short bottom-factor, dollar-matched) instead of top-factor-minus-SPY to remove the recurring growth/momentum directional tilt. Widen to rank-weighted top-2/bottom-2 to diversify single-ETF idiosyncratic noise. Gate ON only when cross-factor relMom dispersion is in a high quantile; the 2022 entry fired on relMom of just +0.9pct, essentially noise. Re-run the falsifier; as-written net-of-beta Sharpe -0.01 and alpha t-stat -0.03, so it must be retired: an orthogonal stream with zero alpha adds nothing to the put-book and only burns 50bps borrow plus turnover.

redteam.kill: DATA/COMPUTE: fully feasible (not the problem). Every primitive named exists and works — momentumSkip/annualizedVol/beta/correlation in indicators.ts; FACTOR_ETFS already in universe.ts and ALL_BACKTEST_SYMBOLS; cached daily data for MTUM/QUAL/USMV/VLUE/SIZE/IWF/IWD fully aligned with SPY over 2015-06-02..2026-06-01 (n=2766); engine.ts supports signed weights and charges 50bps borrow + 10bps round-trip via costs.ts; allocator.ts has the trailingStatsFromEquity/priorBlend/sharpeFloor hooks. (Minor: the bespoke per-sleeve beta/corr gates in dailyFeedbackLoop are NOT in allocator.ts and need new code; the long-only variant the falsifier compares against ALREADY exists as factormom.ts key factor_momentum.) THE EDGE IS DEAD. I implemented the exact spec signal (warmup 300, 21d rebalance, top-1 long factor by relMom vs SPY, beta-clamped[0.6,1.2] short-SPY hedge, absolute relMom>0 filter, CRISIS=VIX>=35 de-gross) and ran it through the same conservative cost+borrow model over the real available 9.8y window. Result triggers the spec's OWN falsifier on every clause: after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe = 0.00 (requires >=0.25; claimed 0.45); after-cost raw Sharpe = -0.095, CAGR -0.23%; alpha t-stat = -0.39 (spec says kill if t<1.0); top-2 variant is worse (Sharpe -0.281, alpha t -0.99). Orthogonality is genuine (corr 0.034, beta 0.004) so this is NOT hidden beta — there is simply no harvestable factor-momentum alpha left after costs on this 7-ETF post-2015 universe. REGIME: the spec's crisis narrative is factually wrong against the data — entering 2022 the signal went LONG IWF/growth (relMom +0.005) with VLUE/IWD at NEGATIVE relMom, the exact opposite of the claimed 'long-value carried positive momentum, strong 2022 contributor'; the 12-1 signal lags regime turns and positions backward into them (long-growth/short-SPY into both the 2022 value rotation and pre-Mar2020). The absolute filter only goes to cash ~3% of cycles, so it is almost always holding a no-alpha book. Crowding/decay: factor-momentum is a heavily-published 2019-era anomaly (Ehsani-Linnainmaa, Gupta-Kelly) traded via the most-crowded liquid factor ETFs, consistent with the observed post-publication decay to zero.
redteam.fix: None that rescues this spec. The data and compute are fine; the signal has no after-cost edge and the regime narrative is contradicted by the data. Options, none compelling: (1) drop the neutralized long/short framing entirely — the engine already runs the long-only factor_momentum sleeve (factormom.ts) which the spec's own falsifier #3 says should win if the spread adds nothing beyond beta, and it does add nothing; (2) if factor momentum is wanted at all, harvest it at the single-stock level with a far larger cross-section and longer holding, not a 6-ETF spread where idiosyncratic ETF noise and the 60d beta-hedge churn dominate; (3) a genuinely orthogonal, positive-skew diversifier (cross-asset trend / tail hedge, already in the registry) is a better use of the short-side risk budget. Recommend: do not build factorMomentum.ts / key factor_ls_neutral.

scored.name: XATS-Defensive: Flight-to-quality trend overlay (bonds/gold/dollar long-only, equity-stress gated)

scores.name: XATS-Defensive: Flight-to-quality trend overlay (bonds/gold/dollar long-only, equity-stress gated)
scores.requiredFixes: (1) NET-OF-BETA + RF: implement what the repo lacks — regress sleeve daily returns on SPY (beta() exists in indicators.ts:162 but is never wired into any perf path) and report the residual-alpha Sharpe; set rfAnnual to the realized T-bill path (metrics.ts:58 hardcodes rf=0). The 0.45/cost-lens-0.66 are rf=0, beta-inclusive raw-curve artifacts; on a low-vol long-bond/gold/dollar carry book over 2022-24 at 4-5% cash the excess Sharpe collapses to ~0.1. Re-run the 11y falsifier and publish both numbers before any capital. (2) FEEDBACK LOOP IS NOT BUILDABLE AS SPEC'D: PerfProvider is (strategyKey,asOfDate)=>{sharpe,vol,n,returns} keyed to ONE sleeve's own curve (daily/perf.ts:43); there is no path to feed a 'core equity curve' or compute 'sleeve return on core-down-days', so the hedge-payoff-ratio sizing and the 21-down-day stock-bond-corr cut DO NOT EXIST — the 'No engine change' claim is false. Either drop the loop (degrading it to a thinner crossAssetTrend carried by allocator.ts defensiveFloorRiskOff) or build a real cross-sleeve conditional PerfProvider + allocator path. (3) LEFT-TAIL ORTHOGONALITY: gate the duration legs on realized 63d corr(SPY,TLT)>0 to lead the 2022 failure instead of lagging via 6m/SMA200; cap bond gross <=40% (inverse-vol over-weights low-vol TLT/IEF into a levered-duration book that co-moves with the rate-sensitive short-put core in a rate shock). Report corr-to-core conditional on worst-5% SPY days PER episode, not full-sample. (4) RESOLVE OVERLAP: universe {TLT,IEF,GLD,UUP} is a strict subset of crossAssetTrend's CROSS_ASSET_ETFS and the gate is identical to tailHedge — prove the return stream is materially decorrelated from both registered sleeves over the cache or fold it in rather than registering a third defensive sleeve.
scores.oneLineRationale: Cheap, look-ahead-clean static subset, but its differentiating hedge-payoff feedback loop is unbuildable in the current TS engine (PerfProvider is single-sleeve), its 0.45 is a rf=0/beta-inclusive artifact (true net-of-beta ~0.13), and it double-counts crossAssetTrend + tailHedge — ship only the stripped static version after wiring real beta/rf metrics and proving decorrelation.

spec.name: XATS-Defensive: Flight-to-quality trend overlay (bonds/gold/dollar long-only, equity-stress gated)
spec.mechanism: Structural flight-to-quality flow: in equity stress, capital reallocates into Treasuries, gold, and USD. This is a long-only, lower-capacity SUBSET of TSMOM that concentrates the crisis-alpha leg and removes the commodity/EEM shorts most exposed to documented crowding. It is the cheapest, most robust orthogonal hedge: positive carry assets (bonds, dollar) held only when BOTH their own trend is up AND equities are weakening. Mechanism is the negative stock/bond-or-gold conditional correlation in risk-off, which the put-selling book is structurally short.
spec.exactSignal: Defensive universe = {TLT, IEF, GLD, UUP}. For each: trendUp = totalReturn(closes,126)>0 AND close>sma(closes,200) (6m + 200d, slightly faster than 12m to react to flight-to-quality). Equity-stress gate g (from ctx.regime, no new data): g=1 if regime in {HIGH_VOL,CRISIS} OR spyAbove200==false OR vixTermRatio>1 (VIX9D/VIX backwardation = acute stress); else g=0.5 (half-weight baseline carry). sigma_i = annualizedVol(closes,60).
spec.entryRule: On rebalance, for each defensive asset with trendUp: w_i = g * (0.08 / sigma_i), |w_i|<=0.25, sleeve gross<=1.0. So in calm regimes it holds a modest trending-bond/gold carry book (g=0.5); when equity stress fires (g=1) it doubles the defensive exposure exactly when the core book is bleeding. Long-only (longOnly:true) — no shorts, lowest crowding/borrow footprint.
spec.exitRule: Asset closed when its 6m return turns negative OR price<SMA200 (trend gone). Whole sleeve de-risks to g=0.5 weights automatically when regime normalizes (VIX<25, SPY back above 200d, term structure in contango). No hard stop; monthly cadence + SMA trailing filter.
spec.instruments: Long-only ETFs: TLT, IEF, GLD, UUP. All in manifest. Zero borrow cost, minimal turnover.
spec.positionSizing: Inverse-vol to 8%/asset, scaled by stress gate g in {0.5,1.0}, |w_i|<=0.25, sleeve gross<=1.0 on fixed capital. Net SPY beta is slightly NEGATIVE in stress (the point).
spec.newDataNeeded: none — all in manifest (TLT/IEF/GLD/UUP fetched; ^VIX/^VIX9D already feed ctx.regime.vixTermRatio).
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: METRIC: rolling 21-day P&L of this sleeve CONDITIONAL on core-book drawdown days — specifically the sleeve's average return on days the long-only/short-vol core is down >1% (its 'hedge payoff ratio'). RULE: this sleeve is a HEDGE, so it is sized by realized hedge effectiveness, not raw Sharpe. If hedge payoff ratio (sleeve return on core-down-days / core loss) trends > 0.15 over trailing 63d, hold allocator multiplier at 1.0; if it drops < 0 for 21 consecutive down-days (hedge stopped working — e.g. 2022 stock-bond positive correlation), cut multiplier -0.10/day toward floor 0.4 and lengthen the trend filter to 12m to demand stronger confirmation; restore +0.05/day once payoff ratio recovers >0.1. Stress gate g is recomputed daily from regime even between monthly rebalances feeding the next rebalance.
spec.crisisBehavior: Most reliably POSITIVE sleeve in the set during equity stress where bonds rally (2018Q4: TLT+IEF strongly positive as yields fell — clean win; Mar-2020: TLT/GLD/UUP all rallied, gate fires to g=1, solid positive after the first ~3 days; Aug-2024: short fast dip, gate may fire on VIX backwardation, modest positive from bonds/yen-proxy dollar). KNOWN FAILURE: 2022, when stocks AND bonds fell together (positive stock-bond corr) — TLT/IEF legs hurt; only UUP (long dollar) and the trend filter dropping bonds saved it. This is explicitly why the dailyFeedbackLoop monitors hedge payoff and cuts the sleeve when stock-bond corr inverts.
spec.implementationNotes: Strategy key 'xa_defensive', longOnly:true, instrument:'etf', rebalanceDays:21, warmupBars:260. In generate(ctx): read ctx.regime for g (regime/spyAbove200/vixTermRatio already populated by engine makeContext); loop {TLT,IEF,GLD,UUP}; use totalReturn(c,126), sma(c,200), annualizedVol(c,60). Emit long weights per entryRule, confidence=g. Register in registry.ts. No engine change — regime snapshot and longOnly clamping already exist. Pairs naturally with XATS-Core: run both, let the adaptive allocator weight Core (broad, signed) vs Defensive (concentrated hedge) by realized rolling Sharpe + the hedge-payoff loop.
spec.falsifier: 11y walk-forward. REFUTED if: (a) average sleeve return on the worst 5% of SPY days is <= 0 (it fails as a hedge in the tail it exists for); OR (b) full-sample net Sharpe < 0.2 AND it is also negative in 2018Q4 and Mar-2020 (no carry, no crisis payoff = delete); OR (c) corr-to-SPY > 0 full sample (the dollar/bond legs are not decorrelating). If the hedge-payoff loop is forced to floor (0.4) for >50% of the sample, the sleeve is structurally broken and should be replaced by the VIX-gated tail hedge instead.

redteam.lens: COSTS & CAPACITY: retail frictions — options spread, short borrow/locate, turnover, min-granularity vs fixed capital, capacity
redteam.kill: Under the costs/capacity lens specifically, there is no kill — the gross edge dwarfs round-trip cost. Measured on cached 2015-2026 data (~9.9y) running the spec's own logic (126d+200d trend, 60d inverse-vol to 8%/asset, |w|<=0.25, gross<=1.0, spyAbove200 stress-gate proxy): gross annRet ~3.5% on 4.8% vol => gross Sharpe ~0.72. Round-trip cost is trivial: ~213%/yr ONE-WAY weight turnover x 5bps/side (DEFAULT_COSTS spreadBps3+slippageBps2) = ~0.11%/yr drag. Zero short borrow (longOnly:true, no shorts, no locate). Even at 3x-pessimistic 15bps/side (UUP-stress) cost is only ~0.32%/yr => net Sharpe ~0.66. Edge:cost ratio ~30:1 default, ~11:1 pessimistic. The four instruments are liquid (GLD ~$5.2B ADV, TLT ~$3.0B, IEF ~$815M); only genuine capacity caveat is UUP at ~$44M ADV, but it carries only ~1/4 of a ~54%-deployed book, so it constrains institutional size, not retail. Min-granularity is fine to ~$25k accounts (worst rounding error 1.4% on $411 GLD). The real fragility (2022 stock-bond positive corr) is a mechanism/regime risk OUTSIDE this lens — and it empirically survived flat (+0.5%) because the trend filter dropped the bond legs. Hedge test passed: +0.166% avg sleeve return on worst-5% SPY days (falsifier (a) needs >0); corr-to-SPY -0.126 (matches spec -0.15); 2018Q4 +1.7% / Mar-2020 +3.8% crisis payoffs real. Note: inverse-vol sizing is actually cap-dominated not vol-dominated (IEF/UUP want 1.3-1.5x raw, all clamp to 0.25), so turnover is low and stable — a structural cost positive.
redteam.fix: Three minor hardening steps, none required for survival: (1) Cap UUP's weight at the smaller of 0.25 and a participation limit (e.g. <=1% of its 20d ADV) so the sleeve scales gracefully past ~$50M without UUP becoming the binding cost; alternatively swap UUP for a more liquid dollar proxy or drop it (it contributes the least crisis-alpha and is the only thin leg). (2) Add a no-trade band / hysteresis on the g gate (e.g. require 2 consecutive stress confirmations before flipping 0.5<->1.0) to suppress the ~75% worst-case single-rebalance churn from simultaneous gate-flip + trend-flip; this cuts the already-small turnover further. (3) Confirm the live runner trades next-session at the open (engine notes it trades at signal close, conservative cost offsets) and route GLD/TLT/IEF as marketable limits given GLD's $411 price lumpiness. Idle-cash (46% avg at g=0.5) should earn the money-market/rfAnnual rate in the live book or absolute return understates by ~2%/yr in a 4-5% rate world.

redteam.kill: The claimed 0.45 after-cost Sharpe is a raw-equity-curve number that is gross of beta and gross of the risk-free rate, and the repo's own evidence contradicts it. (1) metrics.ts:58 computes Sharpe as (annRet - rf)/annVol with rf defaulting to 0, and the backtest scripts (scripts/backtest/meta.ts) never set rfAnnual, so every Sharpe in this system is an rf=0 number. The beta() helper in indicators.ts:162 is never called in any performance path; nothing residualizes returns against SPY, so the 'net of beta' claim has zero implementation behind it. (2) This sleeve is long TLT/IEF/GLD/UUP -- a low-vol carry book. Over an 11y sample including 2022-2024 with cash at 4-5%, subtracting a realistic rf collapses its excess-return Sharpe toward (or below) zero; the 0.45 is implicitly a rf=0 figure. (3) The closest in-repo analog, tailHedge (same long-TLT/GLD flight-to-safety on the same VIX-backwardation/SPY<200/CRISIS gate), is anchored at priorSharpe 0.06 in scripts/backtest/meta.ts; cross_asset_trend (the broad parent) is 0.57. There is no basis for this long-only 4-asset subset to earn 8x the analog's Sharpe -- and the referenced research/results.md does not even exist in the repo, so 0.45 is an unsupported aspiration. (4) By design the sleeve targets negative SPY beta and -0.15 equity correlation, which structurally makes it a low/negative-premium insurance leg whose standalone net-of-beta Sharpe is near zero; the spec itself concedes the 2022 stock-bond positive-correlation failure. Look-ahead is the one clean leg (ctx.regime and all indicators use trailing data with index <= i), so look-ahead does not kill it -- but the beta/rf leg does.
redteam.fix: Re-run the 11y falsifier and report two numbers the current pipeline omits: (a) OLS-beta-residualized Sharpe -- regress the sleeve's daily returns on SPY and compute the Sharpe of the residual (alpha) series; and (b) Sharpe with rfAnnual set to the realized T-bill path instead of 0. If the beta-residual, rf-correct Sharpe clears ~0.3 out-of-sample AND the average return on the worst 5% of SPY days is reliably positive, the sleeve is defensible as a hedge judged on hedge-payoff rather than standalone Sharpe (and should be carried via the allocator's defensiveFloorRiskOff, not on its Sharpe). Until both numbers exist, treat 0.45 as a beta-inclusive rf=0 artifact and size the sleeve as pure insurance, not as a Sharpe contributor.

redteam.lens: ORTHOGONALITY & TAIL: left-tail (crisis) conditional decorrelation from a long-only / short-vol (put-selling) book; hidden beta and shared short-gamma
redteam.kill: The sleeve's claimed orthogonality is full-sample, not left-tail-conditional, and it carries hidden DURATION/RATE beta that co-moves with the rate-sensitive short-put core in a rate-shock crisis. By construction the two largest legs are TLT and IEF (inverse-vol weighting 0.08/sigma is biggest for the lowest-vol bond ETFs), so the sleeve is structurally a levered-toward-duration book. In 2022 stock-bond correlation flipped positive: the put-selling core bled (equities -25%, repeated vol spikes) AND TLT/IEF bled from the same Fed rate shock — the 'hedge' ADDED to the put-book drawdown. The trend exit (6m<0 OR close<SMA200) is lagging, so TLT was already down double digits before it was dropped; the sleeve held positively-correlated losers for months. The dailyFeedbackLoop only cuts after 21 consecutive down-days, i.e. after the damage. For Aug-2024 the failure is timing: a ~3-day VIX-to-65 round-trip cannot be captured by a monthly rebalance with a lagging 6m/200d trend filter — g only scales existing positions, it cannot open new defensive exposure intra-month, so the sleeve delivers near-zero hedge on the one day the short-gamma book gaps. Even Mar-2020 the spec concedes 'solid positive after the first ~3 days' — the acute dash-for-cash window where TLT/GLD also sold off is exactly when the short-put book takes its worst mark, so the hedge is weakest at the gap. The -0.15 full-sample corr averages over a reliably-negative bond-rally crisis subtype (2018Q4, post-day-3 Mar2020) and a positive-corr rate-shock subtype (2022), masking the regime-dependent sign flip the lens explicitly asks about. Net: not reliably orthogonal in the left tail; would have offset in 2018Q4/Mar2020 but ADDED in 2022 and contributed little in fast-crash Aug2024.
redteam.fix: Two structural fixes plus tightening the existing adaptive loop. (1) Cap the bond duration concentration: replace the naive 0.08/sigma inverse-vol weighting (which over-weights TLT/IEF) with a duration-risk budget so bonds cannot exceed ~40% of sleeve gross, and shift weight toward the genuinely diversifying legs in a rate shock (UUP/dollar, GLD). (2) Make the 2022 defense leading not lagging: gate the bond legs on realized stock-bond correlation (e.g. 63d corr(SPY,TLT) > 0 -> drop or short-duration the bond legs immediately) rather than waiting for the 21-down-day hedge-payoff trigger or the lagging 6m/SMA200 trend exit. (3) For fast crashes (Aug2024 archetype), add a fast intra-month trigger that can OPEN defensive exposure on VIX backwardation (the tailhedge.ts sleeve already rebalances every 3 days and reacts to vixTermRatio>1) instead of merely scaling g on a 21-day cadence; pair this sleeve with that faster tail-hedge for the gap days. (4) Re-run the 11y walk-forward and report the corr-to-core CONDITIONAL on the worst-5% SPY days within EACH crisis episode separately (2018Q4 / Mar2020 first-5-days vs full / 2022 H1 vs H2 / Aug2024 week), not the full-sample corr, to prove left-tail orthogonality rather than assume it.

redteam.kill: Two independent failures. (1) DATA/SAMPLE: the entire backtest harness and on-disk cache start 2015-06-02 (~2766 bars, ~11y), and with warmupBars:260 + a 200d SMA usable signal only begins ~2016-2017. The sleeve's SOLE edge is the conditional negative stock-bond/gold correlation in risk-off, yet the window contains exactly ONE bond-hedge-failure regime (2022 positive stock-bond corr, which the spec itself names as the known failure) and only ~2-3 bond-rally crisis clusters (2018Q4, Mar-2020, Aug-2024). No GFC-2008, no 2011, no 2013 taper. So the 'crisis-alpha' is estimated from a handful of effectively-independent episodes — you cannot distinguish a robust hedge from a regime-lucky one, and the falsifier's '11y walk-forward' overstates the true out-of-sample crisis count. (2) COMPUTE: the spec's differentiating dailyFeedbackLoop — sizing by 'hedge payoff ratio = sleeve return CONDITIONAL on core-book down >1% days', with multiplier ratchets (-0.10/day) and dynamic switching of the trend filter from 6m to 12m — is NOT computable in this engine. PerfProvider returns only {sharpe,vol,n,returns} for a single strategy keyed to its own equity curve, and allocateStrategies scores each sleeve by its OWN trailing Sharpe/vol; there is no cross-sleeve conditional mechanism and nothing passes 'core-down days' into perf. The spec's 'No engine change' claim is false for its adaptive loop. Stripped of that loop it degrades to the existing Sharpe allocator, which actively STARVES flat-in-calm insurance sleeves (the defensiveFloorRiskOff hack exists precisely because of this), so the self-correcting 'cut when stock-bond corr inverts' behavior it sells does not exist in code. Compounding both: it is a near-duplicate of two already-registered sleeves (crossAssetTrend holds TLT/IEF/GLD/UUP when trending; tailHedge goes long TLT+GLD on the identical backwardation/SPY<200/CRISIS gate), so it double-counts existing defensive exposure rather than adding orthogonal edge. The headline static signal IS data-feasible and cheap (TLT/IEF/GLD/UUP/^VIX/^VIX9D all in manifest and cached; totalReturn/sma/annualizedVol all exist), but the buildable version is not the version whose 0.45 Sharpe is claimed.
redteam.fix: (1) Drop the conditional hedge-payoff loop entirely (unbuildable) and either (a) ship the plain static subset and let the existing defensiveFloorRiskOff carry it in risk-off, accepting it is just a thinner crossAssetTrend, or (b) actually extend the engine: add a cross-sleeve PerfProvider that takes a 'core equity curve' and computes sleeve return on core-down-days, then a new allocator path that sizes by that ratio — this is real engine work, not 'no engine change.' (2) Before allocating any capital, run the EXISTING red-team harness (scripts/backtest/_xats_redteam.ts pattern) on the long-only subset, but de-weight conclusions: explicitly report per-regime P&L split into the 2022 bond-failure cluster vs the 2018Q4/2020 bond-rally clusters, and treat the result as ~2-3 effective crisis observations, not '11 years.' (3) Resolve the overlap: do not register a third defensive sleeve until you show its return stream is materially decorrelated from crossAssetTrend AND tailHedge over the cache window; if correlation is high, fold it into one of them rather than adding it. (4) Test the g=0.5 calm-carry leg in isolation across 2013/2022/2023 rising-rate windows to confirm it is not a structural duration drag with no offsetting payoff.

scored.name: Bond-Curve Carry (duration-timing IEF vs SHY/BIL)
scored.family: Carry sleeves (bond curve / equity yield / vol term-structure)

scores.name: Bond-Curve Carry (duration-timing IEF vs SHY/BIL)
scores.requiredFixes: 1) Measure Sharpe EXCESS-OF-CASH: run.ts (scripts/backtest/run.ts:78) calls runBacktest with no rfAnnual, so engine.ts:292 and metrics.ts:48 default rf=0 — the reported Sharpe silently credits the ~1.8-5% bill yield the carry thesis is supposed to beat, and the SHY defensive state contributes ~0 excess return. Re-run the falsifier with rfAnnual = realized 3M T-bill series. 2) Report the DURATION-BETA-NEUTRAL residual Sharpe (regress sleeve daily returns on IEF returns); only the roll-down residual is the real edge and it must clear ~0.2 after costs. 3) INCREMENTAL/redundancy test: cross_asset_trend (crossasset.ts) already holds the identical IEF/TLT/SHY on the same 21d/trend gating at only 0.57 RAW prior, and ts_trend (0.61) + tail_hedge add more duration — prove this strict-subset sleeve lifts meta Sharpe by >=0.05 AND adds crisis convexity the existing three duration sleeves don't already supply, else fold a slope tilt into cross_asset_trend instead. 4) TAIL-CONDITIONAL orthogonality: report sleeve-vs-put-book corr in worst-decile weeks of 2018Q4/Mar2020/2022/Aug2024 separately; 2022 (bear-flattening, stocks-and-bonds-down) is the binding case and the lagging 21d/100d-SMA gate de-risks AFTER the loss. 5) Wire the claimed feedback loop that does not exist: perf.ts:87 hardcodes confidence 0.5 (no 0.5/0.35 differentiation), the 63d SPY-corr guard is unimplemented, and family 'carry' is absent from allocator.ts:102 DEFENSIVE_FAMILIES so it gets no risk-off tilt/floor. 6) Replace the momentum proxy with an actual forward-rate slope/roll-down signal (FRED DGS10/DGS2) so the strategy matches its stated mechanism; split-sample 2014-2020 vs 2021-2025 to expose secular-tailwind dependence.
scores.oneLineRationale: Refuted on three Serious lenses: Sharpe is total-return not excess-of-cash (collapses below its own 0.2 floor once rf and duration beta are stripped), its 2022 tail correlation flips wrong-sign for a short-vol book, and it is a strict redundant subset of cross_asset_trend (same IEF/TLT/SHY, 0.57 RAW) plus ts_trend/tail_hedge — momentum-in-carry-costume on a dead bond tailwind, with the claimed feedback loop and allocator wiring not actually implemented.

spec.name: Bond-Curve Carry (duration-timing IEF vs SHY/BIL)
spec.family: Carry sleeves (bond curve / equity yield / vol term-structure)
spec.mechanism: Term-premium risk premium plus roll-down. When the Treasury curve is upward-sloping, intermediate notes (IEF, ~7.5y duration) earn their yield PLUS roll-down as they age toward shorter, lower-yielding points of a static curve — an expected excess return over T-bills (Fama-Bliss; Martens duration-carry Sharpe ~0.68). This is a BOND-BETA stream, not equity beta: its return driver is the level/slope of rates, which is structurally uncorrelated to a long-only/short-vol equity book. Crisis behavior is the prize — in flight-to-quality selloffs the curve typically bull-steepens (front end rallies on cuts) so duration pays exactly when equity bleeds. The sleeve self-protects by sitting in SHY/BIL (zero-duration carry) when the curve is flat/inverted, which is precisely when long-duration carry is negative and rate risk is unrewarded (e.g. 2022).
spec.exactSignal: Daily, using ETFs as curve proxies (no FRED needed, all in manifest). Define slope proxy = 21d total return of IEF minus 21d total return of SHY (momentum spread = realized carry+rolldown of intermediate over short). Define trend confirm = IEF close > its 100d SMA. Define carry-positive state when slope_proxy > 0 AND IEF > SMA100(IEF). Optionally widen the duration leg to TLT only when slope_proxy is in the top tercile of its own trailing 252d distribution (zscore(slopeProxySeries,252) > 0.5) to capture steep-curve regimes. Inverted/flat state = slope_proxy <= 0 OR IEF < SMA100(IEF).
spec.entryRule: Monthly rebalance (rebalanceDays=21). If carry-positive: hold IEF at the sleeve's vol-target weight (add a TLT tilt only in top-tercile-steep regimes, capping combined duration weight). If flat/inverted: rotate fully to SHY (par the brief allows BIL too) as defensive zero-duration carry. Confidence emitted = 0.5 in carry-positive, 0.35 in defensive SHY state.
spec.exitRule: Exit the IEF/TLT duration leg the first monthly rebalance the curve flips to flat/inverted (slope_proxy<=0) or IEF loses its 100d SMA; redeploy to SHY. No intramonth churn — positions drift between rebalances to keep turnover (and thus cost) minimal (~12 round trips/yr).
spec.instruments: IEF (7-10y UST), SHY (1-3y UST), optional TLT (20y+) tilt in steep regimes; all liquid ETFs, ~6bps round-trip in the cost model. No options, no shorts (zero borrow cost).
spec.positionSizing: Vol-target the sleeve to ~6% annualized: weight = min(sleeveCap, 0.06 / annualizedVol(activeETF,60)). IEF realized vol ~6-8% so weight lands ~0.8-1.0 of sleeve capital; SHY vol ~1.5% so the defensive state is near fully invested but contributes little risk. Hard cap sleeve gross at 1.0 of its allocated capital (no leverage inside the sleeve); meta-level gross stays <=1.5 across all sleeves. TLT tilt capped so IEF-equivalent duration never exceeds ~1.3x the IEF-only case.
spec.newDataNeeded: none — IEF/SHY/TLT all in manifest. (Optional refinement: add BIL and FRED DGS10/DGS2 for a cleaner cash-vs-slope read, but not required.)
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: METRIC: the sleeve's own trailing 60d realized Sharpe on the live sim book (trailingStatsFromEquity over the sleeve's daily equity), fed to the meta-allocator as StrategyPerfStat. RULE: allocator blends that realized Sharpe with the backtest prior (priorBlend 0.4), divides by the sleeve's trailing realized vol (risk-parity), and scales the sleeve's capital share up/down accordingly; if trailing Sharpe < sharpeFloor (-0.25) for the window the sleeve is benched to 0 that day. ADDITIONAL SLEEVE-LEVEL GUARD: track a 63d rolling correlation of the sleeve's daily returns to SPY daily returns; if |rho| rises above 0.35 (duration stops being a diversifier, e.g. a 2022-style stocks-and-bonds-down regime), multiply the sleeve's emitted confidence by 0.5 so the allocator trims it. This is the explicit increase/decrease rule.
spec.crisisBehavior: 2018Q4: positive (curve steepened on cut expectations, IEF rallied). Mar-2020: strongly positive early (duration spike) — the orthogonal win. 2022: FLAT/slightly negative is the failure mode (curve inverted, both legs weak) BUT the slope/SMA gate rotates to SHY through most of it, capping damage to low-single-digit drag rather than the -17% IEF buy-and-hold took. Aug-2024 (yen carry unwind): mildly positive (flight to Treasuries). Net: crisis-flat-to-positive, the opposite tail of the equity book.
spec.implementationNotes: New Strategy object in src/lib/strategies/ (e.g. bondcarry.ts), family 'carry', longOnly:true, instrument:'etf', rebalanceDays:21, warmupBars:260. In generate(ctx): compute slopeProxy via totalReturn(ctx.closes('IEF'),21) - totalReturn(ctx.closes('SHY'),21); trendOk via sma(ctx.closes('IEF'),100); if slopeProxy>0 && lastIEF>sma emit IEF weight = clamp(0.06/annualizedVol(ctx.closes('IEF'),60)); else emit SHY weight similarly. Add TLT tilt gated on zscore of a rolling slopeProxy array (build it from ctx.closes over the lookback). Register in registry.ts; add to ALL_BACKTEST_SYMBOLS (IEF/SHY/TLT already present via TREND_ETFS — confirm SHY is fetched, add if missing). The 63d SPY-corr guard uses correlation(simpleReturns(sleeveEquity), simpleReturns(ctx.closes('SPY'))) at the runner level feeding confidence.
spec.falsifier: Run the walk-forward 2014-2025. REFUTED if, after costs, (a) the sleeve's standalone Sharpe is < 0.2, OR (b) its full-sample return correlation to SPY exceeds 0.25 in absolute value (then it is not the orthogonal diversifier claimed), OR (c) adding it to the existing 5-sleeve meta fails to raise the meta's after-cost Sharpe by at least 0.05 and fails to improve Mar-2020 / 2018Q4 drawdown — i.e. it neither lifts Sharpe nor adds crisis convexity.
spec._family: Carry sleeves (bond curve / equity yield / vol term-structure)

redteam.kill: No kill from this lens. Instruments (IEF/SHY/TLT) are among the most liquid ETFs traded — real retail bid/ask + impact is ~1-3 bps round trip, yet the engine's cost model (src/lib/backtest/costs.ts) charges a flat 10 bps round trip (3+2 bps/side spread+slippage), so modeled cost is PESSIMISTIC for these names. No options (zero contract/spread cost), no shorts (zero borrow/locate). Turnover is genuinely low: the sleeve is a binary regime switch (IEF/TLT vs SHY) on a 21d rebalance, and the engine's 0.2%-of-equity dust filter (engine.ts line 235) suppresses most vol-target rescaling churn. Realistic one-way turnover ~6-7 turns/yr (a few regime flips x 2 sides + minor vol-target drift), i.e. ~33 bps/yr cost drag at the sleeve level — even at the spec's generous '12 round trips/yr' it is only ~60 bps/yr. Gross edge from duration carry+rolldown in upward-sloping regimes (Martens Sharpe ~0.68 on a 6%-vol target => ~150-400 bps gross in active periods) dwarfs that cost. Gross edge > round-trip cost by 4-8x. Capacity is effectively unlimited for retail (Treasury ETFs absorb hundreds of millions/day) and there is no fixed-capital min-granularity problem (~$100 share prices quantize cleanly even for a small sleeve). The only real cost-lens nits: the spec mis-states its own model (claims 6 bps, model is 10 bps) and references BIL which is NOT in any universe (universe.ts ships SHY but no BIL) — both immaterial since the error is conservative-direction and SHY suffices. The genuine threats to the 0.45 claim (is post-2010 term premium actually positive net of bond beta? does it survive the 2022 stocks-and-bonds-down regime?) live in the mechanism/regime lens, not here.
redteam.fix: Fix the spec's cost figure to match the actual 10 bps round-trip model (or, more accurately, recalibrate a Treasury-ETF-specific cost of ~2-3 bps round trip since the flat equity model over-charges these names). Add BIL to a universe list if it is to be used, or drop the BIL reference and standardize on SHY (already fetched). Confirm the 0.2% dust filter keeps vol-target rescaling churn negligible by logging realized annual turnover in the walk-forward; if regime flips cluster (whipsaw years), consider a hysteresis band on slope_proxy to avoid round-tripping the full sleeve on marginal slope sign-flips. None of these change the verdict — costs are not the binding constraint.

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY (net of beta + look-ahead): is the claimed Sharpe real after stripping market beta and after costs, with no look-ahead in the signal?
redteam.kill: The claimed ~0.45 Sharpe is a raw, total-return number that is NOT net of the risk-free rate — fatal for a bond-CARRY sleeve whose entire thesis is "earn the term premium OVER T-bills." Verified in code: src/lib/backtest/metrics.ts computes Sharpe with rfAnnual defaulting to 0, and scripts/backtest/run.ts calls runBacktest WITHOUT passing rfAnnual, so the harness reports total-return Sharpe with zero cash subtraction. Over the test window (2015-2026 per research/backtests/summary.json) bill yields averaged ~1.8% and hit ~5% in 2023-2026, so the rf=0 convention silently credits the strategy with the cash yield it is supposed to be measured against. Worse, the DEFENSIVE state literally sits in SHY/BIL, whose return IS the bill rate — on a correct excess-of-cash basis that state contributes ~0 excess return, not positive carry. The empirical anchor is decisive: the ALREADY-EXISTING cross_asset_trend sleeve holds the identical instruments (TLT/IEF/SHY) with the same 21d/SMA trend gating and scores only Sharpe 0.57 RAW/rf=0 (CAGR 3.88%, rhoSPY 0.28) in summary.json. A narrower bond-only version has fewer return sources and the same pure rate (DV01) beta, so on an excess-of-cash, beta-stripped basis it lands well below the spec's own 0.2 falsifier floor. The 'edge' is 100% the level/slope of rates — passive duration beta, not skill; roll-down on a static curve is a real but tiny ~20-40bps/yr gross premium that 6bps x ~12 round-trips/yr largely consumes. Low equity correlation is true but irrelevant: it does not manufacture excess return. Finally, it likely fails falsifier (c): the meta already gets duration via cross_asset_trend AND ts_trend (both hold IEF/TLT/SHY), so a third correlated duration sleeve is very unlikely to lift the current meta_adaptive Sharpe (0.84, meta-summary.json) by the required +0.05. The signal itself is genuinely no-look-ahead (the engine's StrategyContext enforces date<=t), but the top-tercile zscore TLT gate and the 0.5/0.35 confidence constants are narrative-fit to the named historical crises.
redteam.fix: Re-run the falsifier with rfAnnual set to the realized 3M T-bill series (or measure excess return vs BIL directly) so the reported Sharpe is genuinely excess-of-cash — this is the single change that tells the truth. Then regress the sleeve's daily returns on IEF/duration returns and report the BETA-NEUTRAL residual Sharpe; only ship if that residual (the actual roll-down skill) clears ~0.2 after costs. Also run an incremental test: does adding this sleeve raise meta Sharpe given cross_asset_trend and ts_trend already supply duration? If the orthogonality value is the real goal, fold a curve-slope tilt INTO the existing cross_asset_trend sleeve rather than adding a redundant, rate-beta-concentrated sleeve. Drop or pre-register the top-tercile zscore gate and confidence constants to avoid in-sample crisis fitting.

redteam.lens: ORTHOGONALITY & TAIL: left-tail (crisis) correlation of bond-curve carry to a long-only / short-vol put-selling book — hidden bond beta, shared short-gamma/negative-skew carry, and whether it offsets vs adds to the put-book drawdown in Mar2020 / 2022 / Aug2024.
redteam.kill: The -0.05 corr-to-equity is a FULL-SAMPLE average that masks a tail beta that flips sign in the crisis most dangerous to a short-vol book. (1) The 2022 tail is fatal to the orthogonality claim and the spec admits it: that crisis was a rate-shock where the curve bear-flattened/inverted (opposite of the bull-steepening the mechanism needs), so both the put book AND the duration sleeve bled together — long-equity-correlated, stocks-and-bonds-down. The much-touted self-protection is a MONTHLY, LAGGING gate (21d momentum spread + 100d SMA): it rotates to SHY only AFTER IEF loses its trend, so it eats the transition leg of the 2022 drawdown before de-risking. The 63d SPY-corr guard likewise only halves confidence after |rho|>0.35 is already realized — it fires during the loss, not before. (2) Shared short-gamma / negative-skew: bond roll-down carry is a positive-carry/negative-skew trade (collect term premium until a rate spike delivers a large left-tail loss), structurally the same convexity profile as short puts; in an inflation/rate-shock crisis both tails co-fire. That is shared short-gamma, not orthogonality. (3) Redundancy in the GOOD crises: the codebase already holds Treasury duration twice — tail_hedge (src/lib/strategies/tailhedge.ts) goes long TLT+GLD in stress, and cross_asset_trend (src/lib/strategies/crossasset.ts) holds IEF/TLT/SHY on trend. So the Mar2020/2018Q4 'orthogonal win' double-counts duration the meta already owns, concentrating the book onto the same Treasury beta in exactly the window the spec calls the prize. Net across the three crises: offsets the put-book drawdown only where existing sleeves already do (2020, 2018Q4), ADDS to it in the one novel crisis (2022), and Aug2024 is trivially small at monthly resolution. The diversification it claims is real on average but absent (or wrong-signed) in the left tail — the precise failure this lens screens for.
redteam.fix: Re-test orthogonality TAIL-CONDITIONALLY, not full-sample: report correlation of the sleeve to the put/short-vol book in the worst-decile equity weeks of 2018Q4, Mar2020, 2022, and Aug2024 separately; the falsifier must require near-zero-or-negative tail correlation in EACH, with 2022 as the binding case (full-sample |rho|<0.25 is necessary but not sufficient). Add an INCREMENTAL test: does it lift meta Sharpe / crisis convexity AFTER the duration already supplied by tail_hedge and cross_asset_trend is accounted for (regress the sleeve's crisis returns on those two; require residual crisis alpha), else it is redundant. Replace the lagging monthly/100d-SMA self-protection with a faster rate-shock kill (e.g. de-duration on a daily 2s/10s bear-flattening or real-yield-spike signal, not a 21d trailing momentum spread) so the sleeve exits BEFORE the 2022-type transition leg rather than after. Hard-cap combined book duration across all three Treasury-holding sleeves so adding this one cannot silently triple flight-to-quality beta. If, after a tail-conditional and incremental test, 2022 still shows positive equity correlation and no residual crisis alpha over the existing duration sleeves, drop it.

redteam.kill: Data/compute are genuinely feasible (this is the spec's strength, so the kill is on the edge, not the plumbing): IEF/SHY/TLT/SPY are all in ALL_BACKTEST_SYMBOLS (universe.ts:70-80; SHY explicit in CROSS_ASSET_ETFS line 48, and SHY.json/IEF.json/TLT.json are already cached), free+daily via Yahoo (scripts/backtest/data.ts), and every needed indicator (totalReturn, sma, annualizedVol, zscore, correlation) exists with crossasset.ts as a near-identical working template trading the SAME bond ETFs. But the edge does not survive the regime lens. (1) MISLABELED MECHANISM: the signal slope_proxy = 21d return(IEF) - 21d return(SHY) plus IEF>100d SMA is duration MOMENTUM/trend, not term-premium carry/roll-down. The cited Fama-Bliss/Martens ~0.68 Sharpe is for actual forward-rate slope carry, which this proxy never measures — it is single-factor duration trend-following in a carry costume. (2) SECULAR-TAILWIND CONTAMINATION: much of any positive duration return over a 2014-2025 backtest is the non-repeatable bond bull that ended ~2020; the edge is rate-regime-dependent and only 'works' in a falling/steep-curve world. (3) CRISIS DIVERSIFIER IS REGIME-CONDITIONAL, NOT STRUCTURAL: the spec's own 63d SPY-corr guard (halve confidence when |rho|>0.35) is an explicit admission that the stocks-and-bonds-down regime (2022, any inflation-shock crisis) breaks the orthogonality 'prize' — so the crisis convexity it sells is exactly the thing it concedes it cannot rely on; corrToLongOnlyEquity -0.05 and falsifier (b) |corr|<0.25 are the likely real failure. (4) 2022 WHIPSAW: a 21d-return + slow 100d-SMA monthly gate lags through bond bear-market rallies, so 'low-single-digit drag' is optimistic. (5) ENGINE-HOOK GAPS the thesis leans on are not wired: family 'carry' is NOT in allocator DEFENSIVE_FAMILIES = {trend,defensive,rotation} (allocator.ts:102), so it gets neither the risk-off defensive tilt nor the defensive floor; and the rolling-SPY-corr confidence guard plus differentiated 0.5/0.35 confidence emissions do not exist (meta.ts computes only full-sample curveCorrelation for reporting; perf.ts:87 hardcodes confidence 0.5). Cost is ~10bps round-trip (costs.ts:24-25), not the 6bps claimed (immaterial at ~12 trips/yr). Net: feasible to build, but on this lens it is regime-dependent momentum riding a dead secular tailwind, mis-wired into the allocator, with a self-admitted breakable diversification claim — default-to-skepticism kills it.
redteam.fix: Re-fetch IEF/SHY/TLT/SPY back to 2014 (Yahoo supports it; current cache starts 2015-06-02) to honor the falsifier's 2014-2025 window. Reclassify family as 'defensive' (or add 'carry' to DEFENSIVE_FAMILIES) so the sleeve actually receives risk-off tilt/floor. Build the missing 63d rolling SPY-corr confidence guard and the 0.5/0.35 differentiated confidence at the runner level (correlation() helper already exists). Then HONESTLY split-sample test: report the 2014-2020 (falling-rate) Sharpe separately from 2021-2025 (rising/inverted-rate) Sharpe to expose the secular-tailwind dependence, and verify 2022 drag empirically rather than asserting it. If the post-2020 standalone Sharpe is <0.2 or full-sample |corr to SPY| >0.25 (which 2022 likely triggers), drop it. Consider replacing the momentum proxy with an actual slope-carry signal (FRED DGS10-DGS2 or roll-down estimate) so the strategy matches its stated mechanism, but treat that as a different, re-validated spec.

scored.name: HRP-Shrink: correlation-aware sleeve combiner (Hierarchical Risk Parity with Ledoit-Wolf shrinkage), gated by a live A/B vs the current realized-Sharpe allocator

scores.name: HRP-Shrink: correlation-aware sleeve combiner
scores.requiredFixes: 1) Build the missing net-of-beta metric: metrics.ts computeMetrics() has rf=0 and ZERO beta adjustment — regress book daily returns on SPY and report residual Sharpe/IR; the falsifier's 'net-of-SPY-beta Sharpe' is uncomputable today (only curveCorrelation exists). 2) Replace single-linkage with average/Ward and add a no-trade band (L1 weight delta > ~0.05-0.08) plus EWMA smoothing of HRP weights — single-linkage flip jitter on a noisy K=3-6 126d corr matrix generates turnover that can exceed the entire gross edge; verified engine charges costs per symbol notional delta (engine.ts:237-239) and the 0.2% dust filter (engine.ts:235) makes sub-1% HRP nudges unimplementable at retail while a large book pays full turnover. 3) Un-starve the orthogonal sleeve: tailHedge sits in CASH in calm markets (tailhedge.ts:31 returns empty weights), so HRP's own sigma_k<=0/>=60d/K<3 guards drop the one beta-diversifier exactly when needed — represent it by its conditional-on-active return distribution. 4) Use an EWMA/short-half-life or regime-conditioned correlation; a 126d trailing window structurally lags crisis decorrelation by months, inverting the spec's crisis claim. 5) Gate theta on NET (after-cost) Sharpe63 and lower the ceiling. 6) Actually RUN the two-arm walk-forward falsifier and commit research/results.md + research/backtests/*.json — currently ABSENT, so the 0.95 headline and all priors (cross_asset_trend 0.57, tail_hedge 0.06) are asserted, not measured. Ship ONLY if net-of-beta IR rises >=0.05 AND MaxDD/vol improve AND theta does not drift to its 0.2 floor.
scores.oneLineRationale: Sound structural mechanism and cheap/feasible to build, but the incumbent allocator is ALREADY inverse-vol (riskParityPower=1.0) so HRP's marginal gain collapses to noise on the all-equity menu (spec's own admitted null), single-linkage turnover can exceed the ~0.3-0.5%/yr gross edge, the net-of-beta metric the falsifier demands does not exist, the one beta-diversifier is starved by HRP's own guards, and no backtest exists — RESEARCH_ONLY until the metric, turnover-aware A/B, and falsifier are built and pass.

spec.name: HRP-Shrink: correlation-aware sleeve combiner (Hierarchical Risk Parity with Ledoit-Wolf shrinkage), gated by a live A/B vs the current realized-Sharpe allocator
spec.mechanism: STRUCTURAL / estimation-error mechanism, not a risk premium. The current allocator (src/lib/strategies/allocator.ts) sizes sleeves by max(0,blendedSharpe)*confidence/vol then normalizes — this is naive INVERSE-VOL and is CORRELATION-BLIND in the relative weighting (the covariance matrix is built only in meta.ts exAntePortfolioVol() and used solely to scale gross DOWN for vol-targeting). So when two sleeves are 0.9 correlated and one is orthogonal, the current allocator double-counts the correlated pair and starves the diversifier. HRP fixes exactly this: it (a) clusters sleeves by their return correlation, (b) quasi-diagonalizes so similar sleeves sit adjacent, and (c) splits risk top-down by inverse-variance WITHIN each cluster and across clusters. The economic edge is that HRP never inverts the covariance matrix, so unlike min-variance/CLA it does not amplify estimation error on our small, ill-conditioned, highly-correlated sleeve set — the web check confirms HRP achieves lower OOS variance than min-variance itself. Net: same sleeves, better risk budgeting -> higher portfolio Sharpe IF orthogonal streams exist in the menu.
spec.exactSignal: Inputs are the per-sleeve daily realized-return series already supplied by PerfProvider (meta.ts: perf.get(key).returns). On each rebalance, for the K active sleeves (decision.weights>0) build over a trailing W=126d (6m) window: (1) sample correlation matrix C via indicators.correlation pairwise and sample vols sigma_k via indicators.stdev; (2) SHRINK: C_hat = (1-lambda)*C + lambda*C_bar where C_bar is the constant-correlation target (off-diagonals = mean pairwise corr), lambda fixed at 0.30 (cheap proxy for Ledoit-Wolf; avoids per-day LW estimation cost); covariance Cov = D*C_hat*D with D=diag(sigma). (3) DISTANCE d_ij=sqrt(0.5*(1-C_hat_ij)); single-linkage hierarchical cluster on d; quasi-diagonalize to ordering. (4) Recursive bisection: at each split allocate between the two sub-clusters in proportion to inverse cluster-variance (cluster var = w_ivp' Cov w_ivp using intra-cluster inverse-variance weights). Output = HRP weight vector hrp_k summing to 1. Final sleeve capital share = hrp_k * targetGross * volScale (reuse existing gross + vol-target plumbing unchanged).
spec.entryRule: Replace the score->normalize block in allocateStrategies (lines ~132-148) for the cross-sleeve weighting ONLY: keep the existing benching (Sharpe<floor, confidence<=0.05), regime tilt, and per-strategy cap, but compute the surviving sleeves' relative weights via HRP-Shrink instead of (score/sum). A sleeve enters the HRP pool iff not benched and confidence>0.05 and it has >=60 days of realized returns (else fall back to its prior-Sharpe inverse-vol weight, blended in by sampleTrust exactly as today). Cap per sleeve at cfg.maxWeightPerStrategy via the existing capAndRenormalize.
spec.exitRule: A sleeve leaves the HRP pool when benched by the existing rules (trailingSharpe<sharpeFloor with >=minSampleDays, or confidence<=0.05); its HRP weight is set to 0 and recursive bisection re-runs on the remainder. The whole HRP combiner is itself DISABLED for the day (revert to the legacy realized-Sharpe allocator) if K<3 active sleeves or if the shrunk correlation matrix is non-PSD/degenerate (any sigma_k<=0).
spec.instruments: No new instruments. Operates one level above the sleeves on their equity-curve return streams. Trades whatever the underlying sleeves trade (equities, sector SPDRs, multi-asset ETFs TLT/IEF/GLD/DBC/VNQ, ^VIX-gated hedges). Gross<=1.0 normal / 0.6 risk-off as today.
spec.positionSizing: Sleeve shares = hrp_k. Reuse meta.ts volScale = clamp(minVolScale..1, volTargetAnnual / exAntePortfolioVol) with volTargetAnnual=0.10 — but now exAntePortfolioVol is computed on the HRP weights (consistent covariance reused from the same shrunk matrix, so vol target and allocation agree). Per-sleeve cap 0.45, gross<=1.0 (1.5 hard ceiling never approached). Leverage-free: volScale<=1 only de-grosses.
spec.newDataNeeded: None — all in manifest. Uses existing per-sleeve realized return series from PerfProvider and indicators.ts (correlation/stdev/mean already present). HRP clustering + recursive bisection is ~120 lines of pure TS, no external libs, no matrix inversion.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: THE METRIC: rolling 63-day realized net Sharpe of the LIVE blended book under HRP-Shrink vs a SHADOW book run in parallel under the legacy realized-Sharpe allocator (both already computable each day from the same sim DB equity). RULE: maintain an allocator-mix knob theta in [0,1] = fraction of capital combined by HRP vs legacy; update daily theta_t = clip(theta_{t-1} + 0.02*sign(Sharpe63_HRP - Sharpe63_legacy), 0.2, 0.8). HRP must EARN its share — if its trailing-quarter realized Sharpe stops beating the incumbent, theta decays back toward the legacy allocator automatically. This is the same self-tracking pattern the meta already uses for sleeves, applied to the COMBINER itself.
spec.crisisBehavior: Combiner inherits crisis behavior from its sleeve menu. HRP's value-add in crises is allocating MORE to whichever sleeves are decorrelating (e.g. multi-asset trend long TLT/GLD, or a VIX-term-gated de-gross hedge) precisely when the equity sleeves co-crash, because their realized correlation to the book spikes and HRP's clustering pushes them into separate branches and up-weights the diverging branch. In 2018Q4/Mar2020/2022/Aug2024 it is FLAT-to-better than the legacy allocator on the SAME sleeves (legacy keeps over-weighting the correlated equity cluster). It is NOT itself a hedge — if every sleeve is long-only equity it still goes down; the lift only appears when an orthogonal sleeve is present.
spec.implementationNotes: In src/lib/strategies/allocator.ts add hrpWeights(keys, returnsByKey, W=126, lambda=0.30): build shrunk corr+cov, single-linkage cluster (compute pairwise distance matrix, iteratively merge closest pair, record leaf order), quasi-diagonalize, recursiveBisection. Wire into allocateStrategies behind a cfg.combiner: 'legacy'|'hrp' flag; pass each sleeve's returns through from meta.ts (already in perf map). Reuse exAntePortfolioVol covariance. Add a parallel ShadowAllocation in scripts/backtest/meta.ts to compute the legacy Sharpe for the theta feedback loop. Unit-test against a 3-sleeve synthetic where two are identical (HRP must give the identical pair a combined weight ~= the orthogonal sleeve's, NOT 2x).
spec.falsifier: Run walk-forward over the full 11y on the EXISTING 5 long-only equity sleeves with combiner='hrp' vs 'legacy'. FALSIFIED if after-cost net-of-SPY-beta Sharpe improves by <0.03 AND MaxDD/vol do not improve — which is the EXPECTED null on an all-equity menu (the brief's own finding: you cannot diversify shared beta). Then add ONE genuinely orthogonal sleeve (multi-asset trend on TLT/IEF/GLD/DBC) and re-run: FALSIFIED if HRP does not capture a LARGER share of that sleeve than the legacy allocator at equal realized Sharpe, OR if net-of-beta Sharpe still does not rise >=0.05. If theta drifts to its 0.2 floor in live A/B, HRP is refuted on this book.

redteam.kill: This is a combiner change, not a strategy: it only alters cross-sleeve weights, but engine.ts charges costs on symbol-level notional deltas whenever the blended weight vector moves. The gross edge is tiny by the spec's own admission — the falsifier calls the all-equity menu the 'EXPECTED null' (<0.03 Sharpe) and requires only >=0.05 net-of-beta Sharpe even WITH the orthogonal sleeve, so the honest gross edge is ~0.03-0.05 Sharpe units = only ~0.3-0.5%/yr return at the 10% vol target. Against that, HRP re-runs single-linkage clustering on a noisy K=3-6, highly-correlated 126d corr matrix EVERY 5 days. Single-linkage is famously unstable on small/ill-conditioned matrices: tiny pairwise-corr changes flip merge order, reorder leaves, and reshuffle capital across clusters. The legacy allocator is deliberately sticky (priorBlend 0.7, near-static weekly), so essentially ALL incremental turnover is HRP's. Each sleeve weight move propagates to 2-20 underlying names (momentum ~20 large caps, low-vol ~14), generating real trades. At a realistic mid weekly L1 weight jitter ~0.10 that is ~0.25%/yr incremental round-trip cost (5 bps/side both legs, ~50 rebals/yr); at jitter ~0.20 (entirely plausible for single-linkage flips) it is ~0.50%/yr — LARGER than the entire gross edge. Net = +0.4%/yr gross minus 0.25-0.50%/yr cost ~= 0.0-0.15%/yr, i.e. ~+0.015 Sharpe net in the mid case and NEGATIVE in the high-jitter case. The combiner funds its own rebalancing. Retail granularity makes it strictly worse: the 0.2% dust filter rounds the sub-1% HRP weight nudges across 30-50 names to zero on a $25-100k account, so the diversification HRP computes is unimplementable at retail size while a large book still pays the full turnover; the orthogonal sleeve also trades thin ETFs (SLV/USO/UUP/DBC) whose real spread exceeds the 6 bps model. The vol-target plumbing recomputes volScale on the jittering HRP weights weekly, adding a second de-/re-grossing turnover layer my estimate does not fully count.
redteam.fix: Add turnover suppression so HRP only re-allocates when it materially beats the incumbent net of cost: (1) replace single-linkage with average/Ward linkage and freeze the cluster ORDERING for a minimum holding period (e.g. recompute HRP weights weekly but only TRADE the delta when sleeve-weight L1 change exceeds a no-trade band of ~0.05-0.08, well above the engine's 0.2% dust filter aggregated up); (2) EWMA-smooth the HRP weight vector across rebalances to damp single-linkage flip noise; (3) hard-gate the theta feedback on NET (after-cost) Sharpe63 rather than gross, and lower the theta ceiling so HRP can never dominate until it demonstrably clears cost; (4) run the actual walk-forward the falsifier specifies and report incremental annualTurnoverPct (engine already tracks it) and net-of-SPY-beta Sharpe delta with the real DEFAULT_COSTS — only ship if the delta survives at jitter measured, not assumed. Until that turnover-aware A/B is run and shows net improvement > cost, default to the legacy allocator.

redteam.kill: The claimed 0.95 is a RAW Sharpe, not net-of-beta, and the spec self-admits corrToLongOnlyEquity=0.5 at a 10% vol target — so a large slice of that Sharpe is the SPY risk premium in disguise, not combiner alpha. Crucially, the codebase has NO net-of-beta Sharpe routine: metrics.ts computeMetrics() returns sharpe=(annRet-rf)/annVol with rf defaulting to 0 and zero beta adjustment anywhere in metrics.ts/engine.ts/meta.ts. The falsifier and headline both reference 'net-of-SPY-beta Sharpe' that the harness cannot currently compute. On the mechanism itself: HRP only re-weights ACROSS sleeves; it creates no alpha that isn't in the streams. The spec's OWN falsifier concedes the EXPECTED NULL on the real 5/6 long-only equity menu (<0.03 improvement — 'you cannot diversify shared beta'), because all four equity sleeves (xs_momentum, low_vol, factor_momentum, sector_rotation) are long-only equity loaded on the same SPY factor; redistributing risk budget across co-beta branches barely moves residual Sharpe. The entire lift is conditional on the two genuinely orthogonal sleeves (cross_asset_trend prior 0.57, tail_hedge prior 0.06) carrying real weight — but tail_hedge sits in CASH in calm markets (zero/degenerate return stream), so the spec's own HRP gates (>=60d realized returns, drop if sigma_k<=0, K<3 disables the combiner) STARVE the one beta-diversifying sleeve exactly when de-beta'ing would need it. Finally, no backtest exists: research/results.md and research/backtests/*.json are absent on disk, so the priors and the 0.95 are asserted, not measured. Look-ahead is the one clean part (perf provider uses <=asOfDate binary search, equity curves built point-in-time k<=i, 126d window causal).
redteam.fix: Three concrete fixes before this can be believed: (1) Add a real net-of-beta metric — regress the book's daily returns on SPY over the walk-forward, report Information-Ratio/residual Sharpe of the alpha stream, and re-state the headline as that number rather than the 0.5-beta raw 0.95. (2) Actually RUN the two-arm falsifier and commit the output: first on the equity-only menu (expect the null, proving HRP isn't the lever), then with the orthogonal sleeve UN-starved — relax the HRP gating so a cash-heavy hedge sleeve is represented by its conditional-on-active return distribution rather than dropped, otherwise the de-beta sleeve never gets weight. (3) Cost the INCREMENTAL turnover of HRP-vs-legacy explicitly (single-linkage HRP on 6 sleeves produces jumpier weights than inverse-vol on a 5d rebalance) and report the after-incremental-cost delta. If, after all three, the net-of-SPY-beta IR rises >=0.1 AND the theta A/B doesn't drift to its 0.2 floor, the edge is real but small; honest expectation is a modest de-beta'd IR improvement in the 0.1-0.2 range, not a 0.95 standalone Sharpe.

redteam.kill: Data/compute feasibility passes cleanly and is NOT the problem: the signal runs entirely on sleeve equity-curve return streams already in PerfProvider (verified perf.get(key).returns in meta.ts, populated by makeCurveProvider in scripts/backtest/meta.ts), indicators.ts already has correlation/stdev/mean, the underlying instruments (TLT/IEF/GLD/DBC/SLV/USO/UUP/VNQ/TLT-GLD) are free daily Yahoo ETFs already in CROSS_ASSET_ETFS and loaded by scripts/backtest/data.ts, and HRP single-linkage + recursive bisection on K<=8 sleeves is trivially cheap with no libs/no inversion. The edge is what fails the regime lens. (1) HRP clusters on the TRAILING 126d realized correlation of sleeve equity curves, but the two orthogonal sleeves it depends on are STATE-DEPENDENT: tail_hedge (tailhedge.ts) sits in cash unless VIX backwardates/SPY<200d, producing long runs of zeros (sigma_k~0 trips the spec's own degenerate guard, disabling HRP), and at crisis onset the 126d window is still dominated by the prior calm regime. A 126d trailing correlation structurally cannot detect a decorrelation that began last week, so the spec's crisis claim ('HRP up-weights the diverging branch precisely when equities co-crash') is backwards and lagged by months. (2) On the actual menu 6 of 8 sleeves are long-only US equity; the spec's OWN falsifier predicts the all-equity null (improvement <0.03, 'you cannot diversify shared beta'). The only lift comes from cross_asset_trend, which already exists in registry.ts (prior 0.57) and is ALREADY weighted by the incumbent inverse-vol base (riskParityPower=1.0, chosen by the allocator authors precisely so orthogonal diversifiers count). HRP's documented advantage is vs matrix-INVERSION methods (min-variance/CLA); vs the incumbent's existing inverse-vol on a near-block-diagonal N<=8 matrix, the marginal gain collapses toward noise. The claimed 0.95 is a book-level Sharpe, not the incremental combiner contribution, which the falsifier itself bounds at +0.03 to +0.05 net-of-beta.
redteam.fix: Blow-up risk is genuinely low and should be acknowledged: leverage-free (volScale<=1 de-gross only), gross<=1.0, per-sleeve cap 0.45, degenerate/non-PSD and K<3 guards both revert to legacy, and the theta A/B loop caps HRP at 0.8 and auto-decays to 0.2 on underperformance. To salvage any edge: (a) replace the 126d trailing sample correlation with a regime-conditioned or exponentially-weighted (short half-life) estimator so decorrelation is detected faster, and handle the sparse/flat tail_hedge stream explicitly (e.g., correlation only over its active days) instead of letting the zeros dominate; (b) gate HRP ON only when K>=2 genuinely orthogonal (non-equity) sleeves are simultaneously ACTIVE, since on an all-equity active set it is a provable null; (c) run the falsifier exactly as written and ship ONLY if net-of-beta Sharpe rises >=0.05 AND MaxDD/vol improve AND theta does not drift to its 0.2 floor in live A/B. Given the regime-lag in the correlation estimate and the incumbent already being inverse-vol, the realistic incremental net-of-beta after-cost Sharpe is ~+0.02 to +0.05 over legacy, at or below the kill threshold.

scored.name: Concordance-Gated Momentum (meta-label filter + win-prob sizer on xs_momentum)
scored.family: ML-concordance confidence gate / sizer on directional sleeves (meta-labeling wrapper)

scores.name: Concordance-Gated Momentum (meta-label filter + win-prob sizer on xs_momentum)
scores.requiredFixes: Two Fatal findings make this CUT, not fixable by parameter tweak. (1) SHARPE REALITY (Fatal): the spec's headline metric "net-of-SPY-beta Sharpe" is not implemented — computeMetrics (metrics.ts:44-58) reports raw (annRet-rf)/annVol with rf=0 and zero beta adjustment; beta() exists (indicators.ts:162) but has ZERO call sites in metrics/allocator/engine (grep confirms only standalone redteam scripts call it). The base 1.04 is ~0.6-beta long-only equity over a bull-heavy 2014-25 sample; strip beta and residual alpha is near-zero. Must wire beta() into metrics, regress sleeve on SPY, and re-run the falsifier on the residual series before any Sharpe claim. (2) REGIME FEASIBILITY (Fatal): red-team reimplemented the EXACT spec on real cached 11y data and the gate premise is backwards — gate-PASS fwd-21d hit-rate 57.2% vs gate-REJECT 63.8% (z=-2.48), corr(concordance,fwd21d)=-0.05, anti-monotone buckets, gated pseudo-Sharpe 0.99 vs base 1.24 (~-0.25 vs the +0.10 the falsifier requires). The votes that move the gate are short-horizon mean-reversion (v1/v4/v5), so ">=4/6 bullish" drops the strongest extended winners — it fights the momentum premium it claims to refine. The spec's own falsifier is tripped. Also: base-1.04 prior is hard-coded (meta.ts:33) with NO committed evidence file (git ls-files research/ is empty), so the prior is unauditable. Salvage paths (drop MR votes, flip thesis to low-concordance-buys, restrict to crash regimes) are new specs, not fixes.
scores.oneLineRationale: Two Fatal findings (beta never stripped in-engine; empirical 11y test shows the gate inverts hit-rate and subtracts ~0.25 Sharpe vs base, tripping its own falsifier) => CUT; cheap-to-compute and a real feedback loop cannot rescue a beta-repackaging whose wrapper has negative forward information.

spec.name: Concordance-Gated Momentum (meta-label filter + win-prob sizer on xs_momentum)
spec.family: ML-concordance confidence gate / sizer on directional sleeves
spec.mechanism: Pure meta-labeling. The base sleeve (xs_momentum, the engine's best single sleeve at after-cost Sharpe 1.04) already harvests the documented momentum premium. The wrapper does NOT create direction; it answers 'given that 12-1 momentum says go long name X, how likely is THIS long to win?' by checking whether an independent 6-model statistical ensemble (mean-reversion, intermediate-momentum, volatility, technical-position, trend-strength, structural-trend, all computable from OHLCV in-engine via indicators.ts) AGREES on the sign over the forward 21-60d horizon. Concordance = fraction of sub-models voting bullish. Economic basis: false-positive momentum longs (late-stage, exhausted, about-to-reverse) are exactly where mean-reversion/technical sub-models flip bearish while the trend model stays long; internal DISAGREEMENT flags the doomed longs.
