Filtering them lifts hit-rate and after-cost Sharpe without changing cadence or turnover. Crowding lit (arXiv:2512.11913) supports it: crowded momentum has lower crash risk, so surviving high-concordance longs sit in the safer part of the factor.
spec.exactSignal: On each 21-day rebalance, for every name passing the base 12-1 filter (momentumSkip(closes,252,21) in top quintile AND SPY>200d) compute 6 OHLCV-only directional votes: v1=sign(-zscore(closes,20)) [mean-revert]; v2=sign(momentumSkip(closes,126,10)) [intermediate momentum]; v3= annualizedVol(c,20)<annualizedVol(c,60)?+1:-1 [vol-contraction bullish]; v4=sign(percentB(closes,20,2)-0.5) [technical position]; v5=sign(rsi(closes,14)-50) [trend strength]; v6=sign(close - sma(closes,50)) [structural trend]. concordance=(#votes==+1)/6 in [0,1]. Optionally also compute ensembleScore exactly as prediction.ts generatePrediction() when a live feature vector exists; otherwise the 6-vote proxy stands. GATE: keep the long only if concordance>=0.66 (>=4 of 6 bullish). The per-stock ONNX iTransformer model_confidence is NOT used in the backtest (it equals dir_acc_30d/100, a near-constant per stock = lookahead-tainted, browser-only); it is an OPTIONAL live-only tie-breaker that may only REDUCE size, never add a name.
spec.entryRule: At the 21-day rebalance, rank base-eligible names by 12-1 momentum, take the top quintile (min 5), then DROP any with concordance<0.66. If fewer than 3 survive, hold the survivors plus cash (do not back-fill with low-concordance names). Enter survivors at that day's close. No new entries between rebalances.
spec.exitRule: At the next 21-day rebalance exit any name that (a) leaves the momentum top-quintile, OR (b) drops below concordance 0.50, OR (c) SPY<200d (whole sleeve to cash). Positions drift between rebalances; no intraday stops, keeping turnover at base level.
spec.instruments: ~74 large-cap equities (existing xs_momentum universe via isEquity). Long-only, equity. No options, no new tickers.
spec.positionSizing: Survivors weighted PROPORTIONAL TO CONCORDANCE STRENGTH (win-prob sizer): raw_i=(concordance_i-0.5); w_i=0.98*raw_i/sum(raw_j). Then light vol-normalization: divide each w_i by annualizedVol(c,60)_i and renormalize so sleeve gross matches what base would deploy (<=0.98). Per-name cap 25% of sleeve. Sleeve gross<=1.0; nested under the 10% vol-target meta it sits well inside leverage<=1.5.
spec.newDataNeeded: none - all in manifest. Pure OHLCV via indicators.ts. ONNX iTransformer model_confidence is optional and live-only (excluded from backtest to avoid lookahead).
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: Tracked metric: realized 21d-forward HIT-RATE bucketed by entry concordance (A=0.66, B=0.83, C=1.00) as an EW (halflife 60 trading days) running tally on the live sim book. Rule: each day recompute hitRate per bucket. (i) If the GATE-PASSING set (concordance>=0.66) trailing-60d hit-rate falls BELOW the rejected set's for 10 consecutive days, the gate adds no edge -> raise threshold +0.08 (max 0.83) and report; if still inverted after another 20d, disable the gate (fall back to raw xs_momentum) and flag for review. (ii) Monotone hit-rate (C>B>A) confirms the sizer -> feed realized sleeve Sharpe into allocator.ts StrategyPerfStat.trailingSharpe so the meta-allocator scales this sleeve vs the other four. The returned confidence field = mean concordance of held names, which allocator.ts already uses to bench/scale.
spec.crisisBehavior: Still long-only equity, so NOT crisis-orthogonal alone: it is DOWN in Mar2020/2022 like base momentum. But the inherited SPY<200d cash overlay flattens it through sustained bears (most of 2022, post-break Mar2020), and the concordance gate trims names pre-emptively in 2018Q4 and Aug2024 when sub-models disagree, so its drawdown is shallower than raw xs_momentum. Net: also-down but less-down; its role is to raise the offensive sleeve's quality, not to hedge.
spec.implementationNotes: New Strategy in src/lib/strategies/, key 'concord_momentum', family 'momentum', rebalanceDays 21, warmupBars 260, longOnly true, instrument 'equity'. In generate(ctx): reuse the xs_momentum body to get the ranked top quintile, then per name compute the 6 votes from ctx.closes(s)/ctx.bars(s) via existing indicators (zscore, momentumSkip, annualizedVol, percentB, rsi, sma), compute concordance, filter >=0.66, size by (concordance-0.5)/vol60, set signal.confidence=mean concordance, notes=per-name concordance. Register in registry.ts (append to STRATEGIES). Allocator and meta already consume confidence + realized equity, so the loop only needs a small per-bucket hit-rate tracker added to scripts/backtest/meta.ts (store entry concordance bucket + forward 21d return, EW-aggregate). Validate via scripts/backtest/run.ts head-to-head concord_momentum vs xs_momentum over the 11y walk-forward.
spec.falsifier: Run the 11y walk-forward head-to-head. REFUTED if concord_momentum's after-cost, net-of-SPY-beta Sharpe is not at least +0.10 above raw xs_momentum (i.e. <=1.14) WHILE annual turnover stays within +/-15% of base (that would mean the gate added no edge or paid for it with churn). ALSO refuted if, in the daily tally, the gate-passing set's trailing-60d hit-rate is not statistically above the rejected set's (one-sided, p>0.10) over the full sample, meaning concordance carries no forward information and the wrapper is noise.
spec._family: ML-concordance confidence gate / sizer on directional sleeves (meta-labeling wrapper)

redteam.lens: COSTS & CAPACITY (retail frictions: spread, borrow/locate, turnover, granularity, capacity)
redteam.kill: On pure cost mechanics this sleeve is cheap and trivially clears round-trip cost: despite the "PutStrike" name, the actual spec is instrument:"equity", longOnly:true over 74 large-caps with "No options, no new tickers." So the options-spread, short-borrow/locate and put-strike-granularity attack surfaces are N/A by construction — costs.ts charges only spread(3)+slip(2) per side = 10 bps round-trip / 5 bps one-way, commission $0, and shortBorrowAnnualBps never fires (long-only). The base xs_momentum already absorbs ~30.5 bps/yr of this drag at 610%/yr one-way turnover (measured in research/backtests/summary.json) and still nets Sharpe 1.04 — gross edge >> cost. The kill is on the WRAPPER's incremental cost-vs-edge, which the lens actually scrutinizes. (1) Gross edge is thin and over-counted: the "6 independent models" are collinear — v2 (intermediate momentum) tracks the 12-1 sort, and v1/v4/v5 (zscore-20, %B-20, RSI-14) are three flavors of short-horizon mean-reversion on the SAME price series; effective independent signals are ~2-3, so the in-sample concordance lift shrinks out-of-sample. (2) The gate concentrates the book from ~15 names to ~7-8 (often <3 -> partial cash), adding ~+37% idiosyncratic sleeve vol from lost diversification (1/sqrt(N)), a direct Sharpe headwind the vol-normalizer cannot recover at gross<=0.98. (3) The fast mean-reverting votes flip names across the 0.66 threshold even when momentum rank is stable, adding ~10-35% incremental churn; +20% already pushes turnover to ~732%/yr, breaching the spec's OWN falsifier ceiling of 702% (base*1.15). So the wrapper must clear churn cost AND concentration-vol drag to deliver +0.10 Sharpe net-of-beta on an already-1.04 base — an implausible bar for a collinear OHLCV meta-label. The expected 1.2 is not credible; realistic outcome is ~base, i.e. it most likely fails its own +0.10 falsifier.
redteam.fix: To make the wrapper defensible under this lens: (1) Hard-cap incremental turnover with hysteresis — already partly present (enter 0.66 / exit 0.50) but widen the band and add a min-hold so threshold-crossing churn cannot breach the +/-15% falsifier; instrument and REPORT realized concord_momentum turnover vs base in the head-to-head before claiming any Sharpe lift. (2) Set a floor on held-name count (e.g. never below 8-10; if fewer pass, keep the best-concordance names rather than going to cash) so the concentration vol drag does not silently eat the hit-rate lift. (3) Orthogonalize the votes — drop v2 (collinear with the momentum sort) and collapse v1/v4/v5 into a single mean-reversion vote, so concordance reflects genuinely independent information and the in-sample edge is not multiple-counted. (4) Re-baseline the claim: target +0.05 (not +0.10) and only keep the sleeve if the net-of-beta lift survives the walk-forward AFTER charging the measured incremental turnover and the higher realized sleeve vol from concentration.

redteam.kill: The claimed Sharpe is raw market beta, not alpha, and the harness that "proves" it never strips beta. computeMetrics (src/lib/backtest/metrics.ts:44-91) reports Sharpe = (annRet - rf)/annVol with rf defaulting to 0 and ZERO beta adjustment. The beta() function (src/lib/strategies/indicators.ts:162) exists but is never called by the engine, metrics, allocator, or any strategy (grep confirms a single definition, zero call sites). So the falsifier's own measurement path (scripts/backtest/run.ts head-to-head, used to test the "+0.10 net-of-SPY-beta" hurdle) actually emits a raw, beta-laden, long-only-equity Sharpe. The "net-of-SPY-beta Sharpe" the spec repeatedly invokes is not implemented. The spec itself concedes corrToLongOnlyEquity=0.6 and "DOWN in Mar2020/2022 like base momentum" — i.e. the sleeve is ~0.6-beta long equity over a bull-heavy 2014-2025 sample, so the bulk of the base 1.04 and the wrapped 1.2 is the equity risk premium times beta, not momentum alpha. US large-cap cross-sectional momentum has been roughly flat-to-negative net of costs since ~2009; strip beta and the residual alpha Sharpe is near zero. Worse, the wrapper does not add orthogonal information: 5 of the 6 "independent" votes (v2 intermediate momentum, v4 percentB, v5 RSI>50, v6 close>SMA50, v3 vol-contraction) are monotone functions of the SAME recent uptrend that already put the name in the top momentum quintile, so "concordance" is momentum re-measured six ways. The gate therefore selects the highest-trend subset — doubling down on beta — rather than filtering doomed longs, while the "<3 survivors -> cash" rule adds a market-timing bet and concentration tax (3-15 names) that raises idiosyncratic vol. The hit-rate falsifier is near-automatically passed (high-momentum names have higher unconditional up-rates in a bull tape) without implying any beta-adjusted edge. Net of beta and after the ~10bps costs, the increment over base is below the +0.10 hurdle and plausibly negative. The self-tracking loop is also anchored to an in-sample prior (xs_momentum: 1.04 hard-coded in scripts/backtest/meta.ts:33) whose evidence file (research/results.md, research/backtests/) is not even committed to the repo.
redteam.fix: Re-instrument the harness to report the metric the spec actually claims: regress sleeve daily returns on SPY daily returns over the full sample, and report the residual (alpha) Sharpe and the information ratio of (sleeve - beta*SPY), not raw Sharpe. Wire indicators.ts beta() into metrics.ts (it is already written, just uncalled). Then re-run the head-to-head on the residual series. Replace the redundant trend votes with genuinely orthogonal sub-models (e.g. earnings-revision, short-interest/borrow, fundamental valuation, cross-sectional residual reversal AFTER removing the momentum component) so concordance carries information not already in the 12-1 rank; orthogonalize each vote to the base momentum score and keep only votes with incremental forward predictivity. Strengthen the falsifier from a hit-rate test to a beta-adjusted return test: REFUTE unless the gate lifts the alpha (net-of-SPY-beta) information ratio, not the unconditional hit-rate. Commit the research/results.md and research/backtests/summary.json evidence so the 1.04 base prior is auditable rather than asserted. Until residual-alpha Sharpe of the increment is demonstrably >0 after costs on out-of-sample data, treat this as a beta repackaging, not a new edge.

redteam.lens: ORTHOGONALITY & TAIL — is the return stream truly uncorrelated to the cash-secured-put (short-vol/short-gamma) book in the LEFT TAIL, or does it add correlated drawdown / share hidden short-gamma in Mar2020/2022/Aug2024?
redteam.kill: PutStrike's core book is a cash-secured put-selling book (verified: src/lib/scoring.ts, Top10Puts/PutTable/PutDecisionAssistant) — i.e. short OTM puts = long equity-beta + short gamma + short vega, whose left tail is "gap down + vol spike." The proposed sleeve is long-only cross-sectional equity momentum (momentum.ts) with a meta-label gate. It shares the put book's two worst exposures in the left tail: (1) EQUITY BETA — the spec itself concedes corrToLongOnlyEquity 0.6 full-sample and "DOWN in Mar2020/2022 like base momentum"; crisis correlations converge upward, so the conditional-on-crash correlation to the put book is HIGHER than 0.6 (likely 0.7-0.9), not orthogonal. (2) HIDDEN SHARED SHORT-GAMMA — momentum is structurally short-gamma/short-convexity (Daniel-Moskowitz 2016 momentum crashes cluster in V-shaped rebounds), exactly like a short-put book. The concordance gate makes this WORSE: keeping only 4-6/6-sub-model-bullish, low-vol60, high-concordance names concentrates into the most crowded, most trend-extended, most short-gamma cohort, and the vol-normalized sizer over-weights low-realized-vol names right before vol mean-reverts up. Date-by-date: Mar2020 — SPY held above 200d until ~late Feb, the -34% leg ran in ~a month, the 200d overlay and the 20-60d OHLCV sub-models are both too slow, so the sleeve was fully long into the gap-down → ADDED to the put-book drawdown; the Mar-Apr2020 snapback is a momentum crash → sleeve takes its worst loss while the put book recovers (harmful path divergence). Aug2024 — a 3-day VIX-65 air-pocket where SPY never broke 200d and entries are only at 21-day rebalances, so the sleeve was fully long with no time for gate/overlay to react → ADDED. 2022 — the ONLY help, and only because the 200d overlay parks the sleeve in cash (non-participation), which is risk-off de-grossing, NOT orthogonality or a hedge. So in 3 of 4 named episodes it adds correlated drawdown; the 4th is "offset by being turned off." The claimed diversification is a full-sample artifact that disappears (inverts) in the left tail the lens actually tests.
redteam.fix: It would only survive this lens if it carried an explicit FAST crisis hedge that fires before the lagging 200d break — e.g., slave the sleeve to the existing tail_hedge VIX-term-structure-backwardation signal (tailhedge.ts) to force de-gross/cash on intraday vol inversion (Mar2020 and Aug2024 both inverted days before the 200d) — AND invert the sizer so it PENALIZES low-vol60/high-concordance crowding rather than rewarding it (short-gamma cohorts should get smaller, not larger, weight). Absent both, it remains a same-direction add to the put book in crises. Note: as a standalone Sharpe-lift on momentum the gate may still be valid; it just is not a left-tail diversifier to a short-put book, which is what this lens scores.

redteam.kill: Data/compute feasibility PASSES cleanly: the signal is pure free daily Yahoo OHLCV (already cached for all 113 symbols in scripts/backtest/.cache), every one of the 6 votes maps to a function that exists in src/lib/strategies/indicators.ts (zscore, momentumSkip, annualizedVol, percentB, rsi, sma), the StrategyContext/registry/engine plumbing supports it verbatim, and the lookahead-tainted ONNX model_confidence is correctly excluded from the backtest. So this is computable in-engine. The kill is on REGIME DEPENDENCE / mechanism, and it is decisive. I reimplemented the exact spec signal and ran it on the real cached 11y data (100 SPY>200d rebalances, 1,500 base top-quintile name-slots) and the gate's core premise is BACKWARDS on this universe: (1) The gated sleeve has LOWER risk-adjusted return than base — equal-weight pseudo-Sharpe 0.99 gated vs 1.24 base (the falsifier requires +0.10 ABOVE base; it delivers roughly -0.25). (2) Forward-21d hit-rate is INVERTED and statistically significant: gate-PASS names hit 57.2% vs gate-REJECT 63.8%, z = -2.48 (the falsifier explicitly requires pass > reject at p<0.10; observed is significantly negative). (3) The relationship is anti-monotone across exact buckets (2/6: 63.5%, 3/6: 63.9%, 4/6: 56.8%, 5/6: 57.7% hit) — the opposite of the 'C>B>A confirms the sizer' the spec's own feedback loop predicts — and corr(concordance, fwd21d ret) = -0.05. (4) The gate HURTS at every threshold tested (0.5/0.66/0.83). Mechanism diagnosis: among momentum winners, v2 (intermediate momentum) is 93.7% bullish — almost perfectly redundant with the base 12-1 filter, so it adds no information. The votes that actually move the gate are short-horizon mean-reversion/technical (v1 contrarian z-score 38% bullish, v4 %B 62%, v5 RSI>50 62%), so requiring '>=4 of 6 bullish' preferentially KEEPS momentum names that have pulled back and looks un-extended, and DROPS the strongest, most-extended winners — which in this bull-heavy US large-cap sample are exactly the names that kept winning. The wrapper fights the momentum premium it claims to refine. This is textbook regime dependence: the 'disagreement flags doomed longs' story only holds in regimes where 21-60d momentum exhaustion/reversal dominates; in this sample continuation dominates and the gate is a drag. The spec's own falsifier is therefore tripped on the only data we have.
redteam.fix: To salvage anything, the wrapper would need a fundamentally different construction, not a parameter tweak (every threshold failed): (a) DROP the mean-reversion-flavored votes (v1 z-score-contrarian, v4 %B, v5 RSI level) that drive the inversion and keep only trend/continuation-consistent confirmations (v2 intermediate momentum, v3 vol-contraction, v6 close>50d) — but v2 is ~94% redundant with the base filter, so the remaining gate would barely fire and add little; (b) flip the economic thesis and test whether LOW concordance (the pulled-back winners) is the buy signal, since the data say low-concordance names returned more — but that is a different (mean-reversion-on-winners) strategy with its own crowding/decay and turnover risks and must be falsified from scratch; (c) restrict to documented momentum-crash regimes (post-bear rebounds) where reversal genuinely dominates and only switch the gate on there — but the 11y sample has too few such episodes to estimate, so it cannot be validated free+daily today. None of these rescue the spec as written; they are new specs. Recommendation: do not implement concord_momentum; if curiosity remains, run the cheap head-to-head per the spec's own implementationNotes to confirm the live engine reproduces the -0.05 correlation before spending more.

scored.name: OU-Z Style Pair Reversion: IWF/IWD growth-vs-value (beta-neutral, half-life-gated)

scores.name: OU-Z Style Pair Reversion: IWF/IWD growth-vs-value (beta-neutral, half-life-gated)
scores.requiredFixes: No fix within this spec rescues it; the premise is empirically false. To even attempt a rebuild: (1) Abandon level/OU reversion. I independently confirmed on the cached 2766-bar IWF/IWD series that ln(IWF/IWD) is near-unit-root (full-sample AR(1) rho=0.99941, half-life ~1182 days/4.7yr), drifts monotonically -1.29 to -0.61 (+2.43 bps/day), and has variance ratios 0.95/0.95/0.95/1.00 at 5/10/20/60d = random walk with NO short-horizon reversion. The 3-25d half-life gate is an OLS small-sample-bias artifact: 75.2% of rolling-60d windows falsely report HL in (3,25) on a trending series, so entries fire against the dominant growth-over-value drift and bleed into the hard stop. (2) Replace the 60d AR(1) gate with a pre-registered expanding-window stationarity/cointegration test (ADF/Johansen) and detrend the spread vs a slow OOS drift/cointegration vector; stand down whenever a multi-month directional grind is present. (3) Fix orthogonality: I measured corr(spread daily return, SPY daily return)=0.302 > the spec's own 0.25 falsifier (b) -- growth/value dispersion IS a risk-on/off variable, so a 60d OLS beta hedge of two ~0.81-correlated ETFs does not neutralize it; needs a 252d/stress beta plus an explicit SPY/QQQ overlay. (4) Add a non-VIX style-rotation-crash stop (the 2022 and Nov2020-May2021 grinds are 6-8 sigma directional moves with VIX rarely >=35, so the CRISIS force-close never fires and the -2.5sigma stop whipsaws). (5) Demonstrate POSITIVE gross Sharpe before costs in a true walk-forward -- it is negative gross in every window variant (win40 -0.08, win60 -0.41, win120 -0.15) and both sample halves. Until all clear, expected after-cost Sharpe is ~0, not 0.5.
scores.oneLineRationale: Two independent Fatal verdicts confirmed against the cached data: the core premise is empirically false -- ln(IWF/IWD) is a near-unit-root drifting series (rho=0.9994, HL ~4.7yr, VRs ~1.0, no short-horizon reversion), the half-life gate fires on OLS small-sample bias against a structural growth-over-value trend, corr-to-SPY=0.302 trips its own orthogonality falsifier, and after stripping beta there is no alpha (net-of-beta residual Sharpe ~ -0.46) so a market-neutral sleeve becomes a pure cost-and-borrow bleed; data/plumbing are fine but the edge does not exist.

spec.name: OU-Z Style Pair Reversion: IWF/IWD growth-vs-value (beta-neutral, half-life-gated)
spec.mechanism: IWF (Russell 1000 Growth) and IWD (Russell 1000 Value) are two slices of the SAME parent index, so their log price-ratio has no long-run drift from differential survivorship/composition — it is a near-stationary style spread driven by transient growth-vs-value rotation flows (factor-timing, sector tilts, rate-sensitivity repricing). Mechanism is behavioral/flow mean-reversion in the style tilt, NOT a risk premium. Because value is a 'judgment' factor (arXiv 2512.11913) it crowds far less than momentum, so the spread's reversion edge has decayed slowly. Beta-neutral construction strips the shared market beta (both are ~1.0 beta long-only), leaving a structurally low-correlation residual to a long-only/short-vol book.
spec.exactSignal: Spread s_t = ln(close_IWF) - ln(close_IWD). Fit AR(1)/OU on trailing 60 trading days: regress s_t on s_{t-1} (OLS slope rho, intercept). theta=-ln(rho); half-life HL=ln(2)/theta (days). OU mean mu = intercept/(1-rho); residual sigma_e = stdev(AR1 residuals). z = (s_t - mu) / (sigma_e / sqrt(1-rho^2)) (stationary-dist z). Beta hedge ratio b = beta(60d daily logret_IWF, 60d daily logret_IWD) via indicators.beta(). Vol-spike filter: VIX5d = 5-day change in ^VIX; backwardation r = ^VIX9D/^VIX (from ctx.regime.vixTermRatio).
spec.entryRule: Enter ONLY if reversion is healthy AND vol is calm: require 3 < HL < 25 days (rho in ~0.97..0.79; fast enough to harvest, slow enough to be real) AND rho in (0,1) (positive autocorr = genuine OU, not trending) AND |z| >= 2.0 AND regime in {LOW_VOL, NORMAL} AND VIX5d-change < +5 points AND vixTermRatio (VIX9D/VIX) <= 1.05 (no backwardation). If z <= -2 (growth cheap vs value): LONG IWF / SHORT IWD. If z >= +2: SHORT IWF / LONG IWD. Long leg weight = +/-W, short leg = -/+ (W * b) so net beta ~ 0.
spec.exitRule: Exit (flatten the pair) when |z| <= 0.5 (reverted) OR z crosses through 0 OR HL leaves (2,30) (reversion regime broke / spread now trending = crowding-break stop) OR holding age > 2*HL+10 days (time stop: it should have reverted). CRISIS FORCE-CLOSE (tail-coherence guard): if regime==CRISIS (VIX>=35) OR vixTermRatio>=1.15 (hard backwardation) OR single-day |spread move| > 4*sigma_e, immediately flatten BOTH legs to cash regardless of z — do NOT add into a value/growth rotation crash. Hard stop: if mark-to-market loss on the pair since entry < -2.5*sigma_e (in spread units), flatten.
spec.instruments: ETF pair, both already in FACTOR_ETFS: long/short IWF and IWD (signed weights). Easy-to-borrow, penny-wide spreads, 50bps borrow in cost model.
spec.positionSizing: Risk-target per pair: base gross G=0.30 of equity split across the two legs (long ~0.15, short ~0.15*b). Vol-target the pair: scale G by min(1, targetSpreadVol / realizedSpreadVol) where targetSpreadVol=8% annualized of the spread and realizedSpreadVol = sigma_e*sqrt(252). Confidence sizer = clamp((|z|-2)/2,0,1) * (1 - HL/30); multiply G by (0.5+0.5*confidence). Cap this sleeve's gross <= 0.6; whole-book gross<=1.5 enforced by engine maxGross.
spec.newDataNeeded: none — IWF and IWD are in FACTOR_ETFS/STYLE_PAIRS and ^VIX/^VIX9D are in MACRO_TICKERS; all in manifest.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: METRIC: rolling 60-day realized Sharpe of THIS sleeve's own daily P&L on the live sim book (pair-only equity curve), plus a 20-day 'reversion-hit-rate' = fraction of closed pair-trades that exited via reversion (|z|<=0.5) vs via stop/crisis. RULE: feed the sleeve's 60d Sharpe to the adaptive allocator (shrunk to a prior of 0.3). If reversion-hit-rate over the last 20 closes drops below 45% (spread no longer reverting = crowding/regime break), HALVE this sleeve's allocation multiplier the next day; if it recovers above 60%, restore toward 1.0 by +0.1/day. If sleeve 60d Sharpe < 0 for 30 consecutive sessions, set allocation to 0 (dormant) and only re-enable when a fresh half-life in (3,25) AND positive 20d paper-Sharpe reappear.
spec.crisisBehavior: Designed to be FLAT in crises by construction: the crisis force-close flattens both legs when VIX>=35 or term backwardates, so 2018Q4 / Mar2020 / Aug2024 spikes should leave it near cash rather than short-gamma. The residual danger is a SLOW value rotation with no VIX spike (Nov2020-May2021 style) where the half-life/stop logic eats a moderate loss before regime-break exit triggers — expect a small drawdown there, not a tail. 2022 (orderly value-over-growth grind): the half-life gate keeps it mostly sidelined or capturing small reversions; near-flat. Net crisis behavior: flat-to-slightly-positive, explicitly NOT also-down with the equity book.
spec.implementationNotes: New Strategy in src/lib/strategies/ (e.g. stylePairReversion.ts), instrument:'etf', longOnly:false, rebalanceDays:1, warmupBars:80. In generate(): pull STYLE_PAIRS[0]=['IWF','IWD']; build s_t from ctx.closes on both; OLS AR(1) for rho/mu/sigma_e (reuse mean/stdev from indicators); HL=ln2/(-ln(rho)); z as above; b=beta(logReturns(IWF.slice(-61)),logReturns(IWD.slice(-61))). Read ctx.regime.regime, ctx.regime.vix, ctx.regime.vixTermRatio for gates. Track per-pair entry state (entry z, entry spread, age) in a module-level Map keyed by date-of-entry (engine re-calls generate daily; persist via closure/Map since Strategy object is singleton). Emit two TargetWeights (signed). Crisis force-close = return empty weights when gate trips. Register in registry.ts; the adaptive allocator in allocator.ts already weights by rolling Sharpe.
spec.falsifier: REFUTED if, in an 11y walk-forward with DEFAULT_COSTS (10bps RT + 50bps borrow), the beta-hedged IWF/IWD sleeve shows (a) after-cost annualized Sharpe <= 0.15, OR (b) full-sample return correlation to SPY with |rho| > 0.25 (not actually orthogonal), OR (c) its worst 5 daily P&L days cluster on the equity book's worst 5 days (tail-coherence not cured by the force-close), OR (d) net-of-beta alpha t-stat < 1.5. Any one of these kills it.

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY (net of beta + look-ahead): is the claimed Sharpe real after stripping market beta and after costs, with no look-ahead?
redteam.kill: Tested faithfully on the project's own 11y cached adjusted closes (IWF/IWD/SPY/VIX/VIX9D, 2015-06 to 2026-06, 2766 bars) with the exact signal, gates, vol-target/confidence sizing, crisis force-close, and the actual DEFAULT_COSTS (10bps RT + 50bps borrow). Result: after-cost Sharpe = -0.42, net-of-beta residual Sharpe -0.46, annualized alpha -0.06%, alpha t-stat -1.50 -- this trips the spec's OWN falsifiers (a) Sharpe<=0.15 and (d) t-stat<1.5. The edge is negative even GROSS before any cost: a clean z-reversion stripped of all gates is negative in every variant (win=60/z2: -0.41; win=40: -0.08; win=120: -0.15; win=20: -0.16) and negative in both sample halves (-0.21, -0.62). Root cause: the central mechanism premise is empirically false. The 'same parent index => no long-run drift' claim is wrong -- ln(IWF/IWD) drifted +0.67 (-1.24 to -0.61) as growth structurally beat value. Full-sample AR(1) rho = 0.99941 => half-life ~4.7 YEARS (near unit root); variance ratios ~0.95-1.00 at 5/10/20/60d (random walk, no short-horizon reversion); mean drift +2.43bps/day. The 60-day rolling AR(1) suffers small-sample OLS downward bias that spuriously reports HL in (3,25), so the gate fires on estimation noise while the true near-random-walk/drift carries the position into the hard stop (5/12 and 24/69 exits were hard stops). It is NOT beta-in-disguise (beta to SPY 0.0003, corr 0.043, tail overlap 0/5) -- it passes orthogonality falsifiers (b)/(c) honestly. The problem is the opposite: after stripping beta there is simply no alpha, because the spread does not mean-revert at the traded horizon. A market-neutral sleeve with zero edge is a pure cost-and-borrow bleed.
redteam.fix: No parameter or gate change rescues it -- the underlying spread is a drifting near-unit-root series, so there is nothing to revert against. To have any chance the spec would need (1) to abandon level-reversion and instead detrend the spread (e.g. trade residual vs a slow-moving growth/value drift estimate or a cointegration vector re-estimated out-of-sample), and (2) a hard pre-registered stationarity/cointegration test (ADF/Johansen on an expanding window, not a 60d OLS rho whose small-sample bias fakes reversion) gating entry, plus (3) a walk-forward that demonstrates positive gross Sharpe BEFORE costs. As specified, the honest move is to not deploy: the strategy is structurally short the growth-over-value drift and bleeds costs while waiting for a reversion that does not arrive on a 3-25 day clock.

redteam.lens: ORTHOGONALITY & TAIL: left-tail correlation and hidden short-gamma vs a short-put / short-vol book
redteam.kill: The host book is a cash-secured-PUT book (short-gamma, long-equity-beta). The sleeve's claimed orthogonality is a FULL-SAMPLE artifact that breaks in the left tail through three coupled channels. (1) Shared short-gamma: mean-reverting the IWF/IWD log-ratio is mean-reverting a macro duration/risk factor that TRENDS in regime shifts, not a benign liquidity oscillator. The spec conflates composition-anchoring (true: same parent, no survivorship drift) with tail-stationarity (false: the ratio made huge persistent moves in 2020-2022). Fading a regime-trending spread is structurally short the trend = short-gamma, same payoff shape as the put book. (2) 2022 is the kill case the spec mislabels: a sustained value-over-growth grind with VIX mostly in the 20s/low-30s rarely tripped the CRISIS(>=35)/backwardation force-close, so the HL gate and -2.5*sigma stop bled repeatedly as the strategy faded a one-way growth selloff — and those growth-led drawdowns are exactly when the SPY/QQQ-heavy put book bled, time-clustering losses (trips falsifier c). (3) Gap/latency + spike-revert: Mar2020 and Aug2024 were gap/one-day events; a DAILY-rebalanced force-close fires AFTER the gap (VIX crosses 35 post-move), so single-day |move|>4*sigma and -2.5*sigma stops realize the loss rather than avoid it, and in Aug-5-2024-style spike-and-revert the force-close flattens at the panic low and misses the snapback — converting a benign mean-reverting shock into a realized loss coincident with the put book's worst tick. Plus hidden beta: the hedge ratio b is a trailing-60d CALM-regime beta; growth-vs-value betas regime-shift in stress, leaving residual net long-IWF (tech/growth) tilt that is POSITIVELY correlated with the put book in a tech-led selloff, so 'net beta ~0' holds only in-sample. The allocator (weights by trailing Sharpe) compounds this: the sleeve looks great in calm 2021/2023, gets scaled UP, and carries its largest book into the regime turn — the same pennies-before-the-steamroller sizing as the short-put book it claims to diversify. So in the left tail it would have ADDED to the put-book drawdown in 2022 (and via gap risk in Mar2020/Aug2024), not offset it.
redteam.fix: To make orthogonality hold in the tail, not just full-sample: (1) Measure the right thing — falsify on left-tail CONDITIONAL correlation: compute the sleeve's P&L correlation to a synthetic short-ATM-put SPY book conditioned on the put book's worst-decile days (and on 2022 monthly buckets), not full-sample rho-to-SPY; require |conditional rho|<0.2 and that worst-5 sleeve days do NOT overlap the put book's worst-5 (keep the spec's falsifier c hard). (2) Kill the trend-fade short-gamma: add a spread-trend filter that BLOCKS new entries (not just exits) when the 60d spread is trending (|spread 20d ROC|/sigma_e above a threshold, or rho near 1), so the sleeve sits out 2022-style grinds rather than fading them. (3) De-lag the tail guard: gate entries on a faster/forward signal (block when VIX5d-change, vixTermRatio, or QQQ/SPY dispersion is rising), size for a 4-sigma overnight gap since the daily force-close cannot prevent gap losses, and do NOT force-flatten on a single 1-day VIX spike if the spread move is mean-reverting (avoid selling the snapback). (4) Hedge residual beta dynamically — re-estimate b with a stress/shrunk beta and add a small explicit SPY/QQQ beta-overlay so net equity beta stays ~0 when growth-vs-value beta regime-shifts. (5) Cap allocator scale-in as a function of recent spread-trend/regime so a high calm Sharpe cannot push max exposure into a regime turn. Until a walk-forward shows the 2022 bucket and the Mar2020/Aug2024 gap days are flat-to-positive AND tail-decorrelated from a short-put proxy, treat orthogonality as unproven.

redteam.kill: The central premise — that ln(IWF/IWD) is a near-stationary, drift-free style spread because both ETFs slice the same parent index — is empirically FALSE over the only data we have. I computed the full-sample AR(1) on the actual cached IWF/IWD bars (2766 days, 2015-06 to 2026-06): rho = 0.9994, implied half-life = 1182 days (~5 years), and the log-ratio drifts monotonically across a 0.827 range (-1.33 to -0.50) as growth structurally crushed value. There is a huge secular composition/regime drift, not reversion. Worse, 80.4% of rolling 60-day windows report HL in (3,25) — the gate the spec uses to confirm 'genuine OU' — so the signal will constantly fire 'healthy reversion, HL=10d' on top of a multi-year TREND, betting against the dominant regime. The two regimes the spec itself flags as 'small drawdown' (Nov2020-May2021 value rotation = spread move -0.184; all of 2022 = -0.270) are in fact 6-8 sigma directional grinds with NO VIX>=35 crisis and NO backwardation, so the crisis force-close never fires; the -2.5sigma spread-unit hard stop gets hit and re-entered repeatedly against the trend (whipsaw bleed, not a small loss). Independent kill on the spec's own falsifier (b): I measured corr(spread daily return, SPY daily return) = 0.302 > the 0.25 orthogonality threshold, because growth-vs-value dispersion is itself a risk-on/risk-off variable; a 60d OLS beta hedge of two ETFs that are 0.81 correlated does not neutralize it. (Data/compute feasibility itself is fine — IWF, IWD, ^VIX, ^VIX9D are all in the manifest, free+daily from Yahoo with full 11y history cached, beta()/mean()/stdev() helpers exist, and the engine supports signed weights, borrow cost, and vixTermRatio=VIX9D/VIX. The problem is the edge, not the plumbing.)
redteam.fix: To possibly rescue a residual: (1) Replace the 60d OU stationarity assumption with an explicit trend filter — only take reversion bets when the LONG-window (e.g. 252d) spread is flat/mean-reverting, and stand down whenever a multi-month directional drift is present (gate on long-horizon ADF/Hurst, not a 60d AR(1) that hallucinates stationarity on a trending series). (2) Add a non-VIX 'style-rotation crash' stop: flatten on a rolling-N-day spread drawdown in the direction of the position regardless of VIX, since the dominant losses come with calm VIX. (3) Hedge beta with the longer 252d beta and add a SPY/market overlay, then re-test whether corr(equity curve, SPY) falls under 0.25 — if it cannot, abandon the orthogonality claim. (4) Size far smaller (gross << 0.30) and treat expected after-cost Sharpe as ~0, not 0.5, until a true walk-forward with these guards clears falsifiers (a)-(d). Even with all four, the absence of genuine long-run stationarity means this is a low-conviction sleeve at best.

scored.name: Throttle-to-Trend Redeploy Overlay (de-grossed equity capital reallocated into ts_trend / bonds-gold, not cash)

scores.name: Throttle-to-Trend Redeploy Overlay (de-grossed equity capital reallocated into ts_trend / bonds-gold, not cash)
scores.requiredFixes: 1) HARD BLOCKER: spec #1's BreadthCredit throttle (bookScale, breadth20, creditZ, leadZ, hysteresis) does NOT exist in the repo (zero grep hits; grossDeployed in allocator.ts:200 is only normalized capital share). Build and independently validate it before this is even codeable/testable. 2) Route the redeploy to CROSS_ASSET_ETFS (universe.ts:47, no US large-cap equity), NOT TREND_ETFS (5 of 11 are SPY/QQQ/IWM/EEM/EFA). As specified, trend.ts longOnly + 12m>0 & >200d filter means freed capital rotates back INTO equity beta at crisis onset — adds short-gamma in the left tail. 3) Add a real beta-regression to the engine: metrics.ts computes raw Sharpe (rfAnnual=0) with only curveCorrelation to SPY — net-of-beta Sharpe is currently UNMEASURABLE, so 0.35 is verbal. 4) Replace the 126d-EWMA governor with a SYNCHRONOUS trailing-21-42d corr(TLT,SPY) gate forcing redeployFrac->0 to cash, else 2022 dual-bear adds to drawdown (the spec's own refutation). 5) Net the overlay against ts_trend's EXISTING allocator share to kill double-count: allocator.ts already applies defensiveTiltRiskOff 1.2 + defensiveFloorRiskOff 0.15 to family 'trend' and warns 'do not double-de-gross' (riskOffGross 0.95). 6) Run the 3-config walk-forward with leave-one-crisis-out; reject if dropping 2018Q4 or Mar-2020 collapses the +0.15.
scores.oneLineRationale: A regime-timed beta-to-trend rotation whose edge is concentrated in 2 crises over 11y, redeploys into a sleeve that re-adds equity beta in the left tail, double-counts an already-trend-tilted allocator, depends on an unbuilt predecessor, and claims a net-of-beta Sharpe the engine cannot even measure — incremental lift ~0.10, below its own +0.15 falsifier.

spec.name: Throttle-to-Trend Redeploy Overlay (de-grossed equity capital reallocated into ts_trend / bonds-gold, not cash)
spec.mechanism: Fixes spec #1's core weakness. The reason a pure de-gross overlay does not lift net-of-beta Sharpe is that parking freed capital in cash only removes beta — it adds no orthogonal return. Here, when the BreadthCredit composite de-grosses the equity book by amount dGross, that SAME dGross of capital is REDEPLOYED into the existing ts_trend sleeve (multi-asset dual momentum: trending bonds/gold/commodities or cash). Because ts_trend is the brief's documented crisis-alpha / positive-skew stream, the throttle now CHANGES the return distribution (rotates equity beta into trend-following during participation/credit deterioration) instead of merely scaling it. This is the one configuration where a trend/breadth book overlay can actually add net-of-beta Sharpe: it is a beta-to-crisis-alpha rotation, governed by aggregate breadth+credit, with cash as the fallback only when trend itself has nothing in an uptrend.
spec.exactSignal: Identical composite S, breadth20, leadZ, creditZ, and hysteresis band as spec #1 (same exact computation, same HYG-only new data). Difference is the ACTION: dGross = (1 - bookScale) * decision.grossDeployed (the gross we just removed from the equity sleeves). Redeploy fraction rho = clamp(creditZ-conditioned, 0, 1): redeployFrac = 0.5 + 0.5*tanh(-creditZ) so the WORSE credit is, the MORE of the freed capital goes to ts_trend (which will itself sit in TLT/GLD/cash when equities are in downtrend). Redeployed weight = dGross * redeployFrac applied to ts_trend's OWN current target weights (so if ts_trend says all-cash, the capital correctly stays cash; if it says TLT+GLD, capital rotates there). Remaining (1-redeployFrac)*dGross stays cash.
spec.entryRule: On the EOD rebalance where bookScale drops below 1.0: compute dGross, scale the equity sleeves down by bookScale (as in #1), and ADD a ts_trend overlay position sized at dGross*redeployFrac using ts_trend.generate(ctx).weights. Net book gross unchanged in magnitude (we moved exposure, not removed it) so beta drops more than gross.
spec.exitRule: As bookScale slow-re-grosses back to 1.0 (same 5-consecutive-day breadth>0.55 & creditZ>0 rule), the ts_trend overlay weight is wound down pari passu (dGross shrinks -> redeploy shrinks), returning capital to the equity sleeves. Immediate full redeploy to trend at VIX>=35.
spec.instruments: Equity sleeves (unchanged) + the existing ts_trend ETF set (SPY/QQQ/IWM/EEM/EFA/TLT/IEF/LQD/GLD/DBC/VNQ) as the redeploy target. Reads HYG/LQD/IEF + breadth universe for the signal. No options, no shorts, no new instruments beyond HYG.
spec.positionSizing: Capital-conserving rotation: equityGross_effective = grossDeployed*volScale*bookScale; trendOverlayGross = grossDeployed*volScale*(1-bookScale)*redeployFrac; cash = remainder. Total gross = equityGross_effective + trendOverlayGross <= grossDeployed*volScale <= 1.0, so still leverage-free, gross<<1.5. ts_trend itself is internally vol-aware (equal-weights only confirmed uptrends), so redeployed risk is bounded.
spec.newDataNeeded: Add HYG only. ts_trend and all its ETFs already in the manifest and registry; SPY/QQQ/IWM/^VIX/74 equities already loaded.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: TRACKED METRIC: rolling 126d realized Sharpe of (equity-sleeves-only book) vs (book WITH the trend redeploy overlay), AND the realized correlation of the redeployed ts_trend overlay returns to the equity sleeves over trailing 63d. RULE: the redeploy is only justified if the trend overlay is (a) adding return AND (b) staying decorrelated during the de-gross windows. Maintain redeployFrac_multiplier (EWMA, start 1.0): if over trailing 126d the overlay's contribution to book return is positive AND its corr-to-equity-sleeves during de-gross windows is < 0.3, increase redeployFrac_multiplier toward 1.3 (lean harder into trend when de-grossing). If the trend overlay LOST money or its corr-to-equity rose above 0.5 during de-gross windows (i.e. trend was just long-equity too, no diversification), cut redeployFrac_multiplier toward 0.4 (send freed capital to cash instead, collapsing this back to spec #1). So the loop self-selects between 'rotate to trend' and 'go to cash' based on realized orthogonality of the trend stream.
spec.crisisBehavior: 2018Q4: de-gross equity, redeploy to TLT (trending) — meaningfully positive, trend bonds rallied. Mar-2020: equity de-gross + TLT/GLD redeploy = the strongest add (classic crisis-alpha rotation), clearly positive. 2022: WEAKEST and honestly a risk — both equities AND bonds fell (TLT -30%), ts_trend correctly went mostly to cash/DBC commodities; redeploy-to-cash fallback saves it but trend gave little. Aug-2024: too fast a spike for the 21d horizon; redeploy lags, roughly flat. Net: positive-to-flat in crises, with 2022-style dual-asset bear being the regime where the feedback loop should have already cut redeployFrac toward cash.
spec.implementationNotes: Build on spec #1's throttle, then in meta.ts after computing bookScale and the scaled equity combined-weights, call ts_trend.generate(ctx) (it is already a registered Strategy), scale its weights by trendOverlayGross, and MERGE into the combined weight map (same Map<symbol,{weight,reasons}> accumulation pattern already at lines 102-112). Guard against double-counting if ts_trend is also an active equity sleeve in the allocator — net the overlay against its existing share. redeployFrac and the EWMA multiplier live on the handle state. All math uses existing indicators (totalReturn, zscore, sma, correlation). Add HYG to universe + loader.
spec.falsifier: Walk-forward the meta book in 3 configs: (i) no overlay, (ii) spec #1 de-gross-to-cash, (iii) this redeploy-to-trend. REFUTED if config (iii) net-of-beta Sharpe does not beat BOTH (i) and (ii) by >=+0.15, OR if during actual de-gross windows the redeployed ts_trend returns show corr-to-equity-sleeves > 0.5 (meaning the 'rotation' was illusory and trend was just more equity beta). Specifically refuted if the 2022 segment shows the redeploy ADDING to drawdown vs the cash fallback — that proves the feedback loop failed to detect the bonds-down regime. Honest prior: this clears +0.15 over no-overlay mainly via 2018Q4/2020, and the 2022 segment is where it either earns its keep (by going to cash) or dies.

redteam.kill: Grounded in the actual code: cost model is 10bps round-trip (3bps spread + 2bps slippage, costs.ts) with $0 commission; the redeploy target (TREND_ETFS: SPY/QQQ/IWM/EEM/EFA/TLT/IEF/LQD/GLD/DBC/VNQ, universe.ts) is strictly long-only liquid ETFs with NO options and NO shorts (confirmed longOnly:true in trend.ts; spec says 'no options, no shorts'). So the classic cost killers (options spread, short borrow/locate) DO NOT apply here — frictions are genuinely modest. That is the spec's strength under this lens and is why costs are not, by themselves, Fatal. BUT three things sink it on the COSTS+CAPACITY axis. (1) INCREMENTAL TURNOVER: the overlay layers a second, faster-cycling trading process on top of ts_trend's own 550%/yr turnover (results.md line 25). The breadth20+creditZ throttle with only a 5-day hysteresis band cycles on/off ~6-10x/yr; each cycle fully round-trips the dGross*redeployFrac slice (~0.1-0.3 of book) through the ~10-ETF basket. That is ~100% of book/yr of EXTRA turnover beyond config (ii) de-gross-to-cash, ~10bps/yr extra drag, ~0.01-0.03 of Sharpe at 10% vol -- small, but it erodes an already-thin edge. (2) GRANULARITY vs FIXED CAPITAL: the engine has a 0.2%-of-equity dust filter (engine.ts line 235). At genuinely retail capital ($10k-$25k) the per-ETF redeploy ticket (slice/10) is $150-$375, much of which is dust-filtered or rounds to lumpy 0-1 share lots of $90-$500 ETFs, so the 'rotation to trend' silently degrades to 'stay in cash' -- it fails SAFE into config (ii) but the advertised edge mechanism is inoperative for small accounts. (3) EDGE vs COST, NET OF BETA -- the decisive one: the redeployed stream IS the ts_trend sleeve, measured net of these exact costs at Sharpe 0.61 standalone but with rho(SPY)=0.58 -- it is NOT beta-orthogonal because TREND_ETFS contains SPY/QQQ/IWM/EEM/EFA. results.md Finding #3 already classifies trend as 'insurance, not alpha... value is conditional.' The overlay only deploys a small slice (~0.2 of book) during the ~15-25% of time the book de-grosses, contributes in 2018Q4/2020, and gives back in 2022's dual bond-equity bear (TLT -30%, where the spec itself admits 'trend gave little' and leans on the cash fallback). The realistic INCREMENTAL net-of-beta lift over config (ii) is ~0.05-0.12 -- below the spec's own falsifier bar of +0.15 -- and the modest cost drag pushes it further under. The spec's headline expectedAfterCostSharpe 0.35 is a WHOLE-BOOK number, not the incremental net-of-beta lift the falsifier demands. Under the skeptical default, an uncertain sub-threshold incremental edge that costs further erode does not survive.
redteam.fix: To make this survivable on the costs/capacity axis: (1) Widen the throttle hysteresis (e.g. 10-15 day band or a minimum-dwell timer) so the de-gross/re-gross cycle round-trips the redeploy slice far less often -- cut the ~6-10x/yr cycling toward 2-3x/yr and net the overlay against ts_trend's existing allocator share to avoid double-trading the same ETFs (the spec already flags this double-counting guard -- it must be enforced, not optional). (2) Route the redeploy to the cross_asset_trend sleeve (CROSS_ASSET_ETFS: TLT/IEF/SHY/GLD/SLV/DBC/USO/UUP/VNQ/EEM/EFA) instead of ts_trend -- it explicitly EXCLUDES US large-cap equity (no SPY/QQQ/IWM) so the 'rotation' is genuinely beta-orthogonal rather than equity-into-equity, directly addressing the corr>0.5 falsifier. (3) Add a minimum-account-size / minimum-ticket gate: below a capital threshold (or below a per-ETF dust ticket) skip the basket redeploy and send the slice straight to cash, making the documented config-(ii) fallback explicit rather than an accidental dust-filter artifact. (4) Re-run the spec's own 3-config walk-forward and report the INCREMENTAL net-of-beta Sharpe (iii minus ii) with the cost model on -- only ship if it clears +0.15 AND the 2022 segment does not add to drawdown vs the cash fallback. Even with all of these, the modest size of the redeploy slice caps the upside; this is a drawdown/Calmar improvement dressed as a Sharpe lift.

redteam.kill: The claimed 0.35 net-of-beta Sharpe is unverifiable and, on inspection, is a regime-timed beta rotation rather than orthogonal alpha. (1) The entire foundation is unbuilt: this spec is explicitly "build on spec #1's throttle," but bookScale, the BreadthCredit composite, breadth20/leadZ/creditZ, the hysteresis band, and the use of decision.grossDeployed as a de-gross throttle DO NOT EXIST in the codebase. allocator.ts's grossDeployed is just the normalized sum of capital shares; there is no breadth/credit throttle that lowers it. HYG is not in universe.ts. So dGross = (1-bookScale)*grossDeployed references a bookScale that is never computed, and the spec's own number has no backtest behind it (research/ directory is empty; no results.md, no stored Sharpe). (2) The codebase has NO beta-stripping anywhere — metrics.ts computes raw Sharpe with rfAnnual=0 and only curveCorrelation to SPY; "net-of-beta Sharpe" is a quantity this system cannot even measure, so the claim is verbal. (3) The honest mechanism is: in a de-gross window, sell equity beta and buy whatever ts_trend holds (TLT/GLD in 2018Q4/Mar-2020, cash in 2022). The spec ADMITS the +0.15 edge "comes mainly via 2018Q4/2020" — that is 2 crisis episodes in 11 years, which is luck, not a Sharpe, and is a credit-conditioned long-bond/gold timing bet, i.e. timed beta, not orthogonal alpha. (4) Double-count: ts_trend (prior 0.61) is ALREADY an active allocator sleeve that is ALREADY tilted up in risk-off (defensiveTiltRiskOff 1.2 + defensive floor), so the overlay piles more trend on an already-trend-tilted book — the marginal orthogonal content is far smaller than assumed (the spec's own implementation note flags this double-count). (5) The "self-selecting" EWMA feedback loop (126d trailing corr, thresholds 0.3/0.5, multipliers 0.4/1.3) is in-sample parameter fitting on the same known crises, and is structurally LATE — the spec concedes it lags in 2022 and Aug-2024, exactly when bond/equity decorrelation flips matter most.
redteam.fix: Do not credit any Sharpe until spec #1's throttle and the BreadthCredit/creditZ/HYG infra are actually implemented and the walk-forward in the spec's own falsifier is run. Then, to test it through THIS lens: (a) add a real beta-regression to the engine and report alpha-t-stat and information ratio of the OVERLAY stream alone (redeployed-trend returns minus the cash-fallback baseline), not the whole book; (b) measure significance excluding 2018Q4 and Mar-2020 — if the net-of-beta edge collapses without those 2 windows it is episodic luck, mark Fatal; (c) net the overlay against ts_trend's EXISTING allocator share to kill the double-count, and report the incremental Sharpe over the already-risk-off-tilted allocator, not over a naive no-overlay book; (d) freeze the EWMA-loop parameters BEFORE the walk-forward and test out-of-sample, since tuning thresholds/multipliers on the same crises is the overfit. Realistically the defensible version is just letting the existing allocator's risk-off defensive tilt do the rotation, with cash as the de-gross sink (spec #1), which the allocator comments already concede works without double-de-grossing.

redteam.lens: Orthogonality and tail: is the redeployed stream uncorrelated to the long-only/short-vol put book in the LEFT TAIL (crisis onset), not just full-sample?
redteam.kill: The orthogonality thesis targets the wrong sleeve. TREND_ETFS = [SPY,QQQ,IWM,EEM,EFA,TLT,IEF,LQD,GLD,DBC,VNQ]: 5 of 11 redeploy targets are equity beta. The repo's own structurally-decorrelated sleeve is cross_asset_trend (CROSS_ASSET_ETFS, no US large-cap equity), which crossasset.ts explicitly names as the one sleeve decorrelated from the long-only/short-vol equity book. ts_trend holds an asset only if 12m return > 0 AND price > 200d SMA, re-checked every 21 days. At crisis onset (first weeks of Mar-2020) SPY/QQQ/EEM/EFA are still 12m-positive and above their 200d SMA, so freed capital rotates back into equity beta exactly when the put book bleeds: added short-gamma in the left tail, not an offset. The de-gross trigger and the redeploy are phase-aligned, so conditional left-tail correlation is far above the stated full-sample 0.42. TLT/GLD only dominate after the filters flip, lagging the drawdown by weeks; the 21-day rebalance means a stale equity-heavy book during fast spikes (Aug-2024, which the spec calls roughly flat). In 2022 (TLT -30%, equities down) the coarse filter exits slowly and the redeploy adds to drawdown unless the trailing-126d governor has already cut to cash, but that governor reacts only after correlation rises, i.e. structurally late. The spec concedes the safe state collapses back to spec 1 (cash), so the only additive contribution is single-asset bond rallies in 2018Q4/Mar-2020, a thin regime-specific edge that must still clear the falsifier's +0.15 over both no-overlay and cash baselines.
redteam.fix: Redeploy into cross_asset_trend (CROSS_ASSET_ETFS), not ts_trend. The repo already ships the genuinely non-equity, inverse-vol-weighted sleeve documented as decorrelated from the short-vol book, which removes the SPY/QQQ/EEM/EFA crash-onset re-leveraging that is the core kill. Also hard-exclude any equity-beta symbol from the redeploy target, and gate the rotation on the fast VIX-term-structure backwardation signal tail_hedge already uses rather than only the slow 12m/200d filter, so capital flips to TLT/GLD at crisis onset instead of weeks late. Finally make the feedback governor forward-looking: trigger the cash fallback on a contemporaneous bond-equity co-movement or VIX-backwardation flag, not only trailing-126d realized correlation, so 2022-style dual-bear goes to cash on day one. With those changes the stream can offset rather than add to the put-book drawdown in Mar-2020 and stay flat in 2022. As specified it does not survive the left-tail orthogonality lens.

redteam.lens: REGIME & DATA FEASIBILITY (regime dependence, crowding/decay, blow-up risk, free+daily data + TS-engine computability)
redteam.kill: DATA/COMPUTE FEASIBILITY (mixed, leans pass): The raw data is feasible. Only one new symbol is needed (HYG); it is NOT yet cached (confirmed: scripts/backtest/.cache has no HYG.json) but is a liquid 13y-history ETF fetchable free+daily through the exact Yahoo chart API the loader already uses (scripts/backtest/data.ts loadSymbol), and TLT/IEF/GLD/LQD plus all 11 TREND_ETFs are already cached. ts_trend is a real registered Strategy (src/lib/strategies/trend.ts, registry.ts line 11/27), generate(ctx) returns a weight map, and the meta.ts Map<symbol,{weight,reasons}> merge at lines 102-112 is exactly the accumulation the spec wants. Indicators (totalReturn, sma, zscore, correlation) exist. So the OVERLAY mechanics are computable. BUT the signal layer is NOT: this spec is explicitly 'Build on spec #1's throttle' and reuses 'identical composite S, breadth20, leadZ, creditZ, hysteresis, bookScale.' None of that exists in the repo — grep for bookScale/breadth20/creditZ/leadZ/BreadthCredit returns zero hits, and the allocator exposes grossDeployed but no bookScale. The spec's core formula dGross=(1-bookScale)*decision.grossDeployed references a variable that has not been built. So 'compute the signal in our TS engine' = NO until an unbuilt predecessor is written; this spec is not independently buildable or testable.\n\nREGIME & EDGE (the kill): Even granting the data, the net-of-beta edge does not survive skepticism. (1) It is a pure regime-timing bet that is short two regimes at once. The overlay only fires when BreadthCredit de-grosses, and it pays ONLY IF the freed capital's trend target (TLT/GLD) rallies in that same window. That is the bond-equity correlation regime — which flipped positive in 2022. The spec ADMITS 2022 is 'a risk' and 'the WEAKEST' and that survival there depends entirely on the feedback loop having ALREADY cut redeployFrac to cash. But the loop is a 126d-trailing EWMA: it adapts AFTER the regime turns, so in the fast 2022 transition it sends freed capital into TLT (-30%) precisely while detecting that TLT is no longer decorrelated — the falsifier's own '2022 segment adds to drawdown vs cash fallback' refutation condition is the likely outcome, not a tail. (2) Crisis-alpha capture is timing-fragile: the spec concedes Aug-2024 is 'roughly flat' because the 21d horizon and re-gross lag miss fast spikes, and Mar-2020-style wins require the de-gross to fire BEFORE bonds rally — by construction the throttle reacts to breadth/credit deterioration that often coincides with or lags the equity drop, so the rotation buys trend after the crisis-alpha move. (3) The honest expectedAfterCostSharpe of 0.35 with corrToEquity 0.42 is essentially the ts_trend sleeve's own prior (PRIORS.ts_trend=0.61 standalone, allocator already starves it as a near-null), repackaged as an overlay — the allocator ALREADY can hold ts_trend, so the marginal net-of-beta lift over just letting the meta-allocator's existing risk-off defensive tilt carry trend is thin and double-counts (the spec's own implementationNotes flag the double-count guard against ts_trend being an active sleeve). (4) The falsifier demands +0.15 over BOTH no-overlay AND spec-#1-cash across walk-forward; the wins are concentrated in exactly two events (2018Q4, Mar-2020) — a 2-event edge on 11y data is not statistically distinguishable from luck and is the textbook overfit-to-named-crises pattern. Blow-up risk is low (leverage-free, gross<=1, no shorts/options — confirmed by positionSizing), so this won't detonate; it just won't clear the bar.
redteam.fix: If pursued anyway: (a) FIRST build and independently validate spec #1's BreadthCredit throttle as a standalone module (bookScale, creditZ, breadth20, hysteresis) — this spec cannot be coded or backtested until that exists; treat the dependency as a hard blocker. (b) Add HYG to TREND_ETFS-adjacent universe + ALL_BACKTEST_SYMBOLS and run loadSymbol('HYG') to populate the cache (trivial, ~1 line + a fetch). (c) Replace the 126d-EWMA feedback loop with a CONTEMPORANEOUS bond-equity correlation gate: compute trailing 21-42d corr(TLT, SPY) at decision time and force redeployFrac->0 (cash) whenever it is positive, so the 2022 regime is detected synchronously rather than 126d late — this directly attacks the documented failure mode. (d) Pre-register the falsifier as a hard gate and run the 3-config walk-forward (no-overlay / cash / trend) with a leave-one-crisis-out test: if dropping either 2018Q4 or Mar-2020 collapses the +0.15 edge, reject as event-overfit. (e) Cap the redeploy so the overlay can never make book corr-to-equity rise (net the overlay against ts_trend's existing allocator share to kill the double-count). Without (a) and a synchronous bond-correlation gate, do not build.

scored.name: BreadthCredit Book Throttle (asymmetric, credit-led, hysteresis-gated de-gross)

scores.name: BreadthCredit Book Throttle (asymmetric, credit-led, hysteresis-gated de-gross)
scores.requiredFixes: Fatal orthogonality finding => CUT per rubric; the fixes below would only move it to RESEARCH_ONLY, not BUILD. (1) The headline thesis is false for this book: verified registry.ts has 8 long-only, leverage-free sleeves and ZERO short-vol/put-selling, so there is no short-gamma denominator to protect — the spec's core economic premise does not apply. (2) Redundancy/double-count: verified allocator.ts:92 riskOffGross=0.95 ('don't double-de-gross... empirically confirmed'), crisisGross=0.6 (line 93), 10% vol-target in meta.ts, and tailhedge.ts already runs TLT+GLD on VIX backwardation/SPY<200/VIX>=35. The overlay's VIX>=35 hard-trigger and flight-to-safety duplicate machinery already present and a slow-bear de-gross already rejected empirically. To survive it must bind ONLY on the incremental 43% of risk-off days (breadth/credit deteriorate BEFORE SPY<200d or VIX>=35) and prove those incremental fires PRECEDE losses (red-team measured they precede +0.46% rallies = whipsaw). (3) Signal is the book's own beta (S corr +0.59 to trailing SPY, -0.08 to forward SPY) — must show net-of-beta residual return positive specifically in the worst-5% SPY weeks after regressing on SPY AND existing volScale/crisisGross. (4) Data: HYG confirmed absent from .cache/, so the credit-leads leg cannot be built as specified; add HYG to ALL_BACKTEST_SYMBOLS + loader, and use HY-vs-IG (HYG/LQD) not the IG-only LQD/IEF proxy that inverts in 2022. (5) Feedback loop infeasible as specified: PerfProvider returns standalone curves, not a bookScale=1 counterfactual; the self-governing throttle-credit EWMA needs a shadow-book accumulator on the handle. (6) Walk-forward the hand-tuned constants (floor 0.55, band 0.12, weights 0.5/0.25/0.25) OOS (fit 2015-2020, test 2021-2026). Even with all fixes, evaluate ONLY on the falsifier's MaxDD>=15% leg — net-of-beta Sharpe is ~0 and must not be credited.
scores.oneLineRationale: A 4th, beta-correlated de-gross layer whose 'protect the short-vol denominator' thesis is fiction for this long-only leverage-free book, redundant with allocator's already-rejected riskOffGross/crisisGross and the tail_hedge sleeve, with HYG missing from cache and a live feedback loop infeasible as specified — Fatal orthogonality => CUT.

spec.name: BreadthCredit Book Throttle (asymmetric, credit-led, hysteresis-gated de-gross)
spec.mechanism: Structural-flow + behavioral. Aggregate participation (breadth) and credit risk appetite deteriorate AHEAD of index price in deleveraging/credit-stress episodes (dealers widen HY spreads and small/mid caps roll over before the cap-weighted index does). De-grossing the WHOLE book when participation thins and credit risk-off fires harvests the well-documented left-tail of long-only/short-vol books at a moment when realized vol is about to rise. The economic premium captured is NOT alpha on the upside — it is avoidance of the convex losses that destroy the put/short-vol book's Sharpe denominator. Honest framing: this is a denominator (vol/drawdown) tool, not a numerator (return) tool.
spec.exactSignal: Three computable sub-signals, all from data already in the manifest. (A) BREADTH: pct of EQUITY_UNIVERSE (the ~74 large caps) with close > own 200d SMA, then take the 20d SMA of that pct to de-whipsaw (per ChartSchool). breadth20 in [0,1]. (B) LEADERSHIP/RISK-APPETITE: 60d return spread QQQ minus SPY, plus IWM minus SPY 60d (small-cap participation); average the two, z-scored over 252d -> leadZ. (C) CREDIT: ratio HYG/LQD and HYG/IEF; take 20d-vs-100d change of log(HYG/LQD) and log(HYG/IEF), z-scored over 252d, averaged -> creditZ (negative = credit risk-off). Composite risk-on score S = 0.5*(2*breadth20-1) + 0.25*tanh(leadZ) + 0.25*tanh(creditZ), so S in roughly [-1,1]. Map to a throttle via a HYSTERESIS BAND: maintain state on/throttled. throttle target = clamp(0.5 + 0.5*S, floor=0.55, cap=1.0). To kill whipsaw, only MOVE the applied scale when |target - applied| > 0.12 OR regime flips to CRISIS (VIX>=35 -> hard scale to floor immediately). Asymmetry: de-gross fast (apply a worse-than-target scale the day S crosses below 0), re-gross slow (require breadth20>0.55 AND creditZ>0 for 5 consecutive days before stepping scale back up by at most +0.10/week).
spec.entryRule: This is an OVERLAY, not a sleeve that opens single positions. It multiplies the meta book's total gross. 'Entry' = the day the composite S first turns risk-off (S<0 or breadth20<0.45 or creditZ<-1 or VIX>=35): set bookScale to its throttled value at the next EOD rebalance. The per-sleeve signals are untouched; only their summed gross is scaled by bookScale.
spec.exitRule: 'Exit' (return to full gross, bookScale->1.0) only after the slow re-gross path completes: breadth20>0.55 AND creditZ>0 for 5 consecutive trading days, stepping +0.10/week until 1.0. Immediate exit to floor (0.55) on VIX>=35 close. No discretionary override.
spec.instruments: No new instruments traded. Reads SPY/QQQ/IWM + the 74-name EQUITY_UNIVERSE closes + HYG, LQD, IEF, ^VIX. Acts by scaling the EXISTING meta book gross (the volScale slot in meta.ts).
spec.positionSizing: Pure multiplier on book gross: appliedGross = decision.grossDeployed * volScale * bookScale, bookScale in [0.55,1.0]. Multiplicative with the existing 10% meta vol-target (they stack: vol-target handles realized-vol spikes, this handles participation/credit deterioration BEFORE vol spikes). Net gross stays <=1.0 (already leverage-free); hard cap gross<=1.5 never approached. No single-name risk added.
spec.newDataNeeded: Add 3 free ETFs to the fetch list: HYG (HY corp), LQD (already in TREND-ish manifest), IEF (already in manifest). Only HYG is genuinely new; SPY/QQQ/IWM/^VIX and all 74 equities already loaded. So: add HYG only.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: TRACKED METRIC: rolling 63d realized Sharpe of the LIVE meta book AND, critically, the rolling 63d correlation of bookScale-changes to next-20d book drawdown (does throttling actually precede losses?). RULE: maintain a 'throttle credit' EWMA = avg of [ (book return when bookScale<1) minus (counterfactual book return at bookScale=1) ] over the trailing 126d. If the throttle has SAVED money (credit>0), keep the band as-is. If the throttle has COST money for 126d (credit<0, i.e. it de-grossed into rallies = pure whipsaw), WIDEN the hysteresis band (0.12->0.18) and lower the floor relaxation, reducing how often it fires; if credit strongly negative for 252d, shrink the overlay's authority (let bookScale floor rise toward 0.75, i.e. throttle less). This is the self-tracking loop: the overlay's own aggressiveness is governed by whether its past de-gross decisions added or subtracted realized return.
spec.crisisBehavior: 2018Q4: breadth + credit both deteriorated through Oct-Nov, fires correctly, de-gross helps (positive vs holding). Mar-2020: VIX hard-trigger + credit collapse fire, strong help (this is the canonical save). 2022: SLOW grind-down with rates-driven (not credit-driven) selloff — credit lagged, breadth caught it; partial help but several re-gross whipsaws (the weakest regime for this overlay, exactly per the SSRN credit-fails-in-rate-shocks finding). Aug-2024 (yen-carry 3-day vol spike): VIX hard-trigger fires AFTER the worst day — too slow to help the 1-day gap, mild whipsaw on the snap-back. Net across crises: flat-to-positive on drawdown, NOT a consistent positive-return generator.
spec.implementationNotes: Implement as a wrapper around createMetaStrategy, OR cleaner: extend MetaStrategyHandle to accept an optional bookThrottle(ctx, perfProvider) => number computed right beside volScale (meta.ts lines 90-99), then set the combined share = decision.weights.get(key) * volScale * bookScale. Breadth pct: loop EQUITY_UNIVERSE, use sma(closes,200) from indicators.ts, count above, divide; smooth with sma(pctSeries,20). leadZ/creditZ: reuse totalReturn() for 60d spreads and zscore() over 252d; tanh inline. Hysteresis + asymmetry + the 5-day re-gross counter need a tiny persistent state object on the handle (allowed — handle already carries lastVolScale). The throttle-credit feedback needs the engine to optionally pass the realized book return series, which the PerfProvider already exposes for the meta key. Add HYG to ALL_BACKTEST_SYMBOLS and to the data loader.
spec.falsifier: Run the meta book WITH vs WITHOUT this overlay on the 11y walk-forward, conservative costs. REFUTED if net-of-beta Sharpe (regress book returns on SPY, take alpha/residual-vol) does NOT rise by at least +0.10 over the no-overlay meta, OR if MaxDD does not improve by >=15%. Given the literature, base case is that DRAWDOWN improves (~33%->~20%) but net-of-beta Sharpe barely moves (this is the honest expectation: ~0.15 standalone net-of-beta). If even MaxDD fails to improve, the breadth/credit composite has no timing value and the overlay is dead — ship it only as a Calmar tool or not at all.

redteam.lens: COSTS & CAPACITY: realistic retail frictions — options spread, short borrow/locate, turnover, min-granularity vs fixed capital, capacity
redteam.kill: On pure cost mechanics this overlay is nearly unkillable — which is exactly why the cost lens exposes the real problem. Verified against costs.ts and universe.ts: it trades NO new instruments (no option bid/ask, no put spread, no assignment, no short borrow/locate — it only ever de-grosses an already long-only, leverage-free book, so shortBorrowAnnualBps never applies). Its sole friction is turnover on the underlying when bookScale moves, charged at the engine's conservative ~10 bps round-trip equity/ETF cost. I quantified the firing pattern (clean ~3 cyc/yr d=0.30, whipsaw ~8 cyc/yr d=0.20, VIX-snap ~1.5 cyc/yr d=0.40): total turnover drag is only ~30 bps/yr on book NAV. The +0.10/week asymmetric re-gross spreads notional but adds none; the engine's 0.2%-of-equity dust filter suppresses micro-whipsaw retrades. Capacity is a non-issue: SPY/QQQ/IWM/HYG/LQD/IEF + 74 megacaps are the most liquid names on earth and a ≤45% gross throttle has negligible impact at any retail/mid size. So 'gross edge > round-trip spread cost?' is trivially YES on the spread axis. BUT the lens's deeper question — is gross RETURN edge > total cost — fails. The certain recurring cost is ~30 bps turnover PLUS ~20 bps/yr foregone drift from de-grossing a positively-drifting long book (~50 bps/yr certain), while the offsetting benefit is a lumpy crisis-drawdown option that pays off only ~1-2x/decade. The spec ITSELF concedes this is 'NOT a numerator tool,' 'flat-to-positive on drawdown, NOT a consistent positive-return generator,' standalone net-of-beta Sharpe ~0.15, and its own falsifier base case is that net-of-beta Sharpe 'barely moves.' In 8 of 11 years (2022 rate-driven grind, Aug-2024 yen-carry firing AFTER the worst day, every calm year) the de-gross harvests whipsaw and foregone drift that is never recovered. So a small-but-CERTAIN annual cost is being levied against a near-zero, lumpy, non-stationary return edge. Per the skepticism default, I cannot affirm a positive net-of-beta return Sharpe survives; the edge is a drawdown option, not a Sharpe contributor.
redteam.fix: Re-scope and ship it ONLY as a Calmar/MaxDD tool, not a Sharpe contributor — exactly the fallback the spec's own falsifier names ('ship it only as a Calmar tool or not at all'). Concretely: (1) Gate firing to credit+breadth+VIX TRIPLE-confirmation so it only acts in genuine deleveraging episodes (kills the 2022/Aug-2024 whipsaw cost where credit lagged); raising the firing bar trades away ~half the certain turnover and most of the foregone-drift cost. (2) Raise the floor toward 0.70-0.75 so each de-gross trades less notional, cutting per-cycle cost ~40%. (3) Keep the throttle-credit EWMA feedback loop (already specified) as the live circuit-breaker — but make 'shrink authority' trigger faster (126d not 252d) so persistent whipsaw cost auto-disables it. (4) Evaluate ONLY on the falsifier's MaxDD>=15%-improvement leg; explicitly do not credit it with Sharpe in the meta. The turnover cost itself needs no mitigation — at ~30 bps/yr it is immaterial; the fix is firing-frequency discipline so that small cost buys real drawdown reduction instead of being spent on whipsaw.

redteam.kill: As a Sharpe contributor net of beta and after costs, this overlay dies on three grounds verified against the actual codebase. (1) It is a beta-reduction operation in disguise. The book it overlays (Adaptive Meta + 10% Vol Target, meta-summary.json) is already Sharpe 0.85, MaxDD 12.15%, vol 8.09%, corr(SPY) 0.55 — NOT the 33% MaxDD long-only book the spec benchmarks against. De-grossing a corr-0.55 book scales vol and return down together, leaving residual/net-of-beta Sharpe ~unchanged. The spec concedes this ('denominator tool, not numerator', standalone net-of-beta ~0.15, Sharpe 'barely moves'). 'Barely moves' net-of-beta = does not survive this lens. (2) The mechanism is already implemented and was empirically REJECTED in this repo. allocator.ts line 92 sets riskOffGross: 0.95 with the comment 'slow bear: don't double-de-gross; sleeves self-de-risk... empirically confirmed' — book-level de-grossing in slow bears was tried and found to drag return. The VIX>=35 hard-trigger duplicates crisisGross: 0.6; flight-to-safety duplicates the tail_hedge sleeve (TLT+GLD on backwardation/SPY<200/VIX>=35); the Moreira-Muir denominator lever is already harvested by the 10% vol-target (it moved MaxDD only 12.99%->12.15%). Stacking a fourth de-gross layer double-counts. (3) The signal is weak, low-frequency, and regime-conditional: breadth/credit-leads-price fails in rate-driven selloffs (2022, the dominant modern drawdown type, per the spec's own crisisBehavior) and lags the 1-day Aug-2024 gap, producing whipsaw losses (negative numerator) in exactly the regimes that matter now. The hand-tuned constants (floor 0.55, band 0.12, weights 0.5/0.25/0.25, +0.10/wk) and the throttle-credit governor are fit/evaluated on the same ~3-4 enumerated crisis episodes, so any in-sample MaxDD gain is partly curve-fit and the adaptive loop has too few independent events to learn. No hard look-ahead (trailing windows applied next EOD), but the overfit surface plus redundancy with already-rejected machinery means the honest net-of-beta after-cost Sharpe lift is ~0.00, indistinguishable from zero.
redteam.fix: Reframe and ship (if at all) ONLY as a Calmar/MaxDD tool, never as a Sharpe play — the spec's own falsifier already says this. To make it non-redundant and non-double-counting: (a) Run the falsifier WITH vs WITHOUT on the actual Adaptive Meta + 10% Vol Target book (Sharpe 0.85 / MaxDD 12.15%), not against SPY, and require net-of-beta alpha (regress book returns on SPY, take residual) to rise >=0.10 with MaxDD improving >=15% off the 12.15% base — predict both fail. (b) Make it strictly ADDITIVE to existing risk-off logic: only let bookScale bind when the allocator's own riskOffGross/crisisGross is NOT already firing (i.e. credit/breadth deteriorate BEFORE SPY<200d or VIX>=35), otherwise it just re-de-grosses what allocator.ts already cut. (c) Walk-forward the hysteresis constants out-of-sample (fit on 2015-2020, test 2021-2026) instead of tuning on all enumerated episodes; if MaxDD improvement does not replicate OOS, it is curve-fit. (d) Hard-gate the 2022 failure mode: require creditZ to lead, and accept that in rate-driven selloffs it will whipsaw — size the overlay's authority down accordingly. If after (a)-(d) MaxDD does not improve >=15% off the real 12.15% base, do not ship.

redteam.lens: ORTHOGONALITY & TAIL: is the de-gross return stream truly uncorrelated to the book in the LEFT TAIL, or does it share the book's short-gamma / equity beta? Tested empirically against Mar2020 / 2022 / Aug2024 on the actual cached 2015-2026 data.
redteam.kill: The orthogonality claim fails on three independent empirical grounds, all measured on the repo's own cached data (SPY 2015-06 to 2026-06, full crisis coverage). (1) THE SIGNAL IS THE BOOK'S OWN BETA. The composite risk-on score S correlates +0.59 with trailing 60d SPY return and -0.08 with forward 20d SPY return. Breadth (% > 200d SMA), QQQ/IWM/SPY leadership, and credit appetite are all long-equity-risk factors that collapse TOGETHER in a crisis by construction. S co-moves with realized losses; it does not lead them. This is shared short-gamma re-expressed, not an orthogonal stream. (2) 57% REDUNDANT WITH THE BOOK'S EXISTING DE-GROSS. On the 522 days S<0 (overlay wants to de-gross), 299 (57%) are already covered by the meta book's own VIX>=35 -> crisisGross=0.6 and SPY<200d defensive tilt (allocator.ts) plus tailHedge on VIX backwardation. The overlay double-counts the same triggers the book already pulls. The 43% incremental fires have fwd20 SPY of +0.46% (POSITIVE) -- they are de-gross-into-rally whipsaws, the spec's own admitted failure mode. (3) IT LAGS OR WHIFFS IN ALL THREE NAMED CRISES. Mar-2020: at the 2020-02-21 pre-crash top S=+0.35 / throttle=0.68 (near-full gross) three weeks before a -31% drop; S only flips risk-off on 2020-03-16 AFTER VIX hit 82 and SPY already broke 200d (book already de-grossed). 2022: book already in SPY<200d risk-off the whole grind; spec concedes several re-gross whipsaws. Aug-2024: S=+0.03 (NOT risk-off) on the worst day; only the VIX>=35 hard-trigger fired, which the book ALREADY has. SECONDARY FATAL: the entire 'protect the put/short-vol book Sharpe denominator' thesis is fiction here -- the actual meta book (registry.ts) is long-only, leverage-free, ZERO short-vol/put-selling sleeves, vol-targeted 10%, rho(SPY)~0.55. There is no short-gamma to hedge; the overlay just adds a second, redundant, beta-correlated equity-de-risk layer on top of the one the allocator already runs. The one genuinely new input, HYG, is even absent from the data cache, so the 'credit-leads' leg cannot be built as specified.
redteam.fix: To make this survive the orthogonality/tail lens it would have to be rebuilt as a structurally decorrelated stream, not a second beta layer: (a) measure the de-gross stream's NET-OF-BETA residual return after regressing on SPY AND on the existing volScale/crisisGross series, and require the residual to be positive specifically in the conditional left tail (worst-5% SPY weeks), not full-sample; (b) gate the overlay to fire ONLY on the 43% of days incremental to VIX>=35/SPY<200d and prove those incremental fires precede losses (currently they precede +0.46% rallies -> kill that leg); (c) make credit genuinely lead by sourcing actual HYG (missing from cache) and using HY-vs-IG (HYG/LQD) widening, not the IG-only LQD/IEF proxy that inverts uselessly in the 2022 rate shock; (d) honestly re-anchor the thesis to the real long-only book -- the cross_asset_trend + tail_hedge sleeves already provide the orthogonal positive-skew crisis-alpha (long TLT/GLD), so this overlay must show it ADDS drawdown reduction beyond those, on a net-of-beta basis. Absent (a)-(d) it should ship, per its own falsifier, only as a Calmar/MaxDD cosmetic at most -- and even MaxDD improvement is unproven here because it lags VIX/200d in 2 of 3 crises.

redteam.kill:
DATA/COMPUTE: Feasible, and I verified it against the actual code rather than trusting the spec. (1) The signal inputs are all reachable: StrategyContext.closes(symbol) in engine.ts reads from data.closes (the FULL aligned symbol map), not just ctx.universe, so the overlay can read HYG/LQD/IEF, SPY/QQQ/IWM and all 74 EQUITY_UNIVERSE names inside generate(); VIX is on ctx.regime.vix and the CRISIS bucket (VIX>=35) already exists in classifyVix(). (2) Compute primitives exist in indicators.ts: sma(), zscore(window), totalReturn(), stdev — tanh is stdlib.
