The breadth = pct-above-200d-SMA, the 60d QQQ-SPY/IWM-SPY spreads, and the log(HYG/LQD)/log(HYG/IEF) 20d-vs-100d z-scores are all trivially computable. (3) Manifest claim is accurate: LQD and IEF are ALREADY in TREND_ETFS and CROSS_ASSET_ETFS (thus in ALL_BACKTEST_SYMBOLS); only HYG is new, and HYG is a top-liquidity ETF with ~17y of free daily history on the same Yahoo chart API the loader (scripts/backtest/data.ts) already uses. Adding HYG to ALL_BACKTEST_SYMBOLS + loader is a one-line change. So data is FREE + DAILY + computable — no kill on feasibility grounds.

Where it nearly dies (Serious, not Fatal): the dailyFeedbackLoop is partially infeasible AS SPECIFIED. The PerfProvider seam is (key, asOfDate)=>{sharpe,vol,n,returns?} and returns each sleeve's STANDALONE curve. The throttle-credit EWMA wants '(book return at bookScale<1) minus (counterfactual return at bookScale=1)'. The counterfactual does not exist in a single walk-forward pass: bookScale is computed INSIDE generate() and feeds the same book, so the engine emits exactly ONE path. Getting the bookScale=1 counterfactual requires running a shadow book (with vs without overlay) — which the offline falsifier does, but the LIVE self-governing loop cannot pull from the current PerfProvider. The 'overlay governs its own aggressiveness' adaptivity is therefore not implementable without new shadow-book bookkeeping (a second meta run each day, or storing realized-vs-shadow deltas). That is engineering the spec understates ('the PerfProvider already exposes the realized book return series' is only half-true — it exposes the meta sleeve's realized return, NOT a clean bookScale=1 counterfactual).

REGIME/EDGE skepticism: the spec is honest that this is a denominator (drawdown) tool, not alpha — expectedAfterCostSharpe 0.15, corr-to-equity 0.55. That honesty is correct and is itself the strongest argument it's near-zero net-of-beta. The three known failure regimes are real and the spec admits them: (a) 2022 rate-driven grind — credit lagged, breadth whipsawed (the documented 'credit fails in rate shocks' problem); (b) Aug-2024 yen-carry — a 1-day gap where the VIX>=35 hard trigger fires AFTER the worst day, so it de-grosses into the snap-back (pure whipsaw, negative credit); (c) crowding/decay — breadth-thrust and HY-spread risk-off are among the MOST published, most-front-run signals in the industry, so any timing edge is heavily decayed and the de-gross is increasingly likely to coincide with the local bottom. The asymmetry (de-gross fast, re-gross slow at +0.10/week after 5 consecutive days) systematically GUARANTEES participation lag on V-shaped recoveries (2018Q4 Dec->Jan, Mar-2020, Aug-2024), which is precisely where a 0.55-floored book bleeds vs buy-and-hold. So the realistic net-of-beta Sharpe contribution is ~0.0 to +0.05, with the entire value living in MaxDD/Calmar — exactly as the falsifier's base case concedes (DD 33%->20%, Sharpe 'barely moves'). No blow-up risk added (multiplier in [0.55,1.0], gross<=1.0, leverage-free, no new short vol, no single-name), so it cannot HURT tail risk — its downside is opportunity-cost drag, not ruin.
redteam.fix: (1) Drop the self-governing throttle-credit loop from v1 (it's the infeasible-as-spec part) and ship the overlay as a FIXED-PARAMETER de-gross with the offline falsifier deciding band/floor — run book WITH vs WITHOUT via two runBacktest passes (the harness already supports this), and only enable the live overlay if net-of-beta Sharpe rises >=+0.10 AND MaxDD improves >=15%. (2) Reframe and gate on Calmar/MaxDD, NOT Sharpe — the spec's own falsifier says ship it 'as a Calmar tool or not at all', which is the correct bar. (3) De-emphasize the VIX>=35 hard-trigger for gap regimes (it provably fires late in Aug-2024-type 1-day spikes and causes the snap-back whipsaw) — keep it only as a floor backstop, let breadth/credit drive. (4) Soften the re-gross asymmetry (the +0.10/week, 5-day-confirm path) which mechanically causes recovery lag; test a faster re-gross to cut the participation cost that kills net-of-beta Sharpe. (5) If/when the self-tracking loop is wanted, implement it honestly by persisting a shadow bookScale=1 equity alongside the live book (one extra cheap accumulator on the handle), not by pretending the existing PerfProvider already yields the counterfactual.

scored.name: OU-Z Sector Pair Reversion: XLY/XLP cyclical-vs-defensive (beta-neutral, crisis force-close)

scores.name: OU-Z Sector Pair Reversion: XLY/XLP cyclical-vs-defensive (beta-neutral, crisis force-close)
scores.requiredFixes: Two independent Fatal findings => CUT (rubric: any Fatal kills). The core mechanism is empirically false and unfixable as specified: (1) Verified on the repo's own 11y cache — full-sample AR(1) on ln(XLY/XLP) gives rho=0.9975, half-life=280 DAYS, not the claimed 3-25d window; there is no fast reversion to harvest on a 10d horizon, only a slow near-random-walk riding a +0.432 secular cyclical-over-defensive drift. (2) Not beta-neutral: the spec's b=beta(XLY,XLP)=0.83 hedge leaves residual SPY beta +0.63 (betas XLY=1.09, XLP=0.56; true SPY-neutral ratio=1.95, >2x the spec). The z<=-2 'long XLY/short XLP' leg fires into selloffs, making it a long-the-market bet mislabeled market-neutral — it violates the spec's own |corr to SPY|<0.25 and corrToLongOnlyEquity:0 falsifiers. (3) Latent short-crash/short-defensive bias the VIX>=35 force-close does not remove — it fails on the slow-grind VIX 18-33 selloffs (2022 bear bottom, Apr2024) where defensives beat cyclicals but VIX never tags 35. (4) Vaporware dependencies: the IWF/IWD 'sleeve 1' it claims to clone does NOT exist in registry.ts, and the family-gross cap + cross-sleeve correlation down-weight do NOT exist in allocator.ts/meta.ts (only the data plumbing — universe entries + cache — exists). To even research: build/prove IWF/IWD first, re-hedge to SPY-beta-neutral (~1.95x), rewrite the thesis as slow local-residual reversion around a trending mean (not OU fast-reversion), add a directional veto blocking the short-defensive side when SPY<50d SMA, and pass the 5-worst-days coincidence test out-of-sample before any allocation. expectedAfterCostSharpe:0.45 is fabricated.
scores.oneLineRationale: CUT: two Fatal verdicts confirmed on the repo's own data — 280-day half-life (no fast reversion), +0.63 residual SPY beta (long-the-market in disguise), and the claimed sibling sleeve + allocator risk-controls are unbuilt vaporware.

spec.name: OU-Z Sector Pair Reversion: XLY/XLP cyclical-vs-defensive (beta-neutral, crisis force-close)
spec.mechanism: XLY (consumer discretionary) and XLP (consumer staples) are both S&P 500 GICS sector slices of the same parent — the XLY/XLP log-ratio is the classic 'risk-on/risk-off' cyclical-vs-defensive barometer with no structural drift. Short-horizon dislocations in this ratio (sentiment overshoots, single-name shocks in mega-cap holdings like AMZN/TSLA in XLY) revert as the rotation normalizes. Mechanism is flow/sentiment reversion, market-neutral after beta-hedging the two legs (XLY beta>1, XLP beta<1, so the hedge ratio meaningfully de-betas). Complements the IWF/IWD style pair: same machinery, a sector axis instead of a style axis, modestly correlated to the first sleeve so they don't double-count.
spec.exactSignal: Spread s_t = ln(close_XLY) - ln(close_XLP). Trailing 60d AR(1): rho=OLS slope of s_t on s_{t-1}; mu=intercept/(1-rho); sigma_e=stdev(residuals); HL=ln(2)/(-ln(rho)); z=(s_t-mu)/(sigma_e/sqrt(1-rho^2)). Beta hedge ratio b=indicators.beta(60d logret_XLY, 60d logret_XLP). Regime inputs: ctx.regime.regime, ctx.regime.vix, 5-day VIX change, ctx.regime.vixTermRatio. NOTE: XLY/XLP itself is a risk sentiment proxy — also compute its 20d trend slope to avoid fighting a strong persistent rotation.
spec.entryRule: Enter only if: 3 < HL < 25 days AND rho in (0,1) AND |z| >= 2.0 AND regime in {LOW_VOL,NORMAL} AND 5d VIX change < +5 pts AND vixTermRatio <= 1.05 AND |20d slope of s_t| < 1.5*sigma_e (not in a strong trending rotation). z <= -2 (discretionary cheap vs staples): LONG XLY / SHORT XLP. z >= +2: SHORT XLY / LONG XLP. Short leg scaled by hedge ratio b for net-beta ~0.
spec.exitRule: Flatten when |z| <= 0.5 OR z crosses 0 OR HL exits (2,30) OR age > 2*HL+10d. CRISIS FORCE-CLOSE (the tail-coherence cure): XLY/XLP is itself a crash barometer, so if regime==CRISIS (VIX>=35) OR vixTermRatio>=1.15 OR 1-day |spread move|>4*sigma_e, flatten BOTH legs to cash immediately — in a crash defensives (XLP) outperform cyclicals (XLY) hard and a 'reverting' short-XLP/long-XLY bet would compound the equity book's loss; we refuse that trade. Hard P&L stop at -2.5*sigma_e on the pair.
spec.instruments: ETF pair, both in SECTOR_ETFS: long/short XLY and XLP (signed weights). Highly liquid, ~1bp spreads, cheap borrow.
spec.positionSizing: Same vol-target framework as the IWF/IWD sleeve: base gross 0.30 split long/short (short leg * b), scaled to an 8% annualized spread-vol target and by a confidence sizer clamp((|z|-2)/2,0,1)*(1-HL/30). Sleeve gross cap 0.6. IMPORTANT cross-sleeve cap: run BOTH pair sleeves under a combined family gross cap of 0.8 so the two correlated reversion bets can't jointly dominate; engine maxGross=1.5 caps the whole book.
spec.newDataNeeded: none — XLY and XLP are in SECTOR_ETFS and STYLE_PAIRS[1]; VIX series in MACRO_TICKERS; all in manifest.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: METRIC: this sleeve's own rolling 60d realized Sharpe on the live sim pair-equity curve AND a daily 'force-close-frequency' counter (how often the crisis guard fired in the last 60d). RULE: adaptive allocator weights the sleeve by its shrunk 60d Sharpe (prior 0.3). ADDITIONALLY track combined-family correlation: each day compute 60d return correlation between the XLY/XLP sleeve and the IWF/IWD sleeve; if it exceeds 0.6 (the two pairs have collapsed into one bet), down-weight the LOWER-Sharpe of the two by half until it falls back under 0.4. If force-close fired >20% of days in 60d (chronic crisis regime), set sleeve allocation to near-0 (the reversion edge is unavailable in that regime) and re-grow +0.1/day once force-closes stop and 20d paper-Sharpe>0.
spec.crisisBehavior: Crisis force-close makes it flat-to-cash in 2018Q4 / Mar2020 / Aug2024 spikes — critically important here because an UN-gated XLY/XLP reversion is a hidden short-vol/short-crash bet (betting cyclicals recover vs defensives). The gate converts that latent left tail into a no-trade. In an orderly 2022-style de-rating it mostly sits out (HL/slope gate) or harvests small reversions; near-flat. Residual risk identical in spirit to sleeve 1: a slow no-VIX-spike rotation can cost a moderate, bounded loss before the regime-break/slope exit. Net: flat-to-slightly-positive in crises, by design not also-down.
spec.implementationNotes: Same Strategy template as sleeve 1, second instance over STYLE_PAIRS[1]=['XLY','XLP'] (or one parameterized pairReversion(strategyKey, pair) factory producing both Strategy objects). instrument:'etf', longOnly:false, rebalanceDays:1, warmupBars:80. Add the 20d-slope guard (slope = OLS of s over last 20 obs). Persist per-pair entry state in a module Map. Register both in registry.ts; add the combined family-gross cap + cross-sleeve correlation down-weight in allocator.ts/meta.ts. Reuse indicators.beta/mean/stdev/logReturns; no new indicator code needed except the tiny AR(1) OLS (slope/intercept of s_t vs s_{t-1}).
spec.falsifier: REFUTED if 11y walk-forward (DEFAULT_COSTS, 50bps borrow) yields (a) after-cost Sharpe <= 0.15, OR (b) |corr to SPY| > 0.25, OR (c) the crisis force-close FAILS to remove tail-coherence — i.e. the sleeve's 5 worst daily returns still coincide with the long-only book's 5 worst days, OR (d) 60d correlation to the IWF/IWD sleeve averages > 0.7 over the sample (it's not a distinct bet, just a lever on the same factor). Any one refutes adding it as a separate sleeve.

redteam.kill: The cost lens does NOT kill this sleeve, and on the merits it can't: XLY/XLP are among the cheapest, most liquid instruments available (each ~$15-20B AUM, real penny-wide spreads ~0.5-1bp, general-collateral borrow ~25-30bps), yet DEFAULT_COSTS charges 3bps/side spread + 2bps/side slippage (=10bps/leg round trip) and 50bps/yr borrow — the model OVERSTATES costs on this specific pair. Round-trip cost per completed trade is ~40bps on pair notional (2 legs in, 2 legs out at 10bps each) + ~2bps borrow on the short leg over a ~10d hold = ~42bps. A clean OU reversion from |z|=2 to |z|<=0.5 on a pair with sigma_e ~0.5-0.7%/day captures ~1.0-1.5x the equilibrium spread std ≈ ~100-150bps gross on pair notional, so gross edge clears round-trip cost by roughly 2.5-3.5x on a clean trade. Capacity is a non-issue (these ETFs trade hundreds of $M/day; the sleeve at 0.6 gross is invisible) — the real granularity problem is the OPPOSITE: the confidence sizer clamp((|z|-2)/2,0,1)*(1-HL/30) can shrink gross to ~0.05-0.10, producing micro-positions barely worth trading at a retail base. The genuine cost leak the spec glosses over is rebalanceDays:1 daily vol-target + daily 60d-beta re-hedge churn: the 0.2%-equity dust filter (engine.ts:235) catches some of it, but noisy 60d beta b and daily z/sigma_e/HL drift will repeatedly cross 0.2% and re-trade both legs, plausibly adding ~20-40bps of churn per trade and pushing all-in round-trip toward ~60-80bps. The crisis force-close also guarantees a flat-2bps-slippage exit on exactly the gap days when real slippage is 10-50bps. None of these are fatal — they erode, not erase, the edge.
redteam.fix: Switch the hedge/rebalance from daily to event-driven: re-hedge beta and re-size only when |z|, HL, or b move past a band (e.g. re-trade only if target leg weight changes >0.5% of equity, not the engine's 0.2% dust floor), cutting daily-churn cost roughly in half. Freeze the beta hedge ratio b at entry rather than re-estimating a noisy 60d beta every bar (eliminates the largest churn source and reduces tracking error). Model a regime-dependent slippage multiplier (e.g. 5-10x) on crisis-force-close exits so the backtest doesn't flatter the tail. Set a minimum-size floor so confidence-scaled micro-positions below ~0.10 gross are skipped (not worth the fixed friction). With these, all-in round-trip stays near the ~42bps base case and the ~2.5x edge/cost cushion holds.

redteam.kill: Tested on the repo's own 11y cache (2015-2026, scripts/backtest/.cache/{XLY,XLP,SPY,^VIX,^VIX9D}.json). Three independent fatal results: (1) NOT beta-neutral. The spec hedges with b=beta(XLY,XLP)=0.83, which neutralizes the two ETFs' co-movement, NOT market beta. Measured betas-to-SPY: XLY=1.09, XLP=0.56, so the position [+1 XLY, -0.83 XLP] carries a residual SPY beta of +0.63 (the SPY-neutralizing ratio would be bX/bP=1.95, >2x the spec's hedge). Spread daily changes correlate +0.52 with SPY. The z<=-2 'long XLY/short XLP' leg fires precisely when cyclicals have underperformed (selloffs), so it is a +0.63-beta long-the-market bet mislabeled market-neutral — beta in disguise. This violates the spec's own falsifier |corr to SPY|>0.25 and corrToLongOnlyEquity:0. (2) NO reversion edge on horizon. Full-sample AR(1) on the log-ratio: rho=0.9975, half-life=280 DAYS, vs the claimed 3-25d window and 10d horizon. The rolling-60d HL gate passes 77% of days only by overfitting noise; the true process is a slow near-random-walk. Direct simulation of the EXACT signal (rolling 60d AR(1), |z|>=2 entry, |z|<=0.5 exit, LOW/NORMAL+VIX-term gates) with same-close fills and ZERO costs gives gross annualized Sharpe = 0.08. (3) Costs make it negative: 30 round trips x ~20bps = 6.0% cost vs 5.3% gross spread P&L = -0.7% net of leg notional, and that uses the engine's own admittedly-flattering same-close fill (engine.ts L14). Supporting: the IWF/IWD 'sleeve 1' it claims to clone does not exist in registry.ts, and the family-gross-cap + cross-sleeve correlation down-weight are absent from allocator.ts — the de-correlation/risk-control claims are unimplemented and untested. The crisis force-close is fit to in-sample crises (2018Q4/Mar2020/Aug2024 all inside the 2015-2026 cache). The repo's own results.md already proved mean-reversion sleeves get 'annihilated by costs' (RSI-2 -> 0.22); here there is no gross edge to annihilate (0.08). expectedAfterCostSharpe:0.45 is fabricated.
redteam.fix: Not salvageable as specified. To even attempt rescue: (a) replace the XLY-vs-XLP hedge ratio with a true SPY-beta-neutralizing ratio (short ~1.95x XLP per 1x XLY, or hedge each leg to SPY directly) and re-verify |corr to SPY|<0.25 out-of-sample; (b) abandon the OU/fast-reversion thesis — the log-ratio has a 280-day half-life, so there is no 3-25d reversion to harvest; any real edge here would be a slow risk-on/off carry/trend, a different (and beta-laden) bet; (c) build and backtest the claimed IWF/IWD 'sleeve 1' and the allocator family-cap/correlation logic before referencing them; (d) validate the crisis guard on truly out-of-sample crises (pre-2015 data the cache lacks). Even with all of that, the measured gross Sharpe of 0.08 leaves no margin for the conservative cost model — recommend do NOT deploy.

redteam.lens: Orthogonality & Tail: is the de-betaed XLY/XLP reversion stream truly uncorrelated to a long-only / short-vol put book in the LEFT TAIL (crises), or does it carry hidden beta / shared short-gamma and ADD to the put-book drawdown in Mar2020 / 2022 / Aug2024?
redteam.kill: The signal carries a structural short-crash / short-defensive bias that the crisis force-close does NOT remove. Replicating the spec's exact signal on cached 2015-2026 daily data (XLY/XLP/SPY/VIX in scripts/backtest/.cache): of 89 active entries, 38 (43%) are z<=-2 -> LONG XLY (cyclical) / SHORT XLP (defensive). These fire BECAUSE the XLY/XLP ratio collapses, which happens BECAUSE the market is selling off and defensives outperform cyclicals. The signal mechanically reads a risk-off rotation as 'discretionary is cheap, buy it' — going long high-beta and short the hedge right into drawdowns. Result: left-tail corr to SPY = +0.75 on SPY's worst-5% days (vs a benign +0.21 full-sample that PASSES the spec's own |corr|<0.25 falsifier), and the pair LOSES a mean -1.81%/day on those crash days (~-3.6 sigma vs an 8% vol target). The crisis force-close is the wrong tool: the VIX>=35 / vixTermRatio / 1d-spread gate blocks only 12 of the 38 short-defensive entries; 26 of 38 (68%) pass the gate, and 10 of those fired with SPY already down 2-6% over 5 days at VIX 18-33 (2022-10-11 at the bear bottom, 2024-04-18/19, 2025-02-24/25, 2023-10-25/26) — the slow-grind selloffs where defensives beat cyclicals but VIX never tags 35. With force-close ON, tail corr barely moves (0.75->0.71) and crash-day mean barely improves (-1.81%->-1.65%). Direct windows confirm it ADDED to the put-book loss: Mar2020 cum -3.7% (the 2020-03-03/04/05 long-XLY entries are pre-VIX-35, ungated), 2022 bear -2.0%, Aug2024 the lone active day lost. 'Beta-neutral after hedging' is illusory in the tail: the 60d XLY-on-XLP beta is fit in calm and is under-hedged exactly when the legs decorrelate in a selloff. Compounding the verdict, the gross PRE-cost Sharpe of the stream is -1.46 (the 60d AR(1) reversion edge does not exist in this data — the ratio trends in stress, it does not revert), the sibling IWF/IWD 'sleeve 1' it claims to complement is not implemented in the registry, and neither is the family-gross cap nor the cross-sleeve correlation down-weight the spec promises in allocator.ts/meta.ts. This is a latent short-gamma bet whose proposed cure demonstrably does not fire on the trades that hurt.
redteam.fix: Make the gate fire on the actual risk driver rather than on VIX level. (1) Replace/augment the crisis force-close with a hard DIRECTIONAL veto: never take the LONG-XLY/SHORT-XLP (short-defensive) side while SPY < its 50d SMA or while the 20d XLY/XLP trend slope is negative — only allow the SHORT-XLY/LONG-XLP side (which is long defensives, tail-helpful) in risk-off. This removes the structural left-tail beta at the source instead of relying on VIX>=35. (2) Lower the crisis trigger far below VIX 35 for the short-defensive direction specifically (e.g., gate that leg on 5d-SPY < -2% or VIX > 22), since the empirics show the damage is concentrated at VIX 18-33. (3) Re-estimate the leg hedge ratio with a stress-conditional / shrunk-toward-1 beta and cut size when 20d XLY-XLP correlation breaks down. Even with these fixes the pre-cost Sharpe is negative in this sample, so the honest path is to NOT add it as a separate sleeve until a walk-forward shows after-cost Sharpe > 0.15 AND the directional-veto version keeps left-tail corr to SPY below ~0.2 — i.e., treat the spec's own falsifier (c), the 5-worst-days coincidence test, as binding and run it before deployment.

redteam.kill: Data/compute is fully feasible (the only thing that cleanly passes): XLY/XLP are in SECTOR_ETFS and STYLE_PAIRS[1] (universe.ts:33,61), VIX/VIX9D in MACRO_TICKERS, cached 2015-06→2026-06 with zero VIX/VIX9D nulls (VIX peaks 82.7 in Mar2020), and every indicator needed (beta/mean/stdev/logReturns/correlation) plus regime.vixTermRatio already exist. But the edge does not survive the regime lens. (1) The core mechanism claim — 'XLY/XLP log-ratio has no structural drift' — is empirically FALSE in-sample: the spread drifted +0.432 log (~54% XLY-over-XLP) over 11y with full-sample daily AR(1) rho=0.9975 (half-life ~280d, i.e. trending, not 3-25d reverting). The only tradeable reversion lives in the 60d-detrended residual, so the strategy is a local-residual bet riding a strong secular cyclical-over-defensive trend (mega-cap AMZN/TSLA in XLY) that can simply reverse — pure regime dependence. (2) Crowding/sample: this is THE canonical risk-on/off pair, maximally watched and decayed; entry gates pass only ~1.3% of windows on HL+z alone (35 of 2706) before the four additional regime gates, implying ~15-30 round trips in 11y — far too thin to support a 0.45 Sharpe, and trivially capable of tripping the falsifier's own Sharpe<=0.15 bar. (3) Crisis coherence is weaker than claimed: on VIX>=35 days the spread fell only 34/63 days (mean -0.0019), so the 'defensives outperform hard' premise the force-close relies on is noisy, putting falsifier (c) on a coin flip. (4) Engineering gap that compounds the regime risk: the spec is framed as the 'second instance of the IWF/IWD sleeve,' but NO IWF/IWD pair-reversion sleeve exists in registry.ts, and the allocator (allocator.ts) and meta.ts contain ZERO cross-sleeve-correlation down-weight or combined-family-gross-cap machinery — the entire risk-control story that prevents this from double-counting the first sleeve's factor is unbuilt vaporware.
redteam.fix: To make this researchable rather than rejected: (a) build the IWF/IWD sleeve FIRST and prove it standalone, since this spec is meaningless without its sibling; (b) implement the claimed cross-sleeve 60d-correlation down-weight and the 0.8 combined-family gross cap in allocator.ts/meta.ts and unit-test them before adding a second correlated pair; (c) rewrite the mechanism honestly as 'local-residual reversion around a trending mean' and add a hard regime kill if the 20d slope and 60d drift agree in sign and magnitude (don't fade a confirmed rotation); (d) run the 11y walk-forward and report the actual round-trip count — if it is under ~30, the 0.45 Sharpe is not estimable and the sleeve should be shelved regardless of point estimate; (e) since both legs are S&P sub-slices, expect |corr to first sleeve| to be high — pre-commit to the falsifier (d) >0.7 test before allocating capital.

scored.name: ResMom-LH: Long residual-top-decile, SPY-beta-hedged (low-turnover, tail-safe)

scores.name: ResMom-LH: Long residual-top-decile, SPY-beta-hedged (low-turnover, tail-safe)
scores.requiredFixes: Verified against the repo: (1) No residual_momentum_hedged.ts and no ResMom-LS sibling exist; registry.ts holds 8 strategies, none residual — the claimed 11y walk-forward was never run. (2) The marquee risk controls are vaporware: grep for realizedBeta/newHedge/hedge-quality hits ONLY CLAUDE.md prose, not meta.ts/allocator.ts/any live runner. meta.ts is longOnly:true and blends each sleeve by ONE capital-share scalar; it structurally cannot read a sleeve's net beta nor adjust the SPY leg alone, so the daily proportional controller and 10-day-Sharpe bench do not and cannot exist as described. (3) engine.ts:193 trades only on rebalanceDays boundaries, so the static -0.70*B hedge refreshes only every 21d — beta drift runs unmanaged through crisis weeks. (4) beta() (indicators.ts:162) is a stale full-window 252d OLS; high-residual mega-cap-tech longs realize downside beta >> trailing estimate, leaking net-long beta back in during drawdowns (Aug-2024 yen-carry, momentum crashes). (5) metrics.ts has rf default 0, zero beta adjustment, and NO alpha-regression/t-stat machinery — the 'net of beta' Sharpe and falsifier #2 (alpha t>1.5) are untestable with current tooling. To even RESEARCH this: actually implement ResMom-LS + the hedged variant; add an alpha-regression (residual alpha + t-stat + beta-neutralized Sharpe) to metrics; size the hedge to DOWNSIDE/crisis beta not trailing average; run the real 11y walk-forward with rf=T-bill carry and the hedge refreshed ONLY every 21d (no fictional daily controller). Credit only if alpha-t>2 AND net-of-beta after-cost Sharpe>0.45 AND worst-10-SPY-week beta<+0.4.
scores.oneLineRationale: A Fatal SHARPE-REALITY finding plus three Serious ones force CUT: hedging beta with SPY strips the equity premium that powers the 1.04 long-only benchmark, leaving only a thin, likely-crowded-to-zero idiosyncratic tilt on 14 mega-caps; reconciled after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe ~0.2 (below the spec's own 0.45 floor), and the headline risk controls are verified vaporware that cannot exist in the longOnly meta seam.

spec.name: ResMom-LH: Long residual-top-decile, SPY-beta-hedged (low-turnover, tail-safe)
spec.mechanism: Same idiosyncratic-underreaction premium, but expressed as LONG the high-residual-momentum names with the portfolio's net market beta hedged out via a short SPY position — instead of shorting individual losers. This deliberately AVOIDS the single-name short-gamma left tail (you can't get gapped up on a name you don't short), trading a bit of the long-short spread for far cleaner tail behavior and lower turnover/borrow. The short-SPY hedge is liquid, deep, and cheap to borrow, so the de-beta-ing is achieved with one instrument rather than 7 idiosyncratic shorts. This is the version the brief implicitly prefers ('long-only residual top-decile with a SPY-beta hedge').
spec.exactSignal: Residual score identical to ResMom-LS: per equity, residual e[t]=stockRet[t]-(a+b*spyRet[t]) over the trailing 252d window ending 21d ago (skip-month), score = sum(e)/stdev(e). LONG the top 2 deciles (~14 names), equal-weight, total long = +1.0 of sleeve capital. Compute the portfolio's net beta B = average of the longs' betas (weighted). HEDGE: short SPY with weight -B (so net sleeve beta ~0). No single-name shorts at all.
spec.entryRule: Hold the +1.0 long basket of top-residual-momentum names; overlay short SPY at -B where B is the basket's trailing 252d beta to SPY (typically 0.9-1.1, so hedge ~ -1.0 SPY → gross ~2.0 pre-cap, capped to 1.5 by the engine; in practice scale long basket to +0.7 and hedge -0.7*B to keep gross<=1.5). Rebalance every 21 days. No high-vol entry gate needed (the hedge removes the directional risk), but in CRISIS regime widen to top-3-deciles to reduce idiosyncratic concentration.
spec.exitRule: Refresh basket + recompute hedge beta every 21d. Continuous hedge maintenance: if the basket's realized rolling 21d beta drifts >0.25 from the hedge ratio, top up/trim the SPY short at the next rebalance (not intrabar, to control turnover). A name leaving the top-2 deciles is sold at rebalance.
spec.instruments: Long 14 single-name large-cap equities (top-2 residual deciles) + short SPY ETF as the beta hedge. No options, no single-name shorts.
spec.positionSizing: Long basket equal-weight scaled to +0.70 of capital; SPY hedge = -0.70*B (B ~1.0). Net beta ~0, gross ~1.4 (<1.5 cap). Sleeve vol target 8% annualized on trailing 60d; engine charges 50bps/yr borrow on the SPY short (cheap, deep). Turnover is LOW: only the basket churns ~ a few names/month plus a small hedge adjustment → well inside the cost budget that killed reversal.
spec.newDataNeeded: None — SPY + 74 equities already in manifest; all math uses existing indicators (beta, simpleReturns, stdev, annualizedVol).
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: METRIC tracked daily: rolling-60d realized net beta of the FULLY HEDGED sleeve (long basket P&L + SPY-hedge P&L) regressed on SPY daily returns, plus trailing-60d hedged-sleeve Sharpe. RULE: (1) meta scales the sleeve by its shrunk trailing-60d Sharpe as usual. (2) HEDGE-QUALITY OVERRIDE: if rolling net beta leaves [-0.2,+0.2], the live runner adjusts the SPY-hedge notional toward neutrality at the next daily mark (proportional controller: newHedge += 0.5*(realizedBeta)*longNotional) — so the daily loop actively re-neutralizes drift rather than waiting 21 days. (3) If hedged-sleeve trailing-60d Sharpe < 0 for 10 consecutive days, halve the weight; if < -0.25 (allocator floor) bench it. This loop targets the residual alpha staying alpha (positive) AND beta staying ~0.
spec.crisisBehavior: Much cleaner than ResMom-LS. Because the only short is SPY (no single-name gamma), in Mar-2020/Aug-2024 violent reversals the SPY hedge OFFSETS the long basket's drawdown rather than amplifying it — expect roughly FLAT in crises, occasionally slightly positive if the high-residual longs are defensives. In 2018Q4 and 2022 (factor selloffs) it should be flat-to-slightly-positive. It is NOT a deliberate crisis hedge (won't spike positive like trend-on-bonds), but it carries no concentrated left tail — the key reason to prefer it over the dollar-neutral L/S in a put-selling book.
spec.implementationNotes: Strategy in src/lib/strategies/residual_momentum_hedged.ts, longOnly:false, instrument:'equity', rebalanceDays:21, warmupBars:300. generate(ctx): compute residual scores exactly as ResMom-LS; pick top-2-decile longs at +0.70/N each; B = average beta() of the longs; push a single TargetWeight {symbol:'SPY', weight:-0.70*B}. Engine already handles the signed SPY short, borrow, and gross cap. Register in registry.ts with prior Sharpe ~0.7. The daily hedge proportional-controller and the 10-day-Sharpe bench live in the meta/live-runner layer via the existing PerfProvider/realized-equity seam (same pattern meta.ts uses for vol-targeting).
spec.falsifier: 11y walk-forward. REFUTED if: (1) after-cost Sharpe < 0.45 net of SPY beta (i.e. it doesn't beat the existing long-only xs_momentum 1.04 on an ORTHOGONAL basis — it only needs to be uncorrelated, but must clear ~0.45 to earn a sleeve); (2) the residualized long basket's alpha to SPY is statistically indistinguishable from zero (t<1.5) over the sample → there is no residual premium left in this 74-name universe (crowded out); (3) the SPY-hedged sleeve's correlation to the existing long-only meta book exceeds 0.4 full-sample OR its crisis-window (worst-10-SPY-weeks) beta exceeds +0.4 → it is not delivering the orthogonality it was added for.

redteam.kill: The spec passes the literal cost test but fails the cost-EFFICIENCY test: it bought low costs by destroying its own gross edge. Cost drag itself is modest — using the repo's own model (src/lib/backtest/costs.ts: 5bps/side trade, 50bps/yr borrow): SPY borrow on the 0.70 short = 35 bps/yr; long-basket churn at a realistic ~33%/rebalance (residual momentum is NOISIER than total-return momentum because you stripped the persistent market component, so decile-boundary churn is higher, not the spec's 'a few names') = ~55 bps/yr; the DAILY proportional controller the spec under-counts (it fires 0.5*realizedBeta*longNotional every day on a noisy rolling-60d beta of a 14-name basket) adds ~13 bps/yr; hedge re-strike ~6 bps. TOTAL ~110 bps/yr (range 80-150). At the 8% vol target that is only ~0.14 Sharpe units of drag — survivable IF the gross edge were real. It is not. The spec achieved 'tail-safe, low-borrow' by replacing the alpha-bearing single-name SHORTS with a zero-alpha SPY short. Hedging beta with SPY removes the equity risk premium that powers the long-only xs_momentum 1.04 benchmark, leaving ONLY the thin idiosyncratic residual tilt on the long leg. In a 74-name, all-mega/large-cap, heavily-arbitraged universe the realistic net-of-beta gross IR of long-leg-only residual momentum is ~0.30 (optimistically 0.55), giving an after-cost Sharpe of ~0.16 (optimistically 0.41) — below the spec's own 0.45 falsifier floor. To clear 0.45 net of beta after cost requires gross IR ~0.59, a high bar this universe almost certainly cannot meet. Capacity is fine, but the 0.70 SPY short = $7,000 at a $10k retail account is real margin/locate the spec frames as frictionless.
redteam.fix: Three fixes, none fully rescues it. (1) Kill the daily proportional controller — it chases rolling-60d beta estimation noise and adds turnover while potentially increasing realized beta variance; re-strike the hedge only at the 21d rebalance, saving ~13 bps and removing a noise-chasing leak. (2) Re-introduce a SMALL single-name short leg or a residual-LOSER short basket to recapture the half of the L/S spread the SPY substitution threw away — but that reintroduces exactly the borrow/short-gamma tail the spec was built to avoid, so it is self-defeating. (3) Widen the universe well beyond 74 crowded large caps (where residual dispersion to harvest is small) to mid-caps, raising gross IR — but that needs new data the spec explicitly says it does not have ('None needed'). The honest path is to run the 11y walk-forward FIRST and check the long-basket alpha-to-SPY t-stat (falsifier #2): if t<1.5 there is no residual premium left to cost-justify, and the cheap-cost framing is moot.

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY (net of beta + look-ahead): is the claimed Sharpe real after stripping market beta and after costs, with no look-ahead in the signal?
redteam.kill: The 0.7 Sharpe is an unmeasured guess, and the harness cannot measure the net-of-beta Sharpe it claims. (1) No residual_momentum_hedged.ts and no ResMom-LS sibling exist in the repo (registry.ts has 8 strategies, none residual); the promised 11y walk-forward in the falsifier was never run. (2) computeMetrics (metrics.ts:44-90) produces a RAW total-return Sharpe with rfAnnual defaulting to 0 and ZERO beta adjustment, and there is no alpha-regression/t-stat machinery anywhere - so the spec's own falsifier #2 (residual alpha t>1.5 net of SPY) is untestable with current tooling. The 'net of beta' claim is unverifiable. (3) The headline risk-control - daily proportional hedge controller newHedge += 0.5*realizedBeta*longNotional, HEDGE-QUALITY OVERRIDE, and 10-day-Sharpe bench, claimed to live in the meta/live-runner layer - DOES NOT EXIST (grep for realizedBeta/newHedge/hedge-quality: zero hits). The actual meta.ts only does 5-day capital allocation + vol-targeting; the engine trades ONLY on rebalanceDays boundaries (engine.ts:193), so the static -0.70*B SPY hedge is refreshed only every 21 days and carries un-managed beta drift between rebalances. (4) beta() (indicators.ts:162) is a stale full-window 252d OLS applied statically; in Mar-2020/Aug-2024 the high-residual mega-cap-tech longs realize beta well above the trailing estimate, so leftover long beta leaks back in exactly during drawdowns - beta in disguise, not crisis-flat. (5) This is the long-only (weaker) half of an already-thin idiosyncratic-momentum premium in a 74-name, heavily-arbitraged mega-cap universe; after de-beta-ing the surviving gross spread on 14 names is small and plausibly crowded to zero.
redteam.fix: Actually implement ResMom-LS and the hedged variant; add an alpha-regression to the metrics layer (regress sleeve daily returns on SPY using the existing beta() helper, report residual alpha + t-stat and a Sharpe computed on the beta-neutralized residual return, not the raw equity curve); estimate the hedge beta OUT-OF-SAMPLE and run the real 11y walk-forward with rf set to the actual T-bill carry. Only credit the sleeve if the residualized basket's alpha-t > 2 AND the net-of-beta after-cost Sharpe clears ~0.45 with the hedge refreshed only every 21 days (i.e., WITHOUT the fictional daily controller, which inflates the claim). Also verify worst-10-SPY-week beta stays under +0.4 using the realized (not trailing) hedge to expose the stale-beta leakage.

redteam.lens: ORTHOGONALITY & TAIL — left-tail (crisis) independence from a long-only / short-put / short-vol book; hidden beta and shared short-gamma; behavior in Mar2020 / 2022 / Aug2024.
redteam.kill: The orthogonality claim rests entirely on a trailing-252d-beta hedge holding in the left tail, and it does not. (1) STALE + DOWNSIDE-BETA-NAIVE HEDGE: the -0.70 SPY hedge is sized to AVERAGE trailing beta, but crowded high-residual-momentum large caps have downside beta >> trailing beta (correlations and realized betas of the momentum crowd jump toward/above 1 in liquidations). So in the worst weeks the basket falls HARDER than the SPY hedge and the sleeve carries net-positive realized beta — exactly when the short-put book is bleeding. This is structurally short gamma (fixed -B SPY against a state-dependent basket beta: under-hedged on down-gaps, over-hedged on rallies). (2) SHARED FACTOR: long-crowded-momentum / short-SPY is short the dispersion/liquidation/violent-regime-change factor; a short-put book is short that SAME factor (down-gap + correlation-to-1 + vol spike). They are not offsetting — both lose in the same crisis. (3) CRISIS TAPE: Aug-2024 (yen-carry unwind) the high-residual longs (NVDA/semis/momentum crowd) were the EPICENTRE, falling far more than SPY — a -0.70 SPY hedge leaves large net-long-the-crash exposure → ADDS to the put-book drawdown, contradicting the spec's 'flat-to-positive, occasionally defensive' claim. 2022/momentum-crash: the documented Daniel-Moskowitz bear-rebound failure (the repo's own momentum.ts cites it) makes a beta-neutral long-momentum/short-SPY book negatively convex to snapbacks, in high-VIX regimes where the put book is already stressed. Full-sample corr 0.15 is irrelevant — beta-neutrality on the FULL sample is precisely what hides tail beta; the lens demands left-tail orthogonality, which this fails (spec's own falsifier #3: worst-10-SPY-weeks beta > +0.4 → REFUTED). Compounding: (a) the spec's marquee risk-control machinery is VAPORWARE — I read meta.ts/allocator.ts in full; there is NO daily proportional hedge controller (newHedge += 0.5*realizedBeta*longNotional) and NO 10-day-Sharpe bench in the meta/live-runner layer. The PerfProvider seam only returns {sharpe,vol,n,returns} per key; it cannot re-neutralize a sub-strategy's intraday hedge. The hedge refreshes ONLY at the 21-day rebalance, so the very drift the spec promises to control runs uncontrolled THROUGH the crisis week. (b) The residual premium is UNVALIDATED here: there is no ResMom-LS sibling in the repo despite 3+ 'identical to ResMom-LS' references, and residual momentum in a 74-name mega-cap universe is among the most crowded signals — falsifier #2 (alpha t<1.5) is likely to trip, meaning there may be no alpha to even hedge into orthogonality.
redteam.fix: Size the hedge to the basket's DOWNSIDE/semi-beta (or crisis-conditional beta), not trailing average beta; this is the only fix that addresses the tail. Add a real crisis overlay: when regime==CRISIS (VIX>=35) or VIX term structure inverts, increase the SPY short toward the basket's stress-beta OR overlay SPY puts, and HARD-GATE the sleeve toward zero in the put-book's defined crisis regime so it can never ADD to the drawdown. Actually BUILD the daily proportional hedge controller and the 10-day-Sharpe kill-switch in scripts/daily/run.ts before claiming them in the spec — and shorten hedge top-up to a realized-beta trigger, not 21-day. Gate live deployment behind passing falsifier #2 (residual alpha t>1.5) AND falsifier #3 measured on the actual worst-10-SPY-weeks, not full-sample corr. Even fully mitigated it remains a long-crowded-momentum / short-index relative-value book, so cap its sleeve weight and treat it as a return diversifier, never as crisis protection (the tail_hedge sleeve is the real protection).

redteam.kill: Data and standalone compute are genuinely feasible (74 equities + SPY cached free/daily from Yahoo, 2766 bars 2015-2026 covering COVID/2022; residual score = stockRet-(a+b*spyRet), score=sum/stdev computable with existing beta() + a 1-line OLS intercept; engine.ts supports the signed -0.70*B SPY short, 50bps borrow, and 1.5 gross cap). But the spec fails the lens on two grounds. (1) The daily feedback loop is not wired and partly cannot be via the claimed seam: PerfProvider returns only {sharpe,vol,n,returns} and meta.ts blends each sleeve by ONE capital-share scalar in [minVolScale,1] (it is longOnly:true and cannot read a sleeve's realized net beta nor adjust the SPY leg alone). The proportional hedge controller (newHedge += 0.5*realizedBeta*longNotional) and 10-day-Sharpe bench are net-new infrastructure, NOT 'the existing meta pattern' as claimed. The strategy's own generate() only re-hedges every 21d, so the 'daily re-neutralization' the thesis leans on never runs in the falsifier backtest -- the backtested object differs from the described live object, breaking the repo's sim/live parity exactly on the hedge-quality override. (2) Regime/crowding kills the edge under a skeptic prior: residual (idiosyncratic) momentum in an all-mega-cap 74-name universe is the most-arbitraged factor corner; post-2018 net-of-cost large-cap residual momentum is widely documented as decayed, so the falsifier's own t<1.5 alpha condition likely triggers. Crucially, the long-only+SPY-hedge framing removes single-name short-gamma but NOT momentum-crash risk: in bear-market rebounds (Apr-May 2020, Jan 2021, 2022 bear rallies) the long winners mean-revert DOWN while SPY rallies UP, so the short SPY ADDS to the drawdown -- the opposite of the spec's 'roughly flat in crises' claim, and the worst case for a long-winners/short-index book. The static trailing-252d basket beta is also regime-unstable (dispersion spikes in crises) and the >0.25 drift tolerance lets it run mis-hedged for up to 21 days. Net of decayed premium + borrow + the crisis-window +beta, a 0.7 net-of-beta Sharpe is optimistic; realistic is ~0.2-0.35, below the 0.45 sleeve threshold.
redteam.fix: To make it survivable: (a) Build the hedge controller and 10-day-Sharpe bench as REAL code in the live-runner with a backtest equivalent, OR drop the daily-loop claims entirely and run/report it as a plain 21d-rebalanced sleeve so the falsifier tests what actually trades. (b) Replace the static trailing-252d basket beta with a regime-conditional / shrunk beta (e.g. blend 60d and 252d, raise the hedge in CRISIS) to address the crisis mis-hedge. (c) Before claiming a sleeve, run the 11y walk-forward and pre-commit to the falsifier's three conditions, specifically reporting the long-basket alpha-to-SPY t-stat and the worst-10-SPY-week beta -- treat survives=true only if t>1.5 AND crisis beta<+0.4 net of costs. (d) Add an explicit momentum-crash guard (e.g. cut or de-hedge the basket after large negative-then-positive SPY swings) since the SPY short amplifies, not offsets, momentum crashes.

scored.name: Stress-Gated Defensive-minus-Cyclical Sector RV (beta-neutral)

scores.name: Stress-Gated Defensive-minus-Cyclical Sector RV (beta-neutral)
scores.requiredFixes: No fix preserves the brief. The fatal contradiction is structural: the crisis payoff IS the raw spread's -0.48 SPY beta, so beta-neutrality (required by falsifier a) and crisis-positivity (falsifier b) are mutually exclusive. Verified on cache (2015-06..2026-06): gross beta-neutral Sharpe 0.007 (claimed +0.4, wrong sign vs other lenses); Mar-2020 beta-neutral = -4.6% (worst left-tail, the put book's worst day, is NEGATIVE -> fails falsifier b and inverts the 'left-tail-on-the-right-side' thesis); forward-21d |beta|>0.15 in 40% of rebalances -> fails falsifier a (not reliably net-of-beta); worst-1% SPY days mean spread -0.35%, 50% positive -> no tail diversification; defensives 8.6%/yr vs cyclicals 16.1%/yr -> structurally short the winner; turnover ~283% one-way / ~567% round-trip (spec's 12-24 RT/yr is wrong by ~20x); gate is coincident VIX>20dSMA and ON 52% of the time. If a defensive tail hedge is wanted, size it openly as a ~-0.4 beta short-equity overlay (then it is beta, not orthogonal alpha, competing with simply buying puts) -- but that is a different product. The quality/low-beta premium lives in single-name QMJ/BAB across many names, not a 3-vs-3 ETF pair where beta-neutralization annihilates the residual.
scores.oneLineRationale: Four Fatal red-team verdicts plus my own replication: beta-neutral gross Sharpe ~0.0, Mar-2020 negative, 40% forward-beta breach -- the advertised crisis-alpha is pure short-equity beta that vanishes once neutralized; CUT.

spec.name: Stress-Gated Defensive-minus-Cyclical Sector RV (beta-neutral)
spec.mechanism: Cross-sectional dispersion between defensive and cyclical sectors widens predictably when volatility regime shifts: in rising-stress regimes defensives (staples/utilities/healthcare) structurally outperform cyclicals (discretionary/tech/industrials) as institutions de-risk toward low-beta cash-flow. This is a flight-to-quality flow + a low-beta/quality risk premium, NOT directional market beta. Constructing it dollar-neutral and beta-neutral isolates the spread. Crucially for a short-vol/put book the LEFT tail is on the RIGHT side: when equities crash, defensives beat cyclicals, so the spread is crisis-POSITIVE — this is the genuine tail-orthogonality the brief demands (mean-orthogonality is not enough).
spec.exactSignal: Defensive basket D = {XLP, XLU, XLV}; Cyclical basket C = {XLY, XLK, XLI}. Spread proxy s_t = mean(20d return of D) - mean(20d return of C). Stress gate: trade the spread ONLY when ctx.regime.vixTermRatio (^VIX9D/^VIX) >= 1.0 (backwardation / rising stress) OR ctx.regime.regime in {HIGH_VOL,CRISIS} OR VIX has risen (today's VIX > its 20d SMA). When the gate is OFF (calm contango), hold the spread at a small structural baseline weight (carry the low-beta premium) or flat. Sign is fixed long-D/short-C (we do not flip to long-cyclical; the cyclical-outperformance leg is just equity beta and not orthogonal).
spec.entryRule: On monthly rebalance: compute per-sector 60d beta to SPY (indicators.beta). Set long weights on D and short weights on C such that (a) dollar-neutral: sum(longs)=sum(|shorts|), and (b) beta-neutral: weighted long beta = weighted short beta (scale the C-leg notional by betaD/betaC). When stress gate is ON, full weight; when OFF, 0.3x baseline weight.
spec.exitRule: Rebalance monthly (rebalanceDays:21); between rebalances let it drift. Hard de-gross: if the sleeve's own 21d realized vol exceeds 2x its 1y target, halve weights at next rebalance. Flatten the spread entirely if rolling 6-month sleeve Sharpe < -0.3 (mechanism broken).
spec.instruments: Long XLP/XLU/XLV, short XLY/XLK/XLI (SPDR sector ETFs, deep liquidity, hard-to-borrow risk negligible, low borrow cost). Beta-neutral, dollar-neutral pair.
spec.positionSizing: Vol-target the net spread to 6% annualized. Gross is ~0.6-1.0 (3 longs + 3 shorts, each leg ~0.5 gross at full weight), well under the 1.5 cap. Baseline (calm) weight = 0.3x. Per-name weight = (legWeight/3) adjusted by beta-neutral scaling on the short leg.
spec.newDataNeeded: none — XLP/XLU/XLV/XLY/XLK/XLI all in the sector SPDR set already in manifest; ^VIX/^VIX9D already supplied to regime.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: METRIC: rolling 12-month realized Sharpe of the sleeve's daily net (long-minus-short) P&L on the live sim book, PLUS a daily realized-beta monitor (regress sleeve daily return on SPY daily return over trailing 60d). RULE: (1) Sharpe-based allocation multiplier m: S>0.5 -> m->min(1.25,m+0.05); 0<=S<=0.5 -> hold; S<0 -> m->max(0.25,m-0.10); S<-0.3 over 6mo -> m=0 hibernate. (2) Beta-leakage guard: if |trailing-60d beta| > 0.15, scale the short (cyclical) leg notional next rebalance to drive beta back toward 0 — this keeps the stream net-of-beta day by day, preventing the sleeve from silently becoming a short-equity bet.
spec.crisisBehavior: POSITIVE in crashes: long-defensive/short-cyclical is the flight-to-quality trade, so 2018Q4, Mar-2020, the 2022 drawdown, and Aug-2024 are exactly when it earns (defensives outperform cyclicals). This is the rare crisis-alpha, tail-RIGHT-side stream that genuinely DIVERSIFIES a short-vol/put book whose left tail is a crash. The main risk is sharp risk-on rebounds (cyclicals snap back) — mitigated by the stress gate cutting weight to baseline when stress recedes.
spec.implementationNotes: Strategy with rebalanceDays:21, warmupBars:260, longOnly:false, instrument:'etf'. In generate(ctx): pull closes for the 6 sector ETFs, compute 20d returns for the spread sign-confirmation and 60d betas vs SPY via indicators.beta(simpleReturns(sectorCloses), simpleReturns(spyCloses)). Read ctx.regime.vixTermRatio/regime/vix for the gate. Emit signed weights: +w_long on D names, -w_short on C names with w_short scaled by mean(betaD)/mean(betaC) for beta-neutrality, then vol-target via annualizedVol of the synthetic spread series. Engine handles signed weights, borrow cost, and the 0.2% dust filter; monthly cadence keeps turnover ~12-24 round trips/yr -> survives 10bps + borrow comfortably. This is distinct from the existing long-only sector-rotation sleeve (which buys top-momentum sectors and carries full beta); here the construct is explicitly dollar- and beta-neutral with a fixed defensive-long sign.
spec.falsifier: Walk-forward 2014-2025. REFUTED if: (a) the sleeve's trailing-60d beta to SPY cannot be held within +/-0.15 (it is secretly an equity bet, not orthogonal), OR (b) its return in the 4 named crisis windows (2018Q4, Mar-2020, 2022, Aug-2024) is NOT positive on average (the crisis-alpha claim fails), OR (c) after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe < 0.25, OR (d) full-sample correlation to the long-only meta book is > +0.25 (not orthogonal). Any one of these kills it.

redteam.kill: Lens question "gross edge > round-trip cost?" fails because there is barely any gross edge. Reconstructing the exact spec on the repo's 2015-2026 cached bars (3 defensives long, 3 cyclicals short, 60d-beta-neutral short scaling, VIX gate, vol-targeted 6%) yields GROSS pre-cost Sharpe ~0.03, gross mean ~0.14%/yr on 4.5% vol; the ungated raw spread is gross-NEGATIVE (-0.12). With the repo cost model (10bps round trip, 50bps/yr borrow, 0.2% dust) friction is only ~0.34%/yr, but that is ~2.4x the entire gross edge, flipping net to ~-0.05 Sharpe. Turnover claim is wrong: spec says ~12-24 round trips/yr, actual is ~497% one-way / ~994% round-trip notional (gate flips book 0.3x-to-1.0x; monthly vol-target and beta-scale rescale every leg). Costs stay small only because the vol-target base notional is tiny (gross ~0.4, not 0.6-1.0). Sub-period gross Sharpes sign-flip with no persistence (-0.17,+0.18,-0.26,+0.29); 2022, a named crisis-positive window, is gross-NEGATIVE. Beta-neutral and dollar-neutral are mutually exclusive here: betaC>betaD forces short leg to ~0.46x long notional, leaving ~0.21 net-long defensives. Claimed 0.4 after-cost vs honest ~0.0.
redteam.fix: Nothing to fix on the cost axis: SPDR sector ETFs are tier-1 (1-2bps spreads, deep borrow, multi-billion/day capacity); at 6% vol the ~0.34%/yr friction is trivial and capacity is large. The binding problem is upstream: gross edge is ~zero. Must first build a larger, persistent gross spread (replace the coincident VIX>20d-SMA gate with a forward-looking signal; widen the universe; or hold structurally instead of gating on/off, which drives the 994% turnover). Until pre-cost Sharpe clears ~0.3, no cost engineering matters. Also fix the turnover figure (994% RT, not 12-24) and resolve the beta-vs-dollar-neutral contradiction (residual ~0.21 net-long-defensive tilt).

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY: net-of-beta after-cost Sharpe with no look-ahead
redteam.kill: Tested the exact spec on the repo cache (XLP/XLU/XLV/XLY/XLK/XLI, SPY, VIX, VIX9D; 2015-06 to 2026-06, 9.9y). Beta-neutrality works (realized beta 0.007, corr 0.07) but that kills the edge. (1) No edge pre-cost: GROSS Sharpe -0.14, net-of-beta GROSS Sharpe -0.20; after cost -0.5 to -0.8. Claimed +0.4 has wrong sign. (2) Crisis-alpha fails its own falsifier (b): 2018Q4 +0.09%, Mar-2020 -0.96%, 2022 -0.27%, Aug-2024 +0.46% -- the two biggest crises are NEGATIVE. Cause is structural: beta-neutralizing the short cyclical leg removes the flight-to-quality directional move, so beta-neutral and crisis-positive cannot coexist here. (3) Gate is noise: ON Sharpe -0.15 ~ OFF -0.16. (4) Structurally short the winner: defensives 9.7%/yr vs cyclicals 17.3%/yr, a 7.6%/yr headwind. Even the most generous build (daily rebalance, in-sample beta fit, no gate, no cost) gives net-of-beta Sharpe -0.04.
redteam.fix: None rescues the +0.4 from sector ETFs; the quality premium lives in single-name factors (QMJ/BAB across many names), not a 3-vs-3 ETF pair where beta-neutralization annihilates the residual. Options: accept it as a near-zero-Sharpe diversifier, or run it un-neutralized as an explicit long-defensive tail hedge (negative carry as insurance) but then it is beta, not orthogonal alpha, and competes with simply buying puts. Spec falsifiers (b) and (c) are both empirically refuted, so by its own terms it is killed.

redteam.lens: ORTHOGONALITY & TAIL — is the defensive-minus-cyclical RV stream uncorrelated to a long-only/short-vol put book in the LEFT TAIL (crises), not just full-sample? Tested empirically on cached SPDR sector ETFs + ^VIX/^VIX9D (2015-2026): built the beta-neutral spread (long XLP/XLU/XLV, short XLY/XLK/XLI scaled by betaD/betaC=0.567), a short-put/short-vol proxy (long market delta + negative-gamma crash convexity), and conditioned on the worst SPY days and the four named crisis windows.
redteam.kill: The core "left-tail-is-on-the-right-side" claim is empirically INVERTED for the fastest crash, which is precisely the put book's worst tail. In Mar-2020 (worst left-tail event), the beta-neutral spread returned -9.6% — it ADDED to the put-book drawdown, not offset it, because in a dash-for-cash defensives sell off too (XLU -36.1%, XLP -24.2%) while you hold the short-cyclical leg at only 0.567x notional. Feb-2018 volmageddon (the canonical short-vol blowup) also lost -3.3%. Conditioning on the worst 1% of SPY days, corr(spread, put-book proxy) jumps to +0.45 with mean spread -0.61% and only 29.6% of days positive — that is hidden shared short-gamma surfacing exactly in the tail, the opposite of the brief's "tail-orthogonality" requirement. The positive windows it cites (2018Q4 +5.3%, 2022 +4.9%) are SLOW-grind bears where short-XLK = short-tech-beta carried it (2022: XLK -32.5% vs XLP -10.6%) — a beta artifact, not flight-to-quality, and the wrong tail for a put hedge. The spec's own falsifiers also trip: forward-realized 21d |beta|>0.15 in 42% of rebalances (rolling-60d 53%, max 0.67) because sector betas are unstable and spike in stress, so it is NOT reliably net-of-beta; and standalone gross Sharpe is -0.01 vs the claimed +0.4, so the entire edge must be manufactured by the stress gate on a stream with zero unconditional premium. Multiple independent falsifiers (a), (b for Mar-2020), and (c) all fire; by the spec's own rule any one kills it.
redteam.fix: To salvage even a weaker version: (1) Drop XLU from the defensive basket and replace the "flight-to-quality to defensives" thesis with an explicit flight-to-cash/Treasury overlay — utilities are rate-sensitive and crash WITH the market in liquidity events, which is what sank Mar-2020. (2) Re-underwrite the crisis claim on FAST vol-spike days (dVIX > +5, daily SPY < -3%) rather than slow drawdown windows, since the put book's tail is gamma/jump risk, not slow re-rating; the data shows the sleeve only helps in slow bears. (3) Replace static trailing-60d beta-neutralization with a stress-conditional beta estimate (DCC/EWMA with crisis-correlation shock) and de-gross hard when realized correlation regime-shifts, since the 42% forward-beta breach rate means the orthogonality guard fails when it matters most. (4) Validate against a real short-put P&L series (or VIX-futures short proxy) rather than relying on the asserted -0.1 correlation, and require POSITIVE conditional return in the worst 1% tail, not full-sample. Even with these fixes the honest expectation is a low-Sharpe diversifier that helps in grind-down bears and is roughly flat-to-negative in violent crashes — not the crisis-alpha hedge the spec advertises.

redteam.kill: DATA/COMPUTE: fully feasible — no blocker there. All six ETFs (XLP/XLU/XLV/XLY/XLK/XLI) are in SECTOR_ETFS, ^VIX/^VIX9D are in MACRO_TICKERS and feed ctx.regime.vixTermRatio (=vix9d/vix) and regime in engine.ts. The engine handles signed weights, shorts, borrow cost, gross cap, dust filter, and indicators.beta/annualizedVol exist. Only caveat: the cache currently starts 2015-06-02 (fetched years=11 from 2026), so the falsifier's "2014-2025 walk-forward" needs YEARS widened — trivial, Yahoo has the history. So the kill is NOT data; it is the mechanism failing under its OWN beta-neutral constraint.\n\nFATAL EMPIRICAL CONTRADICTION (computed directly from cached data 2015-2026): The spec's two load-bearing claims are mutually exclusive and the falsifier's own conditions fail. The RAW dollar-neutral long-D/short-C spread does post the advertised crisis payoff (2018Q4 +16.7%, Mar-2020 +7.6%, 2022 +17.9%, Aug-2024 +11.2%) — BUT that comes entirely from its -0.48 beta and -0.58 correlation to SPY. It is a short-equity-beta bet, exactly what falsifier (a) warns about. The moment you actually impose beta-neutrality (which falsifier (a) REQUIRES: |beta|<=0.15), the crisis alpha evaporates: beta-neutralized crisis returns collapse to +0.08% / -2.08% (Mar-2020 NEGATIVE) / +0.09% / +3.67%, so falsifier (b) FAILS. You cannot simultaneously be beta-neutral AND crisis-positive here, because the crisis payoff IS the negative beta. Standalone beta-neutral Sharpe is ~0.06 full-sample (vs claimed 0.4); even the stress-gated version reaches only ~0.375 IN-SAMPLE with hindsight gate thresholds, before costs — below the 0.25 net falsifier once 10bps+borrow+monthly turnover hit. DECAY: split-sample Sharpe is +0.33 (2015-19) but -0.08 (2020-26) gross — the edge lives only in the first half and is gone in the last six years, consistent with a crowded, zero-barrier textbook factor.
redteam.fix: There is no mitigation that preserves the brief's requirement. The only way to recover the crisis-positive payoff is to STOP neutralizing beta and run it as an explicit ~-0.4 to -0.5 beta short-equity overlay — but then it is not orthogonal (fails falsifier a and d, corr to long-only book becomes strongly negative-but-via-beta, i.e. just a hedge, not alpha) and it is no longer the 'mean-orthogonal AND tail-orthogonal RV' the spec sells. If forced to keep beta-neutrality, the honest expectation is ~0.06 Sharpe with crisis P&L near zero — it does not diversify a short-vol/put book in any meaningful, non-beta way. Kill the sleeve as specified; if a defensive tilt hedge is wanted, size it openly as a beta hedge, not as 'crisis alpha'.

scored.name: XATS-Core: Vol-scaled cross-asset time-series momentum (non-equity, long/short)

scores.name: XATS-Core: Vol-scaled cross-asset time-series momentum (non-equity, long/short)
scores.requiredFixes: A Fatal finding (Sharpe Reality lens) mandates CUT, and the sleeve trips two of its own falsifiers, so this is a rejection, not a fix-list. If a future revision were attempted it would have to: (1) Drop the short legs and ship the already-registered long-only sibling cross_asset_trend instead -- the L/S shorts add borrow + turnover for NEGATIVE marginal Sharpe and are strictly dominated. (2) Honestly relabel the falsifier window to 2015-06-02..2026-06-01 (the true cached span per .last_run.log; data.ts L26-28 = now - 11y, NOT 2013-2024); re-run on real data, which already shows pre-cost gross Sharpe ~0.25 and net-of-beta residual ~0.10. (3) Cut turnover below 600% BEFORE backtesting (measured 622-633%/yr trips falsifier d) by lengthening cadence / widening the SMA band -- NOT via a post-hoc adaptive guard, which is in-sample tuning. (4) Fix the orthogonality lie: gate risk-on long legs (EEM/EFA/VNQ/SLV) OFF in VIX backwardation / VIX>=35 -- left-tail corr to SPY flips to +0.79 (COVID) / +0.83 (Aug2024) because 12-1 momentum loads long carry INTO the crash, so the sleeve ADDS to put-book losses on the worst days. (5) Implement the claimed feedback loop or stop claiming it: allocator.ts has NO per-sleeve m ratchet, NO corr-to-core gate, NO turnover guard -- only 126d shrinkage-to-prior + regime tilt + inverse-vol; 'no engine change needed' is false. (6) Make costs.ts instrument-aware (per-ETF spread tiers + stress-scaled HTB borrow + locate-fail) -- the flat 10bps + 50bps under-charges the commodity/EM/oil short legs 2-5x precisely in the stress regime that is the sleeve's reason to exist. (7) Remove the m-floor of 0.3 -- a never-zero allocation to a 0.10-residual-Sharpe sleeve is an unfalsifiable escape hatch.
scores.oneLineRationale: Fatal Sharpe-Reality finding (net-of-beta residual ~0.10, pre-cost gross only 0.25 vs claimed 0.55) plus two self-falsifiers firing (turnover >600%, left-tail corr flips to +0.8 in V-crashes), a vaporware feedback loop, and a strictly-better long-only sibling already shipping -> CUT.

spec.name: XATS-Core: Vol-scaled cross-asset time-series momentum (non-equity, long/short)
spec.mechanism: Risk-premium + behavioral underreaction to slow macro information (rate cycles, inflation/commodity trends, flight-to-quality). Moskowitz-Ooi-Pedersen: an asset's own trailing 12m return predicts its next-month sign across bonds, gold, commodities, FX, REITs. Restricting to NON-US-large-cap-equity instruments and allowing SHORTS gives the only genuinely orthogonal stream the book lacks: it can be long TLT/GLD/UUP and SHORT DBC/EEM exactly when SPY is selling off, producing positive skew and crisis convexity rather than shared market beta.
spec.exactSignal: Universe = CROSS_ASSET_ETFS already in manifest: TLT, IEF, SHY, GLD, SLV, DBC, USO, UUP, VNQ, EEM, EFA. For each asset on each rebalance compute: (a) signal12 = momentumSkip(closes,252,21) (12-1 return, skip last month to dodge short-term reversal); (b) confirm with sma(closes,200): require price>SMA200 to go long, price<SMA200 to go short (dual confirmation kills whipsaws); (c) realized vol sigma = annualizedVol(closes,60) (60d). Direction d = +1 if signal12>0 AND close>SMA200; -1 if signal12<0 AND close<SMA200; else 0 (flat). This is intentionally a SLOW signal (monthly cadence, 12m lookback) to keep turnover and crowding exposure low.
spec.entryRule: On a rebalance day, for each asset with d!=0, target weight w_i = d_i * (targetVolPerAsset / sigma_i), with targetVolPerAsset = 10% annualized. Cap |w_i| <= 0.20 per asset (no single ETF dominates). Sum |w_i| is then scaled to the sleeve gross budget (<=1.0 within the sleeve; meta engine caps total book gross<=1.5). Assets with d=0 hold zero (cash). Equal RISK, not equal dollar — low-vol bonds get larger weights than DBC/USO.
spec.exitRule: An asset is closed (w->0) the first rebalance its dual-confirmation flips to d=0 (signal12 and SMA200 disagree) or reverses sign. No intramonth stop (EOD monthly cadence); the 200d SMA filter is the de-facto trailing stop. Because sizing is inverse-vol, a vol spike auto-shrinks the position at the next rebalance even before a sign flip.
spec.instruments: Long/short ETFs: TLT, IEF, SHY, GLD, SLV, DBC, USO, UUP, VNQ, EEM, EFA. All free daily Yahoo OHLCV already in ALL_BACKTEST_SYMBOLS. Shorts are liquid, easy-to-borrow (engine charges 50bps/yr borrow).
spec.positionSizing: Inverse-vol risk parity to 10%/asset annualized, |w_i|<=0.20, sleeve gross<=1.0. On fixed capital. Meta-level 10% vol target already in engine governs the blended book; this sleeve is handed in as one signed-weight stream and the adaptive allocator sizes it. Net beta to SPY is near zero by construction (non-equity + signed).
spec.newDataNeeded: none — all in manifest (CROSS_ASSET_ETFS already defined in universe.ts; SHY/SLV/USO/UUP/DBC/GLD/TLT/IEF/VNQ/EEM/EFA all fetched).
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: METRIC: rolling 63-day realized Sharpe of THIS sleeve's signed-weight P&L on the live sim book, AND its rolling 63-day correlation to the long-only/short-vol core. RULE: the adaptive allocator multiplier m starts at 1.0. Each day: if sleeve rolling Sharpe > 0.3 AND |corr-to-core| < 0.2, raise m by +0.05/day toward cap 1.5; if rolling Sharpe < -0.3 OR |corr-to-core| > 0.4 (orthogonality breakdown), cut m by -0.10/day toward floor 0.3 (shrunk to prior, never zero — preserves the crisis-hedge option). Also a turnover guard: if trailing-12m sleeve turnover > 400%/yr, widen the SMA confirmation band by 1% to slow trading. m feeds the meta vol-target allocation.
spec.crisisBehavior: Designed to be POSITIVE in slow, persistent selloffs (2022 rate-hike bear: long UUP/short TLT-DBC mix and short EEM; 2018Q4: long bonds as yields fell). FLAT-to-slightly-positive in 2018Q4 (fast, only ~3 months — trend barely confirms). The known failure mode is a V-shaped crash like Mar-2020 and Aug-2024: the selloff is too fast for a monthly 12-1 signal to flip, so the sleeve is roughly FLAT-to-small-negative on the crash leg, then catches the post-crash bond/gold/dollar trend. Honest: this is crisis alpha for GRINDS, not for one-week vol spikes (that is what the separate VIX-gated tail sleeve is for).
spec.implementationNotes: New Strategy object key 'xa_tsmom' in src/lib/strategies/, longOnly:false, instrument:'etf', rebalanceDays:21, warmupBars:260. In generate(ctx): iterate CROSS_ASSET_ETFS filtered by ctx.universe; use existing momentumSkip(c,252,21), sma(c,200), annualizedVol(c,60) from indicators.ts. Build signed weights per entryRule, clamp |w|<=0.20, normalize gross to 1.0. Return {weights, confidence: clamp(avg(|signal12|/0.15),0,1)} so the concordance/ensemble gate can scale it. Register in registry.ts; the existing engine already handles signed weights, borrow cost, maxGross, and meta vol-targeting — no engine change needed.
spec.falsifier: Run the walk-forward 11y backtest. REFUTED if: (a) full-sample |corr to SPY daily returns| > 0.25 (then it is not orthogonal); OR (b) net-of-cost annualized return < 0 with Sharpe < 0.2 over 2013-2024 (trend dead post-publication for these liquid ETFs); OR (c) it is NEGATIVE in BOTH 2022 and the 2018Q4 grind (then it provides no crisis alpha in the very regimes it is bought for); OR (d) realized annual turnover > 600% (cost model will then eat it as it ate short-term reversal). Any one of these kills the sleeve.

redteam.lens: COSTS & CAPACITY — realistic retail frictions (spreads, short borrow/locate, turnover, min-granularity vs fixed capital, capacity)
redteam.kill: The engine's cost model (src/lib/backtest/costs.ts) charges a FLAT 3bps/side spread + 2bps/side slippage (10bps round-trip) and a FLAT 50bps/yr borrow to EVERY ETF, regardless of liquidity or short-availability. Both flat assumptions are optimistic for exactly the instruments this long/short sleeve leans on. SPREAD: TLT/IEF/GLD/SHY are genuinely ~1-3bps, but DBC, USO, UUP (~$300M AUM), SLV, EEM, EFA are 5-20bps/side for retail plus USO carries roll/tracking drag — realistic blended round-trip is ~12-16bps, not 10. BORROW is the real attack: the thesis is to SHORT DBC/USO/EEM in selloffs (its 'orthogonal crisis alpha'), but commodity/EM/oil ETF borrow is variable and spikes to 100-300bps (and locate can fail) precisely in the stress regimes the sleeve is bought for — the engine's flat 50bps under-charges the short leg by 2-5x and ignores locate-fail entirely. TURNOVER: the dual-confirmation 12-1 signal is slow on SIGN, but monthly inverse-vol RESIZING (targetVol/sigma reset every 21d as 60d vol drifts 10-30%/mo) pushes realized turnover to ~200-300%/yr (managed-futures empirics), near the spec's own 400% guard. Quantifying: realistic cost drag = ~35bps (turnover 250% x ~14bps) + ~60bps (40% short notional x ~150bps blended borrow) ≈ ~100bps/yr vs the engine's modeled ~45bps. Against a realistic POST-PUBLICATION gross Sharpe of ~0.4 for 11 liquid ETFs over 2013-2024 (~450bps gross at ~11% vol), gross edge (~450bps) DOES exceed round-trip cost (~100bps) — so it is not cost-killed — but net Sharpe ≈ 0.4 − (0.010/0.11) ≈ 0.31, and as low as ~0.25 once stress-borrow and locate-fail are honestly priced. That is roughly HALF the claimed 0.55, and the gap is concentrated in the crisis regime that is the sleeve's entire reason to exist.
redteam.fix: (1) Make the cost model instrument-aware: per-ETF spread tiers (1-2bps for TLT/IEF/SHY/GLD; 6-10bps for DBC/USO/UUP/SLV/EEM) and a hard-to-borrow surcharge that scales borrow with realized vol/stress for commodity/EM/oil shorts (e.g. 50bps easy, 150-300bps in HIGH_VOL/CRISIS) plus a locate-fail flag that flattens a short to 0 when borrow is unavailable. (2) Constrain shorts to the genuinely easy-to-borrow, deep names (short EEM/EFA/TLT acceptable; restrict or cap short DBC/USO/UUP) so the crisis-short leg does not depend on borrow that vanishes in stress. (3) Add a no-resize band: only re-trade inverse-vol weights when |target−current| > a threshold (e.g. 25% relative), which the engine's 0.2% dust filter does not fully achieve — this directly caps the resize turnover that dominates the cost. (4) Re-run the walk-forward with the realistic cost model and report the sleeve's OWN net annual turnover and net-of-borrow Sharpe before trusting the 0.55 prior in the allocator (the allocator currently seeds priorSharpe from results.md, which has no entry for this sleeve). Capacity itself needs no mitigation — at retail size all names absorb the flow with negligible impact; the binding frictions are borrow and resize-turnover, not capacity.

redteam.kill: Implemented the exact spec and ran it through the repo's own no-look-ahead engine + conservative cost model over 2015-2026. Measured after-cost Sharpe = 0.20 vs the claimed 0.55. Critically, this is NOT a cost problem: the PRE-cost gross Sharpe is only 0.25 (costs shave it to 0.20), so the claim is ~2.5x the actual signal, not a net-of-cost rounding error. Net-of-SPY-beta residual Sharpe is 0.10 (beta 0.054, corr 0.10 — orthogonality is the only honest part). CAGR is 1.41%. The strategy also trips its own falsifier (d): measured turnover is 633%/yr, above the spec's 600% kill line. The crisis-alpha story is single-window hindsight: 2022 (+11.6%) carries the entire pitch while 2016/17/18/20 are all negative and 2023 is -13%; 2018Q4 grind was -4% (spec claimed flat-to-positive) and the COVID crash leg was -9%. TSMOM on 11 liquid ETFs post-2015 simply does not reproduce the Moskowitz-Ooi-Pedersen result (58 futures, 1985-2009; documented post-publication decay). Look-ahead is genuinely absent (engine truncates context to <=i, momentumSkip skips last 21 bars) — but a clean, beta-free, look-ahead-free signal worth only 0.10-0.20 Sharpe is not investable, and the existing long-only cross_asset_trend sibling already delivers 0.57, making this L/S variant strictly worse.
redteam.fix: To rescue any version: (1) drop to the long-only sibling (cross_asset_trend) which already measures Sharpe 0.57 in summary.json — the shorts add borrow + turnover for negative marginal Sharpe; (2) cut turnover below the cost wall by lengthening cadence/widening the SMA band BEFORE backtesting (not via a post-hoc adaptive guard that is itself in-sample tuning); (3) remove the m-floor of 0.3 in the feedback loop — a never-zero allocation to a 0.10-residual-Sharpe sleeve is an unfalsifiable escape hatch, set floor to 0; (4) re-state the honest deliverable as a beta-diversifier with ~0.15-0.25 standalone Sharpe whose only value is correlation, not standalone return. As a return-generating sleeve it should be rejected.

redteam.lens: ORTHOGONALITY & TAIL — is the return stream truly uncorrelated to a long-only/short-vol (put-selling) book in the LEFT TAIL of crises, not just full-sample? Hidden beta / shared short-gamma; Mar2020 / 2022 / Aug2024 offset-vs-add test.
redteam.kill: Empirically reconstructing the sleeve's actual signed positions from the repo's own cached price data (freezing the slow 12-1 + SMA200 monthly signal at each pre-crisis date, then holding through the crash leg) shows the orthogonality is a FULL-SAMPLE artifact that evaporates exactly in the left tail. Full-sample daily corr to SPY = -0.004 and left-tail (SPY<-1%) corr = -0.037 — matching the spec's headline -0.05 — BUT inside the actual crash windows the correlation flips strongly POSITIVE: COVID Mar2020 tail-corr = +0.79 (sleeve -13.6% while SPY -33.7%), Aug2024 tail-corr = +0.83 (sleeve -2.8%), 2018Q4 +0.47 (sleeve -11.1%). The reason is hidden risk-on beta / shared short-gamma: in the bull run preceding a V-crash, the 12-1 momentum signal loads the sleeve LONG the risk-on carry assets in the universe (EEM, EFA, VNQ, SLV, GLD), which liquidate WITH equities in a dash-for-cash. So on the short-gamma blow-up days the put-book bleeds, this sleeve ADDS ~-13.6% (COVID) / -2.8% (Aug2024) rather than offsetting. It only offset in the slow 2022 grind (+7.5% to +9.9%, tail-corr +0.20). 2018Q4 actively missed a regime the spec names as bought-for: it was SHORT TLT/IEF/GLD and LONG DBC/USO into a yields-down/oil-collapse quarter (-11.1%), nearly tripping falsifier (c). Worse, two of the spec's OWN falsifiers fire on the repo data: net-of-cost Sharpe ~0.21 (pre-cost only 0.27 on 2016+ liquid ETFs, vs claimed 0.55 — near falsifier (b)'s <0.2), and realized annual turnover ~622% > the 600% kill threshold in falsifier (d), because the dual SMA200+sign-flip vol-scaled book whipsaws far more than the 'slow signal' claim assumes.
redteam.fix: Do not buy this as a left-tail offset; it is a slow-grind diversifier only, and the book ALREADY has the fast-crisis piece (tailHedge: VIX-backwardation-gated long TLT/GLD that fires in 3 days, exactly the Mar2020/Aug2024 regime this sleeve misses). To make it genuinely tail-safe and survive the lens: (1) gate the risk-on long legs (EEM/EFA/VNQ/SLV) OFF when VIX term structure is in backwardation or VIX>=35 — flatten or invert them into the flight-to-safety basket the tailHedge already targets — so the sleeve cannot be long carry into a crash; (2) restrict the LONG side to genuine safe-havens (TLT/IEF/GLD/UUP) and keep risk-on assets short-or-flat only, which is what actually delivered the orthogonality in 2022; (3) re-estimate net Sharpe and turnover on the repo's real data and bring the prior in line with ~0.2 net, not 0.55, and widen the SMA confirmation band materially to get turnover under 600%. With those changes it stops being redundant with tailHedge's job and stops co-falling on the put-book's worst days; without them, the adaptive allocator's |corr-to-core|<0.4 guard will not even detect the problem because the full-sample/126d-rolling correlation stays near zero precisely while the crisis-day damage is being done.

redteam.kill: Two concrete defects plus a crowding problem sink confidence. (1) DATA WINDOW: the falsifier demands a "2013-2024, 11y" backtest, but the data loader (scripts/backtest/data.ts L27-28: period1 = now - 11*365.25d) and the live cache prove all 11 cross-asset ETFs + SPY start 2015-06-02, not 2013. The post-publication-decay test (falsifier b) therefore runs only on ~2015-2026, dropping the 2013-2015 commodity-crash window where TSMOM looked good shorting oil/commodities, biasing the OOS evidence. (2) FEEDBACK LOOP IS VAPORWARE: the spec's described daily mechanism (per-sleeve multiplier m with +0.05/-0.10 daily ratchets, corr-to-core gate at 0.2/0.4 for 'orthogonality breakdown', shrink-to-prior floor 0.3, turnover guard that widens the SMA band) does NOT exist. The real allocator.ts/meta.ts do shrinkage-to-prior + regime tilt + 1/vol on a 126d rolling Sharpe — NO correlation gate, NO daily ratchet, NO turnover guard. So 'no engine change needed' is false, and the sleeve's primary risk control against the crowding it admits to is unimplemented. (3) CROWDING/DECAY: cross-asset TSMOM on exactly these liquid ETFs is the most-published, most-arbitraged factor; the codebase's own prior for cross_asset_trend is only 0.57 (a same-sample backtest artifact, not independent OOS). (4) BLOW-UP: inverse-vol sizing maxes weight on lowest-vol assets (TLT/IEF/SHY); a 60d vol estimate understates forward bond vol, so the sleeve can sit max-long TLT into the 2022 duration crash for up to 21 days before the monthly sign flips. Skeptically, net-of-beta after-cost Sharpe on a 2015-start liquid-ETF sample is more like 0.2-0.4, well under the claimed 0.55.
redteam.fix: Data and compute ARE feasible and mostly truthful, so this is fixable, not dead: (a) Honestly relabel the falsifier window to ~2015-2026 (the true cached span) and re-run; every named crisis test (2018Q4, 2022, Mar-2020, Aug-2024) and the orthogonality test ARE inside this window, so falsifiers a and c remain valid. (b) Actually implement the described feedback loop — add the corr-to-core gate, the daily m ratchet, and the turnover guard to allocator.ts (or drop those claims and rely on the existing 126d-Sharpe + regime tilt the code really has). (c) Stress the bond leg: backtest 2022 specifically and confirm the sleeve is not max-long TLT into the duration crash; consider a faster (e.g. 20-40d) vol estimate or a hard duration cap so inverse-vol does not over-concentrate in Treasuries. (d) Report net-of-SPY-beta Sharpe, not raw, and treat the 0.57 prior as in-sample — require a positive result on a true holdout before sizing up.

scored.name: Long-Only Factor Rotation (FAC-ROTATE) — diversifier, NOT a Sharpe-lifter
scored.family: Factor-ETF momentum rotation (requires adding factor ETFs)

scores.name: Long-Only Factor Rotation (FAC-ROTATE)
scores.requiredFixes: None rescue it as a funded sleeve. (1) Net-of-beta Sharpe is ~0.1-0.15, not 0.85; measured IR-vs-SPY is NEGATIVE (-0.37 to -0.48 full sample), failing the spec's OWN kill gate (IR<0.15) BEFORE costs. (2) ~90% redundant with the already-registered factor_momentum sleeve (factormom.ts: top-2, same FACTOR_ETFS universe, same totalReturn(126) 6m signal, same SPY<200d cash overlay; measured Sharpe 0.90, rho-to-SPY 0.66). Adding factor_rotate_long (top-3) inflates sleeve count with collinear content. (3) The spec's central safeguard — 'cut toward zero if 126d info-ratio-vs-SPY<0.1' — is UNBUILT: StrategyPerfStat (allocator.ts:29-42) carries only absolute trailingSharpe/Vol/priorSharpe, no benchmark-relative metric. (4) family:'rotation' lands in DEFENSIVE_FAMILIES (allocator.ts:102), so this long-equity beta sleeve is tilted UP x1.2 in risk-off (defensiveTiltRiskOff, line 168) — opposite of the spec's 'offensive, tilt DOWN' intent, amplifying the shared left tail in Mar2020/2022. (5) rho-to-equity 0.66-0.92 with zero offsetting long-vol/Treasury/gold/beta-neutral leg ⇒ pure additive market exposure on a short-gamma put book; Aug2024-type spikes (SPY never breaks 200d) get zero offset.
