Honest action: keep ONLY as the research falsifier/control for the beta-neutralized long/short spread (spec 1); do NOT register as an allocatable sleeve.
scores.oneLineRationale: Three Fatal red-team verdicts (Costs, Orthogonality/Tail, Regime/Data) plus admitted rho-0.66-0.92 equity beta force CUT: net-of-beta Sharpe ~0.1, negative IR-vs-SPY fails its own gate before costs, it is a near-duplicate of the existing factor_momentum sleeve, and it adds to (never offsets) the put-book left tail.

spec.name: Long-Only Factor Rotation (FAC-ROTATE) — diversifier, NOT a Sharpe-lifter
spec.family: Factor-ETF momentum rotation (requires adding factor ETFs)
spec.mechanism: Same factor-momentum autocorrelation, but harvested long-only: hold the 2-3 factor ETFs with the strongest own time-series momentum, de-grossing to cash below SPY's 200d. Honest framing per the brief's own root-cause analysis: because every factor ETF is long-only US equity (rho-to-SPY 0.85-0.97 — even HIGHER than the prior sleeves), this CANNOT raise portfolio Sharpe. Its only legitimate role is as a low-turnover, slightly-better-than-SPY equity expression that the meta-allocator can tilt, and as the control leg that proves the neutralized spread above is doing real work.
spec.exactSignal: Universe MTUM, QUAL, USMV, VLUE, IWF, IWD, SIZE. Score each by 6-month total return totalReturn(closes,126) (shorter than 12m: factor momentum decays fast and 6m had marginally better live behavior in the crowding literature). Rank, hold top-3 equal-weight. Absolute-momentum overlay: if regime.spyAbove200===false, go to cash.
spec.entryRule: Every 21 trading days: hold top-3 factors by 6m total return, equal weight 0.98/3 each (small cash buffer), ONLY when SPY>200d SMA. When SPY<200d, hold cash (the documented momentum-crash defense, same as the existing xs_momentum/sector_rotation sleeves).
spec.exitRule: Target-weight refresh each rebalance; engine trades deltas only, so when the top-3 set is stable turnover is near-zero. Full exit to cash whenever regime.spyAbove200===false. No intra-cycle stop.
spec.instruments: MTUM/QUAL/USMV/VLUE/IWF/IWD/SIZE, long only. instrument:'etf', longOnly:true.
spec.positionSizing: Fixed capital, gross ~0.98 when risk-on, 0 when risk-off. Equal-weight top-3. No leverage; gross well under 1.5. Meta vol-target applies at the portfolio level.
spec.newDataNeeded: Same FACTOR_ETFS addition to universe.ts (MTUM/QUAL/USMV/VLUE/IWF/IWD/SIZE) — all free, 10y Yahoo daily. Shared with spec 1.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: Tracked metric: trailing 63d realized Sharpe vs SPY's trailing 63d Sharpe (relative, since this sleeve IS beta). Rule into allocator.ts: weight driven by trailingSharpe shrunk to priorSharpe~0.6, risk-balanced by 1/vol like every other sleeve; treated as an OFFENSIVE/equity family so it gets tilted DOWN in risk-off (defensiveTiltRiskOff path). Critical honest gate: if its trailing 126d info ratio vs SPY (excess-return/excess-vol) is < 0.1 — i.e. it is not beating plain SPY net of its turnover — cut allocation toward zero, because a correlated sleeve that doesn't even out-Sharpe SPY adds nothing.
spec.crisisBehavior: Down WITH the market — it is long equity. 2018Q4: down, partly cushioned if USMV/QUAL rank top-3 (low-vol/quality lead late-cycle). Mar2020: down hard, but the SPY<200d rule exits to cash in late Feb/early Mar, capping the drawdown. 2022: down with stocks early; rotates into VLUE/IWD/USMV as value/low-vol win, so loses less than SPY by mid-year. Aug2024: small dip with market, recovers. This is explicitly NOT crisis alpha — it shares the book's left tail.
spec.implementationNotes: Strategy in same factorMomentum.ts, key 'factor_rotate_long', family 'rotation' (so the allocator treats it as defensive-ish but it's really beta), longOnly:true, rebalanceDays:21, warmupBars:160, instrument:'etf'. generate(): if ctx.regime.spyAbove200===false return cash; score FACTOR_ETFS by totalReturn(ctx.closes(f),126); sort desc; top3 equal weight 0.98/3; confidence ~0.5. Register in registry.ts. Use it primarily as the FALSIFIER/control for spec 1 in research, not as a Sharpe source.
spec.falsifier: Walk-forward 11y. This sleeve is EXPECTED to be cost-killed as a Sharpe-lifter and that is the point: REFUTED-as-useful if its standalone after-cost Sharpe is not materially above SPY's ~0.84 AND its info-ratio-vs-SPY < 0.15 (then it is pure beta and should be dropped in favor of spec 1). CONVERSELY, if adding it to the meta lifts blended portfolio Sharpe by >0.05 over the existing 5-sleeve meta, that would (surprisingly) refute my claim that long-only factor rotation can't help — worth checking, but I expect it cannot, consistent with the brief's root-cause finding.
spec._family: Factor-ETF momentum rotation (requires adding factor ETFs)

redteam.kill: Ran the exact spec through the repo's real engine + conservative cost model on 11y cached data (2015-2026, $100k). The cost attack inverts: costs do NOT kill this sleeve because there is no gross edge to eat. Gross (zero-cost) Sharpe is 0.79 / CAGR 10.92%; net at default 10bps RT it is 0.77 / 10.65%; even at a punitive 50bps RT it is 0.70. Cost drag is tiny (~0.02 Sharpe, ~41 bps/yr, $4,476 total over 11y) because the 520%/yr nominal turnover is mostly the cash-on/off 200d toggle plus a stable top-3 set, and the engine's 0.2%-equity dust filter suppresses churn. The fatal finding is that the GROSS edge is negative versus the only relevant benchmark: SPY does Sharpe 0.84 / CAGR 14.23%, so FAC-ROTATE loses ~3.3% CAGR gross and ~3.6% net. The full-sample information ratio vs SPY is -0.479 (excess -5.7%/yr, tracking error 11.8%), far below the spec's OWN kill gates (cut toward zero if 126d IR<0.1; falsifier IR<0.15). Capacity/granularity/borrow/options-spread are all non-binding (penny-spread mega-ETFs, long-only, fractional-share-friendly at $100k) and therefore cannot rescue it. Net of beta this is a strictly-worse SPY with rho 0.75, so it neither out-Sharpes the benchmark nor diversifies it. Round-trip cost is not the killer; the absence of any positive gross edge is.
redteam.fix: Wire and enforce the spec's own IR-vs-SPY<0.1 hard gate in allocator.ts so this sleeve auto-starves to zero standing capital; retain it ONLY as the research falsifier/control for the market-neutral spread (spec 1), never as a funded return stream. If factor-momentum Sharpe is wanted, harvest it via the beta-neutralized long/short spread, not this long-only leg.

redteam.kill: The spec's own thesis is that this sleeve is beta in disguise, and the repo's measured data confirm it. expectedAfterCostSharpe is 0.85 — statistically indistinguishable from SPY's backtested 0.84 (research/results.md line 21). The near-identical predecessor already in the codebase (factormom.ts 'factor_momentum', top-2, same FACTOR_ETFS universe, same 6m totalReturn(126), same SPY<200d cash overlay) was actually walk-forwarded 11y and scored Sharpe 0.90 at rho-to-SPY 0.66 (research/backtests/summary.json L8842-8862). So this is NOT a 'real' Sharpe edge: net of beta it is near-zero. Decomposing the measured twin: beta ~= 0.66 x 14.0/17.8 ~= 0.52, beta-implied return ~7.4%/yr, residual alpha ~5%/yr over residual vol 14.0 x sqrt(1-0.66^2)=10.5% -> in-sample net-of-beta IR ~0.45 — but that is bull-market-inflated and pre-decay. The repo's own discovery-ranked.json kill note documents a clean ~50% post-publication/crowding haircut on factor momentum (predicted 0.30 alpha 2016-2024, realized 0.15, tracking factor-ETF AUM growth), plus the 4-ETF cross-section degenerates to a growth/value + cyclical/defensive beta-tilt, not a 65-factor harvest. Haircutting for decay + a bull-heavy 2015-2026 sample + the bench measured long-only rotation analog (sector_rotation Sharpe 0.66, BELOW SPY) drives the forward net-of-beta Sharpe toward ~0.1-0.2 — right at the spec's own 'drop it' gate (IR vs SPY < 0.15). The meta evidence seals it: the full meta-allocator already caps at Sharpe 0.84-0.85 (meta-summary.json) because every sleeve is long-only equity; adding one more rho-0.66 equity sleeve cannot lift blended Sharpe by the >0.05 the falsifier demands. Two internal contradictions weaken the spec further: it asserts rho 0.85-0.97 ('even HIGHER than prior sleeves') but the measured factor-momentum rho is 0.66, and it claims 0.85 while the measured twin is 0.90 — the numbers are asserted, not from a backtest of THIS variant. On look-ahead: the signal itself is clean (totalReturn over trailing 126 bars and spyAbove200 over trailing 200 use only data <= i per engine.ts makeContext); the only optimism is same-close execution, which engine.ts L9-14 acknowledges 'slightly flatters fills' but is uniform across all sleeves and the SPY benchmark, so it does not manufacture relative Sharpe.
redteam.fix: Nothing can make this a Sharpe-lifter — it is structurally long-only equity beta and the spec admits it. The only honest 'mitigation' is to use it exactly as the spec ultimately proposes: as the FALSIFIER/control leg for the beta-neutralized long-short spec (spec 1), never as a Sharpe source. Concretely: (1) Replace the asserted 0.85/rho-0.97 numbers with the repo's MEASURED twin values (Sharpe 0.90, rho 0.66) or actually backtest the top-3 variant before quoting any number — do not ship asserted Sharpes. (2) Enforce the spec's own gate as a hard precondition: only admit it to the meta if its trailing-126d info-ratio-vs-SPY clears 0.15 net of turnover; the historical evidence says it will not, so default allocation should be ~0. (3) Drop the duplicate entirely — factor_momentum already exists; registering factor_rotate_long adds a near-collinear second copy that inflates apparent sleeve count without adding orthogonal content. If kept at all, keep ONE factor sleeve. (4) For real net-of-beta Sharpe, pursue the dollar/beta-neutral long-short factor spread (the spec's spec-1 sibling), which this leg is meant to validate, not replace.

redteam.lens: Orthogonality & Tail — is the return stream genuinely uncorrelated to a long-only / short-vol (cash-secured-put) book in the LEFT TAIL (crises), not just full-sample, and would it have OFFSET or ADDED TO the put-book drawdown in Mar2020 / 2022 / Aug2024?
redteam.kill: Through the orthogonality-and-tail lens this sleeve is fatally non-orthogonal — and the spec admits it (rho-to-SPY 0.92, "HIGHER than the prior sleeves," "shares the book's left tail," "explicitly NOT crisis alpha"). The core book is a cash-secured PUT-SELLING book: structurally short-vol / short-gamma on US large-cap equity with a negative-skew left tail that IS an equity crash (brief.md L19). Every instrument here (MTUM/QUAL/USMV/VLUE/IWF/IWD/SIZE) is 100% long US equity beta, so in the left tail this is pure additive market exposure stacked on the put book, not a diversifier. The repo's own root-cause finding (results.md L73-77) already proves it: "every sleeve is long-only equity... diversifying among correlated streams compresses vol but cannot raise Sharpe"; this is the MOST correlated sleeve yet proposed. strategy-universe.md L41 names the exact trap ("Tail orthogonality != mean orthogonality") and lists the ONLY tail-orthogonal streams as cross-asset trend, VIX-term-structure tail hedge, and vega-neutral slope — none of which this is. Crisis-by-crisis it ADDS to the put-book drawdown: (1) Mar2020 — the only defense is the shared spyAbove200 flag, which trips only ~Feb 27 2020 after the crash is underway, and the 21-day rebalance can lag the exit by up to a month; worse, ALL sleeves and the put book de-gross off the SAME flag simultaneously, concentrating crash risk rather than diversifying it. (2) Aug2024 — a ~3-day vol spike where SPY never broke its 200d, so the cash overlay never fires; the sleeve eats the full equity hit alongside the put book (spec admits "small dip with market") — zero offset, pure short-gamma co-move. (3) 2022 — "loses less than SPY" is a relative-beta claim, not absolute crisis alpha; the book still bleeds, and factor momentum crashes hardest at the sharp reversals that co-occur with put-book stress (the momentum-crash risk the spec's own 6m-over-12m lookback choice AMPLIFIES). MTUM/QUAL are themselves crowded longs that gap down in the deleveraging cascades that blow out put books — shared short-gamma, not hidden, but stated and unmitigated. It contains no offsetting (long-vol, long-Treasury, long-gold, or beta-neutral) leg, so there is no mechanism by which it could cushion the put book's left tail.
redteam.fix: There is no mitigation that preserves this as an orthogonal/tail diversifier — the defect is the family, not the parameters. If kept at all, it can ONLY live as what the spec already concedes: a low-turnover, slightly-better-than-SPY beta expression and the falsifier/control leg for the neutralized spread (spec 1), NOT as crisis ballast. To actually offset the put-book left tail you must change the instrument set or strip the beta: (a) run the SPY-neutralized factor-momentum SPREAD (long top factors / short SPY or short bottom factors, dollar-and-beta-neutral) so the residual is the only thing that survives — but strategy-universe.md L41 warns even that carries a shared short-gamma left tail and must be tail-checked, not assumed neutral; (b) pair any deployment with the genuinely tail-orthogonal sleeves the repo already surfaced (cross_asset_trend on TLT/IEF/GLD/DBC, tail_hedge VIX-term-structure de-gross to TLT+GLD) which are negatively correlated to equities precisely in crashes; (c) at the allocator, hard-gate via the spec's own info-ratio-vs-SPY < 0.1 cut AND classify it OFFENSIVE so crisisGross (0.6 at VIX>=35) and offensiveTiltRiskOff cut it — but note even this does NOT make it offset Aug2024-type spikes where the 200d never breaks. Net: usable only as a benchmark/control, never as a left-tail diversifier.

redteam.kill: Data/compute is feasible (the one thing the spec gets right), but the edge fails its own honest gate empirically. I ran the exact spec signal (top-3 of MTUM/QUAL/USMV/VLUE/SIZE/IWF/IWD by 6m totalReturn, eq-wt 0.98/3, cash when SPY<200d) on the actual cached free/daily Yahoo data (2015-06-02->2026-06-01, ~11y). GROSS of costs it returns Sharpe 0.80 vs SPY 0.91, info-ratio-vs-SPY = -0.37, excess CAGR -4.1%/yr, daily rho-to-SPY 0.79. The spec's own falsifier says REFUTED-as-useful if after-cost Sharpe is not materially above SPY's ~0.84 AND IR-vs-SPY<0.15 -- both fail BEFORE costs (0.80<0.84; -0.37 IR). The claimed 0.85 after-cost Sharpe and positive diversifier role are not reproduced; the sleeve under-Sharpes plain SPY pre-cost, so its only honest role (better-than-SPY equity expression) does not exist on this data. REGIME/crowding compound it: median best-minus-worst 6m factor spread is only 12% (ranking is largely noise), and the top-3 set flips on 54/104 = 52% of risk-on rebalances -- so turnover is NOT 'near-zero' as claimed; costs eat a negative gross edge. Regime coverage is thin: only ~11y with a single sustained bear (2022) plus a V-shaped COVID and 2018Q4 (18 risk-off rebalances), too few independent bear regimes to validate the SPY<200d crash defense the whole thesis leans on. Two implementation claims are also fiction: (1) the 'cut allocation toward zero if 126d info-ratio-vs-SPY<0.1' gate does NOT exist in allocator.ts -- StrategyPerfStat carries no benchmark-relative metric, only absolute shrunk Sharpe -- so the central honest safeguard is unbuilt; (2) family:'rotation' lands in DEFENSIVE_FAMILIES (allocator.ts:102), so this long-equity beta sleeve gets tilted UP x1.2 in risk-off, the exact OPPOSITE of the spec's stated 'tilted DOWN as offensive' intent, amplifying the shared left tail precisely when it bites. Finally it is ~90% redundant: factormom.ts/factor_momentum (top-2, same universe, same 6m signal, same cash rule) is already registered in registry.ts.
redteam.fix: If kept at all, demote to a pure research CONTROL leg (as the spec half-admits) and do NOT register it as an allocatable sleeve -- it adds correlated beta with no Sharpe. To make the framing even defensible would require: (a) actually building the info-ratio-vs-SPY metric into StrategyPerfStat + a hard zero-gate in the allocator (currently absent); (b) reclassifying it into an OFFENSIVE family (momentum) so risk-off tilts it DOWN as intended, not UP; (c) collapsing it into the existing factor_momentum sleeve rather than adding a second near-identical strategy; and (d) re-validating across a longer/out-of-sample regime set, since 11y with one real bear cannot confirm the SPY<200d defense. None of these rescue the measured negative gross IR, so the honest action is to drop it in favor of a genuinely beta-neutral spread (spec 1).

scored.name: ResMom-LS: Dollar-neutral residual (idiosyncratic) momentum, tail-gated

scores.name: ResMom-LS: Dollar-neutral residual (idiosyncratic) momentum, tail-gated
scores.requiredFixes: Two Fatal findings make this an automatic CUT under the rubric (a Fatal => CUT). (1) COSTS/CAPACITY Fatal and (2) SHARPE-REALITY Fatal both replicated the EXACT spec on the cached 11y / 74-name data with the engine's own conservative costs.ts (verified: 10bps round-trip + 50bps borrow) and found the gross net-of-SPY-beta edge is ~0 to +1%/yr (gross net-of-beta Sharpe 0.00-0.18), while turnover of ~1670-2087%/yr (holding the 7 decile-EXTREMES that churn ~87%/cycle, vs the existing 15-name quintile xs_momentum) drags 1.7-1.9%/yr -- costs EXCEED the entire gross edge, so after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe goes NEGATIVE (-0.15 to -0.05), failing the spec's own falsifier (<0.35). The root cause is structural and unfixable in this universe: residual momentum needs a 500-1000+ name cross-section for idiosyncratic dispersion (Blitz/Huij/Martens); on 74 mega-caps, SPY-residualizing strips the dispersion and leaves a 7-vs-7 noise spread (beta-neutral forward-21d quintiles are non-monotonic, top-minus-bottom = -0.0%). If anyone wanted to revisit (not bookable as-is): widen the universe to 500+ names (needs data NOT in the manifest -- materially changes the spec), add decile-exit hysteresis + quarterly rebalance to cut turnover ~5-8x, and 2-3 factor residualization (MTUM+SPY). Even then realistic ceiling is ~0.1-0.2, not the claimed 0.6. Separately, the entire advertised defense is vaporware in code: metrics.ts computes only RAW Sharpe (no net-of-beta), meta.ts PerfProvider exposes only {sharpe,vol,n,returns} (no per-sleeve SPY-beta / downside-capture / 6% equity-stop seam), meta blend drops share<=0 and vol-targets only positive weights (cannot host a dollar-neutral sleeve), and allocator.ts has NO cross-sleeve covariance penalty -- so nothing stops the short-loser leg STACKING short-gamma on the put_strike core in a bear-rebound (Mar-2020/Aug-2024), which is the exact tail the book must avoid.
scores.oneLineRationale: Genuinely orthogonal in mean and trivially buildable on existing data, but two Fatal replications show the 74-name universe is too small for residual-momentum dispersion: ~0 gross edge eaten by ~1700%/yr turnover to a negative after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe, while every advertised guardrail (net-of-beta metric, beta/downside-capture feedback, dollar-neutral meta hosting, cross-sleeve tail penalty) is absent from the code -- automatic CUT.

spec.name: ResMom-LS: Dollar-neutral residual (idiosyncratic) momentum, tail-gated
spec.mechanism: Behavioral underreaction to FIRM-SPECIFIC news, isolated from market/sector beta. Standard 12-1 momentum bundles a stock's idiosyncratic drift with a time-varying market-beta bet; that beta bet is what causes momentum crashes in bear-market rebounds (the loser leg becomes high-beta and rips on the bounce). By ranking on the momentum of SPY-beta-RESIDUALIZED returns (e[t]=r_stock[t]-beta*r_SPY[t]), we keep only the idiosyncratic underreaction premium and discard the beta bet. Academic evidence (Blitz/Huij/Martens; van Vliet) shows this halves vol, removes long-term reversal, and survives factor controls — i.e. it is a structurally different, lower-beta return stream than the existing xs_momentum sleeve, which is exactly the orthogonality the book lacks.
spec.exactSignal: For each equity in EQUITY_UNIVERSE with >=300d history: (1) compute daily simpleReturns of the stock and of SPY over a trailing 252d estimation window ENDING 21 trading days ago (skip-month). (2) b = beta(stockRets, spyRets) using the existing indicators.beta(); a = mean(stockRets)-b*mean(spyRets). (3) residual series e[t]=stockRet[t]-(a+b*spyRet[t]). (4) RESIDUAL MOMENTUM SCORE = sum(e over the 252-to-21d window) / stdev(e over that window)  (vol-scaled cumulative residual return, the standard idiosyncratic-momentum definition). Rank all names by this score. Long top decile (~7 names), short bottom decile (~7 names). TAIL GATE: also compute, per name over the same window, downside residual semideviation; if a name's |beta|>1.6 OR its residual is dominated by one >|8 sigma| day (earnings gap), exclude it from the SHORT leg only (never short a high-gamma blow-up candidate).
spec.entryRule: Dollar-neutral: each long name +w, each short name -w, with sum(longs)=+0.5 and sum(shorts)=-0.5 of capital pre-vol-scaling (gross 1.0 before the meta vol-target/leverage cap). Only enter when SPY 21d realized vol < its own 252d 80th percentile (the short-gamma trap opens in high-vol regimes); in CRISIS regime or VIX>=35, take the SHORT leg to ZERO and run long-residual-top only (de-gross the dangerous leg). Rebalance every 21 trading days.
spec.exitRule: Positions are fully refreshed each 21d rebalance to the new top/bottom decile. Intra-cycle hard stop: if the sleeve's own marked equity drops >6% from its last rebalance mark, flatten the short leg immediately (covers the short-gamma gap) and hold longs to next rebalance. A name leaving its decile is closed at the next rebalance, not intrabar.
spec.instruments: Long/short single-name equities from EQUITY_UNIVERSE (74 large caps). SPY used only to estimate beta (regime-only, not traded here). No options.
spec.positionSizing: Equal-weight within each leg, dollar-neutral (longs=+0.5, shorts=-0.5 → gross 1.0). Then sleeve-level vol target: scale the whole sleeve so its trailing 60d realized vol annualizes to 8%; cap sleeve gross at 1.0 (meta enforces total book gross<=1.5). Short leg capped at 0.5 gross and disabled in crisis as above. Borrow charged by engine at 50bps/yr on shorts.
spec.newDataNeeded: None — SPY and all 74 equities already in the manifest; beta/correlation/simpleReturns/stdev already in indicators.ts. (Optional future upgrade: residualize on MTUM+SPY for a true 2-factor residual, requires adding MTUM ETF — not required for v1.)
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: METRIC tracked daily on the live sim book: the sleeve's trailing-60d realized SPY-beta (rolling beta of sleeve daily P&L on SPY daily return) AND its trailing-60d downside capture vs SPY on SPY-down days. RULE: (a) the meta allocator already scales this sleeve by its shrunk trailing-60d Sharpe; ON TOP of that, if rolling sleeve-beta drifts outside [-0.15,+0.15] (residualization breaking down → it's secretly a beta bet) the allocator multiplies this sleeve's weight by 0.5; if |beta|>0.30 for 5 consecutive days, bench it (weight 0) until beta re-enters the band. (b) If downside-capture-on-SPY-down-days exceeds 0.5 (the short-gamma tail is materializing — sleeve falling WITH the market) cut sleeve weight by half that day. This makes the daily loop police TAIL-orthogonality, not just mean-orthogonality.
spec.crisisBehavior: Designed to be roughly FLAT, not a crisis hedge. In 2018Q4 and 2022 (orderly factor-driven selloffs) residual momentum historically held up better than price momentum and should be flat-to-slightly-positive. DANGER ZONES are sharp reversals: Mar-2020 and Aug-2024 VIX spikes can hit the short leg (short-gamma left tail) — which is exactly why the regime gate zeros the short leg in CRISIS/VIX>=35 and the 6% sleeve stop + beta/downside-capture feedback loop exist. Honest expectation: small negative in a violent multi-day reversal, flat otherwise, NOT positive crisis alpha (that's the cross-asset-trend sleeve's job).
spec.implementationNotes: New Strategy in src/lib/strategies/residual_momentum.ts, longOnly:false, instrument:'equity', rebalanceDays:21, warmupBars:300. In generate(ctx): get spyRets=simpleReturns(ctx.closes('SPY')) sliced to [t-252-21, t-21]; for each isEquity name compute beta() and residual series with existing helpers; score=sum(resid)/stdev(resid); sort; slice top/bottom decile; emit +w/-w TargetWeights (engine clamps nothing since longOnly:false). Read ctx.regime.regime/vix for the crisis short-leg kill and ctx.regime via SPY 21d vs 252d vol for the high-vol entry gate (compute SPY vol from ctx.closes('SPY') with annualizedVol). Register in registry.ts; add prior Sharpe ~0.6 to the meta priors map. The 6% intra-cycle stop and rolling-beta feedback live in the meta/live-runner layer using the sleeve's realized equity (same PerfProvider seam meta.ts already uses).
spec.falsifier: Run the 11y walk-forward. REFUTED if any of: (1) after-cost annualized Sharpe < 0.35 net of SPY beta; (2) trailing-60d rolling sleeve-beta to SPY exceeds |0.30| more than 20% of days (residualization isn't delivering neutrality in OUR universe); (3) the sleeve's worst 5-day drawdown coincides with (same week as) SPY's worst 5-day drawdowns with a fitted crisis-beta > +0.4 (the short-gamma tail dominates → fails the tail-orthogonality check and must be cut, regardless of full-sample correlation).

redteam.kill: I replicated this spec's exact signal (SPY-beta-residualized momentum, score=sum(resid)/std(resid) over a 252d window with 21d skip-month, long top decile ~7 / short bottom decile ~7, dollar-neutral gross 1.0, 21d rebalance, vol-gate + VIX>=35 short-kill + |beta|>1.6/>8sigma tail gate) over the same 74-name large-cap universe and 11y of cached data (2016-08..2026-06, ~9.8y) using the engine's OWN cost model (src/lib/backtest/costs.ts: 10bps round-trip spread+slippage, 50bps borrow). Result: the gross (pre-cost) net-of-SPY-beta annual return is only +1.07%/yr (gross net-of-beta Sharpe 0.18). The total cost drag is 1.86%/yr (spread/turnover 1.67% + borrow 0.19%) -- costs EXCEED the entire gross edge. After-cost net-of-beta Sharpe = -0.16 (NEGATIVE), versus the claimed 0.6 and the spec's own falsifier threshold of 0.35. The killer is turnover, not borrow: only 12-13% of decile names are retained cycle-to-cycle, so ~87% of the 14-name book is replaced every 21 days -> ~139% turnover per rebalance, ~1670%/yr. This is structural: ranking 74 large caps on a vol-scaled cumulative residual and holding the 7 extremes produces near-random tail membership churn. The gross edge is also unstable (Sharpe 0.73 in 2016-18, 1.54 in the cherry-picked 2022, but -0.34 since 2024; full-sample no-gate gross net-of-beta Sharpe 0.089). Capacity/granularity adds a second fatal flaw at retail size: at $10k capital, 14 names at ~7% each, whole-share rounding gives ~13% mean weight error (e.g. GOOGL target 1.66 shares -> 2 = 20% weight error), so the dollar-neutral construction and the [-0.15,+0.15] beta band the daily loop is supposed to police are physically unattainable below roughly $250k-$500k. Net: gross edge < round-trip cost at the spec's own (generous) cost model, sign flips negative, and the spec's primary falsifier (after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe < 0.35) fires.
redteam.fix: To have any chance the sleeve must (1) collapse turnover by a no-trade buffer band -- only rotate a name out of a decile when it crosses well past the boundary (e.g. rank moves outside the top/bottom 20%), and lengthen the rebalance to quarterly; modeled crudely this could cut turnover ~5-8x and bring spread drag toward ~0.3%/yr, but with a 74-name universe the residual gross edge (~1%/yr) is too thin to clear even that. (2) Widen the universe to 300-500 names so decile membership is stickier and per-name capacity rises -- but that needs data not in the manifest and changes the spec materially. (3) Run it long-only-residual-tilt (no short leg) to eliminate borrow, locate risk, and the short-gamma tail entirely -- but that discards the dollar-neutrality that is the sleeve's entire orthogonality thesis. None of these salvage a +0.6 after-cost Sharpe; realistic ceiling after a turnover fix is ~0.1-0.2 net-of-beta, not bookable. Recommend reject for the costed book.

redteam.kill: A faithful implementation of the exact spec on its own 11y cached data, 74-name EQUITY_UNIVERSE, indicators.beta()/simpleReturns definitions, and the conservative costs.ts model shows the edge does not exist. Dollar-neutral residual-momentum LS, GROSS (zero cost): net-of-beta Sharpe = 0.00, annual return -0.06%. After the spec's own costs (~2087%/yr turnover, 10bps round-trip + 50bps borrow): Sharpe -0.05, CAGR -0.40%. The long leg's apparent 0.91 Sharpe is entirely market beta (beta 0.50, net-of-beta Sharpe -0.00); the short leg mirrors it (beta -0.52); dollar-neutral combination cancels both the beta and the alpha. This is the exact 'beta in disguise' failure this lens hunts. A beta-neutral forward-21d quintile test confirms zero predictive power: Q1(top)=+4.0% ann, Q3=+5.5%, Q5(bottom)=+4.0%, top-minus-bottom spread = -0.0% before costs (non-monotonic, middle quintile highest). The Blitz/Huij/Martens prior does not transfer because residual momentum needs a broad 1000+ name cross-section for idiosyncratic dispersion; in a 74-name mega/large-cap basket, residualizing on SPY strips the dispersion and leaves a ~7-vs-7 noise spread. No look-ahead inflates this (engine has genuine point-in-time slicing) and the metrics module computes only RAW Sharpe, so the falsifier's 'net of SPY beta < 0.35' guardrail is not even implemented to catch it. Claimed 0.6 vs measured ~0.0 gross, negative net.
redteam.fix: Do not deploy v1 on this universe. To have any chance: (1) expand to a 500+ name S&P 500 cross-section so the idiosyncratic spread has real dispersion; (2) residualize on a 2-3 factor model (the spec's own MTUM+SPY 'future upgrade'), since 1-factor SPY residualization over-strips in a large-cap basket; (3) cut the ~2000%/yr turnover with a multi-day hold band / decile-exit hysteresis (the project's own results.md shows turnover annihilated the reversal sleeve from a real gross edge to 0.22 Sharpe). Before any allocation, REQUIRE a walk-forward that reports net-of-SPY-beta Sharpe (not implemented in metrics.ts today) and a quintile-monotonicity check; both must be positive. On current evidence this is a null sleeve whose only working component is genuine beta-neutrality (rolling |beta|>0.30 on just 3.5% of days), not alpha.

redteam.lens: ORTHOGONALITY & TAIL — left-tail (crisis) correlation to the short-put / long-only core, hidden/asymmetric beta, shared short-gamma
redteam.kill: The core product here is a short-put book (src/lib/black-scholes.ts prices puts; repo is "put_strike"), i.e. structurally short-gamma, short-vol, long-equity-beta. This sleeve's SHORT leg shorts bottom-decile (beaten-down) names — that is the SAME short-gamma exposure as the put core: in a violent crisis-rebound (Mar-24/26-2020 +9-11% loser-leg rips; the Aug-5-2024 VIX-to-65 intraday spike-and-reverse) the loser leg explodes against you, ADDING to the put-book drawdown rather than offsetting it. The claimed orthogonality (corr 0.1, rolling beta policed to [-0.15,+0.15]) is a FULL-SAMPLE / calm-window property: OLS beta from a 252d estimation window ending 21d ago is dominated by calm days, and residual-momentum/short-loser books have well-documented time-varying CRISIS beta far above their unconditional beta (Daniel-Moskowitz momentum-crash beta). So full-sample neutrality coexists with materially positive LEFT-TAIL beta — exactly what this lens flags. The protective machinery is lagging and pro-cyclical: the crisis kill (VIX>=35 / regime==CRISIS) and the trailing-60d beta/downside-capture feedback loop only fire AFTER the breakdown (the feedback needs |beta|>0.30 for 5 consecutive days), and the 6% intra-cycle stop is a same-day mark stop that fills BELOW the stop on a gap. Crucially, the book's allocator (allocateStrategies in allocator.ts) weights sleeves by inverse-vol x shrunk-Sharpe with only a regime tilt — it uses NO cross-sleeve correlation/covariance penalty, and the meta vol-target uses calm-window covariance — so nothing in book construction prevents this sleeve's short leg from STACKING short-gamma on top of the put core in the tail. The spec's own crisisBehavior concedes "small negative in a violent multi-day reversal" in Mar-2020/Aug-2024 — i.e. by its own admission it is additive (not orthogonal/offsetting) in precisely the put book's worst weeks. Under default-skepticism, a sleeve that is flat-to-slightly-negative in the exact crises where the short-put core bleeds does not survive a tail-orthogonality test.
redteam.fix: Make the orthogonality structural, not policed-after-the-fact. (1) Permanently run the short leg ONLY in confirmed-calm regimes and hard-zero it on ANY backwardation signal (ctx.regime.vixTermRatio>1, which fires faster than the 200d break per tailhedge.ts) AND on a 1-day SPY/VIX shock trigger, not just VIX>=35 — this catches the Aug-2024 one-day spike the 21d rebalance/daily regime read otherwise misses; accept that this makes the sleeve a long-only low-beta residual tilt in the tail (which is fine, but then size and prior must reflect long-only economics, not dollar-neutral). (2) Estimate the gate on DOWNSIDE/crisis beta (regress sleeve P&L on SPY using SPY-down days only) and pre-emptively de-gross when downside-beta > 0.2, instead of trailing-60d full-window beta that lags. (3) Add a cross-sleeve correlation penalty to allocateStrategies (it currently has none) so the meta explicitly down-weights this sleeve when its tail co-movement with the long-only/put core rises — measure with a crisis-conditional (worst-decile-SPY-days) covariance, not the calm meta vol-target covariance. (4) Validate against the spec's own falsifier #3 (crisis-beta>+0.4 in the same week as SPY's worst 5-day drawdowns) on the 11y walk-forward BEFORE allocation, and specifically reprice the Mar-2020 and Aug-2024 windows event-by-event; if the long-only-in-crisis version's after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe falls below ~0.35, the dollar-neutral edge was load-bearing on the very short leg that fails this lens, and the sleeve should be cut.

redteam.kill: DATA/COMPUTE is fully feasible: the 74-name EQUITY_UNIVERSE, SPY, and ^VIX/^VIX9D are all in ALL_BACKTEST_SYMBOLS; data is free+daily via Yahoo's public chart API (scripts/backtest/data.ts, adjusted closes, 11y cached); every indicator needed (simpleReturns, beta, stdev, mean, annualizedVol) exists in indicators.ts; the engine supports signed weights for longOnly:false (engine.ts L212-218), charges borrow at exactly 50bps/yr (costs.ts L27 + dailyBorrowCost), and caps gross. So v1 is buildable. The kill is REGIME + ARCHITECTURE, not raw data. (1) The strategy's ENTIRE advertised tail defense -- the 6% intra-cycle equity stop, the rolling-beta [-0.15,+0.15] band kill/bench, and the downside-capture-on-SPY-down-days cut -- does NOT exist in code. PerfProvider (meta.ts L27-30) returns only {sharpe,vol,n,returns}; there is no per-sleeve SPY-beta, downside-capture, or equity-stop seam. The meta blend also drops any sleeve with share<=0 and its vol-target/normalize math all assume long positive gross, so it cannot even host a dollar-neutral sleeve as written. The backtest therefore runs the naked single-name SHORT leg with none of the guardrails the spec relies on, and the falsifier's crisis-beta<+0.4 test is being asked of guardrails that aren't there. (2) REGIME/BLOW-UP: this is short-vol-in-disguise -- a single-name short basket of low-residual-momentum names that rips hardest in sharp bear-market REVERSALS (the momentum-crash left tail: 2009, Mar-2020, Aug-2024). Residualization shrinks but does not remove this (residual momentum still loaded negative in the 2009 rebound in the very Blitz/Huij/Martens evidence cited). VIX>=35 fires AFTER the gap; a 21d rebalance + EOD 6% stop cannot contain an intrabar single-name squeeze/buyout/fraud-rebound gap. (3) CROWDING/DECAY: idiosyncratic momentum is heavily published and institutionally crowded; a 74-name universe yields ~7-a-side baskets that are lumpy and earnings-path-dominated, so the 0.6 Sharpe (estimated on 1000+ name studies) is optimistic and unlikely to clear the 0.35 net-of-beta falsifier in OUR universe.
redteam.fix: Before any allocation: (a) implement the missing live-runner seam -- extend PerfProvider (or add a sibling provider) to expose trailing-60d sleeve-beta-to-SPY and downside-capture, and add a per-sleeve intra-cycle equity stop in the runner; without these the spec is not the spec. (b) Make meta.ts dollar-neutral-aware: allow negative net weights through the blend and exclude this sleeve's gross from the long-only vol-target/normalize logic, or run it as a standalone carve-out with its own gross budget. (c) Re-estimate the prior on OUR 74-name universe, not the academic broad-universe number -- enter the meta prior at ~0.25, not 0.6, until the 11y walk-forward proves otherwise. (d) Harden the short leg: hard pre-trade exclusions (no shorting names with pending earnings inside the 21d cycle, |beta|>1.6, recent >8-sigma gap -- already specced but must be enforced in code), and consider a residual-basket put-overlay or beta-floor rather than relying on a reactive VIX>=35 kill that fires after the gap. (e) Add the falsifier checks (rolling-beta>|0.30| frequency, crisis-beta) as automated gates the harness actually computes.

scored.name: Regime-Gated Turn-of-Month Flow (SPY, stress-only)

scores.name: Regime-Gated Turn-of-Month Flow (SPY, stress-only)
scores.requiredFixes: No parameter fix rescues this; the spec self-refutes. Independent reconstruction on the repo's cached SPY/^VIX/^VIX9D (2015-06 to 2026-06): (1) The spec's OWN falsifier fires decisively — stress-TOM vs non-TOM-stress Welch t=0.007 (needs >1.5; conditioning adds literally nothing), and stress-TOM vs calm-TOM t=-2.88 with the WRONG SIGN (stress-TOM -18bps/day vs calm-TOM +18bps), so the synchronized-stress-flow mechanism is empirically false. (2) Full vol-targeted sleeve Sharpe is NEGATIVE even at zero cost (-0.13 gross; net ann -0.76%/yr; in-market mean -3.6bps t=-0.42). (3) Orthogonality is a low-exposure illusion: full-sample corr 0.22 but IN-MARKET corr to SPY 0.95 with skew -1.25, losing -136 to -251 bps on SPY down-days while live — same-signed beta that ADDS to a put/short-vol book's tail exactly when the crisis gate turns it on. (4) Data does not support the thesis: cache starts 2015-06 (not the claimed 2014), the gate fires on 29% of days with 33% of firings in calm VIX<20 markets, and the "+20bps recession" leg rests on one COVID episode. The only "salvage" (drop the gate, drop the vol-target, fold unconditional TOM into an existing equity sleeve) is no longer this sleeve and still does not clear retail costs. Discard per the sleeve's own falsifier; the daily hibernation loop would correctly drive the allocation to 0.
scores.oneLineRationale: Fatal: the spec's own falsifier fires (stress-conditioning t=0.007, wrong-signed at t=-2.88), full vol-targeted Sharpe is negative even pre-cost, and in-market corr to SPY is 0.95 with crisis down-day losses — a non-existent alpha that is same-signed beta in disguise, not an orthogonal flow premium.

spec.name: Regime-Gated Turn-of-Month Flow (SPY, stress-only)
spec.mechanism: Synchronized institutional rebalancing flow at the calendar turn (pension/401k contributions, index-fund cash sweeps, mutual-fund window-dressing) creates a recurring bid into equities over the last trading day of the month through the first ~3 days of the next. The premium is broadly DECAYED on average (publicly known -> arbitraged), so we do NOT trade it unconditionally. The March-2026 literature shows the TOM-day mean survives and is statistically significant ONLY conditional on stress: +7.8bps after high-vol periods, +20bps in recessions, because that is when forced/synchronized rebalancing flow is largest and price-insensitive. We harvest the conditional, not the unconditional, premium.
spec.exactSignal: Calendar window: define TOM = the set of trading days {last 1 trading day of month} U {first 3 trading days of next month} (computed from the engine's master calendar, no look-ahead). Stress gate (any of): ctx.regime.vix >= its trailing 60d median (compute median of last 60 VIX closes the engine already supplies via regime.vix history is not in ctx, so proxy: regime classifies HIGH_VOL when VIX>=25) OR ctx.regime.regime in {HIGH_VOL,CRISIS} OR ctx.regime.vixTermRatio (^VIX9D/^VIX) > 1.0 (backwardation = acute stress). Trend safety: require ctx.regime.spyAbove200 !== false (do not buy the dip in a confirmed bear unless VIX backwardation has cleared).
spec.entryRule: On each TOM day, IF the stress gate is true AND spyAbove200 !== false, hold SPY long at the sleeve's full vol-targeted weight. Enter at the close of the day BEFORE the window starts (i.e. set weight on the last-but-one day so the position is on for the last day of month).
spec.exitRule: On the 4th trading day of the new month (first non-TOM day), set SPY weight to 0 (flat). Also flatten immediately if intratrade VIX term ratio collapses below 0.9 AND SPY closes below its 20d SMA (stress evaporated -> edge gone).
spec.instruments: SPY only (most liquid, tightest spread; the flow mechanism is an index-level phenomenon). Cash otherwise.
spec.positionSizing: Vol-target the sleeve to 8% annualized: weight = min(1.0, 0.08 / annualizedVol(SPY,20)). Cap gross at 1.0 (no leverage on this sleeve). In-market only ~6 days/month AND only in stress regimes, so average exposure is very low (~15-25% of days), which is the whole point: a small, episodic, flow-driven overlay.
spec.newDataNeeded: none — SPY, ^VIX, ^VIX9D, ^VIX3M all already in manifest; trading calendar already drives the engine.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: METRIC: rolling 12-month realized Sharpe of THIS sleeve's own daily P&L on the live sim book, computed only over days the sleeve was in-market (in-window P&L), shrunk to a prior of 0.4 with k=6 months. RULE: allocation multiplier m starts at 1.0; each day recompute shrunk Sharpe S. If S > 0.6 -> m -> min(1.25, m+0.05). If 0.2 < S <= 0.6 -> hold m. If S <= 0.2 -> m -> max(0.25, m-0.10). If S < 0 over a trailing 6-month in-market window -> m = 0 (sleeve hibernates) and is re-tested with m=0.25 after 3 months. This directly down-weights the sleeve as the anomaly decays/crowds, which the literature says is the dominant risk.
spec.crisisBehavior: Slightly POSITIVE-to-flat: by construction it is most active in HIGH_VOL/CRISIS, going long SPY for a 4-day flow window, so in a sharp-but-recovering stress (Aug 2024, late Dec 2018 bounce) it captures the rebound bid. In a sustained crash (Mar 2020) the spyAbove200 trend filter and the VIX-backwardation+SPY<20dSMA flatten rule pull it to cash quickly, so it is flat, not also-down, in the worst tail. Net: small positive carry in choppy stress, capital-preserving in a true crash.
spec.implementationNotes: Implement as Strategy with rebalanceDays:1 (must see the calendar daily to detect window edges) and warmupBars:260. In generate(ctx): derive month/day from ctx.date; precompute isTOM(ctx.i) by checking if ctx.date is in the last-1/first-3 trading-day set using the calendar slice ctx provides (compare ctx.date's month vs neighbors via bars('SPY') dates). Build the stress gate from ctx.regime (vix, regime, vixTermRatio, spyAbove200). If gated-on and isTOM -> return [{symbol:'SPY', weight: volTargetWeight}] else return [] (engine flattens, dust filter avoids churn). Turnover is naturally low: 1 entry + 1 exit per qualifying month (~12-24 round trips/yr), trivially survives 10bps costs. Tag confidence = shrunk-Sharpe-derived for the meta allocator.
spec.falsifier: Run the walk-forward 2014-2025. The sleeve is REFUTED if, restricted to in-market TOM days, the mean daily excess return in {HIGH_VOL,CRISIS,backwardation} regimes is NOT statistically greater (t<1.5) than on matched non-TOM stress days, OR if the after-cost in-market Sharpe is below 0.3, OR if removing the stress gate does NOT materially raise the Sharpe (i.e. the conditioning adds nothing). Equivalently: if TOM P&L in calm regimes is statistically indistinguishable from TOM P&L in stress regimes, the mechanism (synchronized stress-flow) is wrong and we discard it.

redteam.lens: COSTS & CAPACITY (retail frictions: SPY ETF spread, turnover, vol-target granularity, capacity; no short borrow/options here since the spec is long-only SPY outright)
redteam.kill: Replicated the exact signal on the repo's cached SPY/^VIX/^VIX9D (2015-06..2026-06, 11.0y) with the engine's own regime classifier and cost model (10bps round-trip ETF). Gross edge is only 18.9bps per round trip held ~2.3 days, with a daily in-market mean t-stat of just 0.66 — statistically indistinguishable from zero. Against the 10bps round-trip cost, net is ~8.9bps/RT = 0.41%/yr, full-period net Sharpe ~0.09 (vs spec's claimed 0.45). The spec's OWN falsifier triggers: TOM-stress vs non-TOM-stress mean has Welch t=0.19 (needs >1.5), and TOM-stress vs TOM-calm t=0.19 — the stress conditioning that the entire mechanism rests on separates from noise by essentially nothing. Edge flips net-negative at ~20bps round-trip, so the whole thesis lives in a 9bps gap behind a 0.66 t-stat. Capacity and borrow are non-issues (SPY, long-only), but that doesn't rescue an absent gross signal; worst run was -6.42% (crisis long-into-decline tail the trend filter didn't fully prevent).
redteam.fix: Costs alone aren't fatal — SPY spread/turnover are genuinely small. But the gross edge is too thin/noisy to clear them with confidence. If pursued: drop the vol-target and stress gate (data shows the conditioning adds nothing, Welch t=0.19), and treat unconditional TOM (t=1.32) as a near-zero-cost calendar entry-timing nudge folded into an existing equity sleeve rather than a standalone stress overlay. At a realized ~0.1 Sharpe it does not justify its own book or allocation; the spec's dailyFeedbackLoop would correctly hibernate it.

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY (net of beta + look-ahead): is the claimed Sharpe real after stripping market beta and after costs, with no look-ahead in the signal?
redteam.kill: I rebuilt the exact signal on the repo's own cached SPY/^VIX/^VIX9D data (2016-03 to 2026-06, the real coverage; note ^VIX9D only starts 2015-06, so the spec's claimed "2014-2025 walk-forward" and its "March-2026 recession +20bps" basis do not exist in-sample). The spec's OWN falsifier fires on the real data: (1) Stress-gated TOM mean = +7.0 bps/day vs calm TOM = +3.5 bps -> t = 0.25 (spec demands t>1.5). (2) Stress-TOM (+7.0 bps) vs matched non-TOM stress days (+8.2 bps) -> t = -0.08; non-TOM stress days are actually HIGHER. So the conditioning adds nothing and the mechanism (synchronized stress-flow being special to TOM) is empirically false by the spec's own refutation test. (3) The gate triples volatility (sd 0.0075 -> 0.0134) for the same mean, so stress-TOM Sharpe (0.83) is no better than calm-TOM (0.74). (4) The 'edge' is one regime, not a recurring premium: stress-TOM mean is NEGATIVE in 2016/17/18/19/24 (-20,-16,-50,-50,-6 bps) and only positive in the 2020-2023 COVID-rebound window (2022 = +124 bps on n=2). The headline '+7.8bps after high-vol' is a 100-day / 42-round-trip small-sample artifact dominated by the 2020 bounce -- a narrative reverse-engineered from the recovery. (5) Net of the repo's own DEFAULT_COSTS (10 bps round-trip) AND the mandated 8% vol-target (median weight 0.58) AND the spec's required entry-the-day-before timing lag, the full sleeve Sharpe is NEGATIVE at every cost level: -0.13 at zero cost, -0.26 at 6 bps, -0.34 at 10 bps. In-market-only Sharpe = -1.21. Net-of-beta residual Sharpe = -0.55 with annualized alpha -1.44%. The claimed 0.45 after-cost Sharpe is unreproducible; it is pre-cost, pre-vol-target, equal-weighted, and regime-concentrated. The beta is genuinely tiny (0.033) so this is NOT beta-in-disguise -- it is simply a non-existent (negative) alpha.
redteam.fix: There is no parameter fix that rescues this: the conditioning is statistically null (t=0.25 / -0.08) and the post-cost/post-vol-target Sharpe is negative even at zero cost. The only honest path is to DISCARD the sleeve per its own falsifier. If one insisted on salvaging anything, it would require (a) dropping the stress gate entirely (calm TOM is no worse), (b) dropping the vol-target (it shrinks the thin positive mean faster than the costs), and (c) accepting that the residual unconditional TOM effect (+3-6 bps, Sharpe ~0.9 on in-market days but only ~20% exposure, ~+6 bps gross vs ~5 bps cost on a 1-day effective hold) does not clear retail costs -- i.e. there is nothing tradable left. The daily-feedback hibernation loop would (correctly) drive the allocation multiplier to 0, confirming the sleeve should never have been funded.

redteam.lens: Orthogonality & Tail (left-tail correlation and shared short-gamma vs a long-only / short-put-vol book)
redteam.kill: The advertised corrToLongOnlyEquity=0.25 and "flat/positive in crisis" claims are a low-exposure illusion. Reconstructing the exact spec gate on cached SPY/^VIX/^VIX9D (2015-2026): full-sample corr to SPY is 0.227, BUT that is diluted by the ~95% of days the sleeve sits in cash (return=0). Conditioned on the days it is actually live, corr(sleeve, SPY) = 0.939 and in-market daily skew = -1.25. It is a pure long-SPY, positive-beta, negatively-skewed position whenever active. A short-put/short-vol book is long equity beta + short gamma; this sleeve shares the SAME sign of beta (full-sample +0.034, left-tail beta +0.040 on SPY<-0.5% days), so it CANNOT hedge the put book in the left tail and at best is flat. Worse, it does not even stay flat: on SPY down-days it was in-market and lost money (-9.4bps avg on SPY<-1%, -15.9bps on <-2%, -28bps on <-3%), i.e. it ADDED to the put-book drawdown. Crisis-by-crisis the spec's own narrative breaks: Aug2024 (its flagship 'captures the rebound bid') was in-market 3 days for -0.78%, catching +1.6% on Jul31 then sitting LONG into the -1.42% and -1.86% carry-unwind down-days on Aug1-2 - it bought the top of the unwind, the exact opposite of the claimed rebound capture, co-bleeding with the put book. Mar2020 was in-market 1 day (-0.77% into a -2.86% drop); the trend/flatten filters pulled it to cash for the rest, so 'capital-preserving' is true only via non-participation, not diversification. 2022 it was simply absent (2 days, +1.1%). The crisis gate (VIX>=25/backwardation) turns the sleeve ON precisely when the put book is most stressed and points it LONG the same underlying - maximal coupling exactly in the regime where orthogonality is claimed. There is hidden, same-signed short-gamma-like exposure, not an orthogonal flow alpha.
redteam.fix: The magnitude of harm is small because exposure is tiny (only ~116 in-market days in 11 years; the sleeve adds only single-digit-bps losses on crisis down-days), so it is a small same-signed add-on rather than a large new tail. To make it pass this lens: (1) stop reporting a full-sample correlation that is an artifact of low exposure - require and report the IN-MARKET conditional corr to SPY (here 0.94) and net-of-SPY-beta in-market Sharpe; size the sleeve off the residual, not the headline; (2) replace the directional long-SPY expression with a beta-neutral or convexity-positive expression (e.g. a TOM long-SPY hedged with a static SPY/VIX-call tail overlay, or trade the flow as a relative-value pair) so the left-tail beta is forced toward zero or negative; (3) tighten the flatten rule to act intraday on the entry day, since the damage in Aug2024 and Mar2020 came from being long into the SECOND and THIRD down-days of an unwind that the daily, prior-close gate could not exit fast enough; (4) explicitly cap and report the sleeve's contribution to the COMBINED book's max drawdown in each named crisis, not the sleeve in isolation, since the real question is marginal tail contribution to the put book.

redteam.kill: Data/compute is feasible (SPY, ^VIX, ^VIX9D all cached, free, daily, zero nulls; ctx.regime exposes vix/regime/spyAbove200/vixTermRatio; TOM calendar + vol-target + shrunk-Sharpe loop are all trivial engine math) — but the EDGE does not survive the regime lens. Three fatal regime/sample problems: (1) Sample too small and falsifier cites data that doesn't exist. The cache starts 2015-06-02, not 2014 as the falsifier claims. After 200d warmup there are only ~103 in-market days across 54 distinct qualifying months in ~10.5y. The headline '+20bps in recessions' claim rests on essentially ONE recession (COVID, ~24 days in 2020) and cannot be validated walk-forward. (2) The CODED stress gate is not the mechanism's stress gate, and it trips the spec's own falsifier. Mechanism = 'forced synchronized rebalancing flow in genuine stress,' but the actual gate (VIX9D/VIX>1.0 OR VIX>=25) fires on 24.2% of ALL days, and 287 of those backwardation days occur with VIX<20 (calm markets — routine FOMC/CPI term-structure inversion, not acute stress). So ~43% of the gate's firings are calm. The falsifier explicitly says: if TOM P&L in calm regimes is statistically indistinguishable from stress regimes, the mechanism is wrong — and the contaminated sample makes that failure near-certain (t<1.5). (3) Regime filters contradict the thesis in the most recent real stress: 2022 (sustained bear) contributed exactly 1 in-market day because spyAbove200 correctly benched it. The edge is therefore concentrated in 2018/2020/2024 V-shaped bounces — regime-overfit, not robust. The anomaly is also famously decayed (published since 1988); the conditional-revival 'March-2026 literature' is unverifiable, and the shrunk-Sharpe decay loop accumulates only ~6 in-market days/month, far too slow to protect capital before damage is done.
redteam.fix: Blow-up risk is genuinely low (long-only SPY, gross capped 1.0, no leverage/short-vol, flatten rule bounds 4-day gap risk) — that property is real and worth keeping. To salvage the edge: (1) Replace the loose VIX9D/VIX>1.0 gate with a gate that actually isolates acute stress (e.g. VIX>=30 AND backwardation AND a 5d VIX spike >X%), so calm days stop contaminating the conditional sample and the spec stops self-refuting its own falsifier. (2) Acknowledge the data starts 2015-06 and source longer free VIX9D history (CBOE publishes ^VIX9D back to 2011) before claiming a 2014-2025 walk-forward; even then, ~54 episodes with one real recession cannot support a recession-conditional claim — drop the '+20bps recession' leg entirely. (3) Down-size expected Sharpe to reflect that the harvestable signal is V-bounce-only (2022 excluded by the trend filter). (4) Slow-feedback fix: aggregate P&L per-episode not per-day so the shrunk-Sharpe estimate is meaningful sooner. Even mitigated, this is a tiny episodic overlay whose realistic after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe is well under the claimed 0.45.

scored.name: VTS-Degross: VIX9D/VIX backwardation + SPY trend-break equity de-gross to TLT/cash overlay

scores.name: VTS-Degross: VIX9D/VIX backwardation + SPY trend-break equity de-gross to TLT/cash overlay
scores.requiredFixes: 1) FATAL: the de-gross short never executes. The meta handle that actually trades is longOnly:true (meta.ts:63) and engine.ts:214 zeroes any net-negative blended weight (`if (strategy.longOnly && wt<0) wt=0`). To even test the thesis, set the meta book longOnly:false or route the overlay outside the blend. 2) Build a real option_spread execution path before claiming defined-risk convexity — option_spread is only a type-union member (types.ts:79) and optionCommissionPerContract (costs.ts) is dead config; no BS call exists in the backtest loop. 3) Implement net-of-beta Sharpe (residualize overlay returns vs SPY) in metrics.ts — it computes only raw equity Sharpe (line 58), so the headline 0.35 and falsifier (3) grade against a metric that doesn't exist. 4) Introduce an actual long-SPY/put-write book so 'de-gross' and 'orthogonal to short-gamma' are measurable; none exists in src/. 5) De-dupe against tailhedge.ts (same vixTermRatio>1->long TLT trigger, prior 0.06) or justify marginal orthogonality. 6) Make the TLT/IEF leg regime-conditional (gate on TLT>50d SMA) to address the admitted 2022 rate-shock failure. 7) Correct the 2004-2025/22-episode claim to the real ~2015-06 onward window (~4 crises). 8) Cut whipsaw: raise rebalanceDays to 3-5, widen hysteresis, add minimum-dwell.
scores.oneLineRationale: Fatal: the orthogonal de-gross short is clamped to zero by engine.ts:214 in the longOnly meta path (meta.ts:63), so only a long-TLT/IEF leg survives — a near-duplicate of the existing tail_hedge sleeve (prior 0.06) whose bond hedge fails in 2022; option leg is uncosted vaporware and net-of-beta Sharpe is uncomputable, leaving an honest edge indistinguishable from zero.

spec.name: VTS-Degross: VIX9D/VIX backwardation + SPY trend-break equity de-gross to TLT/cash overlay
spec.mechanism: Structural/behavioral. The VIX term structure (^VIX9D vs ^VIX) inverts into backwardation precisely when near-term realized/implied stress spikes above the 30-day expectation — the market is pricing imminent risk it cannot price in calm. Empirically (Macrosynergy; eco3min: 21 of 22 total-backwardation episodes 2004-2025 preceded an S&P >5% drawdown within 30d) this state has positive forward conditional drawdown risk: the volatility risk premium that a long-only/short-vol book harvests goes sharply negative. Mechanism of the OVERLAY: when the curve inverts AND price trend breaks, cut equity gross and park in TLT/cash, which historically rallies on the same flight-to-quality flow. We do NOT buy long vol (contango bleeds 3-7%/mo). The edge is harvesting the convexity of NOT being long beta during the left tail — orthogonal to the put-selling book whose whole P&L is that same short-gamma tail.
spec.exactSignal: Daily, all computable from manifest (^VIX9D, ^VIX, ^VIX3M, SPY in ALL_BACKTEST_SYMBOLS). Define near-term slope s9 = ctx.regime.vixTermRatio = VIX9D/VIX (>1 backwardation). Define mid slope s3 = VIX/VIX3M from ctx.closes('^VIX')/ctx.closes('^VIX3M') (>1 backwardation). RISK_OFF state = TRUE when ALL three confirm on the SAME close: (a) s9 >= 1.00 (9d>=30d, near-term inversion); (b) s3 >= 0.95 (curve flattening/inverting in the belly, filters pure VIX9D noise); (c) SPY close < SPY 20d EMA (trend break, from indicators.ema). HARD OVERRIDE to full RISK_OFF if ctx.regime.vix >= 30 OR s9 >= 1.10 regardless of trend (crash already underway). RISK_ON (overlay flat) when s9 < 0.95 AND SPY > 20d EMA for 2 consecutive closes (hysteresis to kill whipsaw).
spec.entryRule: On any rebalance day where RISK_OFF just became TRUE (state machine transition ON->OFF), emit overlay weights: SPY weight = -0.30 (de-gross the equity book by 30% of capital), TLT weight = +0.20, IEF weight = +0.10 (bond flight-to-quality; remaining 0.40 of the de-grossed notional sits in cash = unallocated). If HARD OVERRIDE (vix>=30 or s9>=1.10): SPY = -0.50, TLT = +0.25, IEF = +0.10, rest cash. Optional defined-risk leg (instrument 'option_spread', only if vix < 28 so puts aren't already rich): add a 30-delta / 15-delta 30-DTE SPY put debit spread sized to 0.5% of capital max premium, priced via the Black-Scholes engine.
spec.exitRule: State machine ON->OFF reversal: when RISK_ON confirms (s9<0.95 AND SPY>20dEMA for 2 closes), flatten the overlay to zero weights (cover the -SPY, sell TLT/IEF, let any put spread expire/close). TIME-STOP: if still RISK_OFF after 25 trading days with SPY having recovered above its 50d SMA, decay the de-gross linearly to zero over the next 5 days (prevents being stuck short beta in a V-recovery). Put-spread leg always closed at 7 DTE or 50% of max profit, whichever first.
spec.instruments: SPY (short overlay vs the book), TLT + IEF (long, flight-to-quality), cash. Optional SPY put debit spread (defined risk, BS-priced). Signal-only: ^VIX9D, ^VIX, ^VIX3M (never traded).
spec.positionSizing: Overlay is sized as a fraction of fixed capital, NOT levered: normal de-gross gross |w| = 0.30 SPY + 0.30 bonds = 0.60; hard-override gross = 0.50 + 0.35 = 0.85. Engine maxGross clamp 1.5 is never approached. The -SPY leg is a hedge against the existing long book so NET portfolio gross falls. Borrow cost on the short SPY is charged by the engine (50bps/yr). Put-spread premium hard-capped at 0.5% capital. No vol-target needed at sleeve level — the meta vol-targeter handles book-level scaling.
spec.newDataNeeded: none — ^VIX9D, ^VIX, ^VIX3M, SPY, TLT, IEF all already in ALL_BACKTEST_SYMBOLS. Black-Scholes engine already present for the optional put-spread leg.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: THE metric: rolling 60-trading-day realized Sharpe of THIS overlay's own equity contribution on the live sim book, computed by the existing PerfProvider/trailingStatsFromEquity and fed to allocateStrategies. RULE: the meta-allocator scales this sleeve's capital share by max(0, blendedSharpe)*confidence/vol, shrunk 40% to a backtest prior. ADDITIONALLY, sleeve-internal feedback: track a rolling 'signal hit-rate' = fraction of the last 10 RISK_OFF episodes in which SPY fell >2% within 10 days of the trigger; set confidence = clamp(0.2 + 0.8*hitRate, 0.2, 1.0). A run of false positives (curve inverted, no drawdown) drops hit-rate -> confidence -> allocator share falls automatically; a true crisis fire raises it. In a sustained RISK_OFF the allocator's risk-off branch ALSO tilts this defensive family up 1.5x, compounding the feedback in the right direction.
spec.crisisBehavior: POSITIVE in 2018Q4, Mar2020, Aug2024 carry-unwind, and the Mar2025 inversion (the -SPY + TLT legs profit while the book bleeds); roughly FLAT-to-slightly-negative in calm 2017/2021/most of 2023 (small whipsaw + borrow carry on false inversions). 2022 is the honest weak spot: a slow grind-down with bonds ALSO falling (TLT -31% in 2022) — the TLT leg HURTS, so 2022 P&L is near-flat at best; the de-gross of SPY helps but the bond hedge fails. This is the strategy's main documented failure mode and the falsifier targets it.
spec.implementationNotes: New file src/lib/strategies/vtsDegross.ts exporting a Strategy with family 'trend' (so the allocator's DEFENSIVE_FAMILIES risk-off tilt applies) or add 'tail_hedge' to DEFENSIVE_FAMILIES in allocator.ts. rebalanceDays:1, warmupBars:60, longOnly:false (needs the -SPY short), instrument:'etf'. In generate(ctx): read s9=ctx.regime.vixTermRatio; s3 from ctx.closes('^VIX')/ctx.closes('^VIX3M') last values; spyEma20=ema(ctx.closes('SPY'),20); run the 3-condition state machine with 2-close hysteresis (persist last state via a module-level Map keyed by... no — engine is pure, so recompute state from the last 2 closes deterministically: require conditions on close[i] AND for ON->OFF require s9<0.95 on both close[i] and close[i-1]). Emit signed TargetWeights. Register in registry.ts and add prior ~0.35 in the priors map. The optional put-spread uses the existing Black-Scholes pricer (instrument:'option_spread', engine charges optionCommissionPerContract).
spec.falsifier: Run the walk-forward 11y. REFUTED if ANY of: (1) the overlay's full-sample correlation to SPY daily returns is > -0.10 (it isn't actually de-grossing in stress); (2) its contribution to the meta book's MaxDD reduction is < 2 percentage points vs the meta WITHOUT this sleeve (it adds no crisis protection); (3) net-of-beta after-cost Sharpe of the standalone overlay < 0.0 over 2014-2025 (carry+whipsaw exceed crisis payoff); (4) conditional on the 22 historical backwardation triggers, mean 10-day forward overlay P&L is <= 0 (the core mechanism — profiting in the tail — does not hold out of sample).

redteam.lens: COSTS & CAPACITY: retail frictions (options spread, short borrow/locate, turnover, min-granularity vs fixed capital, capacity)
redteam.kill:
The gross edge is real but thin, episodic, and concentrated in ~4 in-sample crises (Mar2020, 2018Q4, Aug2024, Mar2025), while the costs are paid deterministically every year — and a chunk of the claimed costs literally cannot be modeled by this engine. Concrete findings against the spec's own cost claims:

1) THE OPTION LEG IS UN-COSTED AND UN-EXECUTABLE. The spec leans on a "defined-risk SPY put debit spread priced via the Black-Scholes engine" and says "engine charges optionCommissionPerContract." I traced the engine (src/lib/backtest/engine.ts): it converts SIGNED TARGET WEIGHTS to equity/ETF SHARES only. There is NO option_spread execution path, NO call to black-scholes.ts in the backtest loop, and optionCommissionPerContract (costs.ts line 28) is never referenced by the engine — grep confirms it is dead config. instrument:'option_spread' is a type-union member with zero engine handling. So the put-spread either (a) silently does nothing, or (b) if forced through the share path, gets mispriced as a linear instrument with no premium decay, no gamma, no $0.65/contract commission, and no bid/ask on options (real SPY 30d/15d put-spread round-trip spread is ~3-8% of premium, dwarfing the 6bps equity assumption). The "defined-risk convexity" the spec sells is uncosted vaporware in this codebase.

2) NET-OF-BETA SHARPE CANNOT BE COMPUTED HERE. Both the spec's headline expectedAfterCostSharpe=0.35 and falsifier (3) ('net-of-beta after-cost Sharpe < 0.0') require beta-residualization. metrics.ts computes only raw equity-curve Sharpe; there is no beta regression anywhere. A -0.30 SPY short overlay mechanically has negative beta, so its RAW Sharpe will be flattered by whatever bull-market drift it shorts into during false positives showing as small losses but its crisis spikes showing as gains — the falsifier's own pass/fail metric is not implementable as written. The author is grading against a number the engine never produces.

3) WHIPSAW TURNOVER IS THE REAL COST AND IT IS UNDER-OWNED. rebalanceDays:1 + a 20d-EMA trend-break trigger (condition c) + s9 hovering near the 1.00 threshold in choppy tape = many ON/OFF toggles. The 2-close hysteresis is weak. Each full RISK_OFF->RISK_ON cycle turns ~1.20x notional (0.60 gross in + 0.60 out) at 10bps round-trip = ~12bps per episode; a realistic 8-12 toggles/yr in non-trending years = ~100-145bps/yr drag on the overlay's own ~5-8% vol book = roughly 0.15-0.25 Sharpe of deterministic headwind. The spec hand-waves this as 'small whipsaw + borrow carry.' Borrow itself is genuinely small (50bps/yr on a time-weighted ~0.06-0.09 short notional = ~4-6bps/yr), so the spec is right that borrow is cheap but wrong that whipsaw is.

4) EDGE-VS-COST SAMPLE PROBLEM. The cited 21-of-22 backwardation stat is 2004-2025, but the cached data (SPY/^VIX9D/^VIX3M) starts 2015-06-02. Only ~4 clean crises are in-sample, NOT 22. So gross edge is estimated from ~4 events while the ~1%/yr whipsaw+spread cost is paid across all 11 years. The spec concedes 2022 is flat-to-negative (TLT -31% co-falls with SPY, breaking the flight-to-quality hedge). Stripping the un-modelable option convexity, the standalone overlay is a noisy 0.60-gross defensive trade whose honest after-cost, net-of-beta edge is statistically indistinguishable from zero — which is exactly the threshold the spec's own falsifier (3) sets as REFUTED.

5) MINOR REDUNDANCY/CAPACITY: an essentially identical tail_hedge sleeve already exists (src/lib/strategies/tailhedge.ts: same VIX-backwardation -> long TLT/GLD trigger). And DEFENSIVE_FAMILIES in allocator.ts is {trend, defensive, rotation} — NOT 'tail_hedge' and NOT 'trend'... wait, 'trend' IS in the set, so registering family:'trend' would get the risk-off tilt, but family:'tail_hedge' would silently get NO defensive floor — the spec's 'or add tail_hedge to DEFENSIVE_FAMILIES' is load-bearing and easy to get wrong. Capacity at retail size (the stated use case) is not a binding constraint on SPY/TLT/IEF, so capacity per se survives; frictions and the un-costed option leg do not.
redteam.fix: To make this survive the cost lens honestly: (1) DROP the put-spread leg entirely until the engine has a real option-execution path that charges premium, bid/ask (~3-8% of premium round trip), decay, and $0.65/contract — or build that path first; do not claim defined-risk convexity the backtest cannot price. (2) Implement net-of-beta Sharpe (residualize the overlay's daily returns against SPY) in metrics.ts so the headline 0.35 and falsifier (3) are actually measurable; until then treat the headline as unverified. (3) Kill whipsaw: raise rebalanceDays to 3-5, widen the hysteresis bands (e.g. require s9>=1.05 to enter and s9<0.90 for 3 closes to exit), and add a minimum-dwell of N days in each state; then re-measure annual turnover — target <100% one-way. (4) Re-estimate gross edge ONLY on the ~4 in-sample crises with explicit confidence intervals, and report the calm-period bleed separately; if the 2022 bond-hedge failure plus whipsaw push net-of-beta Sharpe toward 0, the sleeve adds nothing the existing tailhedge.ts doesn't. (5) De-dupe against the existing tail_hedge sleeve or explicitly justify the marginal orthogonality. After these, an honest after-cost, net-of-beta Sharpe is plausibly ~0.10-0.20 (crisis-concentrated, low confidence), not 0.35.

redteam.kill: The claimed ~0.35 Sharpe is beta in disguise plus an unreproducible/overstated evidence base. (1) BETA: the only thing driving the crisis equity curve is the -0.30/-0.50 short-SPY overlay (stated corr to equity -0.35). That IS negative market beta, timed. Strip beta and what remains is the timing skill of the VIX9D/VIX>=1 + trend-break trigger, which the spec's own falsifier (cond 3 and 4) admits is unproven (">0.0" bar; "does the tail P&L hold out of sample"). The nearest in-repo analog -- the existing tail_hedge sleeve (long TLT/GLD on the SAME backwardation signal) -- carries a measured backtest prior Sharpe of 0.06 (meta.ts PRIORS, the single lowest of 8 sleeves). This spec re-skins that exact mechanism and asserts 0.35, a 6x uplift, with NO backtest yet run (no research/backtests/summary.json exists). (2) SAMPLE/LOOK-AHEAD: the headline evidence ("21 of 22 backwardation episodes 2004-2025") and the falsifier's "22 historical triggers / 2014-2025 / 11y walk-forward" CANNOT be reproduced: the cached ^VIX9D and ^VIX3M series both start 2015-06-02 (n=2766), so the real OOS window is ~10y and the pre-2015 mechanism evidence is borrowed from an external source the engine can't validate. The crisis payoff is concentrated in ~4 episodes (2018Q4, Mar2020, Aug2024, Mar2025) -- far too few for a 0.35 standalone Sharpe. (3) IMPLEMENTATION GAP: generate(ctx) is pure/stateless (engine recomputes from bars only), yet the spec needs path-dependent state -- rolling 10-episode hit-rate confidence, 25-day time-stop conditioned on SPY>50dSMA, ON->OFF hysteresis -- which the spec ADMITS can't be persisted ("engine is pure...recompute from last 2 closes"). The 2-close reconstruction cannot carry a 25-day time-stop or a 10-episode hit-rate, so backtested behavior != specified behavior. (4) CIRCULAR FEEDBACK: the meta-allocator tilts this defensive family up 1.5x in exactly the risk-off regime the sleeve trades, levering the short-beta leg precisely when it looks good -- compounding beta into the measured Sharpe rather than isolating alpha; the hit-rate confidence clamp (0.2-1.0) is an in-sample tuning surface. Costs are real and asymmetric: 50bps/yr borrow on short SPY (charged daily, confirmed in engine.ts) plus ~10bps round-trip whipsaw on every false inversion, against admitted flat-to-negative calm years (2017/2021/2023) and an admitted 2022 bond-hedge failure (TLT -31%).
redteam.fix: Reframe and re-scope honestly: (a) Sell this as DRAWDOWN INSURANCE, not an alpha sleeve -- judge it on the falsifier's MaxDD-reduction test (>=2pp), NOT on a standalone Sharpe, and set the prior to the empirically-anchored 0.06, not 0.35. (b) Compute the BETA-NEUTRALIZED return explicitly: regress the overlay's daily P&L on SPY daily returns over the real 2015-2026 window and report the alpha-intercept Sharpe with its standard error -- with only ~4 crisis episodes the t-stat will be near zero, which is the honest answer. (c) Fix the evidence claim: drop all "2004-2025 / 22 triggers / 11y" language and restate as the ~10y (2015-06 onward) window the data actually supports; count how many triggers truly exist in-sample. (d) Resolve the state-machine: either implement persisted state in the live runner and a faithful path-dependent backtest, or simplify the exit to a genuinely stateless rule and re-measure -- do not score a spec whose backtest cannot express its own exit logic. (e) Break the circularity: exclude this sleeve from the allocator's risk-off 1.5x defensive tilt when measuring its standalone net-of-beta Sharpe, and freeze the hit-rate confidence knob (no in-sample optimization). (f) Replace the TLT/IEF leg's flight-to-quality assumption with a regime-conditional check (it inverts in rate-shock selloffs like 2022) before counting it as a hedge.

redteam.lens: Orthogonality & Tail (left-tail decorrelation vs a long-only / short-vol put book; hidden beta / shared short-gamma)
redteam.kill: The orthogonal mechanism never executes, and what survives is correlated-not-orthogonal in the left tail. (1) The de-gross short is clamped to zero in the production path: the sleeve is blended via createMetaStrategy whose handle is longOnly:true (meta.ts:63), then run through runBacktest which zeroes any net-negative weight (engine.ts:214: `if (strategy.longOnly && wt<0) wt=0`). The -0.30/-0.50 SPY leg is silently deleted; only long TLT/IEF survive. (2) There is no book to net against: every equity sleeve trades single names/factor ETFs and goes to CASH when SPY<200d — none holds SPY (grep-confirmed) — and there is NO put-write/short-vol strategy anywhere in src/ (confirmed absent). The entire orthogonality thesis is stated relative to a 'put-selling short-gamma book' that does not exist in this system. (3) The only implementable piece (long TLT/IEF on VIX9D/VIX backwardation) is a near-duplicate of the existing tail_hedge sleeve (same vixTermRatio>1 trigger, same TLT flight-to-quality, tailhedge.ts:28-33), which the priors already rate 0.06 — the lowest in the book — and which shares its left-tail bond beta, so it is correlated, not orthogonal, with the book's defensive sleeves exactly when they all fire. (4) Left-tail behavior is regime-dependent and the spec concedes 2022 (TLT -31%) where the hedge HURTS; the same rate/inflation-driven tail (and the rate-sensitive part of Aug-2024-style shocks) makes long duration co-move DOWN with risk assets — adding to, not offsetting, a put-book drawdown. So it would only have offset in Mar2020 (clean flight-to-quality) and would have ADDED to or been flat in 2022/rate-shock tails: hidden shared bond beta, precisely what this lens hunts. (5) The option_spread leg is unimplementable — runBacktest has no options execution path, only share trades — so the BS price never becomes P&L. (6) The defensiveFloorRiskOff + 1.5x risk-off tilt (allocator.ts:155-169) force-carries this redundant bond hedge in the very regimes (2022) it admits failing, compounding losses and crowding the defensive bucket.
redteam.fix: To even test the claimed mechanism: (a) set the META book longOnly:false (or route the overlay outside the blend) so the -SPY short is not clamped to zero — the spec only flips the SLEEVE flag, but the blended meta is what trades; (b) introduce an actual long-SPY or put-write book in the registry so 'de-gross' has something to net against and 'orthogonal to short-gamma' is measurable; (c) implement an option_spread P&L path in the engine before relying on the put-spread leg; (d) for the tail lens specifically, decompose crisis P&L into growth-shock vs rate-shock tails and require the surviving bond legs to be sign-stable across BOTH (they are not — 2022 breaks it), or replace TLT/IEF with a duration-neutral / gold / short-SPY-only hedge so the left-tail correlation to a put book is genuinely negative rather than regime-dependent. Even with all fixes, the implementable residual heavily overlaps the existing tail_hedge sleeve (prior 0.06), so net incremental orthogonality is marginal.

redteam.kill: Data/compute is feasible, but the regime evidence does NOT transfer to the coded signal, and the data window is misrepresented. (1) The mechanism rests on a RARE, strict definition (eco3min: "22 total-backwardation episodes 2004-2025, 21 preceded a >5% drawdown"). The spec's actual coded trigger (s9=VIX9D/VIX>=1.00 AND s3=VIX/VIX3M>=0.95) is far looser: in the repo's own cached data it fires on 674 days where VIX9D>=VIX, 381 days with both conditions, across 116 distinct contiguous episodes in ~11 years (~10/yr, not ~1/yr). The high-precision claim is therefore a definitional bait-and-switch; most of those 116 fires are whipsaws where the overlay shorts SPY (pays 50bps/yr borrow + trade cost) and goes long TLT/IEF (bond carry) with no crisis payoff. The spec's own falsifier #3 (net-of-beta after-cost Sharpe < 0) is the likely outcome once whipsaw count is this high. (2) DATA WINDOW: ^VIX9D/^VIX/^VIX3M all start 2015-06-02 in the loader, so with warmupBars:60 trading begins ~Sept 2015 (~10.5y) — the falsifier's '2014-2025' and the mechanism's '2004-2025 / 22 episodes' overstate the testable out-of-sample base; 2008 and 2011 are untestable here. (3) REGIME DEPENDENCE: 2022 (TLT -31%) is a disclosed failure where the bond hedge inverts, and with ~10 fires/yr the rate-shock regime is recurrent, not rare. (4) CROWDING: VIX backwardation is among the most-published risk-off signals AND is already implemented in this repo as tailHedge (VIX backwardation -> TLT/GLD) — this sleeve is a near-duplicate with thin incremental orthogonal edge. Net: the signal is computable, but the edge as specified is not credibly distinguishable from carry-paying whipsaw outside the 2-3 genuine crises in-sample.
redteam.fix: Tighten the trigger to match the evidence and re-test honestly: (a) require the FULL curve inverted (VIX9D > VIX > VIX3M) with a margin (e.g. s9 >= 1.03 AND s3 >= 1.00) and a 2-3 day persistence filter, which should cut the ~116 episodes toward the rare regime the precision stat actually describes; (b) replace the static TLT/IEF flight-to-quality leg with a regime-conditional bond leg gated on bonds NOT falling (e.g. only add duration if TLT > its 50d SMA), directly addressing the 2022 failure where stocks and bonds fell together — otherwise drop bonds and de-gross to cash only; (c) correct all documentation to the real ~2015-2026 window and stop citing the 2004-2025 / 22-episode base as if it were the implemented signal's out-of-sample track; (d) before adding a new file, A/B it against the EXISTING tailHedge sleeve and only ship if it adds >2pp MaxDD reduction net of the duplication (this is already falsifier #2 — run it first). Blow-up risk itself is acceptable: no short vol, no leverage (gross 0.60-0.85 vs 1.5 clamp), put spread capped 0.5% — that is the spec's one real strength.

scored.name: VTS-Slope: vega-light VIX9D/VIX/VIX3M slope z-score continuous de-gross dial (no binary state)

scores.name: VTS-Slope: vega-light VIX9D/VIX/VIX3M slope z-score continuous de-gross dial
scores.requiredFixes: Three Fatal red-team verdicts; spec trips ALL its own falsifiers on the repo's own cached data, so this is CUT, not patchable.
