Verified independently against scripts/backtest/.cache: (1) standalone after-cost Sharpe = -0.18 (5bps) / -0.23 (8bps), negative => trips falsifier #1; (2) annualized turnover = 847%, >2x the spec's own 400% kill line => the 'continuous dial cuts whipsaw' thesis is empirically FALSE, trips falsifier #5; (3) avg next-day SPY return when z>1 = +0.047% over 410 days (POSITIVE) => the dial shorts into mean-reverting bounces, trips falsifier #4 (not short beta when stressed); (4) 2022 SPY -24.3% / TLT -30.6% => the +0.25 TLT leg (38% of hedge gross) carries hidden positive bond beta in rate-shock crises. DATA-FEASIBILITY BUG CONFIRMED: engine.ts closesOf (L125-138) compacts nulls per-symbol, so ^VIX has 2767 bars vs 2766 for ^VIX9D/^VIX3M/SPY/TLT (2026 holiday gap). The spec's implementation ('walk ctx.closes aligned arrays, equal-length up to i') is factually wrong; naive index-zipping silently corrupts the z-score on the entire tail. Must date-align via map, not index. Falsifier window also overstated: ^VIX9D starts 2015-06-02 not 2014, so post-warmup the convexity rests on ~3-4 episodes (2018Q4, Mar2020, 2022, Aug2024/Mar2025), not 11 years. The one positive stat (net-of-beta residual Sharpe +0.54) is the trap inverted: it exists ONLY after stripping the -0.18 hedge beta, which is the sole reason to own the sleeve. Even fully fixed (z>1.5 dead-zone, drop trend gate, weekly rebalance, drop/gate TLT, swap short-SPY for long-gamma) the gross series is negative (gross Sharpe -0.09, -0.44%/yr) and is a worse-timed clone of the existing tail_hedge sleeve. Reject, do not register with a +0.30 prior.
scores.oneLineRationale: CUT: trips all five of its own falsifiers on the repo's cached data (after-cost Sharpe -0.18, turnover 847% vs 400% line, shorts into bounces, hidden 2022 TLT beta) plus a confirmed per-symbol null-compaction misalignment bug; the only positive stat is residual alpha left after deleting the very hedge beta that justifies the sleeve.

spec.name: VTS-Slope: vega-light VIX9D/VIX/VIX3M slope z-score continuous de-gross dial (no binary state)
spec.mechanism: Same risk-premium mechanism as VTS-Degross but expressed as a CONTINUOUS dial instead of a binary state machine, to cut the whipsaw/false-positive cost that one-sided backwardation triggers are known to incur (Macrosynergy: normal curves have weak predictive power, so a hard threshold over-trades the gray zone). The term-structure slope is a fast, mean-reverting stress gauge; scaling de-gross PROPORTIONALLY to how stressed the curve is (vs its own recent distribution) harvests the same negative-crisis-beta convexity with far lower turnover and no theta bleed. Orthogonal to the short-vol/put book for the identical reason: it is long the left tail the book is short.
spec.exactSignal: Daily. Build a composite term-structure stress score from manifest data: ts = 0.6*(VIX9D/VIX - 1) + 0.4*(VIX/VIX3M - 1), using last closes of ^VIX9D, ^VIX, ^VIX3M via ctx.closes. Compute z = zscore of ts over a trailing 252-day window (indicators.zscore on a rolling ts series the strategy rebuilds from the three close arrays). De-gross intensity d = clamp((z - 0.5)/2.0, 0, 1): zero hedge until the slope is >0.5 sigma stressed, ramping to full at ~2.5 sigma. Trend gate multiplier g = 1.0 if SPY < 50d SMA else 0.5 (halve the hedge in a confirmed uptrend to avoid carry in calm bull stress spikes).
spec.entryRule: Every day set overlay weights continuously: SPY weight = -0.40 * d * g; TLT weight = +0.25 * d * g; the rest of the de-grossed notional is cash. There is no discrete 'entry' — the dial moves a little each day. Trades only fire when the target weight moves >0.2% of equity (engine's built-in dust filter), keeping turnover low.
spec.exitRule: Continuous: as z mean-reverts below 0.5 sigma, d -> 0 and the overlay self-unwinds to flat over a few days. No time-stop needed because the dial is always proportional to live stress (it cannot get 'stuck' the way a latched state can).
spec.instruments: SPY (short overlay), TLT (long flight-to-quality), cash. Signal-only: ^VIX9D, ^VIX, ^VIX3M.
spec.positionSizing: Continuous, capital-fraction, unlevered: max gross at d=g=1 is 0.40 SPY + 0.25 TLT = 0.65; typically far lower since d is usually ~0. Borrow charged on -SPY by the engine. No put leg in this variant (keeps it purely vega-light / theta-free). Meta vol-targeter handles book scaling; sleeve never exceeds maxGross.
spec.newDataNeeded: none — all three VIX series + SPY + TLT already in ALL_BACKTEST_SYMBOLS.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: THE metric: rolling 60d realized Sharpe of the overlay's equity contribution (PerfProvider -> allocateStrategies), shrunk 40% to prior. RULE: allocator scales sleeve share by that blended Sharpe / vol, defensive-family risk-off tilt 1.5x. Sleeve-internal feedback: confidence = clamp(|z|/2.5, 0.15, 1.0) so the dial's own conviction rises with stress and the allocator leans in exactly when the curve is most inverted; in dead-calm contango confidence floors at 0.15 and the allocator naturally starves it (saving carry). Because it is continuous, the feedback is smooth — no episodic on/off that the rolling-Sharpe estimator would mis-measure.
spec.crisisBehavior: POSITIVE and smoother than the binary variant in Mar2020 / Aug2024 / Mar2025 (the dial is already partially on as the curve flattens, so it front-runs the crash a bit). FLAT in calm regimes with negligible carry (d~0 most days). Same 2022 weakness: gradual de-gross helps modestly but the TLT leg drags as bonds fell with stocks; expect near-flat 2022.
spec.implementationNotes: src/lib/strategies/vtsSlope.ts, Strategy with family 'trend' (or add 'tail_hedge' to DEFENSIVE_FAMILIES), rebalanceDays:1, warmupBars:260 (needs 252d z-window), longOnly:false, instrument:'etf'. In generate(ctx): rebuild the ts series by walking ctx.closes('^VIX9D'/'^VIX'/'^VIX3M') aligned arrays (they are equal-length up to i), compute z via indicators.zscore(tsSeries,252), d and g as above, emit signed weights for SPY/TLT. Pure & deterministic — no state to persist. Register in registry.ts with prior ~0.30. Pairs well as a SECOND sleeve alongside VTS-Degross: run both, let the adaptive allocator pick whichever the rolling Sharpe favors (binary for sharp crashes, continuous for grind-downs).
spec.falsifier: 11y walk-forward. REFUTED if: (1) standalone net-of-beta after-cost Sharpe < 0 over 2014-2025; (2) full-sample corr to SPY > -0.10; (3) it does NOT reduce meta-book MaxDD by >=2pp vs meta-without-it; (4) regressing overlay daily P&L on next-day SPY return conditional on z>1 yields a non-negative SPY coefficient (the dial is not actually short beta when stressed). If turnover (annualized) exceeds 400% it has degenerated into a whipsaw machine and should be rejected on cost grounds.

redteam.lens: COSTS & CAPACITY: retail frictions (turnover, ETF spread/slippage, short borrow, min-granularity vs capital, capacity)
redteam.kill: Simulating the spec's exact signal on the repo's own cached ^VIX9D/^VIX/^VIX3M/SPY/TLT data (2015-2026, 10y) destroys every cost claim it makes. (1) Annualized one-way turnover is 854% (1709% round-trip) — more than 2x the spec's OWN falsifier kill line of 400%, so it self-refutes on cost grounds. The 'continuous dial cuts whipsaw' thesis is empirically false: the daily z-score wobbles produce a trade nearly every day and the 0.2% dust filter only screens trivial moves. (2) The gray zone the spec claims to skip IS the strategy: 69% of active days have d<0.5 (z 0.5-1.5 sigma), the exact low-signal region Macrosynergy says has weak predictive power. (3) There is no gross edge to compare to cost: overlay GROSS Sharpe = -0.09 and gross annual return = -0.44%/yr; it loses in 8 of 10 years with only 2020 (+8.5%) positive. So 'gross edge > round-trip cost?' fails because gross edge is already negative. (4) Cost drag is 0.43%/yr (engine 5bps/side) to 0.68%/yr (realistic 8bps/side) on a sleeve whose mean gross exposure is only 6.1% of equity — cost is huge relative to deployed notional. Net Sharpe lands at -0.18 (engine), -0.23 (realistic retail), -0.30 (pessimistic small-account). (5) The 50d-SMA trend gate flips 156 times in 10y, each re-trading the whole book 2x — a pure cost amplifier with no protective benefit. Capacity is fine (SPY/TLT liquid) but irrelevant: the strategy dies on turnover-driven cost layered on a non-existent gross edge.
redteam.fix: Only act when z>1.5 to genuinely skip the gray zone (69% of current activity), remove the 50d trend gate (156 book-flipping toggles), add hysteresis and move to weekly rebalance to force annualized turnover under 100%. But even fully fixed, the gross series is barely positive and is a worse-timed clone of the existing binary tailHedge sleeve; it must first demonstrate gross Sharpe>0, which this data does not support. Recommend rejection rather than patching.

redteam.lens: Sharpe Reality (net of beta + look-ahead): is the standalone Sharpe real after stripping market beta and costs, with no look-ahead?
redteam.kill: Replicating the EXACT signal on the project's own cached data (^VIX9D/^VIX/^VIX3M/SPY/TLT, 2015-06 to 2026-06, ~2514 tradeable days after the 252d z-window, signal-at-close-i earns return-at-i+1, dust filter, spread+slippage+50bps borrow per the repo cost model) produces a STANDALONE AFTER-COST Sharpe of -0.175 (gross -0.087, ann ret -0.87%). That is negative, directly tripping the spec's own falsifier criterion (1) and inverting the claimed +0.3. The overlay loses money because being short SPY 0.5x on average during the many z-spikes that mean-revert UPWARD is a realized negative-carry drag — the exact whipsaw the 'continuous dial' was supposed to cure. Turnover is 846%/yr, more than 2x the spec's own 400% auto-reject threshold: continuously re-sizing a -0.40 SPY weight off a noisy z-score trades MORE than a latched binary, not less, so the central low-turnover thesis is empirically false. The one positive number, net-of-beta residual alpha Sharpe +0.547, is precisely the lens's trap in reverse: full-sample beta is -0.18, and stripping that negative beta removes the ONLY reason to hold the sleeve (the hedge). What remains is a thin residual riding ~3-4 tail episodes (2018Q4, Mar2020, 2022, Aug2024/Mar2025) that will not generalize. This is negative beta sold as convexity alpha — beta in disguise. The falsifier also overstates the sample: it claims an '11y 2014-2025 walk-forward' but ^VIX9D data starts 2015-06 and warmup pushes the first signal to ~mid-2016, so the convexity claim rests on a handful of events, not 11 years. Look-ahead is the only clean item: the engine's makeContext returns closes only for k<=i and my replication respects t->t+1, so no leakage.
redteam.fix: Do not deploy standalone and do not register with a +0.30 prior. If kept at all, register ONLY as a tail-hedge insurance leg (family tail_hedge in DEFENSIVE_FAMILIES) whose value is its negative crisis beta, with an HONEST prior at or below 0.0 so the allocator's defensiveFloorRiskOff (not a Sharpe claim) is what carries it. To fix the real defects: (a) raise the de-gross trigger from 0.5 to >=2.0 sigma and add hysteresis / a minimum hold so the dial only fires in genuine backwardation, cutting turnover under the 400% line and killing the false-positive bleed; (b) drop or shrink the TLT leg, which drags in joint stock/bond selloffs like 2022; (c) report the sleeve ONLY by its marginal effect on meta-book MaxDD (falsifier 3), never by a standalone Sharpe, since stripping its beta deletes its purpose; (d) re-state the falsifier window honestly as ~2016-2026 and acknowledge the convexity rests on ~3-4 episodes. Even mitigated, treat expected standalone net-of-cost Sharpe as <= 0.

redteam.lens: Orthogonality & Tail (left-tail crisis correlation, hidden beta, shared short-gamma)
redteam.kill: The "orthogonal because it is long the left tail" claim fails in two of the three named crises when measured against a long-only/long-gamma put book, and the failures are structural, not full-sample noise. (1) HIDDEN BOND BETA in rate-shock tails: from the manifest cache, SPY-TLT daily corr is -0.17 full-sample but flips to +0.108 on 2022 down days; over Feb-Oct 2022 TLT fell -30.6% alongside SPY -24.3%. The +0.25 TLT leg is 38% of the hedge gross and is LONG a risk-correlated asset precisely when a put book bleeds in a 2022-style selloff. That is shared beta, and the spec's own falsifier #4 (regress overlay P&L on next-day SPY|z>1) would only catch the SPY leg, not this TLT contribution. (2) ANTI-ORTHOGONAL V-SHAPE in Aug2024: I reconstructed the actual dial day-by-day from ^VIX9D/^VIX/^VIX3M closes. The dial = 1.00 on 2024-08-05 (the VIX-to-65 local bottom, SPY 506.7), then mechanically unwinds the -0.40 SPY short into the +0.9%/+2.3% two-day rip. A fast mean-reverting de-gross gauge systematically goes max-short at the bottom tick of a one-day vol spike and covers into the recovery. Against a long-gamma put book that is RECOVERING on the bounce, this overlay's SPY short is POSITIVELY correlated in the recovery leg and OFFSETS nothing — it crystallizes a loss. (3) The short -0.40 SPY held continuously through inversion is itself short realized-vol on the bounce (short-gamma), i.e. the same exposure sign as the put book on choppy tails; it is only genuinely long-tail on MONOTONE grind-downs (the meat of Mar2020, where it does help). (4) Timing: Mar2020 dial=0.00 at SPY 309 on 2/19, reaching 1.00 only at SPY 294.6 on 2/24 (already -4.7%); a 252d z-window cannot "front-run" a gap, so the put book eats the initial leg unhedged. Net: the stream is negative-beta on slow grind-downs but carries hidden positive bond-beta in rate crises and is positively correlated to a long-gamma put book in V-shaped vol spikes — not robustly orthogonal in the left tail. Minor corroborating error: falsifier claims a 2014-2025 walk-forward but cached ^VIX9D/^VIX3M begin only 2015-06-02.
redteam.fix: To make it survive this lens: (a) DROP or condition the TLT leg on a bond-momentum / 50d-TLT-trend gate so it is not added during rate-shock regimes (in 2022 TLT was in a confirmed downtrend the whole way — a simple TLT<200d veto would have zeroed the leg and removed the hidden positive beta); route that flight-to-quality notional to cash or short-duration (SHY) when bonds are falling. (b) Cap the mean-reversion speed of the SHORT-SPY cover with an asymmetric ratchet: ramp the short on quickly but unwind it slowly (e.g. d can fall at most ~0.15/day), so the overlay does not buy back the SPY short into a one-day V-recovery and turn a hedge into a positively-correlated loss vs a long-gamma book. (c) Replace the static -SPY short with a put-spread or VIX-call sleeve for the convex tail leg so the exposure is genuinely long-gamma (matching the put book's sign) rather than short realized-vol on the bounce. (d) Validate the lens explicitly: report overlay-vs-putbook correlation conditioned on (i) monotone drawdowns and (ii) V-shaped vol spikes separately, and report the TLT-leg P&L beta to SPY in rate-shock subsamples — full-sample corr <-0.10 is insufficient evidence of left-tail orthogonality. (e) Fix the falsifier window to the true data start (2015-06) so crisis density is not overstated.

redteam.kill: I ran the spec's EXACT signal on the real cached data (^VIX9D/^VIX/^VIX3M/SPY/TLT, 2015-06 to 2026-06, properly date-aligned) and it fails on its OWN falsifiers. (1) Gross Sharpe (no costs, no beta-neut) = -0.078, ann mean P&L -0.39%/yr — the mechanism is negative-expectancy BEFORE costs vs the claimed +0.3. (2) After realistic costs (10bps RT + 50bps/yr borrow on short SPY) Sharpe = -0.276, ann ret -1.40% — trips falsifier #1 (after-cost Sharpe < 0). (3) Annualized turnover = 983%, ~2.5x the spec's own 400% rejection line — the central 'continuous dial = low turnover' thesis is empirically FALSE because z drifts a little every day through a noisy gray zone and the 0.2% dust filter doesn't catch it; trips falsifier #5. (4) Avg next-day SPY return when z>1 = +0.047% over 410 days (POSITIVE) — term-structure inversion marks local vol-spike bottoms that mean-revert UP, so the short-SPY dial systematically shorts into the bounce; trips falsifier #4 (non-negative SPY coef when stressed). Regime reality: backwardation/stress is NOT a rare tail here (24.2% of days), so the proportional dial is partially short during the many false-alarm blips that resolve upward — re-introducing exactly the whipsaw it claims to cut. The 2022 TLT-leg drag (bonds fell with stocks) is real and the long-TLT leg adds negative carry. SECONDARY data-feasibility bug: the engine's ctx.closes() COMPACTS nulls PER SYMBOL (engine.ts L125-138), so the three VIX arrays are NOT index-aligned — ^VIX has 2767 bars vs 2766 for VIX9D/VIX3M (a 2026-05-25 holiday gap). The spec's stated implementation ('walk ctx.closes aligned arrays, they are equal-length up to i') is wrong; naive index-zipping silently pairs mismatched dates for the entire tail, corrupting the z-score. The family also needs 'tail_hedge' added to DEFENSIVE_FAMILIES (currently {trend,defensive,rotation}) or registration as 'trend' to get the risk-off allocator floor.
redteam.fix: The data and compute ARE feasible (series exist free/daily in cache; indicators.zscore/closes exist), so this is a dead EDGE, not a dead pipe. To salvage anything: (a) MUST align the three VIX series by DATE not index (build a date->value map, drop dates missing in any series) — without this the signal is silently corrupt. (b) Drop the TLT leg or make it conditional on stock-bond correlation being negative (it failed in 2022). (c) Raise the dial's dead-zone dramatically (only engage at z>1.5-2.0, not z>0.5) to stop being short during the 24% routine-backwardation days and to cut turnover toward the 400% line — but my test shows even the engaged tail shorts into bounces, so this likely just shrinks a negative number toward zero rather than turning it positive. (d) Replace 'short SPY when stressed' with 'long convexity' (the mechanism that actually pays in crashes) — but that reintroduces the theta bleed this variant was built to avoid, contradicting the premise. None of these is a small tweak; the core claim (negative-correlation = positive crisis alpha) does not survive contact with the data.

scored.name: Concordance-Breadth De-Gross Overlay (regime veto on the momentum sleeve)
scored.family: ML-concordance confidence gate / sizer on directional sleeves (meta-labeling wrapper)

scores.name: Concordance-Breadth De-Gross Overlay (regime veto on the momentum sleeve)
scores.requiredFixes: FATAL, not fixable as specified. (1) The wrapped base sleeve concord_momentum does not exist in registry.ts (8 sleeves: xs_momentum, low_vol, sector_rotation, factor_momentum, cross_asset_trend, tail_hedge, ts_trend, st_reversal); the real momentum.ts is plain 12-1 with an SPY<200d cash gate, no concordance. Build/register it or retarget the real xs_momentum. (2) The breadth input is uncomputable in-engine: StrategyContext (types.ts) exposes only bars()/closes()/regime; the 6-model concordance lives in prediction.ts (UI path) and needs iTransformer ONNX + atm_iv/iv_skew/pcr/sentiment/alt-data absent from AlignedData, so 'newDataNeeded: none' is false and 'reuse per-name concordance' has nothing to reuse. Redefine breadth as OHLCV-only (e.g. % of universe above 200d SMA, or % with positive 12-1 momentum) so the no-look-ahead context can produce it single-pass. (3) The falsifier is currently un-runnable: metrics.ts computes RAW Sharpe ((annRet-rf)/annVol, rf=0) with no beta regression anywhere; curveCorrelation gives rho-to-SPY but never subtracts beta-explained return. Implement true SPY-residualized (Jensen-alpha) Sharpe + alpha t-stat before any 'net-of-beta must rise' test. (4) Drop or ablate the redundant CRISIS/vixTermRatio legs — they are already live in momentum.ts (SPY<200d cash), tailhedge.ts (backwardation OR CRISIS), and allocator.ts (crisisGross/riskOffGross); prove incremental MaxDD cut beyond the existing stack, not vs no gate. (5) Validate thresholds out-of-sample (the four named crash wins 2018Q4/Mar2020/2022/Aug2024 are in-sample tail-fitting) and stress the Aug2024 V-shape whipsaw, where the >0.55 AND contango re-gross hysteresis converts a 3-day round-trip into a realized loss + missed rebound.
scores.oneLineRationale: A beta-timing de-gross overlay whose only novel input (cross-sectional 6-vote concordance) is uncomputable in the TS engine, wrapping a base sleeve (concord_momentum) that does not exist, with a net-of-beta falsifier that the raw-Sharpe metrics engine cannot even measure — Fatal feasibility finding plus two Serious (near-zero net-of-beta alpha; hidden short-gamma not orthogonality) force a CUT.

spec.name: Concordance-Breadth De-Gross Overlay (regime veto on the momentum sleeve)
spec.family: ML-concordance confidence gate / sizer on directional sleeves
spec.mechanism: A pure RISK overlay, not a stock picker. Momentum's worst crashes cluster in low-breadth, high-disagreement regimes (Daniel-Moskowitz 2016; corroborated by arXiv:2512.11913 where mechanical-factor tail risk concentrates as agreement/breadth deteriorates). Signal: AGGREGATE cross-sectional concordance, the average internal 6-model agreement across the whole universe. When most names show internal model DISAGREEMENT (concordance near coin-flip), forward momentum returns are low and left-tailed; the overlay cuts sleeve gross BEFORE the drawdown, parking capital in cash. This is the genuinely Sharpe-lifting piece: it does not add a correlated long stream, it removes exposure precisely in the regime that drags the base sleeve's Sharpe down, truncating the left tail at near-zero turnover.
spec.exactSignal: Computed daily, acted on at 21d cadence plus a guarded weekly cut-only check: breadth=mean over all universe names of concordance_i (same 6 OHLCV votes as spec 1). agreementDispersion=stdev of concordance_i across names. Regime score R=breadth in [0,1]. stressGate = (R<0.45) OR (ctx.regime.vixTermRatio!=null AND vixTermRatio>1.0 backwardation) OR (ctx.regime.regime=='CRISIS'). The concordance breadth is the NEW orthogonal input; VIX term-structure and CRISIS are existing confirmations so the overlay never acts on a single noisy day.
spec.entryRule: Not a position-initiator; it scales the gross of the concord_momentum sleeve (spec 1). At each 21d rebalance set grossMultiplier=clamp((breadth-0.40)/0.30,0,1): breadth>=0.70 -> full gross; breadth<=0.40 -> 0 (all cash); linear between. If stressGate is true on the rebalance day, hard-cap the multiplier at 0.4 regardless.
spec.exitRule: Re-gross only when breadth recovers above 0.55 AND vixTermRatio<=1.0 at a rebalance (hysteresis band 0.40-0.55 prevents whipsaw). Between rebalances the multiplier is held; one mid-week emergency check may CUT (never raise) gross if breadth collapses below 0.35.
spec.instruments: Acts on the same ~74-name long-only equity sleeve; the 'position' is cash vs equity gross. No new instruments; uses ^VIX/^VIX9D already in AlignedData.
spec.positionSizing: Output is a scalar in [0,1] multiplying the concord_momentum sleeve gross. Freed capital sits in cash (earns rf). Gross never exceeds the base sleeve's gross, so leverage<=1.5 is untouched. Because it moves only on 21d cadence plus rare emergency cuts, added turnover is minimal: de-grossing sells the whole sleeve at most a few times per year.
spec.newDataNeeded: none - all in manifest (OHLCV for breadth; ^VIX/^VIX9D already aligned in the engine).
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: Tracked metric: realized forward 21d sleeve return CONDITIONED on the breadth bucket at decision time (low<0.45, mid 0.45-0.70, high>0.70), EW halflife 90d on the live sim book. Rule: maintain mean & downside-deviation of forward returns per bucket. (i) The overlay is justified only while low-breadth forward returns are materially worse (lower mean AND fatter left tail) than high-breadth; quantify de-gross edge = highBucketSharpe - lowBucketSharpe. While >0 keep the linear map. (ii) If over a trailing 252d window the low-breadth bucket's realized return is NOT worse than high-breadth (edge<=0), the breadth signal has decayed/crowded -> widen the off threshold toward 0 (effectively disable de-grossing) and flag. (iii) Feed the overlaid sleeve's realized Sharpe into allocator.ts so the meta scales the whole sleeve; the overlay's own multiplier is logged daily for audit.
spec.crisisBehavior: This is the orthogonal, crisis-helpful piece. In 2018Q4, Mar2020, 2022 and the Aug2024 spike breadth collapses and vixTermRatio flips into backwardation BEFORE or AT the drawdown, so the overlay is FLAT-to-positive (in cash) while the base sleeve would be down. It does not make money long crisis (no long-vol/long-bond leg) but it removes the loss; its standalone stream is mostly zero with positive skew (occasional avoided crashes), hence low corr-to-equity and modest standalone Sharpe. Its VALUE is the Sharpe lift it gives the COMBINED sleeve, not its own level.
spec.implementationNotes: Implement as a thin decorator around spec-1's Strategy, or as logic inside concord_momentum.generate(): compute breadth over ctx.universe by reusing the per-name concordance already computed for the picker (single pass), derive grossMultiplier + stressGate from ctx.regime (vixTermRatio, regime), and scale every returned weight by grossMultiplier; emit confidence=breadth so allocator.ts benches the sleeve when breadth is low. The mid-week emergency cut fits the rebalanceDays handling: keep rebalanceDays=21 but allow a guarded re-check (compare current breadth to last-rebalance breadth) that only reduces gross. Add breadth-bucket forward-return tracking to scripts/backtest/meta.ts alongside spec 1's hit-rate tracker. Test: run the overlay ON vs OFF on the 11y walk-forward; it must cut MaxDD and lift Sharpe, not merely cut return.
spec.falsifier: REFUTED if, on the 11y walk-forward, applying the breadth de-gross to the concord_momentum sleeve does not improve its after-cost, net-of-beta Sharpe (it must rise, not merely lower vol with proportional return loss) AND does not cut MaxDD by at least 20% (that would mean breadth fires in the wrong regimes: cutting gross in recoveries, staying long into crashes). ALSO refuted if the daily tally shows the low-breadth bucket's trailing-252d forward Sharpe is >= the high-breadth bucket's over the full sample (breadth carries no risk information), in which case the overlay is removed.
spec._family: ML-concordance confidence gate / sizer on directional sleeves (meta-labeling wrapper)

redteam.kill: This overlay is a scalar gross multiplier on a long-only large-cap equity sleeve, not options or shorts, so option-spread and borrow-locate frictions do not apply. Only friction is about 10bps equity round trip per costs.ts. Added turnover is tiny since trades fire only on the few de-gross cycles per year, roughly 30 to 40 bps per year drag at peak and near zero in calm years. Gross edge from momentum-crash truncation dwarfs sub-50bps cost and capacity is not binding at retail scale. Real doubts live in other lenses.
redteam.fix: Hysteresis and cut-only emergency check and the dust filter already keep turnover near zero. Add a minimum multiplier change threshold to kill churn, log per-year overlay turnover and net cost drag in the walk-forward, and stage the cut to cash over a few sessions if scaled beyond retail.

redteam.kill: This is a long-only de-gross (beta-timing) overlay whose entire P&L mechanism is "cut equity gross to cash in bad regimes, restore it in good ones." By construction nearly all of its return contribution IS market beta — the spec itself states corrToLongOnlyEquity=0.15, that its standalone stream is "mostly zero," and that "its VALUE is the Sharpe lift it gives the COMBINED sleeve, not its own level." Strip beta and almost nothing is left: a de-gross timer has a net-of-beta standalone Sharpe near zero; the apparent "lift" is reduced beta/vol/MaxDD during drawdowns, not orthogonal alpha. Worse, the harness cannot even measure the claimed metric: src/lib/backtest/metrics.ts computes a RAW equity-curve Sharpe ((annRet - rf)/annVol, rf defaults to 0) with NO beta regression or SPY-residualization anywhere in metrics/engine/allocator/meta (confirmed: zero "beta" matches in those files; curveCorrelation gives rho-to-SPY but never subtracts beta-explained return). So the falsifier's "net-of-beta Sharpe must rise" is unmeasurable as built. The signal also doesn't exist: the wrapped sleeve "concord_momentum (spec 1)" is not in registry.ts (8 sleeves, none named concord_momentum), and "concordance" in this repo is only a single-stock UI card (ConcordanceCard.tsx, iTransformer vs 6-model ensemble for put-selling) — there is no cross-sectional 6-OHLCV-vote breadth across 74 names in the backtest engine. Finally, the de-gross thresholds (0.45/0.40/0.55/0.70/0.35, 0.4 cap, hysteresis) are hand-tuned on the same 11y walk-forward and the named "crisis" wins (2018Q4, Mar2020, 2022, Aug2024) are the in-sample episodes — in-sample tail-fitting, and the actual firing trigger (VIX backwardation / CRISIS regime) is a crowded beta-timing signal, not a new orthogonal edge.
redteam.fix: (1) Implement true net-of-beta evaluation first: regress the overlaid sleeve's daily returns on SPY over the walk-forward, report the alpha-t-stat and the residual (Jensen-alpha) Sharpe — the overlay must lift the RESIDUAL Sharpe, not the raw one. As built, metrics.ts cannot do this, so the falsifier is currently untestable. (2) Build and register the concord_momentum base sleeve and a real cross-sectional breadth signal in the engine before claiming any wrap behavior; right now both the sleeve and the aggregate concordance signal are absent from registry.ts/the backtest path. (3) Test the breadth signal's incremental value AFTER already conditioning on vixTermRatio and CRISIS regime (orthogonalize breadth to those existing gates) — if breadth adds nothing beyond the VIX-term/regime de-gross, it is redundant beta timing. (4) Validate thresholds out-of-sample (e.g., pick on 2014-2019, test 2020-2025) since the four named crash wins are in-sample; honestly expect MaxDD reduction with proportional return loss (the spec's own "merely lower vol" refutation case) rather than a genuine net-of-beta Sharpe lift.

redteam.lens: Orthogonality & Tail: is the de-gross overlay's return stream truly uncorrelated to a long-only / short-vol book in the LEFT TAIL (crises), or does it carry hidden beta / shared short-gamma, and would it have ADDED to or OFFSET the put-book drawdown in Mar2020 / 2022 / Aug2024?
redteam.kill:
The spec claims orthogonality (corrToLongOnlyEquity 0.15, "genuinely orthogonal input"), but on this lens it fails three ways.

(1) HIDDEN SHORT-GAMMA, NOT ORTHOGONALITY. The breadth input is, by the spec's own words, "the same 6 OHLCV votes as spec 1" — i.e. it is built from the same directional models that generate the momentum/long P&L. In prediction.ts those 6 are MeanReversion/Momentum/Volatility/OptionsImplied/TechnicalComposite/Sentiment; the directional ones are all trend/price models, so aggregate cross-sectional concordance is a near-monotone transform of cross-sectional trend agreement = exactly the state variable that drives long-only and momentum returns. A signal manufactured FROM the book's own directional votes is not an orthogonal input; it is a nonlinear function of the book's own exposure. Its low full-sample correlation (0.15) is the classic artifact of a mostly-flat stream with sparse jumps — it tells you nothing about left-tail conditional correlation, which is the lens. Conditioned on the left tail (breadth collapses precisely when the long book is selling off), the overlay's payoff is +1 only when the book is down — that is the definition of a short-gamma-OFFSETTING leg whose tail behavior is 100% co-determined by the book, the opposite of independent.

(2) IT IS A DUPLICATE OF GATES ALREADY IN THE BOOK — NO NEW LEFT-TAIL PROTECTION. stressGate = (R<0.45) OR (vixTermRatio>1.0) OR (regime=='CRISIS'). Two of those three triggers are ALREADY live: momentum.ts cuts to cash on SPY<200d; tailhedge.ts already fires long TLT+GLD on vixTermRatio>1.0 OR CRISIS; and allocator.ts already sets riskOffGross=0.85 and applies offensiveTiltRiskOff=0.7 on CRISIS/SPY<200. So in Mar2020/2022 the overlay mostly re-cuts gross the allocator and the momentum 200d filter have already cut. The incremental left-tail benefit over the existing stack is small and largely double-counted, while the spec credits the FULL avoided crash to the overlay.

(3) IT IS NOT EVEN COMPUTABLE IN THE FALSIFIER'S ENGINE — AND FAILS ITS OWN TAIL CASES. The falsifier demands an 11y walk-forward showing >=20% MaxDD cut. But (a) the concord_momentum sleeve and the per-name concordance do not exist in registry.ts / the backtest engine; the 6-vote ensemble lives only in the UI path (prediction.ts) and depends on atm_iv/iv_skew/pcr/sentiment features absent from AlignedData — so 'reuse the per-name concordance already computed' has nothing to reuse in-engine. (b) On the lens's own three events: Mar2020 (gap crash) breadth collapses AT or AFTER the gap, and the 21d cadence + 'hold between rebalances' means the cut can lag the fast leg — it does not truncate the gap, it sells the bottom. 2022 was a SLOW grind where breadth stayed mid and VIX9D/VIX spent long stretches in contango (no backwardation despite a 25% drawdown) — the gate under-fires, so the overlay neither offsets nor avoids the 2022 put-book bleed. Aug2024 was a 3-day VIX spike that round-tripped inside one 21d window; the emergency cut-only check de-grosses into the spike low and the hysteresis (re-gross only >0.55 AND vixTermRatio<=1.0) keeps it in cash through the V-shaped recovery — converting a transient into a realized loss and a missed rebound, i.e. it ADDS to drawdown-adjusted P&L in exactly the Aug2024 case named. The whipsaw cost in V-shaped events is the dominant left-tail risk and the spec waves it away as 'minimal turnover.'

Net: the overlay's tail behavior is co-determined by the long book (shared short-gamma, not orthogonal), it largely re-cuts exposure already cut by existing regime gates, and it is gap-lagging / whipsaw-prone in two of the three named crisis windows. Under skepticism, the edge does not survive this lens.
redteam.fix: To make a defensible orthogonality/tail claim: (1) Replace the self-referential breadth input with an input NOT derived from the book's own directional votes — e.g. cross-sectional return dispersion or pairwise correlation computed independently of the 6-model picker — and prove low CONDITIONAL (left-tail) correlation, not just full-sample 0.15: bucket days by the long book's worst-decile returns and show the overlay's incremental contribution there. (2) Decompose incremental value vs the EXISTING stack: run allocator+momentum-200d+tailhedge ON, then add the overlay, and report only the marginal MaxDD/Sharpe delta — the falsifier must be 'beats the existing regime gates,' not 'beats no gate.' (3) Stress the named events explicitly: report the overlay's realized P&L through Mar2020 (gap-lag), 2022 (slow contango grind where backwardation rarely fires), and Aug2024 (V-shape whipsaw with the >0.55 AND contango re-gross hysteresis) — it must not turn the Aug2024 round-trip into a realized loss + missed recovery. (4) Make it actually computable in-engine: implement a real concord_momentum sleeve and an OHLCV-only concordance in the backtest path before any falsifier run, since the current 6-vote ensemble depends on options/sentiment features absent from AlignedData. Until (1)-(4) pass, treat the overlay as a redundant regime de-gross, not an orthogonal tail diversifier.

redteam.kill: The overlay's core input is not computable in the TS backtest engine, and the sleeve it claims to wrap does not exist. (1) NON-EXISTENT BASE SLEEVE: The spec repeatedly de-grosses "the concord_momentum sleeve (spec 1)." There is no concord/concord_momentum strategy anywhere in src/lib/strategies (registry.ts exports only xs_momentum, low_vol, sector_rotation, factor_momentum, cross_asset_trend, tail_hedge, ts_trend, st_reversal; meta.ts PRIORS lists the same 8 — no concord_momentum). The actual momentum sleeve (momentum.ts) is plain 12-1 cross-sectional momentum with zero concordance, zero per-name votes, and zero model agreement. The overlay decorates a phantom. (2) THE SIGNAL IS FABRICATED / NOT FREE+DAILY+COMPUTABLE: The spec asserts breadth = mean of concordance_i = "same 6 OHLCV votes as spec 1," computable "single pass" by "reusing the per-name concordance already computed for the picker." That description is false. In this codebase "concordance" (ConcordanceCard.tsx) means iTransformer-vs-statistical-ensemble agreement, NOT 6 OHLCV votes. Computing it requires (a) the iTransformer ONNX model — GPU-trained on 172 features (itransformer-features.ts), never invoked by the engine, which only sees closes/bars/vix/vix9d; and (b) a 6-model ensemble (prediction.ts) that consumes atm_iv, iv_skew_25d, pcr_volume, options_sentiment, earnings dates, net_sentiment, plus FRED/FINRA/Finnhub/Wikipedia alt-data. None of these live in AlignedData; none are free + daily + survivorship-clean over the 2015-2026 backtest. So the engine's no-look-ahead StrategyContext physically cannot produce concordance_i, and "newDataNeeded: none - all in manifest" is wrong. The signal is uncomputable in sim, which also makes the falsifier (11y walk-forward ON vs OFF, bucketed forward returns) un-runnable. (3) REGIME OVERLAP / DOUBLE-COUNT: Even if breadth were computable, the only genuinely orthogonal piece is the breadth term; the stressGate's other legs (vixTermRatio>1 backwardation, regime=='CRISIS') ARE feasible (VIX/VIX9D cached 2015-06 to 2026-06, full coverage) but already drive de-risking elsewhere — momentum.ts goes to cash on SPY<200d, and meta.ts vol-targets the whole book and de-risks in HIGH_VOL/CRISIS. A breadth/VIX-backwardation gate fires in the SAME low-breadth, high-vol regimes those existing veto/vol-target layers already cover, so the incremental MaxDD truncation is largely redundant, not additive. (4) IN-SAMPLE / DECAY: thresholds (0.45, 0.40-0.70 ramp, 0.55 hysteresis, 0.35 emergency) are hardcoded with no out-of-sample basis; breadth de-gross is a known, crowded post-2016 momentum-crash patch (Daniel-Moskowitz), so the claimed 0.4 standalone Sharpe / Sharpe-lift is optimistic. Net: under default skepticism the edge does not survive — the data/compute is infeasible as written and the wrapped sleeve is fictional.
redteam.fix: To make this even testable: (a) First actually build and register the concord_momentum sleeve it wraps, or retarget the overlay at the real xs_momentum sleeve. (b) Redefine breadth as something the OHLCV-only engine CAN compute single-pass and free+daily: e.g. cross-sectional % of universe above 200d SMA, or % with positive 12-1 momentum, or the dispersion/sign-agreement of 3-4 OHLCV-derived signals (trend, RSI, ROC, vol-regime) per name — explicitly DROP the iTransformer+ensemble concordance, which needs a GPU model and options/alt data not in AlignedData. (c) Drop the redundant CRISIS/vixTermRatio legs (or prove incremental DD cut beyond momentum's SPY<200d cash rule and meta.ts vol-targeting via an ablation: overlay-on vs the existing vetoes alone). (d) Walk-forward the OHLCV-breadth thresholds out-of-sample rather than hardcoding 0.45/0.55. Only after (a)-(b) is the falsifier runnable; until then survives=false.

scored.name: VIX Term-Structure Carry (gated, kill-switched SVXY contango harvest)
scored.family: Carry sleeves (bond curve / equity yield / vol term-structure)

scores.name: VIX Term-Structure Carry (gated, kill-switched SVXY contango harvest)
scores.requiredFixes: Three independent FATAL findings; each alone forces CUT. (1) ORTHOGONALITY (fatal, verified): host product is cash-secured put SELLING (CLAUDE.md L5) = short-gamma VRP; SVXY -0.5x is the SAME premium, same sign. Left-tail corr converges to +1 in gap crises (Feb-2018, Aug-2024, Mar-2020 limit-down) where the EOD gate is still ON at the prior close and cannot front-run an intraday gap. It can only do nothing or AMPLIFY the put-book tail — never offset. Anti-diversifying for THIS book. (2) RISK DISCIPLINE IS STRUCTURALLY NOT EXECUTED (fatal, verified): meta.ts L61 + engine.ts L193 — cadence is gated by the META handle's rebalanceDays (default 5); the sleeve's rebalanceDays:1 is honored ONLY standalone. The 'slow-in/instant-out same-day flatten' that the spec calls 'the entire risk discipline' never runs on the live/meta path; a spike inside a 5-day window is not flattened. (3) FEEDBACK-LOOP VAPORWARE (verified): the 6%-1d bench / 8%-20d-DD halve / 5-clean-day restore throttle does NOT exist in allocator.ts or meta.ts — only inverse-vol + sharpeFloor -0.15 + confidence bench. Plus: allocator gives family 'carry' NO risk-off tilt (allocator.ts L102-103) and riskParityPower 1.0 scales it UP into calm pre-gap; SVXY is ABSENT from scripts/backtest/.cache so 0.35 is asserted not measured; SVXY -1x→-0.5x splice on 2018-02-27 makes the tail budget look-ahead; engine same-close fill (engine.ts L9-14) hides kill-switch gaps; VIX3M/VIX9D cache starts 2015-06-02 so the '2014' claim is uncheckable. To even attempt rescue would require: a true daily same-day hook overriding meta sampling, real throttle code in allocator.ts/meta.ts, a leverage-consistent SVXY series in cache, beta-neutralized Sharpe + backwardation CVaR reporting, and cutting the cap to 3-4%. Even then the spec's own falsifiers (a) Sharpe<0.2 and (d) no meta lift are the likely outcomes.
scores.oneLineRationale: Same short-gamma VRP the put-selling host already harvests (anti-diversifying, tail-corr→+1 in the gap crises the EOD gate cannot front-run), its 'entire risk discipline' is structurally bypassed by the 5-day meta cadence, and its throttle/feedback loop is uncoded — three independent Fatal findings, beta-neutral honest Sharpe ~0.05.

spec.name: VIX Term-Structure Carry (gated, kill-switched SVXY contango harvest)
spec.family: Carry sleeves (bond curve / equity yield / vol term-structure)
spec.mechanism: Volatility risk premium / roll-yield. VIX futures sit in contango ~80% of the time because hedgers overpay for forward volatility; a short-vol position harvests the positive roll-down (front-month converges down toward spot). This is a genuine, separately-priced risk premium (the variance risk premium), distinct from equity directional beta. HONEST CAVEAT (the load-bearing part): it is the MOST crowded and decay-prone carry in the brief, it correlates POSITIVELY with equities in normal times, and it has a catastrophic short-gamma left tail (XIV -96% in a day, Feb-2018). So the only defensible version is tiny, deeply gated, hard kill-switched on backwardation, and expressed through SVXY (-0.5x, capped-loss ETF) rather than naked short VXX/futures. Its job is a small carry top-up in calm regimes, deliberately FLATTENED before stress — not crisis alpha.
spec.exactSignal: Contango proxy from manifest tickers: termRatio = ^VIX (30d) / ^VIX3M (93d). Deep contango when termRatio < 0.92 (front cheap vs 3M => steep upward futures curve => positive roll). Also require absolute calm: ^VIX < 20 AND ^VIX9D/^VIX (the engine's vixTermRatio) < 1.0 (9d not above 30d, i.e. no short-term fear spike). KILL-SWITCH: if termRatio >= 1.0 (backwardation) OR ^VIX >= 22 OR vixTermRatio >= 1.0, force weight to 0 same-day (this is a daily, not monthly, check — the one exception to the slow cadence).
spec.entryRule: Checked daily (rebalanceDays=1 for the gate, but trades only when state flips). ON state (all calm+contango conditions met): hold SVXY at the small vol-targeted weight. OFF state (any kill-switch trips): flat (0% SVXY, hold cash). Require the ON conditions to hold for 2 consecutive days before entering (debounce) to avoid whipsaw entries into a forming spike. Confidence = 0.4 when ON, 0 when OFF (benches the sleeve in the allocator).
spec.exitRule: Exit to cash IMMEDIATELY (same-day EOD) the first day any kill-switch condition trips — no waiting for a rebalance date. This asymmetry (slow in, instant out) is the entire risk discipline. Because exits are event-driven and rare in calm regimes, turnover stays ~20-40 round trips/yr, survivable at ETF spreads.
spec.instruments: SVXY (-0.5x short-VIX-short-term ETF; capped structural loss, no margin call, no naked gamma). Strictly NOT naked short VXX/UVXY/VIX futures. ^VIX/^VIX3M/^VIX9D used as signals only (never traded).
spec.positionSizing: Deliberately small and vol-targeted: weight = min(0.10, 0.04 / annualizedVol(ctx.closes('SVXY'),20)). SVXY realized vol ~25-40%, so weight lands ~0.10-0.15 of sleeve capital and is HARD-CAPPED at 0.10 of total book regardless — this sleeve never exceeds 10% gross. No leverage. The cap is the tail-risk budget: even a one-day SVXY -30% shock costs the book <=3%.
spec.newDataNeeded: Add SVXY (free daily ETF, user-authorized) to ALL_BACKTEST_SYMBOLS. ^VIX/^VIX3M/^VIX9D already in manifest. That is the only addition.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: METRIC: trailing 40d realized Sharpe AND trailing 20d max-drawdown of the sleeve's live sim equity. RULE (two-part): (1) the meta-allocator scales the sleeve by blended-Sharpe/vol like every sleeve (so a decaying contango premium starves it automatically); (2) a HARD sleeve-level deleveraging rule — if trailing-20d sleeve maxDrawdown exceeds 8%, halve the position cap (0.10 -> 0.05) and require 5 clean days before restoring it; if a single-day sleeve loss exceeds 6%, force weight 0 and bench for 10 trading days. This drawdown-throttle is the explicit increase/decrease rule and the defense against the crowding/decay the web check flagged.
spec.crisisBehavior: 2018Q4: NEGATIVE if held — but the termRatio>=1.0 / VIX>=22 kill-switch flattens it before the worst (Feb-2018 and Q4-2018 both backwardated first). Mar-2020: flat by design (gated off in Feb as the curve inverted) — the discipline that separates this from XIV. 2022: mostly off (elevated VIX kept it flat much of the year). Aug-2024: flat/off (vixTermRatio spiked >1 on the yen unwind, kill-switch fires). HONEST: this is NOT crisis alpha — it is a calm-regime carry top-up whose entire value proposition is being ABSENT during crises. If the gate ever fails to flatten in time it is the book's worst tail; hence the 10% hard cap.
spec.implementationNotes: New Strategy in src/lib/strategies/voltscarry.ts, family 'carry', longOnly:true (long SVXY, never short), instrument:'etf', rebalanceDays:1, warmupBars:60. In generate(ctx): read termRatio = lastClose('^VIX')/lastClose('^VIX3M'); read ctx.regime.vix and ctx.regime.vixTermRatio (already populated). Kill-switch checks first -> return {weights:[],confidence:0} if any trip. Else if 2-day debounce of calm+contango holds, emit SVXY weight = min(0.10, 0.04/annualizedVol(ctx.closes('SVXY'),20)). The 20d-maxDD throttle and 1-day-loss bench live in the runner/meta layer keyed off the sleeve's realized equity (use maxDrawdown() from indicators.ts on the trailing sleeve-equity slice). Register in registry.ts; add SVXY to universe ALL_BACKTEST_SYMBOLS.
spec.falsifier: Walk-forward 2014-2025 INCLUDING Feb-2018 and Mar-2020. REFUTED if any of: (a) after-cost standalone Sharpe < 0.2; (b) the gate fails to flatten before the Feb-5-2018 and Mar-2020 vol spikes such that the sleeve's worst single-day contribution to the total book exceeds 3% (proves the kill-switch is too slow and the tail is uncontrolled); (c) full-sample equity correlation > 0.6 (then it is just leveraged long-equity beta, not a separable variance premium); (d) adding it to the meta does not raise after-cost Sharpe at all (premium already arbitraged/decayed away). Given crowding, (a) or (d) is the most likely real-world outcome — treat this sleeve as the speculative one and size it last.
spec._family: Carry sleeves (bond curve / equity yield / vol term-structure)

redteam.lens: COSTS & CAPACITY: options spread / short-borrow / turnover / min-granularity vs fixed capital / capacity
redteam.kill: The standard retail cost worry — options spreads and short-borrow — does NOT apply here: the spec deliberately expresses the trade through long SVXY (an ETF), not naked short VXX/futures. In the repo cost model (src/lib/backtest/costs.ts), instrument:'etf' charges only spread(3bps)+slippage(2bps)=5bps/side => ~10bps round-trip, and dailyBorrowCost() returns 0 for a long position. So no options spread, no locate, no borrow. Turnover is genuinely low: the sleeve is 10% of book, toggles ~20-40 round trips/yr (event-driven exits), so sleeve-level frictions are ~30 round-trips x 10bps = ~30bps/yr at the modeled rate. Gross calm-regime VRP/contango carry on short-vol is large (SVXY compounded strongly 2011-2017 in contango), so gross edge >> ~30bps modeled round-trip cost by a wide margin, and on the total book it is 10% of that. Capacity is a non-issue: SVXY trades tens of millions of shares/day; a retail 10%-of-book sleeve has unlimited capacity and whole-share granularity is irrelevant at that size. So on the COSTS & CAPACITY lens specifically, the edge is NOT killed. HOWEVER the model is one-directionally optimistic in two real ways: (1) it charges a flat 3bps/side spread for SVXY regardless of regime, but real SVXY bid/ask is ~5-15bps/side in calm markets and blows out to 50-100+ bps/side on high-VIX days — i.e. exactly when the same-day kill-switch forces the exit; the model systematically underprices the trades that matter most. (2) SVXY carries ~0.95-1.38%/yr expense ratio plus -0.5x daily-reset decay, and the model charges ZERO holding cost for a long ETF (only the price series itself, if real SVXY closes are used, captures part of the decay). These are real frictions but bounded: ER/decay ~1.4%/yr on a sleeve whose calm-carry gross is plausibly 8-15%/yr, and the stress-exit slippage hits a 10%-of-book position a handful of times a year. They haircut, not kill.
redteam.fix: Override DEFAULT_COSTS for SVXY with a regime-dependent ETF cost block: ~8bps/side spread in calm, and a stress multiplier (3-5x) when VIX>=20 so the kill-switch exit is charged at realistic blow-out spreads — this is the single most important fix because every kill-switch exit is a high-VIX trade. Add an explicit ETF holding-cost line (~1.4%/yr daily-accrued on long SVXY notional) to capture expense ratio + daily-reset decay, since the model currently charges zero carry on longs. Re-run the falsifier walk-forward with these realistic frictions; if standalone after-cost Sharpe still clears the spec's own 0.2 threshold (a), keep it; if not, cut it. Because the sleeve is hard-capped at 10% of book, even pessimistic cost assumptions cap total-book drag at ~15-25bps/yr, so this is a sizing-protected concern, not an existential one.

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY (net of beta + look-ahead): is the claimed Sharpe real after stripping market beta and after costs, with no look-ahead in the signal?
redteam.kill: The claimed 0.35 after-cost Sharpe is unmeasured, beta-contaminated, and rests on a look-ahead structural break. (1) SURVIVORSHIP/LOOK-AHEAD: SVXY's leverage was changed from -1x to -0.5x effective close 2018-02-27 (confirmed: ProShares 8-K; the old -1x SVXY fell ~-90% on 2018-02-05, same event that liquidated XIV). A 2014-2025 walk-forward on one SVXY adjusted-close series splices a -1x fund (2014-Feb2018) onto a -0.5x fund (post-Feb2018), yet the spec's entire tail budget ('one-day SVXY -30% shock costs <=3%', 10% hard cap) is calibrated to the -0.5x regime that did NOT exist for the first ~4 backtest years. Using post-2018 leverage to bound pre-2018 tail risk is textbook look-ahead. The falsifier's own test (b) — worst single-day book contribution >3% at Feb-5-2018 — is essentially guaranteed to fail with the real -1x SVXY of that date. (2) THE KILL-SWITCH DOESN'T RUN DAILY IN PRODUCTION: the meta-allocator (scripts/backtest/meta.ts and src/lib/strategies/meta.ts) samples every sub-strategy on its OWN rebalanceDays:5 cadence — it only calls s.generate(ctx) every 5 days. The sleeve's rebalanceDays:1 is honored only standalone. So the 'slow in, instant out, same-day flatten' asymmetry — described as 'the entire risk discipline' — is structurally not executed on the live/meta path; a spike that forms and crashes inside a 5-day window (exactly Feb-5-2018, Aug-5-2024) is not flattened in time. (3) THE THROTTLE IS VAPORWARE: the 6%-1-day bench, 8%-20d-DD halving, and 5-clean-day restore rules do not exist in allocator.ts or meta.ts; the only real lever is 126-day inverse-vol + a -0.15 Sharpe floor, both far too lagged to stop a one-day short-gamma loss. (4) BETA IN DISGUISE: the spec itself admits rho-to-equity ~0.45 and positive equity correlation in normal times. Short-vol carry IS short equity-vol; the VRP it harvests is largely compensation for equity-crash risk, so stripping market beta removes most of the apparent edge and leaves a thin residual whose left tail is the un-diversifiable equity crash. (5) NO BACKTEST EXISTS: SVXY is absent from scripts/backtest/.cache (never fetched or tested); 0.35 is asserted, not measured — and the spec's own falsifier calls (a) Sharpe<0.2 or (d) zero meta lift 'the most likely real-world outcome.' Net of beta and with the correct pre-2018 -1x tail, honest Sharpe is ~0.
redteam.fix: To even attempt rescue: (a) source a leverage-consistent SVXY series — either reconstruct a synthetic constant -0.5x short-VIX-futures index back to 2011 from VIX front-two futures, OR start the live sample at 2018-03-01 and never claim Feb-2018 protection (which removes the only crisis the spec leans on). (b) Move the kill-switch OUT of the 5-day meta cadence: give the sleeve a true daily same-day evaluation hook in the runner that overrides meta sampling, and actually implement the 6%/8% throttle in allocator.ts/meta.ts — none of it is coded today. (c) Report Sharpe of the BETA-NEUTRALIZED residual (regress sleeve returns on SPY, keep alpha) plus its conditional left-tail (CVaR in backwardation), not the raw Sharpe. (d) Run the real walk-forward with SVXY in the cache including a -1x proxy through Feb-2018 before any number is quoted. Most likely the beta-neutral after-cost Sharpe lands ~0.0-0.1 and fails the spec's own falsifier (a)/(d) — at which point the honest call is to drop it, consistent with the brief's instruction to 'size it last / treat as speculative.'

redteam.lens: ORTHOGONALITY & TAIL: is the return stream truly uncorrelated to the put-selling / long-only book in the LEFT TAIL (crises), or does it share hidden beta / short-gamma and amplify the put-book drawdown?
redteam.kill: SVXY is the SAME risk premium as the host book, not an orthogonal diversifier. PutStrike's core product is cash-secured put SELLING on large caps (CLAUDE.md/README) — itself a short-vol, short-gamma, variance-risk-premium harvest that is long-correlated to equities in calm and blows up in vol spikes. SVXY (-0.5x short-VIX) is short the IDENTICAL VRP with the identical sign. So this is a leveraged concentrate of the put-book's already-dominant risk, not a separable premium. The stated corrToLongOnlyEquity 0.45 is FULL-SAMPLE and structurally understates the LEFT-TAIL correlation, which converges toward +1 with the put-book in crises (both short the same gamma). The spec's sole defense — 'gated off before stress' — fails on the precise events that matter: the gate is an EOD check on signal tickers (^VIX/^VIX3M, ^VIX9D/^VIX), but Feb-5-2018 (SVXY/XIV ~ -83% to -96% intraday) and Aug-5-2024 (record one-day VIX spike) were SINGLE-SESSION GAPS where the curve was still in contango at the PRIOR close — so the sleeve was ON when SVXY crashed intraday, and a same-day-EOD exit cannot front-run an intraday gap. The Mar-2020 limit-down days behave the same way. The spec's own falsifier (b) anticipates and refutes exactly this. The allocator compounds it: the sleeve's family 'carry' is in NEITHER DEFENSIVE_FAMILIES nor OFFENSIVE_FAMILIES (allocator.ts lines 102-103, 167-170), so it gets NO risk-off tilt-down, and the inverse-vol risk-parity base (riskParityPower 1.0) scales it UP during the calm, low-realized-vol, high-carry-Sharpe regime that immediately precedes gap events. The 20d-maxDD throttle is reactive/EOD and cannot prevent the one-day gap that is the entire tail. Net: in slow crises (2022, Mar-2020 ex-gaps) it is genuinely flat and contributes ~0 (neither offsets nor adds); in the gap crises (Feb-2018, Aug-2024, Mar-2020 limit-down) it is still ON and ADDS to the put-book drawdown with matching short-gamma sign. It NEVER offsets the put-book's left tail — it can only do nothing or amplify. That fails the orthogonality-and-tail lens.
redteam.fix: Strongest fix is to drop SVXY entirely: the put-selling host already harvests this VRP, so adding it is anti-diversifying (raises tail concentration, lowers book-level orthogonality). If retained as a pure speculative carry top-up: (1) cut the hard cap to 3-4% gross (not 10%) so a single-day SVXY -80% gap costs <=2-3% — and size it dead last; (2) classify the sleeve into a family the allocator de-grosses in risk-off (or add 'carry' to OFFENSIVE_FAMILIES so the risk-off offensiveTilt applies and the defensive floor never lifts it), because as coded the allocator gives it NO regime tilt-down and inverse-vol-scales it UP into the calm before a gap; (3) replace the EOD gate with an intraday/overnight stop or pre-emptive flatten on rising VIX9D so it is not still ON at the prior close before a gap; and (4) accept and document that this has ZERO crisis-alpha value — its best case is being absent, never offsetting. Even mitigated, the spec's own falsifiers (a) standalone after-cost Sharpe < 0.2 and (d) no lift to meta Sharpe are the most likely real-world outcomes, so treat it as the sleeve that must justify itself against simply doing nothing.

redteam.kill:
Data IS partly feasible but the edge dies on three verified, load-bearing defects.

DATA/COMPUTE (verified against the engine and live Yahoo feed):
- SVXY: fetchable free/daily, 3018 bars from 2014-06-02. termRatio = ctx.closes('^VIX')/ctx.closes('^VIX3M') is computable (both in data.closes); ctx.regime.vix and ctx.regime.vixTermRatio (9D/VIX) are populated by makeContext. So the signal computes. BUT:
- The free ^VIX3M / ^VIX9D history only starts 2015-06-02 (cached files confirm). The spec's stated walk-forward 'INCLUDING 2014' is impossible on this feed; you get 2015-2025 (Feb-2018 and Mar-2020 are covered, 2014 is not).
- The headline risk defense — the 20d-maxDD halving + 1-day-loss bench 'drawdown-throttle' — DOES NOT EXIST in the current meta/allocator. allocator.ts only does inverse-vol/Sharpe blending + confidence/Sharpe-floor benching. The spec's explicit increase/decrease rule is unimplemented vaporware that requires new runner code.

INSTRUMENT REGIME CHANGE (fatal): SVXY was -1x until ~2018-02-27 and -0.5x after. The single price series the backtest consumes contains a -83% SINGLE-DAY crash on 2018-02-06 (143.64 -> 24.48) that belongs to the -1x product. The spec's entire tail budget ('one-day SVXY -30% shock costs <=3%') assumes -0.5x throughout. The backtest splices two structurally different instruments and the spec never segments for it.

LOOK-AHEAD TAIL FLATTERING (fatal): the engine executes at the SAME close on which the kill-switch is computed (engine.ts lines 9-14 admit this 'slightly flatters fills'); the live runner trades NEXT session. On kill-flip days SVXY routinely gapped -8% to -18.3% the same day (2017-05-17 -18.3%, 2017-08-17 -16.6%, 2016-09-09 -16.0%). My sim with same-day exit shows worst book day -1.69% (passes falsifier b), but a live next-session exit eats those gaps — the backtest's tail control is optimistic exactly on the days that matter.

EDGE TOO THIN: my reconstruction shows the sleeve is ON ~51% of days (NOT 'tiny/deeply gated' — termRatio<0.92 is the MEDIAN state, median ratio 0.878), with standalone GROSS Sharpe ~0.28, annual return only ~0.6% on ~2.2% vol at the vol-targeted size. After costs (7.8 exits/yr + entries at ETF spread) this falls below the spec's own falsifier (a) bar of 0.2. The spec itself predicts (a) or (d) — premium crowded/decayed — as the likely outcome, and the data agrees.
redteam.fix: If pursued at all (it should be deprioritized to last, per the spec's own instruction): (1) Build the actual drawdown-throttle in the meta/runner before deployment — it is currently fictional and is the only real tail defense. (2) Hard-segment the SVXY series at 2018-02-27 and either drop pre-2018 (use only the -0.5x era) or explicitly model the -1x -> -0.5x change; never report a single spliced backtest. (3) Force NEXT-session execution in the backtest for this sleeve specifically (override the engine's same-day-close convention) so the kill-switch gap is paid, not hidden; re-check that worst single-day book contribution stays <3% under that honest fill. (4) Re-run only on 2015-2025 (the true free-data window) and accept that the spec's 2014 claim is uncheckable. (5) Tighten the entry threshold far below 0.92 (e.g. <0.85, ~25th pctile) so it is genuinely a rare deep-contango top-up rather than a half-the-time short-vol position, and re-measure after-cost Sharpe; if it does not clear 0.2 net AND raise meta Sharpe (falsifier d), kill it.

scored.name: MinVar-Shrink overlay: shrinkage global-minimum-variance / max-diversification combiner, deployed only on the orthogonal-sleeve sub-book and championed by a live challenger gate

scores.name: MinVar-Shrink overlay (shrinkage GMV/MaxDiv combiner on orthogonal sub-book, challenger-gated)
scores.requiredFixes: Two Fatal findings force CUT; nothing rehabilitates the OVERLAY (the sleeves are fine, the combiner is not). To even make it testable: (1) Build HRP/ERC first and validate it as the baseline — every gate and falsifier in this spec references hrpWeights/combiners.ts, which DO NOT EXIST in the repo (Grep returns zero). (2) The increment-over-HRP edge (+0.05 Sharpe on a 6%-vol sub-book = 0.30%/yr) is 2-6x smaller than the churn cost the falsifier permits (5-15%/day weight turnover = 0.63-1.89%/yr at the engine's own 5bps/side). Replace daily GMV/MaxDiv + online selection + daily phi steps with monthly reweighting and a no-trade band, and tighten the turnover falsifier from 15%/day to ~3%/day, measured AFTER churn cost. (3) The 'orthogonal' set is collinear: tailhedge.ts and crossasset.ts BOTH buy TLT+GLD, so K is effectively <2 and GMV/MaxDiv is exactly the ill-conditioned case shrinkage cannot rescue — dedupe to one crisis sleeve plus a non-Treasury diversifier, or drop GMV for ERC (the spec's own lambda=0 falsifier predicts ERC suffices). (4) The 1.1 claim is ~2x the arithmetic ceiling of its inputs: measured priors are cross_asset_trend 0.57 / tail_hedge 0.06 (production.ts), sqrt-combine = 0.57; re-target to the honest ~0.45-0.55 sub-book number. (5) Options are uncharged: the backtest engine costs the 'put hedge' at the flat 5bps ETF rate (engine.ts:238 tradeCost), optionCommissionPerContract is never invoked, and black-scholes.ts is wired only into screen/analyze routes, never the backtest — so the put leg cannot be priced or held in the 11y walk-forward. (6) AlignedData carries only vix/vix9d (align.ts) — no vix3m/skew and no vega-slope sleeve code, so the VIX3M/SKEW-gated hedge is not computable without engine surgery. (7) Left-tail: the long-TLT/GLD hedge depends on equity-bond correlation staying negative (fails 2022 joint drawdown); add a RATES/duration beta gate, replace part of B with actual long-gamma SPY puts, exempt the hedge from the 63d-Sharpe starvation rule in risk-off, and require non-negative 2022 sub-window Sharpe before phi>0.
scores.oneLineRationale: Two Fatal findings (cost-negative at every permitted turnover; signal not computable and its HRP baseline does not exist) mandate CUT — this is a churn-generating optimizer over two collinear TLT/GLD sleeves whose ~0.57 ceiling lives in the sleeves, not the combiner, with a 1.1 claim ~2x its inputs.

spec.name: MinVar-Shrink overlay: shrinkage global-minimum-variance / max-diversification combiner, deployed only on the orthogonal-sleeve sub-book and championed by a live challenger gate
spec.mechanism: BEHAVIORAL/structural estimation-error mechanism. Once GENUINELY orthogonal sleeves exist (the brief names three: multi-asset trend on non-equities; VIX-term-structure-gated de-gross/tail hedge; vega-neutral term-structure slope), the max-Sharpe combination of low-correlation streams is well-approximated by the global-minimum-variance (GMV) or maximum-diversification (MaxDiv) weights — when expected Sharpes are similar, MV-optimal weights ARE max-Sharpe and you avoid estimating fragile expected returns. The catch the web check confirms: GMV/MaxDiv are the allocators MOST destroyed by covariance misspecification, so they are usable ONLY with Ledoit-Wolf shrinkage AND only on a SMALL set of TRULY decorrelated sleeves (low condition number). This is the aggressive complement to HRP: HRP is the robust default; MinVar-Shrink is allowed to run ONLY on the orthogonal sub-book and ONLY while it out-trades HRP on the live sim.
spec.exactSignal: On the orthogonal sub-book (sleeves whose trailing-126d beta to SPY via indicators.beta is |beta|<0.25 — this is the tail/crisis-loading gate the brief demands, not just full-sample corr), build Cov from trailing 252d returns with Ledoit-Wolf constant-correlation shrinkage: lambda_LW estimated analytically = clip(sum of estimated var of sample correlations / sum of squared (C_ij - C_bar)^2, 0, 1); fall back to lambda=0.5 if the estimate is unstable. GMV weights w = Cov^{-1} 1 / (1' Cov^{-1} 1), long-only via projected-gradient (no shorting of sleeves). MaxDiv variant: maximize (w'sigma)/sqrt(w'Cov w). Pick whichever (GMV or MaxDiv) has the higher trailing-63d realized Sharpe on the sim (online selection). Matrix inversion is safe here because K is tiny (3-5 orthogonal sleeves) and shrinkage guarantees PSD/well-conditioned.
spec.entryRule: The orthogonal sub-book receives a fixed risk budget B (e.g. 35% of gross) carved out BEFORE the HRP/legacy combiner allocates the remaining 65% to the equity-beta sleeves. Within B, sleeves are weighted by MinVar-Shrink (GMV or MaxDiv). A sleeve enters the sub-book only if |trailing beta|<0.25 AND it passes the TAIL check: its worst-5%-day average return is NOT more negative than the book's (rejects false-orthogonality short-gamma sleeves the brief warns about). Requires >=120d of returns for a stable shrunk 252d-window estimate.
spec.exitRule: A sleeve exits the sub-book the day its trailing beta breaches 0.35 (orthogonality lost) or it fails the tail check (left-tail loading appears). The entire MinVar-Shrink overlay is suspended (sub-book reverts to equal-risk-contribution within B) if K_orthogonal<2 or the shrunk Cov condition number >50 (estimation unsafe). Hard kill: if the overlay's trailing-quarter realized Sharpe < HRP's, the challenger gate (below) routes B back to HRP.
spec.instruments: The orthogonal sleeves' instruments only: multi-asset trend on TLT/IEF/LQD/GLD/DBC/VNQ/UUP; ^VIX/^VIX9D/^VIX3M-gated SPY de-gross or defined-risk put hedge (priced by the existing black-scholes.ts engine); optional vega-neutral term-structure slope. No single-name dollar-neutral L/S (brief's false-orthogonality short-gamma warning). Gross within B<=0.5; total book gross<=1.0 (1.5 ceiling).
spec.positionSizing: Within budget B: w_sleeve = MinVar-Shrink weight, scaled so the sub-book hits its own 6% annual vol target (orthogonal streams are low-vol; this lets B punch above its capital share in Sharpe terms without breaching gross). Per-sleeve cap 0.6 of B. The equity-beta remainder (1-B) is combined by Spec 1 (HRP). Total gross = B_gross + (1-B)_gross <= 1.0.
spec.newDataNeeded: Free ETFs to ADD (user-authorized): UUP (dollar), and the bond/commodity ETFs already in manifest (TLT/IEF/LQD/GLD/DBC/VNQ). ^VIX9D/^VIX3M/^SKEW already present for the term-structure-gated hedge. black-scholes.ts already prices the defined-risk put hedge. No paid data.
spec.dailyFeedbackLoop: THE METRIC: trailing-63d realized NET Sharpe of the orthogonal sub-book under MinVar-Shrink, tracked against a CHALLENGER running the SAME sub-book under HRP. RULE (champion/challenger): the sub-book's budget B is split B*phi to MinVar-Shrink and B*(1-phi) to HRP; phi_t = clip(phi_{t-1} + 0.03*sign(Sharpe63_minvar - Sharpe63_hrp), 0, 1). Additionally a sleeve's weight inside MinVar-Shrink is forced to 0 for the day if its realized 63d Sharpe<-0.25 (broken-sleeve bench) OR its beta gate trips. So the overlay continuously earns or loses its budget vs the robust HRP baseline, and individual orthogonal sleeves are starved the moment they stop diversifying.
spec.crisisBehavior: This sub-book is the crisis-alpha engine: multi-asset trend rotates long TLT/GLD (positive skew, profits in 2018Q4 and 2022 bond/dollar moves and Mar2020 flight-to-quality once trend confirms ~2-3 weeks in); the VIX-term-gated de-gross/put hedge is POSITIVE in Mar2020 and the Aug2024 vol spike (^VIX9D/^VIX backwardation trigger). MinVar-Shrink TILTS the budget toward whichever of these is currently decorrelating most, so the sub-book is FLAT-to-POSITIVE in 2018Q4/Mar2020/2022/Aug2024 while the equity remainder draws down — that net-of-beta convexity is the Sharpe lift the all-equity meta could never produce. Caveat: trend is slow, so a V-shaped crash (Mar2020) gives the hedge sleeve, not the trend sleeve, the crisis P&L.
spec.implementationNotes: Add minVarShrink(returns, beta-gate, tail-gate) in a new src/lib/strategies/combiners.ts exporting hrpWeights (Spec 1) + gmvShrink + maxDivShrink (long-only projected gradient, ~80 lines; reuse indicators.stdev/mean/correlation/beta). In meta.ts, partition active sleeves into orthogonalSet (|beta|<0.25 & tail-pass) vs betaSet BEFORE allocateStrategies; allocate budget B to orthogonalSet via combiners, (1-B) to betaSet via HRP/legacy, then concat and feed the existing blend loop. Champion/challenger phi and theta persist in the sim DB across days (deterministic given history). Unit-test GMV on a 2-sleeve negatively-correlated synthetic (must overweight the lower-vol, more-decorrelating sleeve).
spec.falsifier: Walk-forward 11y with the real orthogonal sleeves (multi-asset trend + VIX-term-gated hedge) in the sub-book. FALSIFIED if MinVar-Shrink's after-cost net-of-beta sub-book Sharpe does not exceed HRP's by >=0.05, OR if its weights are unstable (median day-over-day sleeve-weight turnover >15%, signalling estimation-error churn the shrinkage was supposed to kill), OR if removing shrinkage (lambda=0) does NOT degrade it (which would prove the inversion was never the binding constraint and a simpler equal-risk-contribution overlay suffices). In live A/B, FALSIFIED if phi drifts to 0 (HRP wins) within two quarters — meaning the extra optimization buys nothing over robust HRP on this book.

redteam.lens: COSTS & CAPACITY: realistic retail frictions — options spread, short borrow/locate, turnover, min-granularity vs fixed capital, capacity
redteam.kill: As a combiner overlay, the only honest edge is the increment over the HRP baseline. The spec's own falsifier sets that bar at +0.05 Sharpe on a 6%-vol sub-book = just 0.30%/yr of sub-book NAV. Yet the same falsifier tolerates up to 15% median day-over-day sleeve-weight turnover. At the codebase's own modeled cost (costs.ts DEFAULT_COSTS = 5 bps/side spread+slippage), 15%/day one-way churn = 1.89%/yr drag; even 5%/day = 0.63%/yr. Net contribution = -0.06 to -0.26 Sharpe: the overlay is cost-negative at EVERY turnover level it permits itself — edge is 2x-6x smaller than round-trip cost. MinVar (GMV/MaxDiv) re-estimated daily on a 252d cov, with online GMV-vs-MaxDiv selection, daily phi champion/challenger steps, and a 63d-Sharpe per-sleeve kill switch, is a turnover generator; shrinkage stabilizes the matrix inverse but not the day-over-day weight PATH, which is the cost driver. This is DeMiguel-Garlappi-Uppal (2009) on K=4: optimized weights rarely beat equal-risk OOS net of cost. Worse, the backtest engine (engine.ts) costs the 'defined-risk put hedge' at the 5 bps ETF rate — black-scholes.ts is wired only into the screener/trade app-routes, never the backtest path; real SPY-put friction (~5% of premium + $0.65/contract) and roll cost are uncharged, and optionCommissionPerContract is never invoked by tradeCost(). The 6% vol target implies internal leverage that multiplies traded notional and amplifies the drag. The spec's own live falsifier ('FALSIFIED if phi drifts to 0 within two quarters') is the cost lens already forecasting collapse to HRP.
redteam.fix: Charge real options bid/ask (~5% of premium) and roll cost in the backtest cost path, not the flat 5 bps ETF rate; wire black-scholes.ts pricing into engine cost accounting for the put leg. Replace daily GMV/MaxDiv re-optimization with monthly reweighting plus a no-trade band (only act when a sleeve's GMV weight moves >5%), killing the estimation-churn turnover the overlay generates. Tighten the falsifier turnover threshold from 15%/day to ~3%/day and require the +0.05 Sharpe lift to be measured AFTER the modeled churn cost. If, post-fix, the live A/B phi still does not drift to 0 within two quarters, the increment is real; otherwise default to the robust HRP/equal-risk overlay, which already harvests the genuine crisis-alpha that lives in the SLEEVES (trend + VIX-gated hedge), not in this combiner.

redteam.lens: SHARPE REALITY (net of beta + look-ahead): is the claimed 1.1 Sharpe real after stripping market beta and after costs, with no look-ahead in the signal?
redteam.kill: The claimed 1.1 Sharpe is ~2.4x the arithmetic ceiling of its own inputs. This is a COMBINER (GMV/MaxDiv) over existing sleeves, not a source of new alpha, so its Sharpe is bounded by the sleeves it combines. The repo's OWN walk-forward backtest (research/backtests/summary.json) measured the exact two orthogonal sleeves this overlay runs on: cross_asset_trend = Sharpe 0.57 (corr-to-SPY 0.28), tail_hedge = Sharpe 0.06 (corr -0.07). The third sleeve (vega-neutral term slope) is marked 'optional' and was never backtested; the nearest VIX-convexity candidates scored costRobustness 3-4 / verdict REVISE-CUT. For ~uncorrelated streams the best achievable combined Sharpe is ~sqrt(0.57^2+0.06^2) = 0.57, and that ONLY if the optimizer perfectly zeroes the dead hedge sleeve. GMV pours weight into the lowest-vol sleeve and MaxDiv into the most-decorrelated sleeve -- both point at the flat-return tail hedge -- so realized combined Sharpe lands AT or BELOW 0.57, not 1.1. On the lens itself: net-of-beta is the ONE thing this spec gets right (|beta|<0.25 gate, measured corr 0.28/-0.07 -> the sub-book is genuinely beta-stripped), but once you strip beta there is almost no alpha left; the residual is ~3-4%/yr of nearly-beta-free return that vol-targets to a ~0.5 Sharpe. The repo's own results.md (lines 69-77) already states the verdict: diversifying these correlated-to-each-other-low streams 'compresses volatility but cannot raise Sharpe much -- the ceiling is roughly the average sleeve Sharpe'; the path to higher Sharpe is ADDING new alpha (market-neutral L/S, VRP), not a cleverer combiner. Look-ahead hazards compound it: online GMV-vs-MaxDiv selection and champion/challenger phi adaptation are pure 63d performance-chasing (DeMiguel-Garlappi-Uppal, cited in the repo's own allocator, shows this loses OOS), and GMV weights are the single most in-sample-fragile object in portfolio theory -- Ledoit-Wolf reduces but never removes the in-sample-fit advantage that evaporates live. The spec's own falsifier concedes the kill: '>15% day-over-day weight turnover' (estimation churn), 'removing shrinkage does NOT degrade it -> simpler ERC suffices', and 'phi drifts to 0 (HRP wins) within two quarters'. Plus HRP itself doesn't exist in the codebase (no hrpWeights/combiners.ts; Grep returns nothing) -- the entire champion/challenger gate is built against an unimplemented baseline.
redteam.fix: Re-target the claim to reality: this is a low-Sharpe (~0.5), genuinely-low-beta DIVERSIFIER whose value is drawdown/vol compression and mild crisis convexity, NOT a 1.1-Sharpe standalone. To make even that honest: (1) drop the dead tail_hedge sleeve from the optimizer's variance objective or hard-cap its weight -- a 0.06-Sharpe sleeve that GMV/MaxDiv will over-weight on low-vol/decorrelation is pure drag; keep it only as conditional risk-off insurance carried by a separate floor, not as a min-var input. (2) Kill the online GMV-vs-MaxDiv selection and the phi performance-chasing; fix a single robust rule (equal-risk-contribution or shrunk-GMV) chosen out-of-sample, since the spec's own falsifier predicts the optimization buys nothing over ERC. (3) Actually build and measure HRP first (it is referenced but absent), then report the MinVar-Shrink-minus-HRP delta on the SAME two real sleeves over 11y -- if it is not >=+0.05 and stable, ship ERC. (4) Replace the 1.1 expectedAfterCostSharpe with the measured sub-book Sharpe (~0.45-0.55) and judge the overlay on whether it lifts the TOTAL book's net-of-beta Sharpe via decorrelation, not on a standalone number its inputs cannot support. (5) If a real >1 Sharpe is the goal, the repo's own results.md tells you the only path: add a genuinely new beta-neutral alpha sleeve (the discovery file's BAB/QMJ/residual-momentum L/S candidates), not another combiner over the same low-Sharpe streams.

redteam.lens: ORTHOGONALITY & TAIL: left-tail (crisis) decorrelation vs the long-only / short-vol put-selling book, hidden beta and shared short-gamma, and whether the sleeve would have ADDED to or OFFSET the put-book drawdown in Mar2020 / 2022 / Aug2024.
redteam.kill: The spec's crisis-alpha claim rests on two sleeves that, as actually implemented in this repo, do not deliver left-tail convexity reliably — and the MinVar-Shrink overlay actively de-weights the one that does. (1) The "VIX-term-structure-gated tail hedge" is NOT a long-put / long-gamma position. In src/lib/strategies/tailhedge.ts it goes LONG TLT+GLD on the backwardation/SPY<200d/VIX>=35 trigger. That is a flight-to-safety BET whose payoff is contingent on the equity-bond correlation staying negative. In a rate-shock crisis that contingency fails: 2022 was a joint equity+bond drawdown (TLT ~ -30%), so the "orthogonal" sleeve was LONG the same factor (duration/risk-asset) that was selling off, i.e. it carried hidden short-rate-vol/long-beta-to-bonds exposure and would have ADDED to, not offset, a put-book that also bleeds in 2022. The |beta-to-SPY|<0.25 gate does not catch this because the shared exposure is to RATES, not SPY. (2) The cross-asset trend sleeve (crossasset.ts) is trend-gated (totalReturn(252)>0 AND price>200dSMA) and slow; the spec itself concedes it is wrong-footed at a V-bottom (Mar2020, Aug2024), so the entire crisis offset falls on the single long-TLT/GLD hedge sleeve at exactly the moments it is least reliable. (3) The tail-check gate ('worst-5%-day avg return' over the trailing 252d) is computed on calm-period data and is structurally BLIND to latent short-gamma, which by construction only shows up IN the crisis after the window has passed — so it cannot reject the false-orthogonality short-gamma the brief warns about until after the damage.
