(4) Most damning for THIS overlay: GMV/MaxDiv minimize trailing-252d variance and the online rules (63d-Sharpe selection; 'realized 63d Sharpe < -0.25 -> weight 0') will STARVE the pure-insurance hedge sleeve in the calm quarter before a crash (insurance bleeds small negative carry, low Sharpe prior of 0.06 in production.ts), i.e. it sells the airbag right before the crash. The existing allocator added defensiveFloorRiskOff precisely to fix this; MinVar-Shrink discards that floor inside budget B. Net: full-sample corr ~0.15 is real, but LEFT-TAIL orthogonality is not established for 2022 (hidden duration beta) and is timing-fragile for the V-shaped crises (slow trend + de-weighted hedge). Uncertain the edge survives this lens -> survives=false.
redteam.fix: Three concrete fixes would let a revised version survive: (1) Replace the equity-bond-correlation-dependent long-TLT/GLD hedge with an actual long-gamma instrument (the defined-risk SPY put hedge the spec mentions black-scholes.ts can price) for at least part of budget B, so the crisis payoff is convex and does NOT depend on the bond-equity correlation — this directly neutralizes the 2022 rate-shock failure. (2) Add a RATES/duration beta gate (|beta to TLT or to the 10y| < threshold), not just |beta to SPY|, so the gate can see the shared duration exposure that the 2022 joint-drawdown exploited; and stress-test orthogonality on the crisis sub-windows (Mar2020, Oct2022, Aug2024) explicitly, not just full-sample. (3) Carry the insurance sleeve through a defensive FLOOR inside budget B (port defensiveFloorRiskOff into the MinVar overlay) and EXEMPT the gated hedge sleeve from the 63d-Sharpe<-0.25 starvation rule when the regime is risk-off, so the overlay cannot sell the airbag pre-crisis. The spec's own falsifier should be extended: require the sub-book net-of-beta Sharpe to beat HRP specifically on the crisis sub-windows and to be NON-NEGATIVE in 2022, not just full-sample, before phi is allowed above 0.

redteam.kill: The spec is not computable in the current TS engine and its baseline does not exist. (1) HRP ("Spec 1") — the robust default, the live challenger, and the champion/challenger benchmark the whole gate (phi chasing Sharpe63_minvar - Sharpe63_hrp) and the falsifier depend on — is NOT IMPLEMENTED anywhere in the repo. The production combiner is allocateStrategies (inverse-vol + Sharpe/regime tilt), not HRP. The A/B test and falsifier reference code that doesn't exist. (2) The orthogonal sub-book has only 2 implemented sleeves (crossAssetTrend, tailHedge), not the 3-5 the spec assumes, and both are LONG TLT+GLD — the SAME two instruments — so the streams are near-collinear, not 'truly decorrelated/low condition number.' GMV/MaxDiv on a 2-asset, crisis-correlated covariance is precisely the ill-conditioned case Ledoit-Wolf shrinkage cannot rescue, and the spec's own exitRule suspends the overlay when K_orthogonal<2. (3) The backtest engine (runBacktest) trades shares of underlying symbols on a calendar; AlignedData carries closes/bars only — there is NO option leg, no black-scholes integration, no contract/decay/roll P&L. The 'defined-risk put hedge priced by black-scholes.ts' cannot be held or priced in the walk-forward engine, so the 11y falsifier can only run a long-TLT/GLD proxy, not the actual hedge. (4) ^VIX3M and ^SKEW are fetchable free from Yahoo but are NOT wired into the engine: buildAligned plumbs only vix and vix9d, AlignedData has no vix3m/skew fields, and regime computes only vixTermRatio=vix9d/vix. The 'VIX9D/VIX3M/SKEW-gated' hedge and 'vega-neutral term-structure slope' sleeve cannot be computed without engine surgery, and the slope sleeve has no code at all. (5) Regime fragility: cross-asset trend is slow (252d/200d-SMA, 21d rebalance) and gives no crisis P&L in V-shaped crashes (spec concedes this for Mar2020); in 2022 long-TLT trend was a large LOSER (stocks and bonds fell together), and GMV would concentrate into the lower-vol Treasury sleeve right as it blew up. The 6% vol-target that 'lets B punch above its capital share' is leverage on a 2-sleeve crisis-correlated book — a blow-up vector, not a Sharpe source. Net: data is partly feasible to fetch, but the signal as specified is NOT computable in this engine, the baseline and challenger do not exist, and the 'orthogonal' set is collinear.
redteam.fix: To make it testable rather than survivable: (a) First actually implement HRP as a standalone combiner and validate it as the baseline, since every gate/falsifier in this spec is defined relative to it. (b) Extend AlignedData/buildAligned with vix3m and skew channels and add an option-position abstraction (or honestly downgrade the 'put hedge' to the existing long-TLT/GLD proxy and stop claiming black-scholes pricing). (c) Restrict the orthogonal set to genuinely independent streams — the current two both buy TLT+GLD, so dedupe to one crisis sleeve plus a non-Treasury diversifier (e.g. trend on DBC/UUP that does NOT also load long bonds) before any GMV is meaningful. (d) Drop GMV/MaxDiv entirely for K=2-3 collinear sleeves and use equal-risk-contribution — the spec's own falsifier says if removing shrinkage (lambda=0) doesn't degrade it, ERC suffices, which is the likely outcome here. (e) Run the 11y walk-forward with realistic 2022 bond-drawdown and a leverage cap that forbids the 6% vol-target from grossing the 2-sleeve book above 1.0 in any single-instrument-concentrated state. Even with all of this, the honest expectation is that phi drifts toward HRP/ERC within two quarters, which by the spec's own falsifier means the optimization buys nothing.

recommendedSleeves: xs_momentum (cross_sectional momentum, momentum.ts) — proven offensive equity core, prior Sharpe ~1.04; the actual return engine and the single best after-cost representative of the correlated equity cluster
low_vol (lowvol.ts) — defensive low-beta equity tilt, prior ~0.91; the LEAST-correlated of the equity sleeves, earns its keep by lowering equity-side vol, not by adding fresh beta

sector_rotation (rotation.ts) — equity breadth diversifier, prior ~0.66; kept as a thin third equity stream because it is already coded, cost-surviving, and the allocator inverse-vol-sizes it small (NOT a Sharpe-lifter, a stabilizer)

cross_asset_trend (crossasset.ts) — managed-futures TS-momentum on TLT/IEF/SHY/GLD/SLV/DBC/USO/UUP/VNQ/EEM/EFA, prior ~0.57, corr-to-equity ~-0.05; the ONLY structurally non-equity, positive-skew crisis-alpha stream that survives the tail-orthogonality test
tail_hedge (tailhedge.ts) — VIX-term-backwardation / SPY<200d / VIX>=35 gated long TLT+GLD, prior ~0.06; cash in calm (zero carry drag), fires right-tail convexity on the book's worst days

EXCLUDED from funding but kept registered & auto-starved: factor_momentum (collinear with xs_momentum, rho~0.66 — fold its risk budget into xs_momentum rather than double-buying SPY beta), ts_trend and st_reversal (cost-killed nulls the allocator already benches via sharpeFloor)
assembly.combinationAlgorithm: Use the EXISTING meta.ts + allocator.ts unchanged in structure — it is already the right combiner (inverse-vol equal-risk base + light prior-shrunk Sharpe tilt + regime de-gross + Moreira-Muir vol-target), and it beats HRP here (HRP single-linkage turnover on a K=5 / 126d corr matrix exceeds the ~0.3-0.5%/yr edge; the panel scored HRP RESEARCH_ONLY with no backtest). Concrete config (PRODUCTION_ALLOCATOR + meta): (1) riskParityPower=1.0 (inverse-vol equal-RISK base) so the two low-standalone-Sharpe orthogonal sleeves (cross_asset_trend, tail_hedge) get MEANINGFUL budget — converting decorrelation into a Sharpe lift, not just vol compression. (2) Light realized-Sharpe tilt shrunk 0.7 to the backtest prior (priorBlend=0.7; sharpeTiltStrength dialed DOWN from 0.45 to ~0.30 to suppress OOS performance-chasing, per DeMiguel-Garlappi-Uppal). (3) maxWeightPerStrategy=0.35 so no single equity sleeve dominates. (4) Regime overlay: risk-off (SPY<200d) riskOffGross=0.95 (sleeves self-de-risk — do NOT double-de-gross); CRISIS (VIX>=35) crisisGross=0.6; defensiveFloorRiskOff RAISED 0.15 -> 0.30 so the two convex sleeves are pre-paid insurance carried INTO drawdowns, never starved by their flat calm-period Sharpe (this is the consensus Calmar-vs-Sharpe compromise across panelists). (5) Add an offense-FAMILY gross cap ~0.65 (new, small allocator change) so the correlated equity cluster cannot collectively dominate book risk even though each sleeve is under its 0.35 cap. (6) Wrap the blended book in meta.ts vol-targeting at 10% annual, leverage-free (volScale=clip(0.10/exAnteVol, 0.25, 1.0)) using the existing sub-sleeve return-covariance estimator (exAntePortfolioVol). GATING CODE CHANGE before any of the above priors are trusted: wire indicators.ts beta() into metrics.ts + daily/perf.ts to report net-of-SPY-beta residual Sharpe, and set rfAnnual to the realized 3M T-bill path (FRED DGS3MO) instead of the hardcoded rf=0 — today's 1.04/0.91/0.66 priors are raw, rf=0, beta-inclusive bull-sample numbers and overstate every sleeve.
dataToAdd: FRED DGS3MO (3-month T-bill rate) — the realized risk-free path to set rfAnnual; without it every Sharpe silently credits ~1.8-5%/yr cash yield and the carry/defensive sleeves are judged excess-of-NOTHING. Free, FRED key already plumbed in fred.ts.

SPY daily returns as the regression market factor — already in the cache; the missing piece is WIRING beta() into the perf path so net-of-SPY-beta residual Sharpe and rolling 60d beta become MEASURED quantities, not assumptions. No new data, just plumbing.

^VIX3M (already referenced in MACRO_TICKERS) — confirm it is fetched and cached so the full VIX term-structure ratio (^VIX9D/^VIX/^VIX3M) drives tail_hedge's backwardation gate cleanly rather than a 2-point proxy.

UUP / DBC / USO daily bars — already in CROSS_ASSET_ETFS and cached; verify coverage so cross_asset_trend's dollar/commodity legs (the only thing that carried it through 2022) are live, not silently dropped by alignment nulls.

DO NOT add: factor ETFs as a long/short FAC-NEUTRAL spread (6-ETF cross-section too thin, after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe <=0), HYG for a BreadthCredit overlay (panel CUT it; redundant with existing de-gross), or any 500+ single-name residual-momentum feed (not in the free-daily manifest and out of the ship-in-days budget).
buildRoadmap: STEP 0 — Honesty plumbing (GATING, ~1 day): wire indicators.ts beta() into src/lib/backtest/metrics.ts (add residual-alpha Sharpe = regress sleeve daily returns on SPY, report (alpha_ann)/residualVol) and into src/lib/daily/perf.ts so the PerfProvider also emits rolling 60d beta + net-of-beta Sharpe. Set metrics.ts rfAnnual default from FRED DGS3MO instead of 0. Nothing else is trustworthy until this lands.

STEP 1 — Commit the evidence file: run the 11y walk-forward (scripts/backtest/run.ts + meta.ts over the existing .cache) and write research/results.md + research/backtests/*.json with BOTH raw and net-of-beta/rf-excess Sharpe per sleeve. Replace the asserted STRATEGY_PRIORS in production.ts with the committed, net-of-beta numbers (expect them to fall: 1.04->~0.6-0.7 net-of-beta, etc.).

STEP 2 — Lock the funded set in registry.ts/production.ts: fund xs_momentum, low_vol, sector_rotation, cross_asset_trend, tail_hedge; keep factor_momentum/ts_trend/st_reversal registered but rely on the sharpeFloor=-0.15 bench to starve them (no double-buying SPY beta).

STEP 3 — Allocator tuning (allocator.ts): set sharpeTiltStrength~0.30, defensiveFloorRiskOff~0.30, add an OFFENSE-FAMILY gross cap (~0.65) summed over momentum/rotation/defensive-equity families, and add cross_asset_trend's family to the risk-off defensive tilt set so it is carried in drawdowns. Add an orthogonality self-check: if a diversifier's rolling 63d corr-to-core or |60d beta-to-SPY| breaches ~0.4/0.3, halve its share until it re-decorrelates.

STEP 4 — Meta vol-target (meta.ts): confirm volTargetAnnual=0.10, minVolScale=0.25; verify exAntePortfolioVol uses the live sub-sleeve return covariance each rebalance so gross de-grosses automatically as cross-sleeve correlations spike.

STEP 5 — Neon sim wiring: confirm quant-db.ts persists per-day decision.detail, per-sleeve trailing+net-of-beta Sharpe, 63d corr-to-core, volScale, and the realized book equity curve so the feedback loop has a durable audit trail and the UI can render it.

STEP 6 — Vercel cron: vercel.json already hits /api/quant/run at 21:30 UTC Mon-Fri; harden it — ensure CRON_SECRET is set, hasRunFor() idempotency holds, maxDuration=60 is enough for ~110 symbols, and a failed run alerts rather than silently no-ops.

STEP 7 — UI: surface the self-tracking loop — per-sleeve funded weight, trailing vs net-of-beta Sharpe, benched/floored state + reason, book vol vs 10% target, current volScale, regime, and the rolling corr-to-core of the two diversifiers (the orthogonality monitor). This makes 'is it still working / still orthogonal?' visible.

STEP 8 — Validation gate before any capital: leave-one-crisis-out walk-forward (drop 2018Q4 / Mar2020 individually); require net-of-beta book Sharpe improvement to survive AND MaxDD/vol to improve. Ship only if the funded book's net-of-beta after-cost Sharpe clears ~0.95 and MaxDD stays near the prior ~13.5%.
assembly.feedbackLoopSpec: Runs each EOD on the Neon sim book via the seams that already exist (buildCurvePerfProvider single-sleeve PerfProvider) PLUS the Step-0 beta wiring. MEASURED DAILY per sleeve: (1) trailing-126d realized Sharpe + realized vol from its own live-sim equity curve; (2) NEW rolling 60d beta-to-SPY and net-of-SPY-beta residual Sharpe; (3) for the two diversifiers, trailing 63d realized correlation to the core equity book. ADAPTATION (all real in allocator.ts today except where noted): (a) inverse-vol equal-risk base + light prior-shrunk Sharpe tilt re-weights every rebalance, so a decaying sleeve is smoothly starved and a working one scaled up, capped 0.35. (b) BENCH: any sleeve with trailing-126d Sharpe < sharpeFloor=-0.15 and sampleDays>=40 goes to weight 0 that day (the mechanism that auto-zeroes the cost-killed reversal). (c) CONVICTION GATE: a sleeve emitting confidence<=0.05 (tail_hedge in calm contango, cross_asset_trend with no uptrends) is benched, so dead sleeves cost nothing. (d) REGIME TILT + DEFENSIVE FLOOR: in risk-off the defensive/trend family tilts up x1.2 and defensiveFloorRiskOff=0.30 guarantees the two convex sleeves a floor share so insurance is carried into the drawdown, not chased after. (e) ORTHOGONALITY GUARD (NEW, enabled by the beta wiring): if a diversifier's 63d corr-to-core drifts above ~0.4 OR its |60d beta-to-SPY| above ~0.3 (the 2022 stocks-and-bonds-down breakdown), halve its share until it re-decorrelates. (f) VOL-TARGET FEEDBACK: the 10% target recomputes ex-ante book vol from the live sub-sleeve return covariance each rebalance and scales gross down (floor 0.25) as cross-sleeve correlations spike, de-grossing automatically. The loop is honest about its limit: it is single-sleeve + reactive (the corr/beta guards lag the regime flip by their estimation window); the panel's fancier cross-sleeve 'hedge-payoff-ratio' and shadow-book A/B loops are NOT buildable on today's PerfProvider and are deliberately excluded as vaporware.
assembly.keyRisksAndMitigations: BIGGEST RISK — the 2022-style synchronized stocks-AND-bonds-down regime (positive stock-bond correlation, no VIX>=35 spike): both diversifiers degrade at once — cross_asset_trend's TLT/IEF legs lose and tail_hedge's flight-to-Treasuries fails because Treasuries are the thing being sold — so the book collapses back toward its all-equity SPY beta and realized Sharpe regresses toward the proven ~0.84 floor. Mitigations are PARTIAL and reactive: cross_asset_trend's trend filter drops bonds and rotates toward UUP/DBC/USO (the legs that actually carried 2022); the orthogonality guard halves a diversifier when corr-to-core>0.4 or beta>0.3; the vol-target de-grosses as cross-sleeve vol rises. But all three LAG the correlation flip by their estimation window, so a fast rate-shock bear still bleeds. SECONDARY RISKS: (1) priors are inflated — 1.04/0.91/0.66 are raw, rf=0, beta-inclusive bull-sample numbers; net-of-beta after-cost they fall materially, which is exactly why expected portfolio Sharpe is ~1.0, not >1.3 (Step-0/Step-1 make this honest before any capital). (2) Feedback loop is reactive and single-sleeve — it cannot pre-position; mitigated only by the pre-paid defensive floor. (3) Execution/cost drift — costs.ts uses flat spread+borrow; the commodity/EM legs are undercharged in stress, so keep the diversifiers on their existing 21d/3d cadences to bound turnover. (4) Operational — Vercel cron silent failure / Neon book divergence; mitigated by hasRunFor() idempotency, CRON_SECRET auth, and persisting the full decision audit trail.
assembly.whatWeRejectedAndWhy: REJECTED all market-neutral L/S and pair/spread candidates (ResMom-LS, ResMom-LH, OU-Z IWF/IWD growth-value, OU-Z XLY/XLP cyclical-defensive, defensive-minus-cyclical RV, FAC-NEUTRAL 6-ETF spread): every one is a CUT in the scoring — their measured after-cost net-of-beta Sharpe is <=0 on this 74-name/6-ETF universe (too small a cross-section for residual dispersion; ResMom needs 500+ names), and several carry a hidden SHORT-GAMMA left tail that CONCENTRATES crash risk on the put book (tail-orthogonality != mean-orthogonality). The OU pairs are empirically near-unit-root (half-lives 280d-4.7yr, no fast reversion) and the 'beta-neutral' spreads leave +0.3 to +0.63 residual SPY beta — long-the-market in disguise. REJECTED factor_momentum / FAC-ROTATE as a FUNDED sleeve: ~0.66 prior but rho-to-SPY 0.66-0.92, collinear with xs_momentum — registering it just re-buys SPY beta with no incremental net-of-beta lift; fold its budget into xs_momentum. REJECTED HRP-Shrink combiner: sound mechanism but the incumbent allocator is ALREADY inverse-vol, single-linkage turnover exceeds the ~0.3-0.5%/yr edge on a K=5 menu, and no backtest exists — RESEARCH_ONLY, not BUILD. REJECTED the BreadthCredit / Throttle-to-Trend / bond-curve-carry overlays: redundant with the allocator's existing riskOffGross/crisisGross + tail_hedge, require HYG (absent from cache) or an unbuilt shadow-book counterfactual, and add a 4th beta-correlated de-gross layer with net-of-beta Sharpe ~0. REJECTED the panel's fancier daily loops (cross-sleeve hedge-payoff-ratio sizing, shadow-book A/B theta): structurally unbuildable on today's single-sleeve PerfProvider — claiming them would be vaporware.
